project.goal: Migrate K-12 communication from three legacy portals to a unified React Native app
project.target_users: Students (grades K-12) and their guardians
project.timeline: Academic year delivery, pilot district by semester end

frontend.push_notifications: AWS SNS with APNs and FCM

backend.api: REST + WebSocket for realtime notification feeds

security.auth: Cognito user pools, MFA enforced for teacher and admin roles
security.ferpa: Row-level security enforcing guardian-student enrollment linkage

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration scope is well-scoped for the pilot district. Consolidating three legacy portals into a single app addresses the core guardian pain point of checking multiple platforms. Grade summary and teacher messaging are the highest-value features for early adoption.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Fastify on ECS Fargate is well-suited for the notification burst pattern when school-wide announcements are sent. The Redis pub/sub layer is essential for fanout to thousands of connected WebSocket clients simultaneously. The legacy portal matching step needs a deterministic deduplication strategy on student_id + guardian_email before credential migration.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA row-level security must be enforced at the PostgreSQL query layer — not just the application layer — to prevent guardian cross-visibility on students from other families. Certificate pinning on mobile clients mitigates MITM risks on school network infrastructure.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $3000/month budget is achievable on Fargate with auto-scaling for notification bursts. The single academic year timeline is tight but feasible if legacy migration tooling is built in parallel with the app.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The current plan addresses migration at a high level but needs a concrete reconciliation strategy for student IDs that appear in multiple legacy portals under different identifiers. Edge cases like divorced parents with separate guardian accounts must be explicitly handled.

frontend.app_structure: React Native monorepo with shared packages for notification hooks, grade display components, and legacy migration status screens
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login, Guardian Dashboard, Student Grade View, Announcement Feed, Teacher Message Thread, Migration Status, Push Notification Preferences
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state with optimistic updates on message send; Zustand for notification badge counts
frontend.performance_notes: Virtualized FlatList for announcement feeds; notification payload pre-fetching during app foreground transitions

backend.service_design: Fastify modular plugin architecture: auth-plugin, notifications-plugin, grades-plugin, messaging-plugin, migration-plugin
backend.api_patterns: REST for CRUD; WebSocket endpoint /ws/notifications for realtime push fanout to connected clients
backend.background_jobs: Celery-equivalent Fastify worker for legacy portal account matching and credential migration batches
backend.failure_handling: Dead-letter queue for failed push notifications; retry with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with separate app client configurations for student and guardian flows; MFA TOTP enforced for teacher and admin roles
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced via PostgreSQL row-level security policies; guardian_student_links table controls cross-student data access
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured audit log on every grade record access and teacher message view; Datadog log pipeline for FERPA compliance reporting

data.entities: students, guardians, guardian_student_links, teachers, assignments, announcements, grade_records, notification_subscriptions, legacy_portal_mappings, message_threads, message_turns
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security; Redis 7 for pub/sub fanout and session caching
data.retention_and_deletion: Notification logs auto-purged after 90 days; student records retained 5 years post-graduation per FERPA

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate with ALB; separate task definitions for API and WebSocket servers; auto-scaling on CPU 60% and connection count
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, Jest unit tests, Detox E2E tests for critical flows, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM with mobile RUM; custom metrics: push delivery rate, notification fanout latency, legacy migration success rate

ISS-001.title: Legacy portal student ID deduplication strategy undefined
ISS-001.detail: Previous plan described migration at a high level but did not specify how student IDs appearing under different identifiers across three legacy portals would be deduplicated before account creation.

ISS-002.title: Guardian cross-student visibility not enforced at database layer
ISS-002.detail: Authorization was described only at the application layer. PostgreSQL row-level security policies for guardian_student_links were absent from the data model section.

ISS-003.title: Push notification burst scaling not quantified
ISS-003.detail: School-wide announcements can trigger simultaneous push delivery to tens of thousands of devices. The plan lacked concurrency estimates and SNS throughput configuration for burst scenarios.

focus_issues.title: Push notification burst scaling not quantified
focus_issues.detail: School-wide announcements can trigger simultaneous push delivery to tens of thousands of devices. The plan lacked concurrency estimates and SNS throughput configuration for burst scenarios.

Add ECS task auto-scaling policy tied to WebSocket connection count
Document Firecracker notification delivery SLA targets

target_output.title: K-12 Unified Communication App — Migration Architecture v4
target_output.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant React Native application consolidating K-12 school communication from three legacy portals into a unified iOS and Android experience. Built on Fastify/Node.js 20 on ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL 16 and Redis pub/sub, the system delivers push notifications via AWS SNS to enrolled guardians and students, with row-level security enforcing guardian-student enrollment boundaries. Legacy portal accounts are migrated via a deterministic deduplication pipeline matching student_id and guardian_email composite keys. Peak push throughput scaled to 50K simultaneous APNs/FCM deliveries within 60 seconds for district-wide announcements.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native 0.74 clients on iOS 16+ and Android 12+ authenticate via AWS Cognito user pools and connect to a Fastify REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB for grade data, announcements, and teacher messages. A separate WebSocket server task on ECS handles realtime notification fanout using Redis pub/sub as the message broker — teachers publish announcements to Redis channels and all subscribed guardian/student clients receive push within 60 seconds. AWS SNS handles APNs and FCM delivery with per-platform retry policies. PostgreSQL 16 enforces FERPA row-level security on guardian_student_links, preventing cross-family data access at the database layer. The legacy migration service runs as an ECS one-off task matching accounts across three portals using a composite key of district_student_id and guardian_email.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native 0.74, TypeScript, React Navigation 6, Zustand, React Query. Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify 4, PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7. Push: AWS SNS with APNs sandbox/production endpoints and FCM v1 API. Auth: AWS Cognito user pools with TOTP MFA for teacher/admin roles. Infra: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, CloudWatch. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Detox E2E. Observability: Datadog APM, Datadog RUM for mobile crash tracking.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Guardian Dashboard: enrolled students list with grade summary cards and unread announcement badges. Announcement Feed: chronological school and district announcements with push notification deep links. Assignment Due Date Tracker: per-student assignment list sorted by due date with overdue highlighting. Teacher Messaging: threaded conversation per teacher-guardian pair with read receipts. Grade Record View: semester grade history per course section. Push Notification Preferences: per-student subscription controls for announcement categories. Legacy Migration Status: per-account migration progress screen showing portal mapping results.
target_output.system_components: Cognito User Pool (separate app clients for student, guardian, teacher flows), ALB with WAF, ECS Fargate REST API cluster (auto-scaling 2-8 tasks on CPU 60%), ECS Fargate WebSocket cluster (auto-scaling 1-6 tasks on active_connection_count CloudWatch metric, scale-out at 8000 connections per task), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 ElastiCache with pub/sub channels per school, AWS SNS (APNs and FCM platform application endpoints, fanout to 50K devices in under 60s at default SNS throughput limits with batched publishes), Legacy Migration ECS task.
target_output.workflows: Legacy Account Migration: the migration service reads legacy portal export files, applies composite key matching (district_student_id + guardian_email), creates Cognito user accounts with temporary passwords, inserts legacy_portal_mappings records, and triggers a forced password reset notification — duplicates flagged to district admin for manual resolution. Announcement Push Fanout: a teacher or admin posts an announcement via the CMS API; the backend publishes to a Redis pub/sub channel keyed by school_id; the WebSocket server fans out to all connected clients and simultaneously enqueues an SNS publish job; SNS delivers to APNs and FCM platform endpoints in parallel using batched publish calls, targeting full district delivery within 60 seconds for a 50K-user school.
target_output.data_model: students (id UUID PK, district_student_id, first_name, last_name, grade_level, school_id FK, created_at). guardians (id UUID PK, cognito_sub, email, phone_encrypted, created_at). guardian_student_links (guardian_id FK, student_id FK, relationship ENUM parent/guardian/emergency_contact, verified_at — RLS policy restricts guardian queries to their linked student_ids only). teachers (id UUID PK, cognito_sub, school_id FK, subject_areas TEXT[]). assignments (id UUID PK, course_section_id FK, title, due_date, created_by_teacher_id FK). announcements (id UUID PK, school_id FK, author_id FK, body, published_at, push_sent_at). grade_records (id UUID PK, student_id FK, course_section_id FK, period, grade_value, posted_at). notification_subscriptions (student_id FK, guardian_id FK, category ENUM announcements/grades/messages, enabled). legacy_portal_mappings (id UUID PK, student_id FK, portal_name, legacy_identifier, matched_at).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. GET /students/:id/grades — guardian/teacher scoped. GET /schools/:id/announcements?since= — paginated feed. POST /announcements — teacher/admin role required. GET /students/:id/assignments?status=upcoming — due date sorted. POST /messages/:thread_id/turns — teacher-guardian messaging. GET /migration/status/:guardian_id — legacy migration progress. WebSocket: ws://host/ws/notifications — authenticated JWT upgrade, server pushes announcement and grade events. All responses include X-Request-ID correlation header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: PostgreSQL RLS policies on guardian_student_links ensuring guardians can only query students they are linked to; audit log table recording every grade_record and message_thread access with user_id, resource_id, and timestamp forwarded to Datadog. Cognito TOTP MFA enforced for teacher and district_admin roles. Certificate pinning on React Native clients using react-native-ssl-pinning. TLS 1.3 on ALB and RDS. WAF rules blocking SQL injection and path traversal. Redis AUTH token with in-transit encryption for pub/sub. Data sharing agreements with legacy portal vendors documented in compliance registry.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ESLint + Prettier lint, Jest unit tests (75% coverage gate), Detox E2E tests for login/grade/announcement/migration flows on iOS 16 and Android 12 simulators, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Environments: dev (single AZ, reduced task count), staging (multi-AZ, anonymized production migration data), production (multi-AZ us-east-1, REST API 2-8 tasks auto-scaling CPU 60%, WebSocket tasks 1-6 auto-scaling on active_connection_count >8000 per task). SNS platform applications: APNs production certificate and FCM v1 service account credentials stored in Secrets Manager. ALB access logs to S3 for 90-day retention. Database migrations via db-migrate run as ECS one-off task pre-deploy.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM tracing on all Fastify routes with guardian_id and school_id span tags for FERPA-scoped query analysis. Datadog RUM for React Native crash reporting and ANR detection. Custom CloudWatch metrics: push_delivery_success_rate (target >99%), notification_fanout_latency_p95 (target <30s for school-wide), websocket_active_connections, legacy_migration_match_rate. PagerDuty alerts: push_delivery_success_rate <97%, API error rate >1%, WebSocket task count at max capacity.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2400/month for 50K enrolled users at moderate engagement. ECS REST API 2-4 tasks baseline at $180/month; WebSocket tasks 1-3 tasks at $140/month; RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium at $120/month; ElastiCache cache.t3.micro at $30/month; SNS push at ~$0.50 per million deliveries. Peak announcement burst adds ~$5 for a single district-wide send. Scale path: Redis cluster mode for multi-district pub/sub; read replica for grade history queries.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Migration and Core Features (10 weeks): Legacy portal account deduplication pipeline, Cognito user creation with forced password reset, grade summary view, announcement feed with push notifications, teacher-guardian messaging, FERPA RLS policies, CI/CD pipeline with Detox E2E. Phase 2 — Engagement Features (4 weeks): Assignment due date tracker with overdue alerts, push notification preference controls per student, notification history screen. Phase 3 — Scale and Analytics (4 weeks): Multi-district support with Redis cluster mode, engagement analytics dashboard for district admins, LMS grade passthrough integration for additional SIS vendors.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode across React Native and Fastify code. ESLint with react-native community config. Jest for unit tests on business logic; Detox for E2E on critical user flows. No raw SQL — use parameterized queries via pg client to prevent injection. FERPA data access must always specify student_id scope — never broad SELECT across student records without RLS. PR requires 1 approval; branch protection on main. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for phased migration rollout.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: legacy portal student IDs inconsistent across three vendor systems — mitigation: composite key matching with manual resolution queue for unmatched accounts, targeting 98% auto-match rate. Risk: SNS APNs delivery failures during iOS certificate rotation — mitigation: Secrets Manager automated certificate rotation with 30-day pre-expiry alert. Tradeoff: Redis pub/sub over dedicated WebSocket gateway (API Gateway WebSocket) for lower latency at the cost of managing ECS WebSocket tasks manually. Tradeoff: row-level security at PostgreSQL adds query overhead — mitigated by indexed foreign keys on guardian_student_links.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose AWS SNS over Firebase FCM directly for unified iOS and Android delivery with a single publish API. Selected Redis pub/sub over Kafka for notification fanout given the moderate scale (50K users) and lower operational overhead. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer FERPA enforcement for defense-in-depth. Legacy portal deduplication strategy: composite key of district_student_id + guardian_email with manual queue for ambiguous matches.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision resolving ISS-003 push burst scaling; FERPA-compliant K-12 mobile communication migration.

project.goal: Convert prospective students browsing certificate programs into interest registrations
project.target_users: Adult learners exploring online certificate programs
project.timeline: Single sprint delivery for upcoming cohort launch

frontend.realtime: WebSocket client for seat availability counters

backend.framework: Vercel Edge Functions for interest form forwarding

security.auth: none

reasoner_reviews.product: The core conversion mechanic — real-time seat scarcity — is well-suited to a static landing page with a lightweight WebSocket feed. Interest registration forwarded to the CRM via an Edge Function avoids any storage concerns. The countdown timer to cohort start is a high-impact urgency signal.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 ISR with a 60-second revalidation interval can serve seat availability from the enrollment API without a persistent backend. The WebSocket client should degrade gracefully to polling if the connection drops so the counter remains accurate for users with unstable connections. No database constraint is clean for this scope.
reasoner_reviews.security: HTTPS is enforced at the Vercel edge. The interest registration Edge Function must sanitize inputs before forwarding to the CRM to prevent injection. No server-side storage eliminates data breach risk for this page.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Vercel free tier handles the static asset delivery comfortably. The WebSocket feed dependency on the enrollment system means availability counter accuracy depends on that system's uptime — a fallback to last-known count is advisable.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The plan adequately covers the static page concern but the WebSocket feed reliability and fallback behavior were underspecified in prior rounds. The seat counter must not show stale data misleadingly — graceful degradation with a 'seats updated recently' timestamp is needed.

target_output.title: CE Department Certificate Programs Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A performance-optimized Next.js 14 static landing page on Vercel Edge Network for a university continuing-education department, featuring real-time seat-availability counters and cohort countdown timers for online certificate programs. Prospective students can register interest through an Edge Function form that forwards to the department CRM. No server-side database; seat data flows from the enrollment system WebSocket API with polling fallback. Vercel Analytics tracks conversion funnel from program card view to interest submission.
target_output.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 App Router with ISR (60-second revalidation) generates static pages for each certificate_program and cohort from JSON build-time data. Client-side React components subscribe to a WebSocket feed from the university enrollment API to display live seat_availability_counters; if the WebSocket disconnects, the component degrades to 30-second polling of a lightweight JSON endpoint with a 'last updated N seconds ago' timestamp visible to the user. Countdown timers are computed client-side from cohort start dates embedded in the static page data. Interest registration is handled by a Vercel Edge Function with per-IP rate limiting backed by Vercel KV.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router, ISR. Styling: Tailwind CSS. Realtime: native browser WebSocket API with polling fallback. Edge Function: Vercel Edge Runtime for interest form forwarding. CDN: Vercel Edge Network. Analytics: Vercel Analytics with custom conversion events. CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI. No database, no server-side runtime, no authentication layer.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Certificate Program Cards: each program displays current cohort seat count (live), next start date with countdown timer, and a call-to-action for interest registration. Program Detail Pages: expanded curriculum outline, instructor profiles, and seat availability with urgency banner when seats drop below 10. Interest Registration Modal: email capture forwarded to CRM via Edge Function. Waitlist Banner: triggered when seat count reaches zero, with email notification signup. Analytics Events: page_view, program_card_click, seat_counter_viewed, interest_form_submit, waitlist_signup.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge Network (global CDN serving static Next.js pages with ISR), Next.js ISR cache (60s revalidation pulling program data from enrollment system JSON export), WebSocket client component (subscribes to enrollment API ws://enrollment.university.edu/seats, degrades to polling on disconnect), Vercel Edge Function (/api/register-interest with Vercel KV rate limiting), CRM webhook (receives forwarded interest registration payloads), Vercel Analytics (Web Vitals and custom conversion events), GitHub Actions CI (Lighthouse LCP gate).
target_output.workflows: Seat Availability Update: enrollment system publishes seat count changes to the WebSocket endpoint; the browser client receives the event and updates the seat counter component state in real-time; if disconnected, the client polls /api/seats/:cohort_id every 30 seconds and displays the last-updated timestamp. Interest Registration: visitor fills the email interest form, the Edge Function validates the input, checks Vercel KV for rate limit (5 per IP per hour), forwards the payload to the CRM webhook with program_id and cohort_id tags, and returns a 202 confirmation; on CRM unavailability, the Edge Function returns a user-friendly error without storing the email server-side.
target_output.data_model: certificate_programs (id, title, description, duration_weeks, delivery_mode) — static JSON file generated at build time. cohorts (id, program_id, start_date, end_date, seat_capacity, current_enrollment) — refreshed at ISR revalidation. seat_availability_counters (cohort_id, available_seats, last_updated_at) — live via WebSocket, fallback via polling endpoint. interest_registrations — managed entirely by CRM; not stored on Vercel. analytics_events (event_type, program_id, cohort_id, timestamp) — Vercel Analytics client-side.
target_output.api_design: Edge Function: POST /api/register-interest — body: {email, program_id, cohort_id}; rate limited 5/IP/hour via Vercel KV; forwards to CRM webhook; returns 202 Accepted or 429 Too Many Requests. External dependency: GET /api/seats/:cohort_id (enrollment system polling fallback) — returns {cohort_id, available_seats, updated_at}. WebSocket: ws://enrollment.university.edu/seats — client subscribes with cohort IDs; server pushes {cohort_id, available_seats} on change.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced at Vercel Edge for all requests. Edge Function /api/register-interest: input sanitization (email format validation, max length 320 chars), per-IP rate limiting 5/hour via Vercel KV to prevent form spam. No PII stored on Vercel; email forwarded to CRM only. Content Security Policy header set in next.config.js to restrict WebSocket origin to the enrollment system domain. No cookies set; no tracking pixels beyond Vercel Analytics (GDPR-exempt aggregated Web Vitals).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: ESLint + TypeScript type-check, Lighthouse CI with LCP <2s gate on 3G throttled mobile profile, Next.js build, Vercel deploy via Vercel GitHub integration. Vercel preview deployments auto-created on every PR for stakeholder review. Production deploy on merge to main. ISR cache invalidation via Vercel revalidateTag API on enrollment data refresh. Rollback: Vercel instant rollback to previous deployment via dashboard. No environment-specific secrets beyond CRM webhook URL stored in Vercel environment variables.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics tracking LCP, FID, CLS across geographic regions; alerts when LCP exceeds 2s in any region. Custom conversion funnel events tracked via Vercel Analytics: program_card_click, interest_form_submit, waitlist_signup. Edge Function invocation logs in Vercel dashboard for form submission volume monitoring. No backend APM required — static page with no server-side runtime.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Hobby plan ($0/month) covers static pages and Edge Functions for initial cohort launch. Upgrade to Pro ($20/month) if traffic exceeds Hobby limits during back-to-school campaigns. Vercel KV for rate limiting adds minimal cost at $0 for low submission volumes. WebSocket feed hosted by enrollment system — no incremental Vercel cost. Scales to global traffic spikes via Vercel Edge CDN with no infrastructure management.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Launch (1 sprint): static program listing pages with ISR, WebSocket seat counters with polling fallback, countdown timers, interest registration Edge Function with rate limiting, Lighthouse CI LCP gate, Vercel Analytics conversion events. Phase 2 — Optimization (post-launch): A/B test program card CTA copy using Vercel Edge middleware, add waitlist email automation via CRM trigger, improve countdown timer visibility for mobile users.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. Tailwind CSS with design tokens for consistent program card styling. No client-side data fetching for program content — all served as static props from ISR. WebSocket component must handle reconnection and polling fallback in a single useEffect with proper cleanup on unmount. Edge Function must sanitize all inputs before CRM forwarding. Lighthouse CI LCP budget enforced in CI — PR blocked if regression detected.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: enrollment system WebSocket API downtime causes stale seat counters — mitigation: polling fallback with visible last-updated timestamp prevents misleading display. Risk: CRM webhook unavailability during peak campaign traffic causes lost interest registrations — mitigation: Edge Function returns user-visible error with retry prompt; consider Vercel Queue for durable forwarding. Tradeoff: ISR 60s revalidation over on-demand revalidation for simplicity, accepting up to 60s seat count lag on the static page layer.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose ISR over full SSR to keep pages edge-cacheable and fast globally. Selected Vercel KV for Edge Function rate limiting over custom Redis to stay within the no-persistent-backend constraint. Confirmed WebSocket polling fallback interval at 30 seconds as acceptable balance between accuracy and enrollment API load.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision resolving Edge Function rate limiting; static landing page with real-time seat counters for CE certificate programs.

project.goal: Automate faculty workload contracting and tenure milestone tracking across multiple campuses
project.target_users: Faculty, department chairs, deans, provost, HR staff
project.timeline: Pilot campus in 12 months, full rollout 18 months

security.auth: Azure AD Entra ID SSO with MFA

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions and distributed locking

reasoner_reviews.product: Faculty workload governance is a high-stakes process — contracts directly affect compensation and tenure decisions. The microservices decomposition mirrors the organizational separation between contracts, scheduling, HR, and reporting, which will simplify ownership by different campus IT teams. The batch contract generation from enrollment projections is a high-value automation for department chairs.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot microservices on AKS is sound for this scale. Each microservice should own its PostgreSQL schema to avoid cross-service coupling. The scheduling service depends on the SIS enrollment data — a daily Airflow-equivalent batch job is safer than event-driven for a university system where SIS data is typically batch-updated overnight. Distributed locking via Redis is needed to prevent concurrent contract edits by department chairs.
reasoner_reviews.security: SOC 2 requires audit logging, access controls, and encryption at rest. Each microservice should write to its own append-only audit_log table — cross-service audit correlation handled by a shared correlation_id header. Azure Key Vault for all secrets; no credentials in AKS pod environment variables. RBAC must be enforced at the API gateway layer and repeated at the service layer for defense in depth.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Workday HR sync must handle rate limits on the Workday REST API — exponential backoff with a dead letter queue for failed sync records. The $8000/month budget is achievable on AKS with right-sized node pools and reserved instances.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia state management, route-based code splitting per capability domain (contracts, scheduling, tenure, reporting)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Faculty Dashboard, Contract Authoring Wizard, Course Assignment Grid, Tenure Milestone Timeline, Department Allocation Dashboard, HR Sync Status Admin, Provost Approval Queue
frontend.state_management: Pinia stores per domain; Vue Query for server state; optimistic updates for contract field edits
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA required for state university public institution compliance

backend.service_design: Four Spring Boot microservices: ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, ReportingService — each with own PostgreSQL schema and REST API
backend.api_patterns: REST with OpenAPI 3.0; internal service-to-service calls via Feign client with circuit breaker
backend.business_modules: Contract state machine: draft > submitted > department_approved > dean_approved > provost_approved > executed
backend.background_jobs: Daily batch job: SchedulingService ingests SIS enrollment projections and generates draft workload contracts; nightly HRSyncService reconciles Workday faculty records

security.auth_design: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with Spring Security OIDC integration; JWT validation at API gateway (Azure API Management)
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced at API gateway and repeated at service method level via Spring Security annotations
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault with AKS Workload Identity — no credentials in pod specs
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Each microservice appends to its audit_log table on every state-changing operation; correlation_id propagated via HTTP header for cross-service trace reconstruction

data.entities: faculty_members, workload_contracts, course_sections, tenure_milestones, department_allocations, campus_calendars, hr_sync_records, audit_logs
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with separate schemas per microservice; Redis 7 for distributed locking on contract edit sessions
data.retention_and_deletion: Contract records 10-year retention; audit_logs 7 years; automated Azure Blob archival for records exceeding active retention

devops.deployment_topology: AKS with 3-node general-purpose node pool and 1-node high-memory node pool for reporting batch jobs; Azure Container Registry for images
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit tests, SonarQube quality gate, Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor for cluster metrics; Grafana dashboards for contract throughput and Workday sync lag; PagerDuty for batch job failure alerts
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback on failed health checks; database migration versioned with Flyway

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a flawed first-pass row — the plan intentionally contains a contract violation: the backend_stack field in the frozen_requirement_contract correctly specifies Java 21 with Spring Boot, but the technology_stack section of the plan mentions Node.js as the backend runtime, creating an internal inconsistency. Additionally, the compliance_context field is present but the security_and_compliance section omits SOC 2 audit controls, which are required given the confidential data sensitivity and high risk level.
target_output.title: Faculty Workload and Tenure Governance Platform — Initial Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A fullstack academic governance platform for a multi-campus state university system managing faculty workload contracts, course section assignments, and tenure milestone tracking. Built with Vue 3 on a microservices backend using Java 21 Spring Boot services on Azure Kubernetes Service, with PostgreSQL 16 per-service schema isolation, Azure AD SSO, and full audit logging. Integrates with the university Workday HR system via nightly batch sync. NOTE: this plan contains intentional flaws for training purposes — technology_stack section incorrectly references Node.js backend.
target_output.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA authenticates via Azure AD Entra ID OIDC and calls four Spring Boot microservices through Azure API Management: ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, and ReportingService. Each microservice owns its PostgreSQL 16 schema. Redis 7 provides distributed locking for concurrent contract edits. Daily batch jobs in SchedulingService ingest SIS enrollment projections to generate draft workload contracts. Tenure milestone tracking and multi-level approval workflows are orchestrated by ContractsService with role-gated endpoints for department_chair, college_dean, and provost reviewers.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, PrimeVue, Vue Router. Backend: Node.js 20 with Express (intentional contract violation — contract specifies Java 21 Spring Boot). Database: PostgreSQL 16 per-microservice schema. Cache: Redis 7. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC. Infrastructure: AKS, Azure Container Registry, Azure API Management. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Contract Authoring Wizard: faculty or department chair drafts workload contract with course assignment grid and credit hour calculation. Multi-Level Approval Workflow: state machine routing contracts through department_chair > college_dean > provost reviewers with email notifications at each stage. Tenure Milestone Timeline: faculty view of tenure review schedule with document upload for dossier submission. Department Allocation Dashboard: chair view of total workload hours across all faculty in department. Batch Contract Generation: SchedulingService automatically generates draft contracts from SIS enrollment projections each semester. HR Sync Status: admin panel showing Workday sync last run, records updated, and error counts.
target_output.system_components: Azure API Management (JWT validation, RBAC routing), Vue 3 SPA (Azure Blob Static Sites), ContractsService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL contracts schema), SchedulingService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL scheduling schema, batch job), HRSyncService (Spring Boot, Workday REST API integration), ReportingService (Spring Boot, ClickHouse read-only reporting replica), Redis 7 (session cache, distributed locking), Azure Key Vault (secrets management), Azure Monitor (cluster and application metrics).
target_output.workflows: Semester Contract Generation: SchedulingService batch job runs at 02:00 on the Monday before the semester opens; fetches course_sections with enrollment projections from SIS via REST; generates draft workload_contracts for each faculty_member with assigned sections; notifies department chairs via email. Tenure Review Initiation: the milestones service detects faculty members within 60 days of their tenure_review_date; generates a review record; routes dossier submission task to faculty; upon submission, routes approval workflow to department, college, and provost levels sequentially with configurable deadline escalation.
target_output.data_model: faculty_members (id UUID PK, azure_oid, employee_id, name, rank ENUM assistant/associate/full/adjunct, department_id FK, tenure_status, hire_date, campus_id FK). workload_contracts (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, academic_year, semester, status ENUM draft/submitted/dept_approved/dean_approved/provost_approved/executed, total_credit_hours, created_at, approved_at). course_sections (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, course_code, section_number, credit_hours, enrollment_cap, sis_section_id). tenure_milestones (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, milestone_type ENUM annual_review/midpoint/tenure_vote/promotion, due_date, status, dossier_s3_key). hr_sync_records (id UUID PK, employee_id, workday_worker_id, sync_status, last_synced_at, error_detail). audit_logs (id UUID PK, service_name, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, correlation_id, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: ContractsService: GET /contracts?faculty_id= (role scoped), POST /contracts (draft creation), PATCH /contracts/:id/submit, PATCH /contracts/:id/approve (role: chair/dean/provost), GET /contracts/:id/audit-log. SchedulingService: GET /course-sections?semester=, POST /batch/generate-contracts (admin trigger). HRSyncService: GET /sync/status, POST /sync/trigger (admin). TenureService: GET /milestones/:faculty_id, POST /milestones/:id/submit-dossier. All services: GET /health, X-Correlation-ID header required.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with MFA enforced. JWT validation at Azure API Management layer with RBAC claim checking. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations at method level for defense in depth. TLS 1.3 on all ingress. Azure Key Vault with AKS Workload Identity — pod identity bound to service account for secret access. NOTE: SOC 2 Type II audit controls are intentionally underspecified here — audit log retention policy and access review procedures missing despite compliance_context requiring them.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit unit and integration tests, SonarQube quality gate (80% coverage), Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS namespace. Environments: dev (shared 2-node cluster), staging (production mirror, single campus dataset), production (3-node general pool + 1-node high-memory for reporting). Flyway database migrations applied pre-deploy via Kubernetes Job. Helm rollback on failed liveness probes. Azure Blob lifecycle policy archives records exceeding retention tiers.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor collects AKS node and pod metrics. Grafana dashboards: contract approval throughput by campus, Workday sync lag per faculty cohort, tenure milestone overdue count. Application logs structured JSON with correlation_id. PagerDuty alerts: batch job failure (SchedulingService), Workday sync failure for >100 records, API error rate >2%, AKS node NotReady.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $6500/month on AKS for 3 campuses at 800 faculty. AKS general-purpose nodes 3x D4s_v3 ~$700/month; PostgreSQL Flexible Server 2x ~$400/month; Azure Cache for Redis ~$150/month; API Management Developer tier ~$50/month. Reserved instance discounts reduce compute ~30%. Scale path: horizontal pod autoscaling on CPU 70% for ContractsService during semester start spikes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Pilot Campus (12 weeks): ContractsService with approval workflow, Vue 3 frontend for contract authoring, Azure AD SSO, PostgreSQL schema setup, CI/CD pipeline, Workday HR sync (nightly batch). Phase 2 — Scheduling Automation (8 weeks): SchedulingService batch contract generation from SIS, course section assignment grid, campus calendar integration. Phase 3 — Tenure Tracking (8 weeks): tenure milestone timeline, dossier upload, provost approval workflow, department allocation dashboard. Phase 4 — Multi-Campus Rollout (6 weeks): multi-campus namespace isolation, cross-campus reporting in ReportingService, analytics dashboards.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with virtual threads for blocking I/O in sync tasks. Spring Boot 3 with Spring Security for auth. Flyway for database migrations — no manual schema changes. OpenAPI 3.0 specs code-generated from annotations. SonarQube quality gate in CI. RBAC annotations required on all controller methods — no unannotated endpoints. Correlation ID injected into all outbound Feign client calls via RequestInterceptor.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Workday REST API rate limiting during large HR sync batches — mitigation: chunked batches of 100 records with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue for failed records. Risk: multi-campus PostgreSQL schema drift if migrations run out of order — mitigation: Flyway with ordered versioned migrations and pre-deploy validation. Tradeoff: per-microservice PostgreSQL schema over a single shared database for service isolation at the cost of cross-service reporting complexity, addressed by a read-only ClickHouse replica fed by logical replication.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected per-service PostgreSQL schema over separate database instances for cost efficiency while maintaining logical isolation. Chose daily batch Workday sync over event-driven for compatibility with Workday's batch-update cadence. Confirmed AKS Helm deployments over Azure App Service for microservice orchestration flexibility.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: technology_stack references Node.js despite Java 21 Spring Boot in contract; SOC 2 audit controls missing from security section despite compliance_context.

project_goal.value: Migrate a mid-size community college's on-premise VMware cluster to AWS, encompassing student information system databases, LDAP directory services, and course material file storage, with zero student-facing downtime during the transition window
project_goal.rationale: End-of-life VMware infrastructure driving cloud migration

target_users.value: Community college students, faculty, and staff dependent on SIS, directory services, and file storage
target_users.rationale: Service continuity for entire campus user base is the primary success metric

project_class.rationale: Platform migration project with Terraform and Ansible; no application development

capabilities.rationale: Infrastructure provisioning and cutover orchestration only

complexity_level.rationale: Zero-downtime database migration, LDAP directory sync, and weighted DNS cutover add operational complexity

risk_level.rationale: Student SIS data and FERPA obligations; any data loss or access control regression is a critical incident

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student enrollment records, grades, and LDAP identity data are personally identifiable under FERPA

external_exposure.rationale: Migrated systems remain internal to the college network via VPN and Direct Connect

access_model.value: AWS Directory Service AD Connector linked to on-premise AD during migration; post-migration AWS Managed Microsoft AD
access_model.rationale: Transparent directory service handoff preserving existing user accounts

feature_scope.value: Terraform infrastructure provisioning, Ansible VM configuration, RDS database migration, S3 course material transfer, LDAP directory sync and cutover, Route 53 DNS weighted routing, cutover runbook
feature_scope.rationale: Full migration scope for all three workload categories

mvp_scope.value: SIS database migration to RDS with Route 53 weighted cutover, LDAP sync to AWS Directory Service
mvp_scope.rationale: Highest-risk workloads migrated first to prove the process

security_baseline.value: AWS KMS encryption for RDS and S3, TLS 1.3 on ALB, IAM roles with least-privilege, CloudTrail enabled
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA minimum controls maintained post-migration

frontend_stack.value: None — infrastructure migration; no frontend component
frontend_stack.rationale: Platform migration with no UI development

backend_stack.rationale: IaC standard for infrastructure provisioning

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS Multi-AZ; S3 for course material file storage
data_platform.rationale: Managed RDS for SIS database; S3 for file storage replacing VMware NFS

hosting_target.value: EC2 + RDS + S3 in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: AWS region matching FERPA US data residency requirement

privacy_retention_policy.value: Student SIS records retained per FERPA 5-year minimum; LDAP account records purged 1 year post-separation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FERPA and HR record retention requirements

future_scope.value: Full VMware decommission after 90-day parallel run; migrate remaining legacy applications in Phase 2
future_scope.rationale: VMware license cost savings after full decommission

constraints.value: Zero student-facing downtime; migration window limited to summer break; all data must remain in us-east-1
constraints.rationale: Academic calendar and FERPA data residency constraints

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for RDS and EC2 metrics; CloudTrail for access audit
observability_baseline.rationale: AWS-native monitoring sufficient for migration phase

execution_preference.value: Phased migration approach with parallel run and incremental traffic shift
execution_preference.rationale: Risk mitigation through gradual cutover

compliance_context.value: FERPA — student SIS records must maintain equivalent access controls post-migration; data residency must remain within US; LDAP access controls must be replicated exactly in AWS Directory Service
compliance_context.rationale: Federal student privacy law obligations during infrastructure migration

project.goal: Migrate VMware on-premise cluster to AWS with zero student-facing downtime
project.target_users: All campus users dependent on SIS, directory, and file storage
project.timeline: Summer break migration window

security.auth: AWS Directory Service AD Connector during migration

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for Terraform plan and apply

reasoner_reviews.product: The zero-downtime constraint during the summer break window is achievable with Route 53 weighted routing if the pre-migration parallel run is validated thoroughly. The LDAP directory reconciliation job is the highest-risk component — user attribute divergence before final cutover could lock students out of SIS on the first day of fall semester.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The weighted routing approach starting at 5% AWS traffic is sound. The key dependency is that the SIS application can be configured to connect to either the on-premise PostgreSQL or RDS endpoint without code changes — confirm this via an application-level connection string test before the summer window. The LDAP differential sync must run at least weekly, increasing to daily in the final two weeks before cutover.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA requires that post-migration RDS access controls replicate the on-premise access controls exactly — document the on-premise PostgreSQL role assignments and verify identical IAM/RDS role mapping pre-cutover. CloudTrail must be enabled on all S3 buckets containing course materials before migration starts.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Summer break window is 8-10 weeks — sufficient for the migration if AWS environments are provisioned in the preceding semester. The 90-day parallel run post-cutover is critical for rollback capability.

backend.service_design: Terraform modules: vpc, rds_postgres, s3_course_materials, ec2_bastion, directory_service, alb, route53_weighted
backend.api_patterns: no application API; Ansible playbooks for SIS application configuration pointing to RDS endpoint
backend.background_jobs: LDAP differential sync cron job runs nightly during migration period; escalates to hourly in final 2 weeks; Route 53 weight adjustment script increments AWS share by 10% per week
backend.failure_handling: Rollback checkpoints after each Route 53 weight increment; automated CloudWatch alarm triggers pause script if error rate exceeds 0.5%

security.auth_design: AWS Directory Service AD Connector proxies authentication to on-premise AD during migration; cutover to AWS Managed Microsoft AD post-migration
security.secrets_management: RDS master credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; Ansible vault for playbook secrets
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail enabled on all S3 buckets and RDS API calls; VPC Flow Logs enabled for forensic analysis
security.privacy_controls: KMS CMK for RDS encryption; S3 server-side encryption with KMS; bucket policies blocking public access

data.entities: vm_inventory, rds_instances, s3_buckets, ldap_directory_entries, dns_records, cutover_runbooks, rollback_checkpoints
data.storage_design: RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL in us-east-1; S3 with versioning enabled for course materials; DMS continuous replication for SIS database during parallel run
data.retention_and_deletion: S3 lifecycle: current course materials active; versions older than 1 year transitioned to S3 Glacier

devops.deployment_topology: VPC with public and private subnets; RDS in private subnet; ALB in public subnet; EC2 bastion in public subnet; Direct Connect or Site-to-Site VPN for on-premise connectivity during migration
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: terraform fmt, terraform validate, tfsec security scan, terraform plan output reviewed before apply; Ansible syntax check
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch dashboards: RDS connection count, SIS application error rate, Route 53 resolver query count by endpoint; CloudTrail for audit
devops.rollback_strategy: Route 53 weight set to 100% on-premise via rollback runbook; RDS DMS replication remains active for 90 days post-cutover as fallback

ISS-001.title: LDAP attribute reconciliation strategy not specified
ISS-001.detail: Prior rounds described daily LDAP sync but did not specify which attributes are compared and how divergent attributes are resolved before final cutover.

ISS-002.title: RDS DMS continuous replication lag not quantified
ISS-002.detail: The plan did not specify acceptable DMS replication lag thresholds or what happens if lag exceeds threshold during the cutover window.

ISS-003.title: Route 53 rollback automation not documented
ISS-003.detail: The cutover runbook references a rollback but the automated trigger conditions and Route 53 weight reset procedure are not specified.

focus_issues.title: Route 53 rollback automation not documented
