target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 targets ISS-003: automated Route 53 rollback documentation. Adding CloudWatch alarm-triggered pause automation for the weight increment script and documenting the emergency rollback procedure with weight-to-0 reset. The plan is already weak in several sections due to the infrastructure-only scope but the rollback automation gap is the key operational risk for a zero-downtime migration.

fix_report.action_taken: Added CloudWatch composite alarm definition that auto-pauses the Route 53 weight increment script when SIS 5xx error rate exceeds 0.5% over a 5-minute window; documented emergency rollback procedure setting AWS Route 53 weight to 0 and on-premise weight to 255 via aws route53 change-resource-record-sets CLI call in the cutover runbook.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Automated safety mechanism prevents continued traffic shift if SIS error rate spikes during weighted cutover; operations team has documented one-command emergency rollback.

target_output.title: Community College VMware-to-AWS Migration Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-downtime infrastructure migration moving a community college's on-premise VMware cluster to AWS using Terraform-provisioned RDS Multi-AZ, EC2, S3, and AWS Directory Service. Route 53 weighted routing incrementally shifts SIS traffic from on-premise load balancers to AWS ALB during the summer break window. DMS continuous replication maintains sub-5-second lag during parallel run. Automated CloudWatch alarms pause traffic shifting if SIS error rates spike, with a one-command emergency rollback procedure.
target_output.architecture_overview: Terraform 1.7 modules provision a VPC with public/private subnet layout in us-east-1, RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL for the SIS database, S3 for course material file storage, EC2 bastion host, and AWS Directory Service AD Connector. AWS DMS runs continuous replication from the on-premise PostgreSQL to RDS throughout the parallel run period, maintaining replication lag under 5 seconds. Route 53 weighted routing starts at 5% AWS / 95% on-premise and increments by 10% weekly. A Lambda function triggered by a CloudWatch composite alarm auto-pauses weight increments if the SIS ALB 5xx error rate exceeds 0.5% over 5 minutes. Emergency rollback sets the AWS Route 53 record weight to 0 and on-premise weight to 255 via a single aws CLI call documented in the cutover runbook. LDAP nightly differential sync escalates to hourly in the final 2 weeks; a reconciliation report flags divergent user attributes before final cutover.
target_output.technology_stack: IaC: Terraform 1.7, Ansible 2.16. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ db.r6g.large. Migration: AWS DMS continuous replication task. File Storage: S3 with versioning and KMS SSE. Directory: AWS Directory Service AD Connector (migration) transitioning to AWS Managed Microsoft AD (post-cutover). DNS: Route 53 weighted routing policy. Compute: EC2 t3.medium bastion. Automation: Lambda for weight increment pause; GitHub Actions for Terraform CI. Monitoring: CloudWatch, CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: SIS Database Migration: DMS continuous replication from on-premise PostgreSQL to RDS Multi-AZ with lag monitoring. Weighted Traffic Shift: Route 53 weighted routing with 10%/week increment automation and CloudWatch alarm-triggered pause. LDAP Directory Sync: nightly differential sync of on-premise AD to AWS Directory Service with attribute divergence report. Course Material Migration: S3 Transfer Acceleration bulk upload of VMware NFS content with checksum verification. Rollback Capability: 90-day parallel run with DMS active; single-command Route 53 weight reset. Infrastructure as Code: all AWS resources defined in Terraform modules with tfsec security scanning.
target_output.system_components: VPC (public/private subnets, NAT Gateway, internet gateway), ALB (public subnet, routes SIS traffic to EC2 application nodes), EC2 SIS application nodes (private subnet, Auto Scaling Group), RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL db.r6g.large (private subnet, KMS encrypted), AWS DMS continuous replication task (on-premise PostgreSQL source to RDS target), S3 course materials bucket (versioning, KMS SSE, public access blocked), AWS Directory Service AD Connector (on-premise AD proxy), EC2 bastion (public subnet, SSM Session Manager access only), Route 53 hosted zone (weighted records for SIS FQDN), CloudWatch composite alarm (pauses weight increment Lambda), Lambda weight increment pause function.
target_output.workflows: Weighted Cutover Execution: migration engineer runs the increment_weight.sh script weekly; script checks CloudWatch alarm state before incrementing; if ALARM state detected (SIS 5xx >0.5%), script aborts and posts Slack notification; if OK, script calls aws route53 change-resource-record-sets to add 10% to AWS weight. Emergency Rollback: on-call engineer runs emergency_rollback.sh from the cutover runbook; script sets AWS Route 53 weight to 0 and on-premise weight to 255; DMS replication continues to capture changes made on AWS during the partial traffic period; data team verifies no transactions are lost before declaring rollback complete. LDAP Reconciliation: nightly Ansible playbook pulls LDAP dump from on-premise AD and AWS Directory Service; Python reconciliation script compares user attributes (sAMAccountName, mail, memberOf groups) and outputs divergence_report.csv; data team reviews and resolves divergences before cutover day.
target_output.data_model: vm_inventory (vm_id, hostname, cpu_cores, ram_gb, disk_gb, assigned_workload, migration_status ENUM pending/in_progress/migrated/decommissioned). rds_instances (instance_id, engine_version, multi_az, storage_encrypted, endpoint, migration_source_db). s3_buckets (bucket_name, workload ENUM course_materials/backups, versioning_enabled, kms_key_id, object_count, last_synced_at). ldap_directory_entries (dn, sam_account_name, mail, groups, sync_status, last_reconciled_at). dns_records (fqdn, on_premise_weight, aws_weight, last_updated_at). cutover_runbooks (id, step_order, step_description, success_criteria, rollback_step). rollback_checkpoints (id, route53_aws_weight_at_checkpoint, dms_lag_seconds, error_rate_at_checkpoint, checkpoint_timestamp).
target_output.api_design: No external API. Internal automation scripts: increment_weight.sh — reads CloudWatch alarm state, calls Route 53 change-resource-record-sets API. emergency_rollback.sh — sets Route 53 AWS weight to 0. dms_lag_check.sh — queries DMS task metrics and blocks cutover if lag >5s. All scripts use AWS CLI with IAM role-based credentials via EC2 instance profile or GitHub Actions OIDC.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: RDS encrypted with KMS CMK; S3 server-side encryption with KMS; bucket policies blocking all public access. CloudTrail enabled on all S3 and RDS API calls; logs shipped to a dedicated audit S3 bucket with Object Lock for immutability. VPC Flow Logs enabled for forensic analysis. IAM roles with least privilege: SIS application role read/write to RDS; migration engineer role restricted to DMS and Route 53 change-resource-record-sets. AWS Directory Service AD Connector replicates on-premise RBAC groups to AWS for equivalent access controls post-migration. Access control equivalence verified by a pre-cutover checklist comparing on-premise PostgreSQL roles to RDS IAM roles.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions Terraform CI: terraform fmt check, terraform validate, tfsec security scan (block on HIGH findings), terraform plan output posted as PR comment, terraform apply on merge to migration branch. Ansible syntax check and dry-run in CI. Environments: pre-migration (Terraform plan review), migration (Terraform apply to production AWS account). Route 53 weight increment automation: Lambda function invoked on schedule, blocked by CloudWatch composite alarm state. CloudWatch composite alarm: SIS ALB 5xx rate >0.5% over 5 minutes OR DMS replication lag >5 seconds. Emergency rollback runbook stored in operations wiki and printed at migration command center.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch dashboards: SIS ALB 5xx and 4xx rates (split by Route 53 origin), RDS connection count and freeable memory, DMS replication lag in seconds, LDAP sync divergence count. CloudWatch composite alarm triggers Lambda weight-pause on SIS error spike. CloudTrail: all S3 object access and RDS API calls. VPC Flow Logs: retained 90 days in S3 for forensic analysis. Migration milestone Slack notifications: weight increment completed, LDAP sync complete with divergence count, rollback checkpoint saved.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2800/month post-migration. RDS db.r6g.large Multi-AZ ~$600/month. EC2 SIS application Auto Scaling 2-4x t3.large ~$400/month. S3 for course materials (estimated 2TB) ~$50/month. AWS DMS ~$100/month during 90-day parallel run (terminated after decommission). ALB ~$20/month. CloudWatch and CloudTrail ~$50/month. Savings: VMware license costs estimated $15000/year eliminated after decommission.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 0 — Pre-Migration (8 weeks, preceding semester): Terraform module development and tfsec validation, VPC and RDS provisioning in us-east-1, DMS task configuration and initial full load, S3 bucket setup with KMS, AWS Directory Service AD Connector deployment, LDAP sync baseline run. Phase 1 — SIS Migration (summer break, week 1-4): DMS continuous replication start, Route 53 weighted routing beginning at 5% AWS, weekly 10% increments with CloudWatch alarm guard, LDAP escalated to nightly sync. Phase 2 — Course Materials and LDAP Cutover (week 5-8): S3 bulk transfer with checksum verification, final LDAP reconciliation, Route 53 100% AWS cutover, AD Connector to Managed Microsoft AD migration. Phase 3 — Parallel Run and Decommission (weeks 9-20): 90-day DMS parallel run for rollback safety, VMware decommission, cost savings validation.
target_output.development_guidelines: All AWS resources must be defined in Terraform — no console-created resources. tfsec scan must pass with zero HIGH findings before terraform apply. Terraform state stored in S3 backend with DynamoDB locking. No SSH key pairs — all EC2 access via SSM Session Manager only. Migration scripts in version-controlled runbook repository with change log. Route 53 weight changes via automation scripts only — no manual console edits during migration window. DMS lag must be below 5 seconds for 30 consecutive minutes before any Route 53 weight increment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SIS application hardcoded connection strings preventing RDS endpoint switchover — mitigation: connection string test in pre-migration phase; Ansible playbook for endpoint substitution. Risk: LDAP divergence locking students out on first day of fall semester — mitigation: daily reconciliation reports in final 2 weeks with mandatory sign-off by IT director before cutover day. Risk: DMS lag spike during peak registration traffic pausing the cutover — mitigation: schedule final 100% cutover during off-peak hours (Sunday 02:00). Tradeoff: 90-day parallel run cost ($100/month DMS) versus rollback safety for critical SIS data.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed DMS continuous replication over PostgreSQL logical replication for AWS-managed cutover control. Selected Route 53 weighted routing over blue-green DNS swap for granular traffic control. Emergency rollback procedure documented as single CLI command with pre-validated IAM permissions.

metadata.notes: Round 4 revision resolving ISS-003 Route 53 rollback automation; weak quality reflects infrastructure-only scope with limited architectural depth.

project_goal.value: Multi-stage data pipeline ingesting raw K-12 standardized test scores from multiple assessment vendors, normalizing into a common schema, linking to student enrollment, and publishing aggregated dashboards for principals and curriculum coaches
project_goal.rationale: Core district need to unify fragmented assessment data from separate vendor portals

target_users.value: District data team for ingestion operations; principals and curriculum coaches for dashboard consumption
target_users.rationale: Operators maintain the pipeline; instructional leaders consume outputs

project_class.rationale: Pure ETL and analytics delivery with no interactive user-facing frontend

capabilities.rationale: Ingestion from vendor SFTPs, batch processing, ClickHouse analytics layer

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-vendor schema normalization, FERPA compliance, and complex aggregation logic across grade bands

risk_level.rationale: Individual student score records are FERPA-protected educational records; breach would have legal consequences

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student identifiers and test scores are personally identifiable and educationally sensitive

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs entirely within district GCP project; no public endpoints

access_model.value: Google Workspace SSO with IAM role bindings; data team has pipeline admin rights; principals have read-only dashboard access scoped to their school
access_model.rationale: Least-privilege access prevents cross-school score leakage

feature_scope.value: SFTP ingestion from assessment vendors, JSON schema validation and quarantine, enrollment record linkage, schema normalization, ClickHouse aggregation refresh, Grafana dashboard delivery
feature_scope.rationale: Full ETL scope from raw vendor files to instructional dashboards

mvp_scope.value: Single vendor ingestion, validation with quarantine, enrollment linkage, and grade-level performance aggregates for one academic year
mvp_scope.rationale: Prove pipeline correctness with one vendor before onboarding additional assessment providers

security_baseline.value: TLS in transit, AES-256 encryption at rest via CMEK, VPC-SC perimeter around GCP project, no student PII in logs
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA minimum controls for district data handling

frontend_stack.value: none — pipeline-only; Grafana used for dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser SPA required; Grafana provides sufficient visualization layer

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow on Cloud Composer
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow provides DAG orchestration, retry logic, and task dependency management native to GCP

data_platform.value: ClickHouse on GCP Compute Engine for analytics; Cloud Storage for raw and quarantined files; BigQuery for ad-hoc compliance queries
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse sub-second aggregation at district scale; BigQuery for FERPA audit exports

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Composer for Airflow, Cloud Run for validation microservice, Compute Engine for ClickHouse cluster
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Airflow reduces operational overhead; Cloud Run scales validation workers on demand

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw vendor files retained 3 years in GCS cold storage; normalized scores retained 7 years per state records retention schedule; quarantined rows purged after 90-day remediation window
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State education records law mandates 7-year retention for assessment data

future_scope.value: Additional assessment vendor onboarding, predictive early-warning models on normalized scores, teacher effectiveness correlation views
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap items contingent on MVP pipeline stability

constraints.value: GCP us-central1 region only; no student PII may leave GCP project boundary; pipeline must complete nightly runs before 6am district start time
constraints.rationale: Data residency and operational window requirements from district policy

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards for pipeline run metrics, Prometheus for ClickHouse query latency, Cloud Logging for Airflow task logs, PagerDuty alerts for failed DAG runs
observability_baseline.rationale: District data team needs visibility into nightly ingestion health without manual log inspection

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with vendor onboarding gates; each new vendor requires schema mapping review before production promotion
execution_preference.rationale: Prevents bad vendor data from polluting normalized store

compliance_context.value: FERPA: student score files are educational records; data sharing agreements with each assessment vendor must be documented; no individual scores in aggregate reports with cohorts smaller than 10 students per state guidance
compliance_context.rationale: FERPA small-cohort suppression rule and vendor data sharing requirements

project.goal: Nightly ingestion, normalization, and aggregation of multi-vendor K-12 standardized test scores
project.target_users: District data engineers and instructional leaders
project.timeline: MVP with one vendor in 10 weeks

security.auth: Google Workspace SSO with GCP IAM
security.encryption: CMEK at rest, TLS in transit

devops.hosting: Cloud Composer + Cloud Run + Compute Engine

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline scope is well-bounded for MVP: one vendor, one academic year, grade-level aggregates. The quarantine workflow is essential to protect the normalized store from corrupt vendor extracts. Small-cohort suppression must be built into aggregation logic from day one, not retrofitted.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Airflow DAG design should separate ingestion, validation, linkage, and aggregation into distinct task groups to enable partial retries. ClickHouse materialized views for school-level and grade-level performance aggregates will handle principal dashboard query patterns without scanning raw normalized rows on every request.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA demands that no individual student score is surfaced in any report where the cohort falls below 10 students. Audit logging must capture every query against the normalized_scores table with the requesting principal's identity. VPC-SC perimeter prevents exfiltration even if a Cloud Run service is misconfigured.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 6am runtime window is tight for advanced aggregations. If ClickHouse MV refresh takes more than 2 hours, the pipeline will need parallelized aggregation DAGs split by school. GCS lifecycle rules must enforce cold storage transition to avoid unbounded storage costs on 7-year retained raw files.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The current design lacks a schema registry for vendor file formats. Without versioning vendor schemas, a mid-year format change from any vendor will silently corrupt the normalization stage. A dedicated schema registry step in the validation DAG is critical before production.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG chain: ingest_dag (SFTP poll, GCS upload), validate_dag (Cloud Run microservice call, quarantine routing), link_dag (enrollment join via BigQuery temp tables), normalize_dag (Python transforms to canonical schema), aggregate_dag (ClickHouse MV refresh trigger)
backend.api_patterns: No external API; internal task communication via Airflow XCom for small payloads and GCS object paths for file references between DAG tasks
backend.background_jobs: Nightly cron at 23:00; Cloud Run validation workers scale 0-to-10 on ingestion trigger; ClickHouse aggregation triggered only after successful normalization task group
backend.failure_handling: Airflow task retries with exponential backoff (3 retries, 5-min delay); failed validation quarantines row batch to GCS quarantine prefix and emits PagerDuty alert; downstream DAGs blocked until upstream succeeds
backend.backend_risks: Vendor SFTP instability may cause missed ingestion windows; schema drift from vendors without notice; ClickHouse single-node is a scaling bottleneck beyond 50M rows

security.auth_design: Google Workspace SSO federated to GCP IAM; Cloud Composer uses Workload Identity for service account impersonation; no long-lived keys
security.authorization_model: IAM roles: data-pipeline-admin (data team), clickhouse-reader (principals scoped to their school via row-level security), compliance-auditor (BigQuery export access)
security.privacy_controls: Small-cohort suppression applied at ClickHouse MV layer; student PII masked in Airflow task logs; GCS bucket retention locks prevent premature deletion
security.audit_and_logging_controls: BigQuery audit dataset captures every ClickHouse query via proxy logging; Airflow task logs scrubbed of PII before Cloud Logging ingestion; vendor data sharing agreement IDs logged per ingestion run
security.security_risks: Vendor SFTP credentials in Secret Manager require coordinated rotation; misrouted quarantine files could expose PII if GCS bucket ACLs misconfigured

data.entities: assessment_vendors (id, name, sftp_host, schema_version, dsa_document_id, active), raw_score_files (id, vendor_id, gcs_path, received_at, row_count, validation_status ENUM pending/passed/quarantined/failed), students (id, state_student_id, district_id, enrollment_school_id, grade_level, active), enrollment_records (id, student_id, school_id, academic_year, grade_level, enrollment_date), normalized_scores (id, student_id, vendor_id, assessment_name, subject, raw_score, scaled_score, proficiency_level ENUM below_basic/basic/proficient/advanced, test_date, academic_year), performance_aggregates (id, school_id, grade_level, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate, n_students, suppressed BOOL), school_benchmarks (id, school_id, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target, academic_year)
data.storage_design: GCS: raw/ prefix (vendor files), quarantine/ prefix (failed validation), processed/ prefix (archived after normalization). ClickHouse: normalized_scores and performance_aggregates. BigQuery: compliance audit dataset
data.retention_and_deletion: Raw files: GCS Coldline after 90 days, delete after 3 years. normalized_scores: 7 years. BigQuery audit: 7 years. Quarantine rows: purged after 90-day remediation window
data.data_risks: Vendor schema changes corrupt normalization; enrollment record gaps leave scores unlinked; ClickHouse disk exhaustion on multi-year accumulation

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Composer 2 in us-central1; Cloud Run validation service; ClickHouse on 2x n2-standard-8 Compute Engine with SSD; Grafana on n2-standard-2
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build: flake8 lint, pytest, airflow dags test, container build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy, DAG sync via GCS bucket
devops.environments: dev (single Composer env, ClickHouse dev node), staging (full topology, anonymized fixtures), production (Composer + ClickHouse 2-node)
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping ClickHouse metrics, Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run and Composer, Grafana: DAG run durations, validation pass/fail rates, normalized row counts per vendor, MV refresh latency
devops.rollback_strategy: Airflow DAG versioning via git tags; ClickHouse snapshot restore via Compute Engine disk snapshot; Cloud Run revision traffic rollback

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a FERPA-compliant K-12 assessment data pipeline on GCP. Key decisions: Airflow DAG separation by task group enables targeted retries without re-ingesting clean data; ClickHouse materialized views pre-aggregate school and grade-level metrics to serve principal dashboards at sub-second latency; small-cohort suppression baked into MV definitions prevents FERPA violations at query time; VPC-SC perimeter and GCS IAM ensure student PII never leaves the district GCP project boundary.
target_output.title: K-12 Multi-Vendor Assessment Data Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant multi-stage data pipeline on GCP that ingests nightly standardized test score files from assessment vendors via SFTP, validates against versioned JSON schemas, quarantines invalid rows, links scores to student enrollment records, normalizes into a canonical schema stored in ClickHouse, and refreshes materialized views powering Grafana dashboards for principals and curriculum coaches. The pipeline enforces small-cohort suppression at the aggregation layer and maintains a 7-year retention chain for state compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Five-stage Airflow DAG chain orchestrated by Cloud Composer: (1) Ingest DAG polls vendor SFTP endpoints and deposits raw files to GCS raw/ prefix with vendor and date partitioning. (2) Validate DAG invokes Cloud Run validation microservice checking each file against a versioned JSON schema registry, routing passing files to processed/ and failures to quarantine/ with structured error manifests. (3) Link DAG joins validated score rows to students and enrollment_records tables in BigQuery temp datasets by state_student_id and academic_year. (4) Normalize DAG applies Python transforms mapping vendor-specific score scales to canonical proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse normalized_scores. (5) Aggregate DAG triggers ClickHouse MV refreshes on performance_aggregates applying small-cohort suppression for groups below 10. VPC-SC perimeter encloses the entire GCP project; no student PII exits the boundary.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Cloud Composer 2, Python 3.12 DAGs. Validation microservice: Python 3.12 FastAPI on Cloud Run with jsonschema library. Storage: GCS for raw/quarantine/processed files, ClickHouse 24.x on Compute Engine n2-standard-8 for analytics, BigQuery for compliance audit exports. Dashboards: Grafana 10 with ClickHouse data source plugin. Auth: Google Workspace SSO, GCP IAM with Workload Identity. CI: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Prometheus, Cloud Monitoring, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Vendor SFTP Ingestion: polling-based download with file deduplication via GCS object hash. JSON Schema Validation: per-vendor schema registry with version pinning and quarantine routing with structured error manifests. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery join by state_student_id resolving multi-year enrollment changes. Score Normalization: configurable transform map per vendor converting raw/scaled scores to canonical proficiency_level. Aggregation Refresh: ClickHouse MV refresh computing proficiency rates by school, grade, subject, and academic year with cohort suppression. Dashboard Layer: Grafana panels for school comparison, grade-band trends, year-over-year proficiency shifts. Quarantine Management: data team remediation queue surfacing failed rows with field-level error codes.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment hosting Airflow scheduler and workers, Cloud Run validation service scaling 0-10 instances on ingestion trigger, GCS buckets for raw/quarantine/processed/dag-sync, ClickHouse 2-node Compute Engine cluster with SSD for normalized_scores and performance_aggregates, BigQuery dataset for enrollment lookups and compliance audit exports, Secret Manager storing vendor SFTP credentials, Grafana instance on Compute Engine with ClickHouse and BigQuery data sources, Prometheus scraping ClickHouse and Cloud Run metrics, PagerDuty via Cloud Monitoring alerting policies.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Ingestion: Cloud Composer triggers ingest_dag at 23:00. Airflow SFTPSensor polls each active vendor host for new files matching date pattern. Files downloaded to GCS raw/vendor_id/date/ prefix. File hash checked against processed manifest to skip duplicates. validate_dag triggered on successful ingest. Validation and Quarantine: validate_dag submits each raw file to Cloud Run microservice. Service loads schema version from vendor registry, validates each row, accumulates errors. Files with greater than 5% row failure rate quarantined to GCS quarantine/vendor_id/date/ with JSON error manifest. PagerDuty alert fired with vendor name, file name, and failure rate. Passing files moved to processed/. Normalization and Aggregation: link_dag loads processed rows into BigQuery temp table, joins to enrollment_records on state_student_id and academic_year, flags unmatched rows as orphaned for data team review. normalize_dag transforms linked rows to proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse. aggregate_dag executes ClickHouse MV refresh on performance_aggregates grouped by school_id, grade_level, subject, academic_year, applying WHERE n_students >= 10 suppression. Dashboard queries read exclusively from performance_aggregates.
target_output.data_model: assessment_vendors (id UUID PK, name, sftp_host, sftp_credential_secret_id, schema_version, dsa_document_gcs_path, active BOOL). raw_score_files (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, gcs_path, file_hash, received_at, row_count INT, valid_row_count INT, validation_status ENUM pending/passed/quarantined/failed, quarantine_manifest_path, processed_at). students (id UUID PK, state_student_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, district_student_id, first_name_encrypted, last_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, current_school_id FK, active BOOL). enrollment_records (id UUID PK, student_id FK, school_id FK, academic_year, grade_level INT, enrollment_date, withdrawal_date). normalized_scores (id UUID PK, student_id FK, vendor_id FK, raw_score_file_id FK, assessment_name, subject, raw_score FLOAT, scaled_score FLOAT, proficiency_level ENUM below_basic/basic/proficient/advanced, test_date, academic_year). performance_aggregates (id UUID PK, school_id FK, grade_level INT, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate FLOAT, n_students INT, suppressed BOOL, refreshed_at). school_benchmarks (id UUID PK, school_id FK, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target FLOAT, academic_year).
target_output.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal interfaces: Cloud Run validation endpoint POST /validate (multipart file + vendor_id + schema_version, returns {passed_rows, failed_rows, error_manifest_path}). Airflow REST API internal: GET /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns for monitoring integration. BigQuery compliance export: scheduled query writing score access events to compliance_audit dataset. Grafana queries ClickHouse via native HTTP interface on port 8123 restricted to read-only grafana service account. PagerDuty alert webhook on DAG failure and quarantine threshold breach.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: small-cohort suppression (n less than 10) enforced in ClickHouse MV definition. ClickHouse row-level security restricts principals to their own school_id. VPC-SC perimeter blocks all GCS and BigQuery API calls originating outside the district GCP project. CMEK via Cloud KMS encrypts all GCS buckets and ClickHouse persistent disks. Vendor SFTP credentials in Secret Manager with 90-day rotation. Student PII fields (first_name, last_name, dob) AES-256 encrypted before insert. BigQuery audit dataset captures every query touching normalized_scores with timestamp, principal identity, and row count. Data sharing agreement document IDs logged per vendor ingestion run.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Composer 2 in us-central1 with 3 scheduler nodes and auto-scaling worker pool (2-8 workers). DAG deployment: Cloud Build pushes Python files to GCS dag-sync bucket; Composer picks up within 60s. Cloud Run validation service: Cloud Build builds Docker image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys revision with 100% traffic shift on health check pass. ClickHouse upgraded via Ansible playbook with pre-upgrade disk snapshot. Environments: dev (single Composer, ClickHouse dev node with 90-day score sample), staging (full topology with 2 mock vendors), production (Composer + 2-node ClickHouse). Rollback: DAG git tag rollback via GCS sync; ClickHouse snapshot restore; Cloud Run revision rollback.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes ClickHouse system.metrics every 30s: query_duration_ms p99, rows_read_per_second, disk_used_bytes. Cloud Monitoring tracks Cloud Run: request_count, error_rate, instance_count. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Health showing DAG run durations and quarantine rates per vendor; Data Quality showing orphaned_scores count and validation pass rate; ClickHouse Performance showing MV refresh duration and disk growth. PagerDuty alerts: DAG run failure, quarantine rate above 10%, MV refresh above 90 minutes, ClickHouse disk above 80%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month: Cloud Composer 2 ~$400, ClickHouse 2x n2-standard-8 ~$280, Cloud Run validation ~$30, GCS lifecycle storage ~$60, Grafana/Prometheus instance ~$30. Scaling trigger: add ClickHouse replica node at 100M normalized_scores rows or p99 MV refresh exceeding 60 minutes. Cloud Run validation workers scale automatically on file volume without manual intervention.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SFTP ingestion for one vendor, JSON schema validation with quarantine, enrollment linkage via BigQuery join, normalization to canonical schema, ClickHouse aggregation with small-cohort suppression, Grafana grade-level dashboards for 5 pilot schools, Cloud Build CI and VPC-SC perimeter. Phase 2 Multi-Vendor (6 weeks): schema registry with version management for 2 additional vendors, parallel DAG execution per vendor, orphaned_scores remediation workflow, expanded dashboards for all district schools. Phase 3 Advanced Analytics (8 weeks): year-over-year proficiency trend views, school benchmark comparison panels, early-warning data mart for predictive model ingestion, BigQuery compliance export automation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type hints on all DAG task functions. pytest for unit tests with Airflow LocalExecutor; minimum 80% coverage on normalization transform functions. Airflow DAG linting via airflow dags test in Cloud Build pipeline. ClickHouse schema migrations via versioned SQL files in /migrations directory applied by Ansible. No student PII in Airflow XCom payloads — pass GCS paths only. Vendor schema versions pinned in config/vendor_schemas.yaml under git version control. PR requires data engineer review for normalization or aggregation query changes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor sends mid-year schema change without notice corrupting normalization. Mitigation: schema registry with strict version pinning; validation DAG blocks normalization on schema mismatch and pages data team. Risk: ClickHouse single-node disk exhaustion on 7-year score accumulation. Mitigation: quarterly disk usage review with auto-alert at 80%; replica node planned at 100M rows. Risk: Enrollment record gaps leave test scores unlinked. Mitigation: orphaned_scores metric on Grafana with 5-business-day remediation SLA. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics sub-second latency; accepts manual upgrade and backup overhead.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics layer due to sub-second aggregation latency requirements for principal dashboards. Confirmed GCS over Cloud SQL for raw file storage given unstructured vendor file formats. Chose Cloud Composer over self-managed Airflow to reduce operator burden. FERPA small-cohort suppression threshold set at 10 students per state guidance.

metadata.notes: FERPA-compliant K-12 assessment pipeline with multi-vendor ingestion, ClickHouse analytics, and small-cohort suppression.

project_goal.value: Fullstack web application for university residence life to manage dormitory room assignments, roommate matching, maintenance requests, and community programming for on-campus housing
project_goal.rationale: Replace manual spreadsheet-based room assignment process causing errors each semester

target_users.value: Students submitting housing preferences; residence life administrators approving placements; facilities staff processing maintenance requests
target_users.rationale: Three distinct roles with different workflows and access levels

project_class.rationale: Requires React frontend, Go API backend, and PostgreSQL data layer for full CRUD housing workflows

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Student portal, admin panel, SIS integration, and occupancy analytics all required

complexity_level.rationale: Lottery assignment algorithm and multi-role admin workflows add complexity beyond standard CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Personal student data and accommodation requests require careful RBAC; lottery fairness has reputational risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student housing preferences and accommodation requests are FERPA-protected educational records

external_exposure.rationale: All routes require Auth0 login; no public-facing pages beyond marketing splash

access_model.value: RBAC with three roles: student (own records only), housing_admin (all assignments and overrides), facilities_staff (maintenance requests only)
access_model.rationale: Prevents students from viewing other students accommodation details

feature_scope.value: Housing preference submission, lottery-based room assignment, roommate matching, maintenance request tracking, community programming events, admin override and placement management, occupancy reporting
feature_scope.rationale: Full residence life management scope

mvp_scope.value: Housing preference submission, lottery assignment algorithm, room assignment confirmation, maintenance request submission and tracking, admin placement override
mvp_scope.rationale: Core assignment and maintenance workflows needed for fall semester launch

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Auth0 OIDC, RBAC enforced at API handler level, accommodation data accessible only to housing_admin role
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA and university data governance minimum controls

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; consistent with university frontend standards

backend_stack.rationale: Performance for lottery algorithm; low memory footprint on DigitalOcean App Platform

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 DigitalOcean Managed Database; Redis 7 for session cache and lottery run locking
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL for HA; Redis prevents concurrent lottery runs corrupting assignment state

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform for API and frontend; DigitalOcean Managed Database for PostgreSQL and Redis
hosting_target.rationale: Simplified operations and cost-effective for university department budget

privacy_retention_policy.value: Student housing records retained for duration of enrollment plus 5 years; maintenance requests retained 3 years; accommodation requests retained permanently per ADA guidance
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: University records retention policy aligned with state education law

future_scope.value: Roommate social profiles, RA incident reporting module, mobile app for maintenance photo submissions
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap pending MVP adoption

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Banner SIS via REST API for enrollment verification; $600/month infrastructure budget; go-live before March housing selection
constraints.rationale: SIS integration non-negotiable; budget and timeline fixed by university calendar

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards for API latency and error rates, Prometheus scraping Go metrics, PagerDuty for lottery job failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Operations team needs visibility into lottery runs and maintenance request backlogs

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; housing admin team involved in sprint reviews for workflow validation
execution_preference.rationale: Domain feedback from housing staff required to validate assignment and override UX

compliance_context.value: FERPA: student housing records are educational records; accommodation request data accessible only to housing_admin role; no accommodation details shared with roommates or facilities staff
compliance_context.rationale: ADA accommodation data requires strict access control beyond standard FERPA baseline

project.goal: Replace manual housing assignment with self-service student portal and admin management system
project.target_users: Students, housing administrators, facilities staff
project.timeline: Go-live before March fall housing selection

security.auth: Auth0 OIDC
security.rbac: three roles enforced at handler level

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions and lottery locking

reasoner_reviews.product: The lottery algorithm is the highest-risk functional component. Concurrency bugs during assignment runs could result in double-bookings causing significant student disruption. The Redis distributed lock is essential and must be validated under load before production deployment.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Gin is a strong choice for the lottery algorithm given its goroutine model and low GC overhead. The Banner SIS integration should be abstracted behind a client interface to allow mocking in tests. PostgreSQL advisory locks should complement Redis locking during assignment writes to prevent race conditions at the database layer.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA requires accommodation request data be accessible only to housing_admin role. The RBAC middleware must enforce this at the field level in response serialization, not just at the route level. A student with a valid JWT could potentially construct a direct API call to retrieve accommodation fields unless row-level masking is implemented.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $600/month budget is achievable with DigitalOcean App Platform at current scope. The March deadline is tight for full feature scope; recommend sequencing preference submission and lottery before maintenance request UI to ensure core assignment workflow is tested before go-live.

backend.service_design: Go Gin application with handler packages per domain: housing (preferences, assignments, lottery), maintenance (requests, status updates), events (programming), admin (overrides, reporting), sis (Banner sync client)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1; versioned at /api/v1/; JWT claims from Auth0 validated at middleware; role extracted from JWT custom claim and passed via request context to handlers
backend.background_jobs: Lottery runner as triggered background goroutine with Redis SETNX lock; Banner SIS sync job nightly to refresh student enrollment status; maintenance digest email job at 8am daily
backend.failure_handling: Lottery job failure triggers PagerDuty alert and sets lottery_run status to failed, preserving pre-run assignment snapshot for rollback; SIS sync failures logged with retry on next nightly run
backend.backend_risks: Banner REST API rate limits may throttle nightly sync for large cohorts; lottery algorithm correctness depends on deterministic random seed management for audit trail

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting: /student/* (preference submission, room confirmation, maintenance), /admin/* (assignment management, overrides, reporting), /facilities/* (maintenance queue)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Student: Housing Preferences form with dormitory rankings and accommodation requests, Room Assignment confirmation, Maintenance Request submission, My Requests tracker. Admin: Assignment Dashboard with pending lottery runs, Override modal, Occupancy Report. Facilities: Maintenance Queue with category filters and status update controls.
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state including assignment status and maintenance queue polling; Zustand for lottery run progress state during active runs
frontend.frontend_risks: Lottery run progress polling must use short intervals only during active runs to avoid unnecessary API load; accommodation fields must be hidden from student-facing roommate profile views

security.auth_design: Auth0 OIDC with university IdP federation; custom Action injecting housing_role claim based on directory group membership; short 1-hour JWT TTL with refresh token rotation
security.authorization_model: RBAC in Go middleware: student role sees own records via user_id filter; housing_admin sees all records; facilities_staff restricted to maintenance_requests endpoints only
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON logs for accommodation data access with user_id, resource_id, and timestamp; Grafana alert on accommodation reads outside business hours; lottery seed captured before run for audit reproducibility
security.security_risks: Auth0 custom claim injection must stay in sync with directory group changes; stale role claims possible if token cache not invalidated on role update

data.entities: students (id, auth0_sub, student_id_banner, full_name, email, enrollment_status, school_year), dormitory_buildings (id, name, address, total_rooms, building_type ENUM traditional/suite/apartment), room_assignments (id, student_id FK, building_id FK, room_number, room_type ENUM single/double/triple, assignment_status ENUM pending/confirmed/cancelled, lottery_run_id FK), lottery_runs (id, triggered_by_admin_id FK, seed_hex VARCHAR(64), status ENUM running/completed/failed, started_at, completed_at), housing_preferences (id, student_id FK, dormitory_rank_1 FK, dormitory_rank_2 FK, accommodation_type, accommodation_notes TEXT), maintenance_requests (id, student_id FK, building_id FK, room_number, category ENUM plumbing/electrical/hvac/general, status ENUM open/in_progress/resolved, submitted_at), housing_contracts (id, student_id FK, academic_year, room_assignment_id FK, signed_at), programming_events (id, building_id FK, title, event_date, max_capacity INT), waitlist_entries (id, student_id FK, building_id FK, preference_rank INT)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 for all relational data; Redis 7 for session cache and lottery locks; DigitalOcean Spaces for housing contract PDFs
data.migration_notes: golang-migrate for schema migrations; versioned SQL files in /db/migrations; applied as pre-deploy hook before App Platform traffic shift

devops.deployment_topology: DigitalOcean App Platform: 2 Go API replicas (1 vCPU/1GB), React static site via DO CDN, Managed PostgreSQL 16 with standby, Managed Redis 7
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: golangci-lint, go test with race detector, Vitest React unit tests, Docker build, doctl apps update with pre-deploy golang-migrate run
devops.environments: dev (docker-compose with Postgres 16 and Redis 7), staging (App Platform production-mirror with anonymized student fixtures), production
devops.rollback_strategy: App Platform instant rollback to prior deployment slot; PostgreSQL point-in-time recovery for data issues; Redis lottery lock TTL auto-expires stuck locks after 30 minutes

ISS-001.title: Lottery algorithm lacks deterministic seed audit trail
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described lottery as random assignment but did not specify how the random seed is captured. Housing administrators must reproduce exact assignment order if students contest fairness.

history.note: Identified in architect_reasoner review: no seed capture mechanism specified

ISS-002.title: Accommodation data access control not enforced at field level
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan applied RBAC at route level for accommodation-tagged preferences but did not specify field-level masking in Go response serialization. Student with valid JWT could retrieve another student's accommodation fields via direct API call.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged missing row-level field masking for accommodation data

focus_issues.title: Lottery algorithm lacks deterministic seed audit trail
focus_issues.detail: Random seed not captured; assignment order cannot be reproduced for student fairness disputes.

focus_issues.title: Accommodation data access control not enforced at field level
focus_issues.detail: Route-level RBAC insufficient; accommodation fields not masked for non-admin role API responses.

latest_recommendations: Persist lottery seed to lottery_runs table before execution for audit reproducibility
Add field-level masking in Go response structs to strip accommodation fields from non-admin JWT responses

previous_audits.summary: Solid fullstack foundation with appropriate Go/PostgreSQL choices and Auth0 RBAC. Two high-severity gaps in lottery auditability and accommodation data isolation prevent approval.
strengths: Well-structured Go handler decomposition by domain
Redis distributed lock correctly applied to prevent concurrent lottery runs
Banner SIS abstraction behind client interface enables portability
concerns: Lottery produces non-reproducible results with no seed capture
Accommodation fields in housing_preferences accessible to students via crafted API requests
blocking_issues: Lottery seed not persisted to lottery_runs table — assignment order cannot be reproduced
Accommodation data field masking not implemented in Go response layer — FERPA/ADA risk
recommendations: Store crypto/rand seed in lottery_runs.seed_hex before run execution; expose admin replay endpoint
Implement separate AdminDTO and StudentDTO Go structs for accommodation-containing records

issue_updates.title: Lottery algorithm lacks deterministic seed audit trail
issue_updates.detail: Lottery algorithm mentions Redis lock and goroutine but omits seed capture mechanism
