previous_plan.title: University Housing Management Portal — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A fullstack application for university residence life with React 18 frontend, Go 1.22 Gin API, and PostgreSQL 16 on DigitalOcean App Platform. Manages housing preferences, lottery-based room assignments, maintenance requests, and admin overrides with Auth0 RBAC.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 OIDC with housing_role custom claim. RBAC enforced at route level in Go middleware. Accommodation request data restricted to housing_admin routes. TLS 1.3 enforced at App Platform edge.
previous_plan.workflows: Lottery run triggered by admin; goroutine acquires Redis SETNX lock, shuffles eligible students, assigns rooms sequentially, releases lock. Students notified by email on completion.
previous_plan.data_model: students, dormitory_buildings, room_assignments, roommate_preferences, housing_preferences, maintenance_requests, housing_contracts, programming_events, waitlist_entries in PostgreSQL 16.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixing ISS-001 by persisting crypto/rand lottery seed to lottery_runs.seed_hex before any assignments are written, enabling cryptographic audit replay. Fixing ISS-002 by implementing separate AdminDTO and StudentDTO Go structs for housing_preferences records so accommodation_type and accommodation_notes are structurally absent from non-admin serialized responses — not suppressed by omitempty which can be bypassed but by using distinct response types.

fix_report.action_taken: Added lottery_runs table with seed_hex VARCHAR(64) column. Lottery handler generates 32-byte crypto/rand seed, stores as hex in lottery_runs before initializing math/rand seeded shuffle. Admin endpoint GET /api/v1/admin/lottery-runs/{id}/replay re-executes assignment order from stored seed against preference snapshot captured at run time.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Housing admins can reproduce exact student assignment order from any historical lottery run; seed captured before writes prevents post-hoc tampering

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented HousingPreferenceAdminDTO (includes accommodation_type, accommodation_notes) and HousingPreferenceStudentDTO (omits accommodation fields by structural absence, not json omitempty). Handler selects DTO based on housing_role claim from JWT context. Integration tests assert accommodation field absence in student and facilities_staff role responses.
fix_report.expected_outcome: No accommodation data reachable through any API endpoint for student or facilities_staff JWT holders; confirmed by role-parameterized integration test suite

target_output.title: University Housing Management Portal
target_output.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant fullstack web application for university residence life built on React 18 and Go 1.22 Gin, deployed to DigitalOcean App Platform with Managed PostgreSQL 16. Manages the full on-campus housing lifecycle: student preference submission, lottery-based room assignment with cryptographic audit trail, maintenance request tracking by facilities staff, and admin placement overrides. Accommodation request data is isolated at the API serialization layer using role-aware Go DTO structs that structurally exclude sensitive fields from unauthorized role responses.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA served as a static site via DigitalOcean CDN communicates over HTTPS to a Go 1.22 Gin API deployed as a managed App Platform component with 2 replicas behind a load balancer. API connects to DigitalOcean Managed PostgreSQL 16 primary/standby and Managed Redis 7 for session caching and lottery distributed locking. Auth0 OIDC handles authentication with a custom Action injecting housing_role claims from university directory group membership. Banner SIS client polls nightly to sync student enrollment status, gating housing preference eligibility. Lottery runs execute as Go goroutines with Redis SETNX lock; crypto/rand seed captured to lottery_runs table before any assignment writes, enabling deterministic audit replay. Role-aware Go DTOs enforce field-level accommodation data isolation at the serialization boundary.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, Vitest for component tests. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin framework, golang-migrate for schema management, crypto/rand for lottery seed generation, sendgrid-go for assignment notification emails. Database: PostgreSQL 16 DigitalOcean Managed with standby replica, Redis 7 for sessions and lottery distributed locks. Auth: Auth0 OIDC with university IdP federation and custom role claim Action. Storage: DigitalOcean Spaces for housing contract PDFs. CI: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint and go test race detector. Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Housing Preference Submission: dormitory building rankings (up to 5), roommate preference by student ID lookup, accommodation request form visible only in housing_admin view. Lottery Assignment Engine: deterministic seed capture to lottery_runs, sequential room assignment respecting accommodation priority tier, post-run SendGrid notification emails. Admin Management: placement override modal with reason logging, occupancy dashboard by building and room type, lottery run history with audit replay endpoint. Maintenance Requests: category-tagged submissions (plumbing/electrical/hvac/general), facilities staff queue with building filter, student notification on status transitions. Community Programming: building-scoped event creation, student RSVP with capacity tracking. Banner SIS Sync: nightly enrollment refresh via REST client, eligibility gate on preference submission form.
target_output.system_components: DigitalOcean App Platform API component (2x 1vCPU/1GB Go containers), App Platform static site component (React SPA via DO CDN edge), Managed PostgreSQL 16 primary with standby in nyc3 region, Managed Redis 7 for session cache and lottery distributed locks, DigitalOcean Spaces bucket for housing contract PDFs, Auth0 tenant with university SAML IdP federation and custom role injection Action, Prometheus /metrics endpoint on Go API, Grafana for dashboard visualization, PagerDuty for lottery failure and high error rate alerting.
target_output.workflows: Housing Preference Submission: Student authenticates via Auth0, receives housing_student JWT. Accesses /student/preferences form. Selects dormitory rankings, optionally selects preferred roommate by Banner student ID lookup, submits accommodation request if applicable (accommodation fields stored but returned only in AdminDTO). Banner SIS sync cache checked for enrollment status before accepting submission. Lottery Assignment Run: Housing admin triggers POST /api/v1/admin/lottery/run. Handler acquires Redis SETNX lock (lottery:run:active, 30-minute TTL). Generates 32-byte crypto/rand seed, stores seed_hex to lottery_runs with status running. Goroutine loads preference snapshot into memory, shuffles eligible students using seeded math/rand with accommodation-priority students processed first, iterates assignment pool, writes room_assignments. On completion updates lottery_runs status to completed and releases lock. SendGrid emails sent to assigned students. Admin Audit Replay: GET /api/v1/admin/lottery-runs/{id}/replay loads seed_hex, re-initializes seeded rand, re-executes assignment on captured preference snapshot, returns ordered list matching original run. Maintenance Request Flow: Student POSTs /api/v1/maintenance/requests with building_id, category, description. Routes to facilities_staff queue by building. Staff PATCHes status transitions. Student receives email on each state change.
target_output.data_model: students (id UUID PK, auth0_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, student_id_banner VARCHAR UNIQUE, full_name, email, enrollment_status ENUM enrolled/not_enrolled, current_school_year INT, created_at). dormitory_buildings (id UUID PK, name, address, total_rooms INT, building_type ENUM traditional/suite/apartment, gender_policy ENUM coed/male_only/female_only). lottery_runs (id UUID PK, triggered_by_admin_id FK, seed_hex VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, status ENUM running/completed/failed, started_at, completed_at, assignments_count INT). room_assignments (id UUID PK, student_id FK, building_id FK, room_number VARCHAR, room_type ENUM single/double/triple, assignment_status ENUM pending/confirmed/cancelled, lottery_run_id FK, accommodation_flagged BOOL, assigned_at). housing_preferences (id UUID PK, student_id FK, lottery_run_id FK, dormitory_rank_1 FK, dormitory_rank_2 FK, dormitory_rank_3 FK, accommodation_type VARCHAR, accommodation_notes TEXT, submitted_at). roommate_preferences (id UUID PK, student_id FK, preferred_roommate_student_id FK, preference_rank INT, submitted_at). maintenance_requests (id UUID PK, student_id FK, building_id FK, room_number, category ENUM plumbing/electrical/hvac/general, description, status ENUM open/in_progress/resolved, submitted_at, resolved_at). housing_contracts (id UUID PK, student_id FK, academic_year, room_assignment_id FK, signed_at, contract_spaces_key). programming_events (id UUID PK, building_id FK, created_by_admin_id FK, title, event_date, max_capacity INT, rsvp_count INT). waitlist_entries (id UUID PK, student_id FK, building_id FK, preference_rank INT, added_at).
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1/ with Auth0 JWT validation in Gin middleware, housing_role extracted to context. Student: POST /preferences (submit with dormitory rankings), GET /assignments/me (own assignment, StudentDTO), POST /maintenance/requests, GET /maintenance/requests/me, POST /events/{id}/rsvp. Admin: GET /admin/preferences (all with AdminDTO including accommodation fields), POST /admin/lottery/run (trigger with Redis lock), GET /admin/lottery-runs/{id}/replay (deterministic audit replay from seed_hex), PATCH /admin/assignments/{id}/override (manual placement with reason), GET /admin/occupancy-report (by building and room type). Facilities: GET /facilities/maintenance?building_id={id} (queue), PATCH /facilities/maintenance/{id} (status update). Banner SIS sync: internal nightly cron, not externally exposed.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA/ADA controls: HousingPreferenceAdminDTO and HousingPreferenceStudentDTO are distinct Go structs; StudentDTO does not declare accommodation_type or accommodation_notes fields — structural absence prevents any serialization path from exposing them. Handler selects DTO based on housing_role from JWT context. Integration tests parameterized across all three roles assert accommodation field absence in student and facilities_staff responses. Lottery seed_hex column in lottery_runs is written once before run execution; API service account granted INSERT but not UPDATE on seed_hex preventing tampering. Auth0 custom claim injection validated on each token refresh; 1-hour JWT TTL limits stale role claim window. TLS 1.3 enforced at App Platform load balancer. Accommodation data access logged to structured JSON audit trail with user_id, resource_id, and timestamp; Grafana alert fires on after-hours accommodation reads.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform manages Go API containers with zero-downtime rolling deploy on git push to main after GitHub Actions pipeline passes. Pre-deploy hook runs golang-migrate up before traffic shift. GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck, go test -race with 80% coverage gate, Vitest React tests, doctl apps create-deployment. Environments: dev (docker-compose with Postgres 16 and Redis 7, Auth0 dev tenant), staging (App Platform production-mirror config, anonymized student fixture data, Auth0 staging tenant), production (2 API replicas, Managed PostgreSQL with standby, Managed Redis). Rollback: App Platform instant rollback to prior deployment; PostgreSQL point-in-time recovery within 7-day backup window; Redis lottery lock auto-expires at 30-minute TTL.
target_output.observability: Prometheus /metrics on Go API scraped every 15s: http_request_duration_seconds by handler, active_goroutines, db_connection_pool_size. Grafana dashboards: API Latency showing p50/p99 per endpoint group (student/admin/facilities); Maintenance Queue showing open request count by building and category; Lottery Runs showing duration and assignments-per-second rate; SIS Sync showing last sync timestamp and enrollment count change. PagerDuty alerts: lottery run stuck in running status beyond 20 minutes, API error rate above 2%, PostgreSQL replication lag above 30 seconds.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $420/month: App Platform 2 API replicas ~$24, static site CDN ~$5, Managed PostgreSQL basic ~$50, Managed Redis ~$15, DigitalOcean Spaces ~$5, Auth0 free tier under 7K MAU, Grafana Cloud free tier. Well within $600/month budget. Scaling: upgrade App Platform API to professional tier for auto-scaling if concurrent preference submissions during selection window exceed single-replica capacity.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Auth0 OIDC with university IdP federation, housing preference form with dormitory rankings and accommodation request, lottery assignment engine with crypto/rand seed capture and Redis lock, room assignment confirmation emails, maintenance request submission and tracking, admin override UI with reason logging, Banner SIS nightly sync, CI/CD pipeline with migration pre-deploy hook. Phase 2 (5 weeks): Roommate social profile matching, community programming events with RSVP, occupancy analytics dashboard, accommodation audit logging Grafana alert, housing contract PDF generation to DigitalOcean Spaces. Phase 3 (post-launch): RA incident reporting module, mobile maintenance photo submission per roadmap.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: strict golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck. Separate AdminDTO and StudentDTO structs required for any handler returning accommodation-containing records — never use json omitempty to suppress sensitive fields. Integration tests using testcontainers-go must cover accommodation field masking across all three role types. Lottery algorithm unit tests assert deterministic output for identical seed and preference input. React: Vitest tests for preference form validation and admin override modal. PR requires two-engineer approval for changes to lottery algorithm or accommodation data handling.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Concurrent preference submissions during peak selection window overwhelms PostgreSQL write throughput. Mitigation: preference submission window closes 24 hours before lottery run; lottery executes off-peak with Redis lock preventing re-entry. Risk: Banner SIS API outage blocks enrollment refresh, potentially gating eligible students. Mitigation: SIS sync failure surfaces non-blocking warning on form but does not prevent submission; admin can manually override enrollment status. Risk: Auth0 custom claim stale after directory group change. Mitigation: 1-hour JWT TTL with refresh token rotation limits exposure window. Tradeoff: separate AdminDTO/StudentDTO structs over runtime field filtering adds struct maintenance overhead but eliminates accidental accommodation leakage risk from omitempty misuse.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed crypto/rand seed capture to lottery_runs resolves ISS-001 fairness audit requirement; separate Go DTO structs for accommodation masking resolves ISS-002 field-level access control gap. Selected DigitalOcean over AWS for $600/month budget fit. Confirmed Banner SIS REST API integration over direct DB access per university IT policy.

metadata.notes: Round 2 fixes lottery audit trail (ISS-001) via crypto/rand seed capture and accommodation data masking (ISS-002) via Go DTO struct separation for FERPA-compliant university housing management.

project_goal.value: CLI tool for K-12 district IT administrators to convert legacy fixed-width flat-file course descriptions into structured JSON and upload them to the district curriculum management API
project_goal.rationale: Decade-old fixed-width format incompatible with new curriculum API; manual re-entry would take months

target_users.value: District IT administrators running batch migrations from command line on district servers
target_users.rationale: Non-developer staff running scripted migrations; requires clear CLI feedback and error reporting

project_class.rationale: Standalone command-line tool with no browser UI; executes locally on district servers

capabilities.rationale: Parsing, transformation, and API upload capabilities; no frontend or auth service required

complexity_level.rationale: Fixed parsing logic with configurable field mappings; limited branching complexity

risk_level.rationale: Internal tool handling non-sensitive course catalog data; no student PII involved

data_sensitivity.rationale: Course catalog data is internal operational data; not student records under FERPA

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on district servers with no external network exposure; API calls to internal curriculum management service only

access_model.value: Custom JWT authentication for curriculum API calls; JWT provisioned per IT admin from district identity system
access_model.rationale: Curriculum management API requires authenticated requests; JWT passed via CLI flag or environment variable

feature_scope.value: Fixed-width file parsing with configurable field-mapping schemas, JSON serialization with validation, batch upload to curriculum management API with retry logic, error reporting to local log file
feature_scope.rationale: Full transformation and upload scope

mvp_scope.value: Parse single fixed-width file using bundled default field-mapping config, validate transformed JSON, upload batch to API, write success/error summary to stdout and log file
mvp_scope.rationale: Single-file processing covers 80% of migration workload

security_baseline.value: JWT stored in environment variable, not passed as CLI argument to avoid shell history exposure; TLS verified on all API calls
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents credential leakage in process lists and shell history files

frontend_stack.value: none — CLI only; stdout and log file output
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI required; CLI feedback via stdout progress indicators and structured log file

backend_stack.rationale: District IT team familiar with Python; argparse and requests available in standard library and common packages

data_platform.value: No database; input from local flat files, output to curriculum management API and local log files
data_platform.rationale: Stateless transformation tool; no persistence layer required

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal district servers running Python 3.12; distributed as pip-installable package or single-script bundle
hosting_target.rationale: District servers are self-managed; no cloud deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Local log files retained 30 days then purged by district IT policy; no student PII processed or stored
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Internal operational logs only

future_scope.value: Support for additional legacy formats (CSV-based older exports), scheduled batch processing via cron, dry-run mode for pre-upload validation without API submission
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements after MVP migration completes

constraints.value: Must run on Python 3.12 without external dependencies beyond requests and jsonschema; no internet access from district servers; curriculum API rate limit of 100 records per batch request
constraints.rationale: District server policy restricts pip installations; API batch size limit enforced by curriculum vendor

observability_baseline.value: stdout progress output during run; structured JSON log file with per-record transformation results; summary counts at completion
observability_baseline.rationale: IT admins need actionable feedback on transformation errors without parsing verbose logs

execution_preference.value: Single-sprint delivery; IT admin review of field mappings before production run
execution_preference.rationale: Simple tool scope fits single-sprint; field mapping review prevents mass-upload errors

project.goal: Convert fixed-width legacy course catalog files to JSON and upload to curriculum management API
project.target_users: District IT administrators
project.timeline: Single sprint

security.auth: JWT from environment variable CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN

data.input: Fixed-width flat files from legacy SIS export
data.output: JSON batches to curriculum management API

reasoner_reviews.product: The transformation scope is well-contained: parse fixed-width rows, validate against JSON schema, batch upload. The configurable field-mapping schema is essential because different schools in the district may have slight column-width variations in their legacy exports. The error summary report is the most important user-facing output for IT admins managing large migrations.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The tool should be structured as three discrete pipeline stages: parse (fixed-width to dict), validate (jsonschema), and upload (requests with retry). Each stage should be independently testable and the pipeline should be halted per-record on validation failure rather than aborting the entire batch. A dry-run flag preventing API calls is essential for pre-migration verification.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT must be read exclusively from the CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN environment variable. If the tool accepted the token as a --token CLI argument, it would appear in process lists and shell history on shared district servers. TLS certificate verification must not be disabled even on internal network.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 100-record API batch limit requires the tool to chunk input files into batches of 100 and track per-batch upload status independently. If one batch fails after retries, the tool should continue uploading remaining batches and report the failed batch range in the summary.

backend.service_design: Three-stage pipeline implemented as Python module: FixedWidthParser (reads field-mapping config, extracts column slices), CourseRecordValidator (jsonschema validation of parsed dicts), CurriculumAPIClient (requests session with retry adapter, 100-record batching)
backend.api_patterns: No server-side API; outbound HTTP POST to curriculum management API at /api/v1/courses/batch; JWT in Authorization: Bearer header from env; 100-record batch chunking with per-batch retry
backend.failure_handling: Per-record validation errors appended to transformation_errors list; failed batches retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff; after max retries, batch range logged as failed and processing continues; final summary shows successful_records, failed_records, failed_batch_ranges
backend.backend_risks: Fixed-width column offsets may vary between school exports without documentation; curriculum API rate limiting may reject rapid consecutive batch submissions; JWT expiry mid-run will fail remaining batches without clear error message

security.auth_design: JWT read from CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN environment variable at tool startup; validation that variable is set before processing begins; no token logging in any output path
security.secrets_management: Environment variable injection via shell profile or systemd EnvironmentFile on district servers; no .env file bundled with tool distribution
security.security_risks: Shared district server accounts could expose CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN in environment if process listing is unrestricted; recommend per-admin token provisioning

data.entities: legacy_course_records (raw fixed-width row string, parsed fields: course_code, course_title, grade_level_min, grade_level_max, credit_hours, department_code, curriculum_subject), field_mapping_configs (column_name, start_offset, end_offset, strip_whitespace, required BOOL), transformation_errors (row_number, field_name, error_type, raw_value), api_upload_batches (batch_index, record_range_start, record_range_end, status ENUM pending/success/failed, attempt_count)
data.schema_notes: Field mapping config stored as YAML file allowing district IT to adjust column offsets without code changes; JSON schema for validated course record stored alongside mapping config for version control
data.data_risks: Malformed fixed-width rows with incorrect line endings (CRLF vs LF) from legacy system exports may shift column offsets; course_code uniqueness not enforced locally — duplicates in source file will be uploaded and may cause API conflicts

devops.deployment_topology: Single Python script or pip package deployed to district servers via internal file share or git clone; no container required
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: flake8 lint, pytest unit tests (parser, validator, API client mock), package build; no deployment step — IT admin manually places script on district servers
devops.environments: Local dev (pytest with mock API server), staging (district test server with curriculum API sandbox), production (district servers against live curriculum API)
devops.devops_risks: No automated deployment to district servers; version drift possible if IT admins run different script versions; recommend including --version flag printing git commit hash for support troubleshooting

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a simple CLI tool for K-12 district IT use. The plan is intentionally flawed: it incorrectly includes a frontend/dashboard section that does not apply to a CLI tool, and uses a web-app deployment topology description instead of a CLI package distribution approach. These inconsistencies reflect the flawed quality tier for this row.
target_output.title: District Legacy Course Catalog Migration CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 command-line tool for K-12 district IT administrators that reads fixed-width legacy course catalog exports, applies a configurable field-mapping schema to extract course code, title, grade level band, credit hours, and department code, validates each record against a JSON schema, and uploads validated batches of 100 records to the district curriculum management API. Transformation errors are written to a structured JSON log file and a summary of successful and failed records is printed to stdout on completion.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-stage local pipeline executing entirely on district servers with no inbound network exposure. Stage 1: FixedWidthParser reads the input file line-by-line, applying column offset slices from a YAML field-mapping config to produce dicts per course record. Stage 2: CourseRecordValidator runs each dict through jsonschema validation against the curriculum record schema, collecting per-record errors without halting the pipeline. Stage 3: CurriculumAPIClient chunks validated records into 100-record batches and POSTs each to the curriculum management API with JWT auth from CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN env variable. The tool outputs a structured JSON run log and stdout summary. Note: this plan incorrectly includes a web dashboard monitoring section below that is not applicable to a CLI tool — a flaw introduced by scope confusion.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. Libraries: argparse (stdlib) for CLI argument parsing, requests for HTTP with retry adapter, jsonschema for record validation, PyYAML for field-mapping config loading. No external database, no web framework, no container runtime. Packaging: setuptools with console_scripts entry point for pip install, or single-file script for direct distribution. GitHub Actions for CI with flake8 lint and pytest. NOTE: plan incorrectly lists React 18 in technology stack as a dashboard framework — this is a flawed entry inconsistent with CLI project class.
target_output.functional_feature_map: parse command: reads --input flat file and --mapping config YAML, emits parsed record count to stdout. validate command: runs parsed records against --schema JSON schema file, outputs validation error count and --errors-output JSON file. upload command: reads validated records, chunks into 100-record batches, POSTs to --api-url with JWT from env, retries failed batches up to 3 times, writes run log. run command: executes parse-validate-upload pipeline in sequence with --dry-run flag skipping API upload stage. --version flag: prints git commit hash for support troubleshooting.
target_output.system_components: FixedWidthParser class reading column slices per YAML offset config, CourseRecordValidator class wrapping jsonschema Draft7Validator, CurriculumAPIClient class with requests.Session and HTTPAdapter retry configuration (3 retries, exponential backoff), RunLogger writing structured JSON per-record results to output file, SummaryPrinter formatting stdout completion table. CLI entrypoint via argparse subcommand dispatcher.
target_output.workflows: Migration Run: IT admin sets CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN env variable from district identity system token. Invokes: catalog-migrate run --input courses_legacy.txt --mapping configs/default_mapping.yaml --api-url https://curriculum.district.internal/api/v1 --log-output run_log.json. FixedWidthParser reads file line-by-line, extracts course_code (cols 1-8), course_title (9-58), grade_level_min (59-61), grade_level_max (62-64), credit_hours (65-68), department_code (69-73) per mapping config. Each parsed record passed to CourseRecordValidator; failures appended to transformation_errors list with row number and field name. Validated records chunked into 100-record lists. Each batch POSTed to /api/v1/courses/batch with Authorization: Bearer JWT. On 4xx response, batch logged as failed and processing continues. On 5xx, retry up to 3 times with 2s/4s/8s backoff. Final stdout summary: 'Processed: 1420 records. Uploaded: 1380. Validation errors: 28. Upload failures: 12 (batches 3, 11)'. Dry Run: Same pipeline with --dry-run flag; CurriculumAPIClient skips POST and prints 'DRY RUN: would upload 100 records to /api/v1/courses/batch' per batch.
target_output.data_model: legacy_course_records: raw fixed-width line string, parsed fields: course_code (VARCHAR 8), course_title (VARCHAR 50), grade_level_min (INT), grade_level_max (INT), credit_hours (DECIMAL 3.1), department_code (VARCHAR 5), curriculum_subject (derived from department_code lookup). field_mapping_configs (YAML): column_name, start_offset INT, end_offset INT, strip_whitespace BOOL, required BOOL, type ENUM str/int/decimal. transformation_errors (JSON log entries): row_number INT, course_code, field_name, error_type ENUM missing_required/type_mismatch/schema_violation/encoding_error, raw_value. api_upload_batches (runtime tracking): batch_index INT, record_range_start INT, record_range_end INT, status ENUM pending/success/failed, attempt_count INT, http_status_code, error_message.
target_output.api_design: Outbound API calls only. POST /api/v1/courses/batch on curriculum management API: request body [{course_code, course_title, grade_level_min, grade_level_max, credit_hours, department_code, curriculum_subject}] (100-record array max). Headers: Authorization: Bearer {JWT from env}, Content-Type: application/json. Expected responses: 201 Created with {imported_count, rejected_records[]}; 400 Bad Request with {errors[]}; 429 Rate Limited triggers retry with Retry-After header respect; 5xx triggers exponential backoff retry. No inbound API server. CLI does not expose any HTTP listener.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT authentication: CURRICULUM_API_TOKEN environment variable read at startup; tool exits with error if variable not set; token value never written to log files or stdout. TLS: requests library verifies SSL certificates by default; verify=False is not used even on internal network. Process security: token not accepted via --token CLI flag to prevent shell history and process list exposure. Log files: structured JSON run log contains only course_codes and error descriptions, no JWT values or student data. File permissions: run log written with 0o640 permissions restricting to owner and group.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: pip install from internal PyPI mirror or git clone of repository to district servers. Entry point registered via setuptools console_scripts: catalog-migrate. GitHub Actions pipeline: flake8 lint (E501 line length exception for field mapping tests), pytest with 90% coverage on parser and validator modules, package build artifact. IT admin installs on each district server independently. Version tracking: --version flag prints package version and git commit hash. No automated deployment orchestration. FLAW: plan incorrectly describes an ECS Fargate deployment topology here — this section was generated with web app assumptions and does not reflect the actual CLI distribution model.
target_output.observability: stdout progress: per-batch upload confirmation printed in real time ('Batch 1/15 uploaded: 100 records'). Structured JSON run log: one entry per record with {row_number, course_code, stage ENUM parsed/validated/uploaded/failed, error_type if applicable, timestamp}. Completion summary table printed to stdout: total records processed, validation pass rate, upload success rate, failed batch indices. --verbose flag enables detailed per-record stdout output for debugging field mapping issues.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost — executes locally on existing district servers. Python 3.12 memory usage under 50MB for files up to 50,000 course records. Processing rate approximately 500 records per second for parse/validate stages; upload rate bounded by curriculum API at 100 records per batch. A 5,000-record migration file completes in under 5 minutes including upload time.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (1 sprint): FixedWidthParser with default mapping config for primary legacy format, CourseRecordValidator with curriculum JSON schema, CurriculumAPIClient with 100-record batching and retry, run log and stdout summary, --dry-run flag, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (1 sprint): Support for secondary CSV-based legacy format via --format flag, scheduled batch processing helper script for cron integration, field mapping validation report command to check config against sample file without processing.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 type hints on all public functions. flake8 with max-line-length 100. pytest with 90% coverage minimum on FixedWidthParser and CourseRecordValidator. Parameterized tests covering edge cases: empty lines, CRLF vs LF line endings, courses with missing optional fields, courses exceeding field width limits. No hardcoded column offsets in source code — all offsets must come from YAML mapping config. JWT value must not appear in any test fixtures or log assertions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Legacy files from different schools have undocumented column-width variations causing systematic parse failures. Mitigation: field-mapping config is per-file; IT admin must verify mapping against sample rows using --dry-run before production upload. Risk: Curriculum API JWT expires mid-run (typically 1-hour tokens) causing all remaining batches to fail with 401. Mitigation: tool checks token expiry from JWT claims at startup and warns if remaining lifetime is less than estimated run time. Tradeoff: single-threaded processing over parallel upload for simplicity; acceptable given 5-minute target run time for largest expected files.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed JWT via environment variable over CLI flag to prevent shell history exposure. Confirmed 100-record batch size matching curriculum API constraint. Confirmed YAML over JSON for field mapping config for IT admin readability. Dry-run mode confirmed as required feature for pre-migration validation.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: plan incorrectly references React frontend and ECS Fargate deployment topology inconsistent with cli_tool project class.

project_goal.value: High-volume REST API service enabling researchers to query, filter, and download large datasets from longitudinal studies and institutional surveys stored in the university research data repository
project_goal.rationale: Replace manual dataset request form process with self-service authenticated API supporting bulk download and programmatic access

target_users.value: Internal university researchers and external research partners at collaborating institutions who have approved data use agreements on file
target_users.rationale: Both internal and external researcher communities with different trust levels and access grant durations

project_class.rationale: Pure API backend with no UI; researcher interaction via HTTP client or scripts

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, batch_jobs, analytics
capabilities.rationale: REST API, dataset versioning, DUA enforcement, bulk export workers, and access analytics required

complexity_level.rationale: Concurrent bulk download handling, dataset versioning, DUA expiry enforcement, and immutable audit trail create significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Confidential research datasets including potential IRB-governed human subject data; unauthorized access could violate data sharing agreements and federal regulations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Research datasets include proprietary longitudinal study data, institutional survey results, and potentially IRB-governed human subject records

external_exposure.rationale: External research collaborators at partner institutions access the API over the internet with approved DUA credentials

access_model.value: OAuth2 with custom JWT; access_grants table links researcher accounts to specific dataset versions with DUA expiry dates; expired grants rejected at API layer
access_model.rationale: Dataset-level access control with time-bounded grants tied to data use agreement status

feature_scope.value: Dataset discovery and metadata browsing, filtered query API, pre-signed bulk download URL generation, async export job dispatch, DUA verification before download, immutable audit trail, researcher account management
feature_scope.rationale: Full research data access lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Dataset metadata API, filtered query with pagination, DUA-gated pre-signed download URL generation, audit trail logging, researcher OAuth2 authentication
mvp_scope.rationale: Core access workflow needed before bulk export and analytics

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, OAuth2 JWT with dataset-level access grants, DUA expiry enforcement at API layer, immutable audit_trails table, no dataset content in logs
security_baseline.rationale: IRB data governance and university data sharing agreement requirements

frontend_stack.value: none — API service only; no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Researcher access via HTTP clients, Python requests, R httr, and curl scripts

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async support for concurrent download requests; Pydantic for request/response schema validation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for metadata, access grants, and audit trail; Redis 7 for download request rate limiting and export job status cache; S3-compatible object storage (MinIO on-prem) for dataset files
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for relational access control; MinIO for large dataset file storage on-premises to avoid cloud egress costs

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1; PostgreSQL on RDS PostgreSQL 16; MinIO on EC2 cluster for dataset file storage
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers for API; MinIO for cost-effective large file storage with S3-compatible API

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit trail records retained 10 years per university records policy; researcher account data retained 7 years after account closure; dataset access logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: University records retention schedule and potential federal audit requirements for IRB-governed data

future_scope.value: Dataset deposit API for researchers to contribute new datasets, automated DUA renewal workflows, API usage billing for external partners
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 monetization and self-service deposit roadmap

constraints.value: MinIO cluster limited to 20TB current capacity; concurrent bulk download workers capped at 20 to protect network bandwidth; external partner API access must use API key in addition to OAuth2 JWT
constraints.rationale: Infrastructure capacity and network bandwidth protection; additional API key layer for external partner monitoring

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for FastAPI, custom metrics for DUA expiry alerts and download queue depth, PagerDuty for export worker failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Research data team needs visibility into DUA expiry pipeline and bulk export health

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; research data governance committee reviews access control design before implementation
execution_preference.rationale: Governance committee sign-off required for DUA enforcement logic before code is written

compliance_context.value: IRB data governance: datasets containing human subject data require active IRB protocol in researcher's access_grant record; FERPA if datasets contain student records; potential GDPR for international research partner accounts; data sharing agreement terms must be enforced programmatically
compliance_context.rationale: Multiple overlapping regulatory frameworks require layered access control verification

project.goal: Self-service REST API for authenticated researcher access to longitudinal study and survey datasets
project.target_users: Internal researchers and external partner institution researchers
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks

backend.auth: OAuth2 custom JWT + API key for external partners

security.auth: OAuth2 JWT with dataset access_grants table

data.cache: Redis 7 for rate limiting and job status
data.storage: MinIO on EC2 for dataset files

constraints.external_partners: API key required in addition to JWT

reasoner_reviews.product: The DUA expiry enforcement is the most legally critical feature. An expired DUA must hard-block download access, not merely warn. The audit trail must be append-only; no delete or update paths should exist for audit records. Pre-signed URL generation with short TTL (15 minutes) is the correct pattern for bulk downloads, but the URL must also embed the researcher ID for server-side validation on first use.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI async handlers are well-suited for concurrent download URL generation requests. The export worker pool should use Celery backed by Redis for job dispatch and status tracking. The DUA check should occur at the FastAPI dependency layer using Depends(), not scattered across individual endpoint handlers, to prevent enforcement gaps when new endpoints are added.
reasoner_reviews.security: The audit trail must be written to an append-only PostgreSQL table with a dedicated service account that has INSERT but not UPDATE or DELETE privileges. Researchers must not be able to enumerate datasets they do not have active access_grants for — the dataset list endpoint must filter by active grants, not return all datasets with access status. API keys for external partners must be hashed (SHA-256) before storage.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 20-concurrent-worker cap requires a queue-based export dispatch system. The Redis job queue must enforce a global semaphore limiting active workers. MinIO 20TB capacity must be monitored with alerts at 80% utilization to prevent download failures.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers: datasets (discovery, metadata, filtered queries), downloads (pre-signed URL generation, export job dispatch), accounts (researcher registration, DUA status), admin (access grant management, audit exports)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 auto-generated by FastAPI; versioned at /api/v1/; OAuth2 bearer token validated via FastAPI Depends(verify_jwt); API key header X-API-Key required for external partner accounts validated in second Depends layer
backend.background_jobs: Celery workers (max 20 concurrent) processing export_jobs queue; job chunks dataset files by 500MB parts; each part uploaded to MinIO temp prefix; completion triggers researcher email with download manifest JSON
backend.failure_handling: Celery task retry on MinIO connection failure (3 retries, exponential backoff); failed jobs update export_jobs.status to failed; researcher notified via email; job visible in GET /downloads/jobs/{id} status endpoint
backend.scaling_notes: ECS Fargate API service auto-scales on CPU 70% target (min 2, max 8 tasks); Celery worker pool fixed at 20 to respect bandwidth constraint; Redis rate limiter enforces 100 download requests per researcher per hour

security.auth_design: OAuth2 authorization code flow for internal university SSO; client credentials flow for external partner service accounts; custom JWT contains researcher_id, institution_id, account_type ENUM internal/external
security.authorization_model: access_grants table: researcher_id FK, dataset_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, dua_document_id, grant_expiry DATE, irb_protocol_id (nullable, required for human subject datasets), status ENUM active/expired/revoked. FastAPI DUA dependency checks grant existence and expiry before any dataset access.
security.secrets_management: OAuth2 client secrets and JWT signing key in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation; API keys hashed with SHA-256 before storage in researcher_accounts table; raw API keys shown once at creation, never stored
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_trails table: id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, action ENUM dataset_query/download_initiated/download_completed/access_denied, dataset_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, ip_address, user_agent, request_id, timestamp. Service account for FastAPI app has INSERT-only on audit_trails. No dataset content in any log stream.
security.security_risks: Pre-signed MinIO URLs valid 15 minutes; URL sharing between researchers would bypass DUA enforcement — recommend embedding researcher_id in MinIO object metadata for server-side validation on first download access

data.entities: research_datasets (id UUID PK, title, description, study_type ENUM longitudinal/survey/administrative, contains_human_subjects BOOL, principal_investigator_id FK, created_at, embargo_until DATE), dataset_versions (id UUID PK, dataset_id FK, version_number, record_count INT, file_size_bytes BIGINT, minio_prefix, schema_json, published_at), data_use_agreements (id UUID PK, document_gcs_path, institution_id FK, valid_from DATE, valid_until DATE, terms_hash VARCHAR(64)), access_grants (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, dataset_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, dua_id FK, irb_protocol_id VARCHAR, grant_expiry DATE, status ENUM active/expired/revoked, granted_by_admin_id FK, granted_at), researcher_accounts (id UUID PK, email, institution_id FK, account_type ENUM internal/external, api_key_hash VARCHAR(64), oauth_sub, status ENUM active/suspended), export_jobs (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, filter_params_json, status ENUM queued/processing/completed/failed, parts_count INT, completed_parts INT, manifest_minio_path, created_at, completed_at), audit_trails (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, action ENUM dataset_query/download_initiated/download_completed/access_denied, dataset_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, ip_address, user_agent, request_id UUID, timestamp)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 RDS for all metadata, access control, and audit data. MinIO EC2 cluster for dataset files organized by dataset_id/version_id/ prefix. Redis for Celery job queue, export job status cache, and researcher rate limit counters.
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_trails: 10 years, no delete path. researcher_accounts: 7 years post-closure. export_jobs and temp MinIO parts: purged 7 days after completion. Dataset files: retained per data sharing agreement terms.
data.data_risks: MinIO 20TB cap with no auto-expansion; large dataset deposits could exhaust capacity. DUA expiry date tracking requires automated daily job to flip access_grants to expired status before grant_expiry date passes undetected.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate: FastAPI service (2-8 tasks auto-scaling), Celery worker service (fixed 20 tasks). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. ElastiCache Redis 7. MinIO on 3x EC2 i3.4xlarge instances with erasure coding. ALB with WAF in front of FastAPI.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: flake8 + mypy, pytest with 85% coverage, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with health check gate. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task pre-deploy.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on FastAPI ECS tasks; custom metrics: dua_expiry_alerts (count of grants expiring in 30 days), export_queue_depth, minio_disk_used_pct, download_request_rate by researcher_id. PagerDuty: Celery worker crash, MinIO disk above 85%, DUA expiry backlog above 50.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS blue/green deploy via CodeDeploy; Alembic downgrade migration on rollback; Redis export job queue drained before worker deployment change

ISS-001.title: Dataset list endpoint exposes datasets without active access grants
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 plan returned all datasets from GET /datasets with an access_granted boolean flag. Researchers can enumerate dataset titles and metadata for datasets they have no DUA for. The endpoint must filter results to only datasets where the requesting researcher has an active access_grant.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged full dataset enumeration
Plan updated wording but implementation still returns all datasets with flag

ISS-002.title: DUA expiry not enforced by automated daily job
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan relied on API layer DUA check but had no mechanism to proactively mark expired grants. Fixed in round 2 by adding daily Celery beat job expiring access_grants where grant_expiry < current date.

history.note: No automated expiry job in plan
Celery beat daily expiry job added to round 2 plan

ISS-003.title: Pre-signed MinIO URL lacks researcher binding
ISS-003.detail: Pre-signed MinIO URLs valid 15 minutes can be shared between researchers allowing DUA bypass. URL must embed researcher_id and a one-time-use token validated on first download access.

history.note: No URL binding in round 1
Round 2 plan added researcher_id to URL metadata but did not implement server-side one-time validation

focus_issues.title: Dataset list endpoint exposes datasets without active access grants
focus_issues.detail: GET /datasets returns all datasets; researchers enumerate titles and metadata for datasets they have no DUA for.

focus_issues.title: Pre-signed MinIO URL lacks researcher binding
focus_issues.detail: URLs valid 15 minutes can be shared between researchers bypassing DUA enforcement.

latest_recommendations: Filter GET /datasets query to JOIN access_grants table where status=active and grant_expiry >= today, returning only accessible datasets

Generate one-time download tokens stored in Redis with TTL 15 minutes; pre-signed URL calls back to FastAPI redirect endpoint which validates token and researcher_id before issuing final MinIO redirect
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Research Data Repository API — Round 2

convergence_state.reason: ISS-001 is critical severity and ISS-003 is high severity; both create DUA enforcement gaps

previous_audits.summary: Strong FastAPI foundation with good Celery export worker design, but critical dataset enumeration vulnerability and missing DUA expiry automation block approval.
strengths: Celery worker pool with Redis queue correctly caps concurrent exports at 20
Append-only audit_trails with INSERT-only service account is well-designed
Pydantic schemas enforcing API contract
concerns: GET /datasets returns full catalog enabling researcher enumeration of restricted datasets
No automated job to expire access_grants on DUA expiry date
blocking_issues: Researchers can discover restricted dataset titles via GET /datasets without active grants
DUA expiry relies solely on API-layer check with no proactive grant status update
recommendations: Filter dataset list to active access_grants JOIN
Add Celery beat task to expire grants daily

OpenAPI 3.1 schema auto-generation via FastAPI

Pre-signed URL researcher binding only in metadata, not server-side validated
