previous_plan.title: Research Data Repository API — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: FastAPI REST API for authenticated researcher access to longitudinal study datasets with OAuth2 JWT and DUA enforcement. Celery workers handle bulk export jobs dispatched to Redis queue. DUA expiry automated via Celery beat job. Deployed on ECS Fargate with RDS PostgreSQL and MinIO object storage.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 JWT with access_grants table gating dataset access. DUA expiry checked at FastAPI Depends() layer. Celery beat job expires grants daily. Append-only audit_trails with INSERT-only service account. Pre-signed MinIO URLs with 15-minute TTL and researcher_id in URL metadata.
previous_plan.api_design: GET /datasets returns all datasets with access_granted BOOL flag. POST /downloads/request generates pre-signed MinIO URL after DUA check. POST /exports/jobs dispatches Celery bulk export task. GET /exports/jobs/{id} returns job status.
previous_plan.data_model: research_datasets, dataset_versions, data_use_agreements, access_grants, researcher_accounts, export_jobs, audit_trails in PostgreSQL 16.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixing ISS-001 by rewriting GET /datasets to JOIN access_grants WHERE status=active AND grant_expiry >= today, returning only datasets the requesting researcher is authorized to know exist — not a flag on a full catalog. Fixing ISS-003 by routing all downloads through a FastAPI redirect endpoint that issues and validates one-time Redis tokens, so pre-signed MinIO URLs are never shared directly with researchers. This is round 3 and the plan is intentionally flawed: the data_model section below incorrectly describes a MongoDB document model inconsistent with the PostgreSQL 16 contract, and the deployment section omits the MinIO component despite it being a key infrastructure element.

fix_report.action_taken: Rewrote dataset list handler to execute SELECT rd.* FROM research_datasets rd JOIN access_grants ag ON rd.id = ag.dataset_id WHERE ag.researcher_id = :researcher_id AND ag.status = 'active' AND ag.grant_expiry >= CURRENT_DATE. Researchers receive only datasets they are authorized to access; restricted dataset titles are not disclosed. OpenAPI spec updated to remove access_granted flag from DatasetListResponse schema.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Researchers cannot enumerate restricted dataset titles or metadata; confirmed via integration tests asserting dataset isolation between researchers with different active grants

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented one-time download token flow: POST /downloads/request generates a UUID download_token stored in Redis with TTL 600 seconds keyed as download:{researcher_id}:{token}. Response returns /downloads/redirect/{token} URL instead of direct MinIO pre-signed URL. GET /downloads/redirect/{token} FastAPI endpoint validates token exists in Redis for requesting researcher_id, deletes token (one-time use), then generates MinIO pre-signed URL with 60-second TTL and issues HTTP 302 redirect. Token sharing between researchers fails because Redis key includes researcher_id validated against JWT.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Pre-signed MinIO URL unreachable without valid one-time token bound to requesting researcher JWT; URL sharing yields 403 for second researcher

target_output.title: Research Data Repository API
target_output.executive_summary: A high-volume REST API service on AWS ECS Fargate enabling authenticated university researchers and external partners to discover, query, and bulk-download longitudinal study and institutional survey datasets from the university research data repository. Access is gated by dataset-level OAuth2 grants tied to data use agreements with automated expiry enforcement. Bulk export jobs dispatch to a Celery worker pool capped at 20 concurrent workers. All dataset access events are written to an append-only audit trail for IRB compliance. This round resolves the critical dataset enumeration vulnerability (ISS-001) and pre-signed URL sharing bypass (ISS-003).
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI application on ECS Fargate (2-8 tasks, auto-scaling on CPU 70%) behind an ALB with WAF. OAuth2 JWT validation and DUA enforcement via FastAPI Depends() dependency chain applied on every dataset and download endpoint. Dataset list queries filter to researcher-authorized datasets only via access_grants JOIN, never returning full catalog. Download flow routes through a FastAPI redirect endpoint validating one-time Redis tokens before issuing final MinIO pre-signed URL — MinIO URLs are never delivered directly to researchers. Celery workers (fixed 20 tasks) consume export_jobs Redis queue, chunk dataset files from MinIO into 500MB parts, and email researchers a download manifest on completion. Celery beat task runs daily at 02:00 to expire access_grants where grant_expiry is past. Append-only audit_trails in PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ captures every dataset access event.
target_output.technology_stack: API: Python 3.12, FastAPI with async handlers, Pydantic v2 for schema validation, python-jose for JWT, boto3 for MinIO S3-compatible API calls. Workers: Celery 5.x backed by Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ, Alembic for migrations. Cache: Redis 7 for Celery queue, job status, one-time download tokens, and rate limit counters. Storage: MinIO on 3x EC2 i3.4xlarge with erasure coding for dataset files. Auth: custom OAuth2 with university SSO and client credentials for external partners. Infra: ECS Fargate, ALB, WAF, RDS, ElastiCache, ECR, Secrets Manager. CI: GitHub Actions with flake8, mypy, pytest. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Dataset Discovery: filtered GET /datasets returns only researcher-authorized datasets by access_grants JOIN; GET /datasets/{id}/versions lists available versions. Filtered Queries: GET /datasets/{id}/records with filter params, pagination, field selection. Download Token Flow: POST /downloads/request issues one-time Redis token; GET /downloads/redirect/{token} validates and redirects to MinIO pre-signed URL. Bulk Export: POST /exports/jobs dispatches Celery task; GET /exports/jobs/{id} returns status and manifest path. DUA Management: GET /accounts/grants lists researcher's active grants with expiry dates. Admin: POST /admin/grants (create access grant), DELETE /admin/grants/{id} (revoke), GET /admin/audit-exports (compliance report).
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate FastAPI service (2-8 tasks), ECS Fargate Celery worker service (fixed 20 tasks), ECS Fargate Celery beat scheduler (1 task for daily grant expiry job), ALB with WAF protecting FastAPI, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ for metadata and audit trail, ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for Celery queue and one-time download tokens, MinIO 3-node EC2 cluster for dataset files, Secrets Manager for OAuth2 keys and MinIO credentials, Datadog APM agent sidecar on API tasks, PagerDuty for critical alerts.
target_output.workflows: Dataset Discovery: Researcher calls GET /api/v1/datasets with Bearer JWT. FastAPI Depends(verify_jwt) validates token. Depends(check_active_grants) executes SQL JOIN filtering to researcher's active grants. Only authorized dataset metadata returned. Restricted datasets not disclosed. Single Record Query: Researcher calls GET /api/v1/datasets/{id}/records with filter params. DUA Depends() checks active grant for specific dataset_version_id. Query executed against PostgreSQL with pagination. Audit event dataset_query written to audit_trails. Download Token Flow: Researcher calls POST /api/v1/downloads/request with dataset_version_id. DUA Depends() verifies active grant. UUID download_token generated, stored in Redis as download:{researcher_id}:{token} with 600-second TTL. Response: {redirect_url: /api/v1/downloads/redirect/{token}}. Researcher GETs redirect URL. FastAPI validates Redis token for requesting researcher_id, deletes token, generates MinIO presigned URL (60-second TTL), returns HTTP 302. Audit event download_initiated written. Bulk Export: Researcher POSTs /api/v1/exports/jobs with dataset_version_id and filter_params. DUA check passes. export_jobs record created status queued. Celery task dispatched to export_jobs Redis queue. Worker claims job, reads dataset from MinIO, chunks into 500MB parts, uploads to MinIO temp/researcher_id/job_id/. On completion, writes manifest JSON, emails researcher. Audit event download_completed written. Daily Grant Expiry: Celery beat at 02:00 UTC executes UPDATE access_grants SET status='expired' WHERE grant_expiry < CURRENT_DATE AND status='active'. Expired grant count logged to Datadog metric.
target_output.data_model: FLAW: The following data model incorrectly describes MongoDB collections inconsistent with the PostgreSQL 16 contract. research_datasets collection: {_id, title, description, study_type, contains_human_subjects, principal_investigator_id, created_at, embargo_until}. access_grants collection: {_id, researcher_id, dataset_id, dua_id, grant_expiry, status}. This MongoDB document model is architecturally inconsistent with the PostgreSQL 16 RDS platform specified in the contract. Correct model should be: research_datasets (id UUID PK, title, description, study_type ENUM longitudinal/survey/administrative, contains_human_subjects BOOL, principal_investigator_id FK, embargo_until DATE). dataset_versions (id UUID PK, dataset_id FK, version_number, record_count INT, file_size_bytes BIGINT, minio_prefix, schema_json, published_at). access_grants (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, dataset_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, dua_id FK, irb_protocol_id VARCHAR, grant_expiry DATE, status ENUM active/expired/revoked, granted_at). researcher_accounts (id UUID PK, email, institution_id FK, account_type ENUM internal/external, api_key_hash VARCHAR(64), oauth_sub). export_jobs (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, filter_params_json, status ENUM queued/processing/completed/failed, parts_count INT, manifest_minio_path, created_at). audit_trails (id UUID PK, researcher_id FK, action ENUM dataset_query/download_initiated/download_completed/access_denied, dataset_id FK, dataset_version_id FK, ip_address, request_id UUID, timestamp).
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1/ with FastAPI OpenAPI 3.1 auto-generation. All endpoints require OAuth2 Bearer JWT; external partner endpoints additionally require X-API-Key header. Dataset endpoints: GET /datasets (returns researcher's authorized datasets only via access_grants JOIN), GET /datasets/{id} (metadata for authorized dataset), GET /datasets/{id}/versions, GET /datasets/{id}/records (filtered with pagination). Download endpoints: POST /downloads/request (body: {dataset_version_id}, returns {redirect_url, token_ttl_seconds}), GET /downloads/redirect/{token} (validates one-time Redis token, 302 to MinIO). Export endpoints: POST /exports/jobs (dispatch Celery task), GET /exports/jobs/{id} (status with completed_parts count). Account endpoints: GET /accounts/grants (researcher's active grants with expiry dates). Admin endpoints: POST /admin/grants, DELETE /admin/grants/{id}, GET /admin/audit-exports. Rate limiting: 100 download requests per researcher per hour enforced via Redis counter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IRB/DUA controls: FastAPI dependency chain Depends(verify_jwt) -> Depends(check_active_grant_for_dataset) applied on all dataset endpoints; expired or revoked grants return 403. Dataset enumeration prevention: GET /datasets SQL query filters to researcher's active grants — no full catalog disclosure. One-time download tokens: Redis keys scoped to researcher_id prevent URL sharing between researchers; token deleted on first use. Append-only audit_trails: FastAPI service account has INSERT-only PostgreSQL privilege on audit_trails table; no UPDATE or DELETE paths exist. API key hashing: external partner API keys hashed SHA-256 before storage; raw keys shown once at creation. Secrets Manager stores OAuth2 JWT signing key and MinIO credentials with 90-day rotation. IRB protocol required: access_grants for datasets with contains_human_subjects=True must have non-null irb_protocol_id; Depends() layer rejects grants missing IRB protocol for human subject datasets.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate in us-east-1. ALB with WAF (OWASP rule set) routing to FastAPI service target group. GitHub Actions pipeline: flake8, mypy, pytest with 85% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy with ALB health check gate. Alembic migration runs as ECS one-off task before traffic shift. FLAW: MinIO EC2 cluster deployment omitted from this section — a critical infrastructure component missing from operations runbook. Environments: dev (docker-compose FastAPI + Postgres + Redis + MinIO), staging (ECS mirror with anonymized dataset fixtures), production (ECS + RDS Multi-AZ + ElastiCache + MinIO cluster). Celery worker service uses KEDA-style fixed task count (20 workers); beat scheduler deployed as separate ECS service.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM traces FastAPI requests with dataset_id and researcher_id tags for access pattern analysis. Custom Datadog metrics: dua_grants_expiring_30d (count of grants expiring within 30 days, alert above 10), export_queue_depth (Celery queue length, alert above 50), minio_disk_used_pct (alert above 80%), download_redirect_token_miss_rate (ratio of invalid token attempts, alert above 5%). PagerDuty: Celery worker task failure rate above 5%, MinIO disk above 85%, API error rate above 2%, export job stuck in processing above 2 hours.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,200/month: ECS Fargate API (2-8 tasks) ~$400, Celery workers 20 tasks ~$800, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ ~$400, ElastiCache Redis ~$100, MinIO 3x EC2 i3.4xlarge ~$450, ALB + WAF ~$50. Scaling: ECS API auto-scales on CPU 70%. Celery worker count fixed at 20 by bandwidth constraint. MinIO expansion requires adding EC2 nodes to cluster when 20TB capacity alert fires.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): OAuth2 JWT auth with university SSO, dataset discovery with access_grants filter, filtered record query with pagination, one-time download token flow with MinIO redirect, append-only audit trail, Celery beat DUA expiry job, admin grant management endpoints, CI/CD pipeline, Datadog APM. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Bulk export Celery workers with email manifest, API key management for external partners, IRB protocol enforcement for human subject datasets, advanced audit export reporting for compliance officers. Phase 3 (future): Dataset deposit API, automated DUA renewal workflow, API usage billing for external partners.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. FastAPI Depends() chain for all auth and DUA checks — never inline JWT decode in handlers. Parameterized pytest fixtures covering researcher access scenarios: active grant, expired grant, no grant, revoked grant, missing IRB protocol. audit_trails INSERT must use a separate SQLAlchemy session from the main request transaction to ensure audit events persist even on request rollback. API key creation tests must assert raw key not stored in database. 85% pytest coverage gate in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: MinIO 20TB capacity exhaustion from large dataset deposits. Mitigation: Datadog alert at 80% capacity; MinIO node expansion procedure documented in runbook. Risk: Celery worker crash leaves export_jobs stuck in processing status. Mitigation: Celery task heartbeat with 2-hour timeout; stuck job detection in Datadog alert. Risk: OAuth2 JWT signing key compromise would allow arbitrary access_grant impersonation. Mitigation: Secrets Manager rotation every 90 days; JWT key rollover procedure without service downtime via dual-validation during rotation window. Tradeoff: MinIO over S3 for dataset storage eliminates cloud egress costs at 20TB scale but introduces manual cluster operations overhead.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved by filtering GET /datasets to access_grants JOIN — researchers cannot enumerate restricted datasets. ISS-003 resolved by one-time Redis token flow routing downloads through FastAPI redirect endpoint. Confirmed OAuth2 client credentials flow for external partner service accounts. Confirmed IRB protocol requirement for human subject dataset grants.

metadata.notes: Round 3 fixes ISS-001 dataset enumeration and ISS-003 URL binding. Intentionally flawed: data_model uses MongoDB description inconsistent with PostgreSQL contract; deployment section omits MinIO.

project_goal.value: Automation tool that decomposes the manual process of syllabus collection, policy compliance checking, and archival into discrete automated microservices; faculty submit syllabi through a department portal and the tool validates required sections, flags missing ADA accommodations language, and archives approved versions
project_goal.rationale: Manual compliance checking by department coordinators takes 2-3 weeks each semester and produces inconsistent results

target_users.value: Faculty submitting syllabi; department coordinators reviewing and approving; academic affairs administrators managing compliance rules and viewing institution-wide reports
target_users.rationale: Three distinct roles with different interaction patterns across the submission-review-archival workflow

project_class.rationale: Backend automation pipeline with department portal for faculty submission and admin panel for rule management; no public-facing interface

capabilities.rationale: Text extraction pipeline, rule engine, Celery batch jobs, and admin rule management panel required

complexity_level.rationale: PDF text extraction, configurable rule engine, multi-step approval workflow, and archival with hash verification add moderate complexity

risk_level.rationale: Internal academic data with no student PII; compliance violations are administrative not legal risks

data_sensitivity.rationale: Syllabus content is internal academic policy data; not student records under FERPA

external_exposure.rationale: Department portal and admin panel restricted to authenticated university users via Azure AD; no public internet access

access_model.value: Azure AD (Entra ID) with three roles: faculty (own submissions only), coordinator (department submissions and review queue), admin (institution-wide rules and reports)
access_model.rationale: RBAC scoped to department prevents cross-department data access

feature_scope.value: Syllabus PDF upload via department portal, text extraction, configurable rule engine checking required sections (grading policy, ADA statement, late work policy, contact information), compliance report generation, department review queue, coordinator approval workflow, timestamped hash archival
feature_scope.rationale: Full syllabus compliance lifecycle from submission to archival

mvp_scope.value: Faculty PDF upload, text extraction, rule engine with bundled required-section rules, compliance report, coordinator review queue with approval/revision-request actions, SHA-256 hash archival to Azure Blob Storage
mvp_scope.rationale: Core submit-check-review-archive workflow covers primary use case

security_baseline.value: Azure AD OIDC, RBAC enforced at API layer, TLS 1.3, no faculty PII beyond name and department in syllabus records
security_baseline.rationale: Internal tool; Azure AD provides SSO without additional auth infrastructure

frontend_stack.value: none — department portal and admin panel handled by lightweight server-side rendered views; no dedicated SPA
frontend_stack.rationale: Simple form submission and queue review UI does not warrant full React SPA; FastAPI Jinja2 templates sufficient for portal

backend_stack.rationale: Celery handles async text extraction and rule engine tasks; Python 3.12 for pdfplumber PDF text extraction

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for syllabus submissions and compliance reports; Redis 7 for Celery task queue; Azure Blob Storage for archived syllabus PDFs
data_platform.rationale: MongoDB flexible document model suits variable syllabus compliance report structures; Blob Storage for durable PDF archival

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps for API and Celery workers; Azure Cache for Redis; Cosmos DB MongoDB API for data; Azure Blob Storage for archived PDFs
hosting_target.rationale: Azure-native hosting aligns with university Azure enterprise agreement and existing AD tenant

privacy_retention_policy.value: Archived syllabus versions retained 7 years per accreditation body requirements; compliance reports retained 7 years; faculty submission metadata retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HLC and SACSCOC accreditation bodies may audit syllabus compliance records up to 7 years retrospectively

future_scope.value: AI-assisted compliance suggestions using course description context, integration with course management system for automatic syllabus import, cross-semester trend reporting for deans
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 AI integration and analytics roadmap

constraints.value: Rule engine must be configurable by admin panel without code deployment; archived PDFs must be immutable (write-once Blob container); Celery processing must complete within 5 minutes per syllabus for faculty portal responsiveness
constraints.rationale: Admin rule updates during semester without engineering involvement; immutability for audit defensibility; 5-minute SLA for faculty experience

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for container health and error rates, structured logging for Celery task durations, email alerts on processing pipeline failures
observability_baseline.rationale: Operations team needs visibility into processing backlog during peak submission periods

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprints; academic affairs office validates rule engine configurations before semester launch
execution_preference.rationale: Rule accuracy validation by domain experts required before faculty submission window opens

project.goal: Automate syllabus compliance checking and archival for higher education departments
project.target_users: Faculty, department coordinators, academic affairs administrators
project.timeline: MVP before fall submission window

backend.framework: FastAPI for API, Jinja2 for portal templates, Celery for async processing

security.auth: Azure AD OIDC

data.db: MongoDB 7 via Cosmos DB MongoDB API

reasoner_reviews.product: The admin-configurable rule engine is the most valuable feature for sustainability. If updating compliance rules requires engineering deployment, the tool becomes a maintenance burden. The rule engine must support at minimum: required-section presence checks (keyword/phrase matching), and minimum-length checks. The ADA statement rule is non-negotiable and should be pre-seeded as a locked base rule that admins cannot disable.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The rule engine should store rules as MongoDB documents with a rule_type ENUM and matcher_config JSON field. Celery task execution should be idempotent — reprocessing a syllabus on rule update should produce a new compliance_report document linked to the same faculty_submission ID. The pdfplumber extraction step should handle multi-column and scanned PDF layouts gracefully with a fallback warning.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD group membership should map to portal roles via ID token claims. Faculty submissions should be scoped to their own department_id — a faculty member should not be able to see another department's coordinator queue by manipulating API query parameters. Coordinator role should be scoped to their department_id claim from Azure AD.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 5-minute SLA requires Celery worker scaling during peak submission periods. Azure Container Apps scale rules should trigger on Celery queue depth. The write-once Blob container must be enforced via immutability policy at the Azure storage account level, not just application logic.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with three modules: portal (faculty submission upload, coordinator review queue, Jinja2 templates), compliance_engine (Celery task for text extraction and rule evaluation), admin (rule management CRUD, institution-wide reporting)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with FastAPI; faculty portal routes server-side rendered via Jinja2; JSON API for coordinator and admin operations; Azure AD OIDC validated via fastapi-azure-auth middleware
backend.background_jobs: Celery task extract_and_evaluate triggered on syllabus upload: pdfplumber text extraction, rule engine evaluation, compliance_report document creation in MongoDB. Nightly Celery beat task generating department compliance summary for coordinator dashboard refresh.
backend.failure_handling: pdfplumber extraction failure (scanned PDF, password-protected) sets faculty_submission.status to extraction_failed and sends coordinator notification; Celery task max retries 3 with 60-second backoff; failed task surfaces in admin error queue
backend.backend_risks: pdfplumber accuracy degrades on scanned PDFs requiring OCR pre-processing not in current scope; MongoDB Cosmos DB API compatibility limitations with MongoDB 7 aggregation pipeline features may require query adjustments

security.auth_design: Azure AD OIDC with fastapi-azure-auth; ID token contains department_id and portal_role custom claims from Azure AD group mapping; faculty role scoped to own department_id in all queries
security.authorization_model: faculty: read/write own faculty_submissions where department_id matches token claim. coordinator: read all faculty_submissions in own department, write coordinator_review_actions. admin: read/write compliance_rules, read institution-wide faculty_submissions and compliance_reports.
security.secrets_management: Azure Key Vault for MongoDB connection string, Azure AD client secret, and Blob Storage SAS token; Container Apps reference Key Vault secrets via managed identity
security.security_risks: department_id claim injection if Azure AD group-to-role mapping is misconfigured; Blob immutability policy must be set at storage account level before any writes to prevent accidental deletion

data.entities: faculty_submissions ({_id, faculty_id, department_id, course_code, academic_term, original_filename, blob_storage_path, extracted_text_blob_path, status ENUM uploaded/processing/processed/extraction_failed, submitted_at}), compliance_reports ({_id, submission_id, rule_set_version, results [{rule_id, rule_name, passed BOOL, matched_text, violation_detail}], overall_status ENUM compliant/violations_found, generated_at}), compliance_rules ({_id, rule_name, rule_type ENUM section_presence/keyword_match/minimum_length, matcher_config {keywords, min_length}, severity ENUM required/recommended, locked BOOL, active BOOL, created_by_admin_id, updated_at}), coordinator_review_actions ({_id, submission_id, coordinator_id, action ENUM approved/revision_requested, notes, acted_at}), archived_versions ({_id, submission_id, approval_action_id, sha256_hash, blob_path, archived_at, rule_set_version}), department_review_queues ({_id, department_id, pending_submission_ids [], last_refreshed_at}), notification_events ({_id, recipient_id, event_type ENUM submission_received/compliance_report_ready/revision_requested/approved, payload_json, sent_at, status ENUM pending/sent/failed})
data.storage_design: MongoDB 7 via Cosmos DB MongoDB API for all document data. Azure Blob Storage with immutability policy: original PDFs in faculty-uploads container (mutable until archived), archived PDFs in syllabus-archive container (write-once, immutability policy enforced at storage account). Redis 7 via Azure Cache for Celery task queue and department queue cache.
data.retention_and_deletion: archived_versions and compliance_reports: 7 years per accreditation requirement. faculty_submissions metadata: 5 years. notification_events: 90 days. Blob Storage lifecycle rules purge original uploads 30 days after archival.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps environment: FastAPI app (min 1, max 5 replicas, KEDA scale on HTTP requests), Celery worker app (min 1, max 10 replicas, KEDA scale on Redis queue depth). Cosmos DB MongoDB API serverless. Azure Cache for Redis Basic tier. Azure Blob Storage LRS.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: flake8 lint, pytest with 80% coverage, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, Container Apps deploy via az containerapp update. MongoDB collection index creation in pre-deploy script.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor container logs with structured JSON. Custom metrics via Application Insights: syllabus_processing_duration_ms, compliance_violations_per_department, celery_queue_depth, extraction_failure_rate. Email alerts via Logic Apps on extraction_failure_rate above 10% or Celery queue depth above 50.
devops.rollback_strategy: Container Apps traffic split for gradual rollout; instant rollback via az containerapp update --image to previous tag; MongoDB document schema changes are additive-only to avoid migration rollback complexity

ISS-001.title: Coordinator queue not scoped to department_id from token claim
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 coordinator queue endpoint accepted department_id as a query parameter rather than reading it from the Azure AD token claim. A coordinator could access another department's queue by manipulating the parameter. Fixed in round 2 by reading department_id exclusively from token claims.

history.note: Security reviewer flagged department_id as user-controlled parameter
Endpoint now reads department_id from Azure AD token claim only

ISS-002.title: ADA compliance rule can be disabled by admin
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 and round 2 plans allowed admins to set any compliance_rule to active=False including ADA Section 508 statement rules. ADA compliance rule must be locked (locked=True) and non-disableable to ensure institutional legal obligation is always enforced.

history.note: Rule engine allowed all rules to be disabled
locked field added to schema but admin panel UI still shows disable toggle for locked rules

ISS-003.title: Blob immutability policy described as application logic only
ISS-003.detail: Rounds 1-2 described immutability as enforced by application logic (not calling delete API). This is insufficient; an admin with direct storage access or a misconfigured service principal could delete archived syllabi. Azure Blob immutability policy must be set at the storage account level, not application logic.

history.note: Immutability described as app-level only
Terraform note added but infrastructure-as-code not yet specifying the WORM policy

focus_issues.title: ADA compliance rule can be disabled by admin
focus_issues.detail: locked=True field added to schema but admin UI still shows disable toggle for locked rules allowing ADA rule to be turned off.

focus_issues.title: Blob immutability policy described as application logic only
focus_issues.detail: Azure Blob WORM immutability must be enforced at storage account level via Terraform, not application logic.

latest_recommendations: Hide disable toggle in admin panel for compliance_rules where locked=True; backend API must reject PATCH to active field for locked rules
Add Terraform azurerm_storage_account_blob_container_immutability_policy resource for syllabus-archive container specifying time_based_retention_in_days=2555 (7 years) and permanent_delete=false
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Syllabus Compliance Automation — Round 2

convergence_state.reason: Two medium-severity issues unresolved; ISS-002 is an institutional legal compliance gap; ISS-003 is an infrastructure enforcement gap

previous_audits.summary: ISS-001 resolved with token-scoped coordinator queue. Round 2 plan shows progress but ISS-002 ADA rule disabling and ISS-003 application-layer immutability remain open medium-severity issues.
strengths: Coordinator queue correctly scoped to Azure AD department_id token claim
Admin-configurable rule engine with MongoDB document model is well-designed
Azure Container Apps KEDA scaling on Redis queue depth addresses 5-minute SLA
concerns: Admin panel shows disable toggle for locked ADA rule — UI inconsistency with locked field
Blob immutability described only as application behavior not infrastructure policy
blocking_issues: ADA compliance rule can be disabled via admin panel despite locked=True in schema
Blob archive immutability not enforced at Azure storage account level
recommendations: Filter admin panel rule list to hide active toggle for locked=True rules; backend rejects PATCH active for locked rules
Add Terraform immutability policy for syllabus-archive Blob container with 7-year WORM retention

issue_updates.title: ADA compliance rule can be disabled by admin
issue_updates.detail: locked field in schema not enforced at API or UI layer

issue_updates.title: Blob immutability policy application logic only
issue_updates.detail: Terraform resource for WORM policy not included in deployment section

previous_plan.title: Syllabus Compliance Automation — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python 3.12 automation tool on Azure Container Apps. Faculty upload syllabi via portal; Celery workers extract text and evaluate against admin-configurable rule engine. Coordinator review queue scoped to department_id from Azure AD token. SHA-256 hash archival to Azure Blob Storage. MongoDB 7 via Cosmos DB for document storage.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OIDC via fastapi-azure-auth. Coordinator queue reads department_id from token claim only (ISS-001 resolved). Compliance_rules schema has locked BOOL field. Admin panel shows toggle for all rules including locked ones.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps with KEDA on HTTP requests and Redis queue depth. Azure DevOps Pipelines CI. Blob Storage with application-level write-once behavior.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixing ISS-002 by adding backend API enforcement rejecting PATCH to the active field for compliance_rules where locked=True, and filtering the admin panel rule list template to hide the active toggle for locked rules. Fixing ISS-003 by specifying the Terraform azurerm_storage_management_policy and immutability policy resources for the syllabus-archive Blob container, moving enforcement from application logic to Azure storage account infrastructure.

fix_report.action_taken: Added backend enforcement: PATCH /admin/rules/{id} handler checks if compliance_rule.locked=True and returns 403 with error body {detail: 'ADA and other locked compliance rules cannot be disabled'} before processing any field update. Admin panel Jinja2 template updated to conditionally render the active toggle as a disabled input with tooltip 'This rule is required by institutional policy' when rule.locked is True. Seeded ADA Statement rule and Late Work Policy rule with locked=True in initial migration.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Admin cannot disable ADA compliance rule via UI or direct API call; pytest integration test asserts 403 on PATCH active=False for locked rule

fix_report.action_taken: Added Terraform resource azurerm_storage_blob_container (syllabus-archive) with has_immutability_policy=true and azurerm_storage_management_policy specifying time_based_retention_in_days=2555 (7 years, matching accreditation requirement) and permanent_delete=false. Azure Blob Storage account upgraded to StorageV2 LRS to support immutability policies. Application layer write-once logic retained as defense-in-depth but primary enforcement is now infrastructure-level.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Azure enforces 7-year WORM retention on syllabus-archive container at infrastructure level; no principal including storage account admin can delete archived syllabi within retention window

target_output.title: Syllabus Compliance Automation
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 automation tool on Azure Container Apps that decomposes the manual syllabus compliance process into discrete services: a faculty submission portal, a Celery-based text extraction and rule evaluation pipeline, a coordinator review queue scoped by Azure AD department claims, and a write-once Azure Blob archive with infrastructure-enforced 7-year WORM retention. Admin-configurable compliance rules in MongoDB include locked rules for ADA Section 508 and other legally mandated sections that cannot be disabled. This round resolves ADA rule disabling (ISS-002) and application-layer immutability (ISS-003).
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI application on Azure Container Apps serves two interfaces: a Jinja2 server-side rendered portal for faculty syllabus submission and coordinator review, and a JSON admin API for compliance rule management. Faculty PDF uploads trigger Celery tasks that run pdfplumber text extraction, evaluate extracted text against MongoDB-stored configurable rules, and create compliance_report documents. Coordinator review queue filtered exclusively by department_id from Azure AD token claims. Approved syllabi archived to Azure Blob Storage syllabus-archive container with Terraform-enforced WORM immutability policy (7 years). Azure AD OIDC via fastapi-azure-auth with three portal roles mapped from Azure AD groups. Nightly Celery beat job refreshes department compliance summary views.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Jinja2 for server-side portal templates, Celery 5.x with Redis broker, pdfplumber for PDF text extraction, pymongo for Cosmos DB MongoDB API. Database: Cosmos DB MongoDB API 7 for document storage. Cache/Queue: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Storage: Azure Blob Storage StorageV2 LRS with WORM immutability policy on syllabus-archive container. Auth: Azure AD OIDC via fastapi-azure-auth, Key Vault for secrets via Container Apps managed identity. Infra: Azure Container Apps with KEDA scaling, Azure DevOps Pipelines, Terraform for infrastructure. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Logic Apps for email alerts.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Faculty Portal: PDF upload form with course code and academic term, submission status page showing processing state and compliance report results, revision resubmission on coordinator request. Compliance Engine: pdfplumber text extraction, rule engine evaluation against all active compliance_rules, compliance_report document creation with per-rule pass/fail results and matched-text excerpts. Coordinator Review Queue: department-scoped pending submission list, compliance report viewer, approve or request-revision actions with notes, notification to faculty on action. Rule Engine Admin: add/edit/disable non-locked rules, view compliance_rules with locked indicator, download institution-wide compliance report by department and term. Archival: SHA-256 hash of approved PDF captured at archival time, written to write-once Blob container, archived_versions document created with hash and blob_path.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps environment: FastAPI portal/API app (min 1, max 5 replicas, KEDA on HTTP RPS), Celery worker app (min 1, max 10 replicas, KEDA on Redis queue depth). Cosmos DB MongoDB API serverless account for faculty_submissions, compliance_reports, compliance_rules, coordinator_review_actions, archived_versions, notification_events. Azure Cache for Redis Basic tier for Celery broker and task results. Azure Blob Storage StorageV2 LRS: faculty-uploads container (mutable, 30-day lifecycle purge after archival), syllabus-archive container (WORM immutability policy, 7-year retention). Azure Key Vault for Cosmos DB connection string, AD client secret, Blob Storage managed identity. Application Insights for custom compliance metrics. Logic Apps for email notification delivery.
target_output.workflows: Faculty Submission: Faculty authenticates via Azure AD portal login. Uploads syllabus PDF with course_code and academic_term via /portal/submit form. FastAPI writes faculty_submission document (status: uploaded) to MongoDB and uploads PDF to faculty-uploads Blob container. Celery task extract_and_evaluate queued. Faculty sees processing status page with auto-refresh. Compliance Check: Celery worker loads submission PDF from Blob Storage, runs pdfplumber extract_text(). Extracted text evaluated against all compliance_rules where active=True, ordered by severity. For each rule: section_presence rules search for keyword list matches; minimum_length rules check extracted section word count. Results collected as list of {rule_id, passed BOOL, matched_text, violation_detail}. compliance_report document created with overall_status compliant or violations_found. faculty_submission.status updated to processed. Notification event queued to Logic Apps for faculty email. Coordinator Review: Coordinator views /portal/coordinator/queue showing all pending submissions for their department_id (from token claim). Opens submission to view compliance_report. Selects Approve (writes coordinator_review_action, triggers archival flow) or Request Revision (writes action with notes, sends revision_requested notification to faculty). Archival: On coordinator approval, FastAPI reads faculty PDF from Blob, computes SHA-256 hash, uploads to syllabus-archive WORM container under department_id/term/course_code/submission_id.pdf. Creates archived_versions document with hash, blob_path, approval_action_id, and rule_set_version. Application-level code never calls Blob delete on syllabus-archive; Azure infrastructure WORM policy enforces retention independently.
target_output.data_model: faculty_submissions ({_id, faculty_id, faculty_name, department_id, course_code, academic_term, original_filename, faculty_uploads_blob_path, extracted_text_blob_path, status ENUM uploaded/processing/processed/extraction_failed, submitted_at, last_updated_at}). compliance_reports ({_id, submission_id, rule_set_version, results [{rule_id, rule_name, rule_type, passed BOOL, matched_text, violation_detail}], overall_status ENUM compliant/violations_found, generated_at}). compliance_rules ({_id, rule_name, rule_type ENUM section_presence/keyword_match/minimum_length, matcher_config {keywords: [], min_word_count: INT}, severity ENUM required/recommended, locked BOOL, active BOOL, created_by_admin_id, updated_at}). coordinator_review_actions ({_id, submission_id, coordinator_id, coordinator_name, department_id, action ENUM approved/revision_requested, notes, acted_at}). archived_versions ({_id, submission_id, approval_action_id, sha256_hash VARCHAR(64), blob_path, archived_at, rule_set_version}). department_review_queues ({_id, department_id, pending_count INT, last_refreshed_at}). notification_events ({_id, recipient_id, recipient_email, event_type ENUM submission_received/compliance_report_ready/revision_requested/approved, payload_json, sent_at, status ENUM pending/sent/failed}).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI routes: Faculty portal (Jinja2 rendered): GET /portal/submit (upload form), POST /portal/submit (file upload + metadata), GET /portal/submissions/{id} (status + compliance report). Coordinator portal (Jinja2 rendered): GET /portal/coordinator/queue (department-scoped queue), GET /portal/coordinator/submissions/{id} (report detail), POST /portal/coordinator/submissions/{id}/approve, POST /portal/coordinator/submissions/{id}/request-revision. Admin JSON API: GET /admin/rules (list with locked indicator), POST /admin/rules (create rule), PATCH /admin/rules/{id} (update; returns 403 if locked=True and active field in body), DELETE /admin/rules/{id} (returns 403 if locked=True), GET /admin/reports/compliance-summary (by department and term). Internal Celery task: extract_and_evaluate(submission_id) — not an HTTP endpoint. Azure AD OIDC enforced on all routes via fastapi-azure-auth dependency.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD OIDC with department_id and portal_role claims from Azure AD group mapping. Coordinator and faculty routes read department_id exclusively from token claims — not from query parameters. Locked compliance rules: backend PATCH handler for /admin/rules/{id} checks locked field before processing; returns 403 for any update to active, matcher_config, or rule_type on locked rules. Admin portal template renders locked rules with disabled active toggle and policy tooltip. ADA Statement rule and Late Work Policy rule seeded as locked=True in initial migration. Blob WORM: syllabus-archive container has Terraform-provisioned azurerm_storage_management_policy with time_based_retention_in_days=2555 and permanent_delete=false; application code never calls blob delete on this container. Key Vault stores Cosmos DB connection string, Azure AD client secret with 90-day rotation via managed identity.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure Container Apps environment in the university's Azure subscription. FastAPI app and Celery worker app deployed from Azure Container Registry via Azure DevOps Pipelines. Pipeline: flake8, pytest 80% coverage, docker build, az acr build, az containerapp update. Pre-deploy: MongoDB index creation script for faculty_submissions (department_id, status) and compliance_reports (submission_id). Terraform manages: Container Apps environment, Cosmos DB account, Azure Cache for Redis, Blob Storage account with WORM policy (azurerm_storage_management_policy specifying syllabus-archive container retention 2555 days), Key Vault, Application Insights. Environments: dev (docker-compose FastAPI + Celery + MongoDB + Redis), staging (Container Apps with anonymized submissions), production. Rollback: Container Apps traffic split; instant rollback via az containerapp update --image to previous revision.
target_output.observability: Application Insights custom metrics: syllabus_processing_duration_ms (Celery task duration, alert above 4 minutes), compliance_violations_per_department (weekly count for coordinator dashboards), celery_queue_depth (alert above 20 pending tasks), extraction_failure_rate (alert above 5%). Azure Monitor Container Apps metrics: replica count, CPU utilization, memory. Logic Apps email alerts: extraction_failure_rate threshold breached, Celery queue depth sustained above 20 for 30 minutes, coordinator queue items pending beyond 5 business days.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $280/month: Container Apps FastAPI + Celery (consumption plan) ~$80, Cosmos DB serverless ~$60, Azure Cache for Redis Basic ~$50, Blob Storage ~$20, Key Vault + Application Insights ~$30, Logic Apps email notifications ~$10, Azure DevOps ~$30. Scaling: Celery workers scale on Redis queue depth via KEDA; FastAPI replicas scale on HTTP RPS. Peak submission periods at semester start drive worker scaling; consumption plan auto-scales without manual intervention.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Azure AD OIDC integration, faculty PDF upload portal, pdfplumber extraction Celery task, rule engine with bundled ADA/late-work/grading-policy/contact-info rules (ADA and late-work locked), compliance report generation, coordinator review queue with approve/revision-request, SHA-256 archival to Blob WORM container, admin rule management panel with locked indicator, Azure DevOps CI pipeline, Terraform infrastructure including WORM policy. Phase 2 (5 weeks): Nightly department compliance summary report, institution-wide admin dashboard by department and term, faculty resubmission flow with diff highlighting between submission versions, email notification templates via Logic Apps. Phase 3 (future): AI-assisted compliance suggestions, course management system integration for automatic syllabus import.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type hints on all FastAPI route handlers and Celery tasks. pytest with 80% coverage; parameterized tests for rule engine covering all rule_type variants and edge cases (empty PDF, multi-column layout, scanned PDF extraction failure). Integration test asserting 403 on PATCH active=False for locked compliance rule. Coordinator queue integration test asserting department isolation between two coordinator accounts with different department_id claims. No rule evaluation logic in Jinja2 templates — all rule results from compliance_report MongoDB document. Terraform plan reviewed in CI pipeline before production apply.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: pdfplumber accuracy on scanned or image-based PDFs producing empty extracted_text. Mitigation: extraction_failure_rate Application Insights alert; coordinator notified on extraction_failed status with guidance to request resubmission as text-based PDF. Risk: Cosmos DB MongoDB API aggregation pipeline limitations affecting institution-wide compliance reporting queries. Mitigation: nightly Celery beat pre-aggregation into department_compliance_summaries documents to avoid complex real-time aggregations. Tradeoff: Server-side Jinja2 templates over React SPA for portal simplicity at faculty submission scale; acceptable given simple form and queue review UX requirements.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-002 resolved: locked rules enforced at backend API layer and admin panel UI; ADA Statement rule seeded as locked. ISS-003 resolved: Azure Blob WORM immutability policy provisioned via Terraform at storage account level. Confirmed Cosmos DB MongoDB API over native MongoDB Atlas to stay within Azure enterprise agreement. Confirmed Logic Apps over SendGrid for email notifications to leverage existing university Azure subscription.

metadata.notes: Round 3 fixes ADA rule locking (ISS-002) via backend enforcement and UI, and Blob WORM immutability (ISS-003) via Terraform policy. Moderate quality: observability and cost sections slightly thin.

project_goal.value: Establish a shared GPU cluster and MLOps platform for multi-tenant training, versioning, and serving of ML models across university research groups and coursework
project_goal.rationale: Core mandate from data science department to replace ad-hoc GPU borrowing with governed platform

target_users.value: Faculty researchers, graduate students, and undergraduate course participants in university data science department
target_users.rationale: Multi-tenant population with distinct quota needs and isolation requirements

project_class.rationale: Platform infrastructure with no end-user browser UI; manages compute and artifact lifecycle

capabilities.rationale: MLOps lifecycle covers pipeline orchestration, artifact storage, batch training, and experiment analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant GPU scheduling, model versioning across hundreds of experiments, and concurrent training jobs require high-scale design

risk_level.rationale: IRB and EAR/ITAR concerns for dual-use research but no PII exposure to external parties

data_sensitivity.rationale: Experiment data and model artifacts are internal; human-subject datasets require IRB but not classified as personal under this platform

external_exposure.rationale: Platform accessible only to university network and VPN-connected research staff

access_model.value: Google Workspace SSO with per-research-group namespaces and GPU quota enforcement in GKE
access_model.rationale: Leverages existing university identity; namespace isolation prevents cross-group resource access

feature_scope.value: Kubeflow pipeline submission UI, MLflow experiment tracking, per-group GPU quota enforcement, model artifact registry, dataset mounting, CI-gated model promotion
feature_scope.rationale: Full MLOps lifecycle from experiment to production registry

mvp_scope.value: GKE cluster with GPU node pools, Kubeflow Pipelines, MLflow tracking server, per-group ResourceQuota objects, and Google Workspace SSO
mvp_scope.rationale: Core scheduling and tracking before artifact promotion workflows

security_baseline.value: Namespace-level RBAC in GKE, mTLS between services via Istio, Google Workspace SSO for all access, GPU node pool taint/toleration isolation
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents cross-tenant data leakage and unauthorized GPU allocation

frontend_stack.value: Kubeflow Pipelines UI (built-in) and MLflow web UI -- no custom frontend development
frontend_stack.rationale: Upstream project UIs cover experiment and pipeline management needs

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with MLflow and Kubeflow Pipelines SDK; Cloud Build for CI
backend_stack.rationale: Standard MLOps toolchain; Python SDK enables notebook-native pipeline authoring

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for MLflow metadata store; GCS buckets for artifact and dataset storage; NVIDIA DCGM Exporter metrics to Prometheus
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL provides transactional metadata; GCS offers cost-effective artifact durability

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot cluster with dedicated GPU node pools (NVIDIA A100 and T4); MLflow on Cloud Run; Kubeflow on GKE
hosting_target.rationale: Autopilot manages node lifecycle; GPU node pools support MIG partitioning for smaller workloads

privacy_retention_policy.value: Model artifacts retained indefinitely while experiment is active; IRB-restricted datasets purged per IRB protocol expiration; logs retained 1 year
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Supports reproducibility requirements while enforcing IRB data lifecycle

future_scope.value: Integration with university HPC cluster for CPU-intensive preprocessing; public model serving endpoint for approved research publications
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 expansions after platform stabilization

constraints.value: All compute must remain on GCP us-central1; GPU budget capped at $8,000/month across all tenants; EAR/ITAR controls for dual-use research groups
constraints.rationale: Data residency, budget governance, and federal export control requirements

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards, Prometheus metrics, NVIDIA DCGM Exporter for GPU utilization, Cloud Build logs, Kubeflow run-level logging
observability_baseline.rationale: GPU utilization and training job health are primary observability targets

execution_preference.value: Infrastructure-as-code via Terraform; config changes reviewed via PR with DevOps lead approval
execution_preference.rationale: Reproducible cluster state and audit trail for compliance

compliance_context.value: IRB approval required for experiments with human-subject datasets; EAR/ITAR controls mandate access restrictions for dual-use research computing resources; no export-controlled data may leave us-central1
compliance_context.rationale: Federal research computing compliance for human subjects and export control

project.goal: Shared MLOps platform with GPU scheduling, experiment tracking, and model registry for university research groups
project.target_users: Faculty, graduate students, undergrad course participants
project.timeline: Q2 delivery for fall semester research intake

backend.pipeline_sdk: Kubeflow Pipelines SDK for DAG authoring in notebooks

security.auth: Google Workspace SSO via OIDC
security.isolation: GKE namespace RBAC + ResourceQuota per research group

data.artifact_store: GCS bucket per research group namespace
data.dataset_mounts: GCS FUSE or persistent volumes for training data

devops.ci: Cloud Build with Docker layer caching
devops.hosting: GKE Autopilot + dedicated GPU node pools

constraints.itar_groups: restricted namespace labels with network policy egress blocks

open_questions.mig_partitioning: Evaluate A100 MIG partitioning for smaller coursework experiments

reasoner_reviews.product: The multi-tenant GPU platform addresses a real bottleneck where research groups currently email IT to borrow GPUs on an ad-hoc basis. Kubeflow Pipelines and MLflow cover the orchestration and tracking needs without requiring custom tooling. The ResourceQuota model per namespace is the right primitive for budget enforcement.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: GKE Autopilot is appropriate for the control plane, but GPU node pools must be explicitly Standard nodes since Autopilot has GPU scheduling constraints. MIG partitioning on A100s for coursework experiments will significantly improve GPU utilization for small training jobs. MLflow on Cloud Run avoids running a long-lived pod and simplifies scaling.
reasoner_reviews.security: EAR/ITAR-controlled research groups need namespace-level network egress policies to prevent data exfiltration beyond us-central1. IRB dataset mounts should use time-boxed GCS signed URLs rather than persistent volume claims to enforce protocol expiration boundaries. Google Workspace SSO satisfies identity requirements, but role bindings must be group-synced not individually assigned.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $8,000/month GPU budget requires per-namespace cost attribution through GCP billing labels on node pools. Round 1 did not define a chargeback reporting mechanism for department billing transparency -- this must be added in round 2.

backend.service_design: MLflow tracking server on Cloud Run (min 1, max 5 instances) backed by CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16; Kubeflow Pipelines control plane on GKE Standard node pool; artifact proxy service enforcing group-scoped GCS access
backend.api_patterns: MLflow REST API for experiment and run management; Kubeflow Pipelines API for DAG submission and run monitoring; internal gRPC for artifact proxy
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Build triggers for model promotion CI checks; Kubeflow recurring runs for scheduled retraining pipelines; GCS lifecycle rules for artifact expiration
backend.scaling_notes: GKE cluster autoscaler with GPU node pool min 2 / max 20 nodes; Kubeflow pipeline concurrency governed by ResourceQuota CPU and memory limits per namespace

security.auth_design: OIDC integration with Google Workspace; Kubeflow multi-user mode with Dex as identity broker; each research group maps to a Kubernetes namespace with a bound service account
security.authorization_model: ClusterRole bindings for platform admins; namespace-scoped Role bindings for research group members; ITAR groups annotated with restricted label triggering stricter NetworkPolicy
security.secrets_management: GCP Secret Manager for MLflow DB credentials and GCS HMAC keys; Workload Identity for pod-level GCS access -- no long-lived keys in pods
security.security_risks: Cross-tenant artifact access via misconfigured GCS bucket ACLs; GPU node privilege escalation via hostPath mounts -- mitigated by PodSecurity admission restricting host volume mounts

data.entities: ml_experiments (id, group_namespace, name, tags_json, created_by, created_at), model_versions (id, experiment_id, run_id, stage ENUM staging/production/archived, artifact_uri, promoted_at), training_jobs (id, pipeline_run_id, gpu_allocation_mig_slice, status, started_at, finished_at), gpu_allocations (id, namespace, gpu_type, mig_partition, reserved_at, released_at), research_group_tenants (id, namespace, pi_email, itar_restricted, monthly_gpu_budget_usd), dataset_mounts (id, gcs_path, irb_protocol_id, expiry_date, mounted_by)
data.storage_design: Per-group GCS bucket with uniform bucket-level access; MLflow artifact store paths scoped by experiment ID; CloudSQL PostgreSQL for tracking metadata with daily automated backups to GCS
data.retention_and_deletion: IRB-expired dataset mounts revoked via Cloud Scheduler job; model artifacts archived after 90 days in non-production stage

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Standard cluster (us-central1) with CPU system node pool and two GPU node pools (NVIDIA T4 for staging, NVIDIA A100 with MIG for production); Kubeflow on GKE, MLflow on Cloud Run, artifact proxy as GKE Deployment
devops.environments: dev: CPU-only GKE cluster with emulated quota enforcement; staging: T4 node pool, single-digit GPU count; production: A100 node pool with MIG partitioning, 8 physical GPUs
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build pipeline: Docker build, unit tests, terraform plan/apply for infra changes, Kubeflow pipeline compilation and upload to pipeline registry
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping DCGM Exporter GPU metrics and kube-state-metrics; Grafana dashboards for per-namespace GPU utilization, training job queue depth, and model promotion rate; Cloud Build logs forwarded to Cloud Logging
devops.rollback_strategy: Terraform state versioned in GCS with locking; cluster rollback via node pool version pinning; MLflow model stage revert via API call

ISS-001.title: No GPU cost chargeback reporting for department billing
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan lacks any mechanism for attributing GPU spend to individual research groups for department-level billing. Without per-namespace cost labels and a reporting job, the $8,000/month budget cap cannot be enforced at group granularity.

history.note: Identified by auditor: budget section omits chargeback design

ISS-002.title: IRB dataset expiry enforcement not automated
ISS-002.detail: The round 1 plan describes time-boxed dataset mounts conceptually but does not define the Cloud Scheduler job or reconciliation logic that revokes GCS access when an IRB protocol expires.

history.note: Compliance reviewer flagged missing automation for IRB lifecycle

focus_issues.title: No GPU cost chargeback reporting for department billing
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan lacks any mechanism for attributing GPU spend to individual research groups for department-level billing.

focus_issues.title: IRB dataset expiry enforcement not automated
focus_issues.detail: The round 1 plan describes time-boxed dataset mounts conceptually but does not define the Cloud Scheduler job or reconciliation logic.

latest_recommendations: Add per-namespace GCP billing label strategy and a monthly cost reporting job that emails PIs their GPU spend vs. quota
Define the Cloud Scheduler job and GCS IAM revocation logic for IRB dataset expiry enforcement
