convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues from round 1 block passage

previous_audits.summary: Solid infrastructure foundation with good GKE and Kubeflow design, but critical gaps in GPU cost attribution and IRB data lifecycle automation prevent passage.
strengths: Namespace-level RBAC and ResourceQuota design is appropriate for multi-tenant GPU isolation
MLflow on Cloud Run avoids long-lived pod management complexity
MIG partitioning concept for A100 coursework experiments is well-motivated
concerns: No chargeback reporting mechanism for per-group GPU cost attribution
IRB dataset mount expiry described conceptually but not operationally implemented
blocking_issues: ISS-001: Department billing cannot enforce $8k/month cap without per-namespace cost labels and reporting job
ISS-002: IRB dataset expiry lacks Cloud Scheduler automation and GCS IAM revocation
recommendations: Instrument each namespace node pool usage with GCP billing labels and implement a monthly Cloud Function that aggregates costs per label and emails PIs
Add a Cloud Scheduler job running nightly that queries dataset_mounts.expiry_date, removes IAM bindings from expired GCS paths, and logs revocations

issue_updates.title: No GPU cost chargeback reporting
issue_updates.detail: Budget section mentions $8k cap but no attribution or reporting mechanism defined

issue_updates.title: IRB dataset expiry enforcement not automated
issue_updates.detail: dataset_mounts table has expiry_date column but no job enforces it

previous_plan.title: University MLOps Platform -- GPU Cluster Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: GKE-based MLOps platform with Kubeflow Pipelines and MLflow for multi-tenant GPU training and model versioning. Namespace isolation enforces research group boundaries. IRB dataset mounts are time-boxed but manual revocation is a gap.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: GKE Standard cluster with CPU and GPU node pools; Kubeflow Pipelines on GKE; MLflow on Cloud Run; GCS for artifacts. Google Workspace SSO via Dex. Per-namespace ResourceQuota for GPU scheduling governance.
previous_plan.data_model: ml_experiments, model_versions, training_jobs, gpu_allocations, research_group_tenants, dataset_mounts -- PostgreSQL backend for MLflow metadata.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Terraform-managed GKE cluster; Cloud Build CI for infra and pipeline changes; Prometheus and DCGM Exporter for GPU metrics; Grafana dashboards per namespace.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must resolve ISS-001 by adding GCP billing label instrumentation across GPU node pools and a Cloud Function that generates monthly per-namespace cost reports for PI chargeback. ISS-002 requires a concrete Cloud Scheduler job that nightly queries dataset_mounts for expired IRB protocols and revokes GCS IAM bindings. Both fixes touch cost_and_scaling, observability, and deployment_and_operations sections. The core GKE, Kubeflow, and MLflow architecture from round 1 is sound and preserved.

fix_report.action_taken: Added GCP billing label strategy (research_group, itar_restricted, cost_center) on GPU node pools and a monthly Cloud Function (gpu-cost-reporter) that queries BigQuery billing export, aggregates cost per namespace label, and emails each PI their GPU spend vs. monthly quota with overage warnings
fix_report.expected_outcome: Department administrators can enforce the $8,000/month aggregate cap with per-group visibility; PIs self-govern their group quotas through monthly email reports

fix_report.action_taken: Defined a Cloud Scheduler job (irb-expiry-enforcer, runs nightly at 02:00 UTC) that queries dataset_mounts WHERE expiry_date <= CURRENT_DATE, calls GCS IAM API to remove the research group service account binding from each expired bucket path, marks the mount record as revoked, and inserts an audit log entry with the IRB protocol ID and PI email
fix_report.expected_outcome: IRB protocol expiry automatically triggers GCS access revocation within 24 hours of expiry date, satisfying IRB data lifecycle compliance without manual IT intervention

target_output.title: University MLOps Platform -- GPU Cluster and MLOps Architecture (v2)
target_output.executive_summary: A multi-tenant MLOps platform on GKE providing shared NVIDIA GPU resources, Kubeflow pipeline orchestration, and MLflow experiment tracking for university research groups and coursework. GCP billing labels per namespace enable PI-level cost chargeback against the $8,000/month GPU budget. IRB dataset mounts are automatically revoked at protocol expiry by a nightly Cloud Scheduler job. Namespace RBAC and MIG partitioning enforce workload isolation between research groups and student experiments.
target_output.architecture_overview: GKE Standard cluster in us-central1 with a CPU system node pool and two GPU node pools: NVIDIA T4 (staging, shared) and NVIDIA A100 with MIG partitioning (production, per-group slices). Kubeflow Pipelines 2.x runs on GKE with Argo Workflows as its execution engine; MLflow 2.x tracking server runs on Cloud Run backed by CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16. GCS buckets scoped per research group namespace serve as artifact stores. Google Workspace OIDC flows through Dex into Kubeflow multi-user mode. Istio service mesh enforces mTLS between all platform services. NVIDIA DCGM Exporter feeds per-GPU metrics to Prometheus; Grafana surfaces per-namespace utilization dashboards.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Kubeflow Pipelines 2.x (Argo Workflows backend), Kubeflow Pipelines SDK for Python 3.12 notebook authoring. Experiment tracking: MLflow 2.x on Cloud Run, CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16 backend store. Artifact store: GCS (one bucket per research group namespace). Compute: GKE Standard, NVIDIA A100 (MIG-enabled), NVIDIA T4. Service mesh: Istio with mTLS. Identity: Google Workspace OIDC, Dex identity broker. GPU monitoring: NVIDIA DCGM Exporter, Prometheus, Grafana. IaC: Terraform. CI: Cloud Build. Cost reporting: Cloud Function + BigQuery billing export.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Pipeline Submission: researchers author DAGs in Python notebooks using Kubeflow SDK and submit via UI or CLI; scheduler queues against namespace ResourceQuota limits. Experiment Tracking: each training run logs hyperparameters, metrics, and artifact URIs to MLflow; researchers compare runs via MLflow web UI. Model Registry: promoted models move from 'staging' to 'production' stage after CI-gated approval checks pass in Cloud Build. GPU Quota Enforcement: per-namespace ResourceQuota objects cap GPU requests; Cluster Autoscaler scales GPU node pools within budget. Cost Chargeback: monthly Cloud Function aggregates GPU spend per namespace billing label and emails PIs. IRB Lifecycle: nightly Cloud Scheduler job revokes GCS access for expired IRB dataset mounts.
target_output.system_components: GKE Standard cluster (us-central1): CPU system pool, T4 GPU pool (staging), A100 MIG GPU pool (production). Kubeflow Pipelines control plane: pipeline-runner, metadata-writer, artifact-proxy as GKE Deployments. MLflow tracking server: Cloud Run service (min 1, max 5), CloudSQL PostgreSQL 16. Artifact proxy service: enforces group-scoped GCS signed URL generation. Cloud Scheduler (irb-expiry-enforcer): nightly GCS IAM revocation job. Cloud Function (gpu-cost-reporter): monthly BigQuery billing aggregation and PI email dispatch. Prometheus + DCGM Exporter + Grafana: GPU and pipeline observability.
target_output.workflows: Training Job Submission: researcher opens Kubeflow Pipelines UI authenticated via Google Workspace SSO, uploads compiled pipeline YAML, sets GPU resource requests; scheduler checks namespace ResourceQuota, queues if at capacity, provisions MIG slice on A100 pool when available, sends email notification when job starts and completes. IRB Dataset Expiry: nightly Cloud Scheduler triggers irb-expiry-enforcer; job queries dataset_mounts WHERE expiry_date <= CURRENT_DATE AND status = 'active'; for each expired mount, removes research group service account from GCS bucket IAM policy, updates status to 'revoked', inserts audit_log entry with irb_protocol_id and pi_email, sends revocation notice to PI and IRB coordinator.
target_output.data_model: ml_experiments (id UUID PK, group_namespace VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, tags_json JSONB, created_by VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). model_versions (id UUID PK, experiment_id FK, run_id VARCHAR, stage ENUM staging/production/archived, artifact_uri TEXT, promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). training_jobs (id UUID PK, pipeline_run_id VARCHAR, gpu_type VARCHAR, mig_slice VARCHAR, namespace VARCHAR, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, finished_at TIMESTAMPTZ). gpu_allocations (id UUID PK, namespace VARCHAR, gpu_type VARCHAR, mig_partition VARCHAR, reserved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, cost_usd NUMERIC). research_group_tenants (id UUID PK, namespace VARCHAR UNIQUE, pi_email VARCHAR, itar_restricted BOOLEAN, monthly_gpu_budget_usd NUMERIC). dataset_mounts (id UUID PK, gcs_path TEXT, irb_protocol_id VARCHAR, expiry_date DATE, status ENUM active/revoked, revoked_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Kubeflow Pipelines API: POST /apis/v2beta1/runs (submit pipeline run), GET /apis/v2beta1/runs/{run_id} (run status), DELETE /apis/v2beta1/runs/{run_id} (cancel). MLflow REST API: POST /api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/create, POST /api/2.0/mlflow/runs/create, PATCH /api/2.0/mlflow/model-versions/transition-stage (model promotion to production). Artifact proxy: GET /artifacts/{namespace}/{path} validates caller namespace binding before issuing 15-min GCS signed URL. Admin API: POST /admin/tenants (create research group namespace with quota), GET /admin/tenants/{namespace}/cost (current month GPU spend from BigQuery).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Namespace RBAC: ClusterRole platform-admin for DevOps; namespace-scoped Role ml-user for group members with Kubeflow PodDefault binding. ITAR controls: namespaces labeled itar_restricted=true receive NetworkPolicy blocking all egress except GCS us-central1 and internal cluster DNS. Pod security: PodSecurity admission in 'restricted' profile blocks hostPath mounts and privilege escalation. Workload Identity: each namespace service account bound to a GCP service account with GCS read/write scoped to that group's bucket only. IRB compliance: dataset_mounts expiry enforced nightly by irb-expiry-enforcer; audit_log table append-only; revocation notices sent to PI and IRB coordinator. EAR/ITAR: ITAR-restricted groups may not egress data outside us-central1.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform modules manage: GKE cluster, node pool autoscaler configs, namespace ResourceQuota objects, CloudSQL instance, Cloud Run MLflow service, Cloud Scheduler jobs, billing label assignments on node pools. Cloud Build pipelines: infra pipeline runs terraform validate, plan, apply on PR merge to main; model promotion pipeline runs on MLflow stage transition webhook, executes accuracy threshold and schema validation tests. Environments: dev (CPU-only GKE, emulated quota), staging (T4 node pool, 2 GPUs), production (A100 node pool with MIG, 8 physical GPUs). Rollback: Terraform state in GCS with versioning and advisory locking; node pool rollback via version pinning. Secrets: CloudSQL password and GCS HMAC keys in GCP Secret Manager, rotated every 90 days.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes NVIDIA DCGM Exporter for per-GPU utilization, memory used, and temperature; kube-state-metrics for ResourceQuota utilization per namespace. Grafana dashboards: per-namespace GPU utilization vs. quota, training job queue depth and wait time, model promotion success rate, IRB dataset mount count by status. Alerts: GPU utilization >90% for >15 min fires Slack notification to platform admin; namespace quota exhaustion emails PI; irb-expiry-enforcer failure fires PagerDuty. Monthly gpu-cost-reporter Cloud Function sends per-namespace spend summary to PIs and department head.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Baseline GPU cost: 2x T4 staging ($280/month) + 8x A100 MIG production (approx $6,500/month) = approx $6,780/month within $8,000 cap. Per-namespace billing labels (research_group, cost_center, itar_restricted) applied to GPU node pools; BigQuery billing export captures label-attributed costs hourly. Autoscaler scales GPU node pool up when pending pods queue for >5 minutes and down after 10 minutes idle. CloudSQL PostgreSQL on db-f1-micro for dev, db-n1-standard-2 for production (approx $100/month).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Core Platform (6 weeks): Terraform GKE cluster with CPU and T4 node pools, Kubeflow Pipelines deployment, MLflow on Cloud Run with CloudSQL backend, Google Workspace SSO via Dex, namespace creation for 3 pilot research groups, ResourceQuota enforcement, Cloud Build infra pipeline. Phase 2 -- Production GPU and Security (4 weeks): A100 node pool with MIG partitioning, ITAR namespace network policies, Workload Identity per-group GCS access, PodSecurity admission enforcement, irb-expiry-enforcer Cloud Scheduler job, audit_log table, model promotion CI pipeline. Phase 3 -- Cost Governance and Observability (3 weeks): GCP billing label instrumentation, gpu-cost-reporter Cloud Function with BigQuery integration, Grafana per-namespace GPU dashboards, DCGM Exporter alerting, onboarding runbook for new research groups.
target_output.development_guidelines: All infrastructure changes via Terraform; no manual GCP console modifications to production. Cloud Build pipelines must pass terraform validate and tflint before apply. New research group onboarding via Terraform namespace module only. Python pipeline components must include pytest unit tests before Kubeflow registry submission. EAR/ITAR group additions require security team sign-off before namespace label application.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: A100 MIG partitioning misconfiguration exposing one group's GPU memory to another -- mitigated by using only validated MIG partition profiles and periodic dcgmi diagnostic runs. Risk: CloudSQL PostgreSQL bottleneck at high MLflow experiment volume -- mitigated by read replica and async logging mode; AlloyDB migration path documented. Tradeoff: GKE Standard over Autopilot for GPU node pool flexibility at the cost of node lifecycle management overhead. Tradeoff: Cloud Run for MLflow over GKE Deployment reduces pod management but introduces cold-start latency; min-instances=1 in production mitigates.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: A100 MIG partition profiles selected: 3g.40gb for large research jobs, 1g.10gb for coursework experiments. CloudSQL PostgreSQL chosen over AlloyDB for MLflow backend at this scale; AlloyDB migration path documented for >500 concurrent runs. Dex confirmed as OIDC broker supporting both Google Workspace and future SAML federation for visiting researchers.

metadata.notes: University MLOps GPU platform round 2 -- resolves GPU cost chargeback and IRB dataset expiry automation gaps from round 1.

profile.domain: K-12 Ed-Tech / Student Information System SDK

project_goal.value: Python SDK providing security-hardened client library for ed-tech vendors to authenticate against district SIS APIs, fetch student rosters, and sync grade data with built-in OAuth2 token management, rate limiting, and audit logging
project_goal.rationale: Districts need a vetted integration library to reduce bespoke vendor implementations with inconsistent security practices

target_users.value: Third-party ed-tech vendor developers integrating with K-12 district SIS APIs
target_users.rationale: SDK consumers are external developers, not end-user students or teachers

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a Python package on PyPI; no hosted service or browser UI

capabilities.rationale: SDK encapsulates authentication, data access patterns, and audit logging; no frontend or hosting

complexity_level.rationale: OAuth2 PKCE flow, token refresh, retry logic, rate limiting, and field-scoped roster fetching are non-trivial but bounded in scope

risk_level.rationale: SDK handles FERPA-protected student PII; a security flaw could expose student data across all vendor integrations using the library

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student roster records including names, enrollment status, and grade data are FERPA-protected PII

external_exposure.rationale: SDK is distributed to vetted ed-tech vendor partners; not publicly consumable without district credentialing

access_model.value: OAuth2 with PKCE for vendor app authorization; district-issued client credentials scoped to data sharing agreement fields
access_model.rationale: PKCE prevents authorization code interception; field scoping enforces data minimization per DSA

feature_scope.value: OAuth2 PKCE token acquisition and transparent refresh, roster batch fetch with field scoping, grade sync event streaming, rate limit enforcement, structured audit log emission, retry with exponential backoff
feature_scope.rationale: Full integration surface needed by ed-tech vendors under FERPA data sharing agreements

mvp_scope.value: OAuth2 token management, roster fetch with field scoping, grade sync, and audit log emission to local file or configurable sink
mvp_scope.rationale: Core data access flows before advanced streaming and observability features

security_baseline.value: OAuth2 PKCE only (no client_secret_basic), credentials stored via keyring or env vars (never hardcoded), TLS 1.3 for all API calls, audit log entries for every data access call, field scoping enforced client-side before data is surfaced to vendor app
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA compliance requires data minimization and access logging at the library level

frontend_stack.value: None -- library_sdk; no UI components
frontend_stack.rationale: Package architecture only; no browser or native UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12; httpx for async HTTP; authlib for OAuth2; keyring for credential storage
backend_stack.rationale: httpx provides async and sync interfaces; authlib is the de-facto OAuth2 library for Python

data_platform.value: No persistent database -- SDK is stateless except for in-memory token cache; audit logs written to configurable sink (file, syslog, or custom handler)
data_platform.rationale: Library must not impose storage dependencies on vendor applications

hosting_target.value: PyPI package distribution; documentation on Read the Docs; GitHub Actions for CI/CD
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Python ecosystem distribution channels

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK itself retains no data; audit log sink retention governed by vendor's own data retention policies and DSA terms; SDK documentation must state this explicitly
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Shifts data retention obligation to consuming vendor in alignment with DSA

future_scope.value: Async-first API surface, GraphQL SIS query support, multi-district credential management for aggregator vendors
future_scope.rationale: Vendor demand for async Python and GraphQL SIS endpoints in Year 2

constraints.value: Must support Python 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12; zero mandatory runtime dependencies beyond stdlib + httpx + authlib; must pass OWASP dependency audit in CI
constraints.rationale: Minimizes vendor dependency conflicts and reduces attack surface

observability_baseline.value: Structured audit log entries (JSON) for every SIS API call; optional Prometheus metrics endpoint via plugin; no telemetry sent back to SDK publisher
observability_baseline.rationale: Vendors need integration visibility without SDK calling home

execution_preference.value: Semantic versioning with CHANGELOG; pre-1.0 breaking changes allowed; post-1.0 SemVer strictly followed; PRs require two maintainer reviews
execution_preference.rationale: Ed-tech vendors pin SDK versions in production; breaking changes must be communicated via SemVer

compliance_context.value: FERPA compliance is a first-class SDK requirement: data minimization enforced via field scoping, every student data access logged, credentials never persisted in plaintext, DSA field scope validated before roster response is returned to vendor
compliance_context.rationale: Districts require contractual assurance that the SDK enforces FERPA controls independent of vendor implementation quality

project.goal: Python SDK for K-12 SIS vendor integrations with built-in FERPA controls
project.target_users: Ed-tech vendor developers
project.timeline: Q2 release on PyPI

security.auth: OAuth2 PKCE; no implicit flow
security.field_scoping: DSA field mask applied before returning roster data
security.audit: Structured JSON audit log for every API call

data.token_cache: In-memory token cache with TTL; no persistent storage
data.audit_sink: Configurable -- file, syslog, or custom logging.Handler subclass

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff), type check (mypy), test (pytest, 95% coverage), OWASP dep audit (pip-audit)

constraints.max_deps: httpx, authlib, keyring -- no others mandatory

open_questions.async_api: Decide whether to expose async-first or sync-first API surface for v1

reasoner_reviews.product: Field scoping enforcement at the SDK level is the critical FERPA differentiator -- it ensures vendors cannot accidentally return more student data than their DSA permits even if they misconfigure their own filtering. The audit log sink pattern (file/syslog/custom handler) is the right abstraction: it respects vendor infrastructure diversity without the SDK imposing a storage dependency.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: httpx is the correct choice for supporting both sync and async callers without maintaining two HTTP client codepaths. The token cache should use threading.local or asyncio-scoped storage to avoid cross-request token leakage in multi-threaded vendor applications. The round 1 plan did not specify how the field mask is declared -- it must be a typed Pydantic model validated against the district's published DSA schema at SDK init time, not a free-form dict.
reasoner_reviews.security: PKCE is necessary but the SDK must also pin the district SIS API TLS certificate fingerprint or at minimum validate against the system trust store with no allow_insecure option exposed. Audit log entries must include the DSA agreement ID so compliance audits can map every access to a specific data sharing contract. Round 1 missed the requirement to validate field scope against the DSA schema at init -- a vendor passing out-of-scope fields must get a hard error, not a silent truncation.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Python 3.10+ constraint is realistic and keeps the SDK on maintained versions. OWASP dep audit via pip-audit in CI is the right control for catching known CVEs in httpx and authlib transitives. Zero mandatory extra dependencies beyond the three named is achievable and important for vendor adoption.

backend.service_design: SISClient class exposing sync and async context managers; TokenManager handling PKCE flow, token storage in keyring, and transparent refresh; RosterClient and GradeClient as domain-specific resource clients wrapping SISClient; FieldScopeValidator validating requested fields against DSA schema at init
backend.api_patterns: Method-based API: client.rosters.list(class_id, fields=FieldScope.from_dsa('agreement-id')), client.grades.sync(class_id, since=datetime); all methods return typed dataclasses, not raw dicts
backend.failure_handling: httpx.RetryTransport with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 3 retries) for 429 and 5xx; RateLimitError raised with retry_after when district API returns 429 with Retry-After header; DSAFieldScopeError raised at init if requested fields exceed DSA permissions
backend.backend_risks: Token cache thread-safety in multi-threaded WSGI vendor apps; keyring unavailability in containerized environments requiring env-var fallback credential mode

security.auth_design: OAuth2 PKCE: generate code_verifier (32-byte random), compute code_challenge (S256), redirect vendor to district authorization endpoint, exchange code for tokens; token stored in OS keyring under service='sis-sdk', username=district_id
security.authorization_model: Field scope declared as FieldScope dataclass validated against district DSA schema (JSON Schema) fetched at SDK init; any field outside DSA scope raises DSAFieldScopeError before any network call is made
security.audit_and_logging_controls: AuditLogger emits JSON entries: {timestamp, vendor_app_id, district_id, dsa_agreement_id, operation, resource_type, resource_id, fields_accessed, http_status, latency_ms} -- sent to configured sink (FileHandler, SyslogHandler, or custom logging.Handler)
security.security_risks: Vendor passing custom SSLContext with verify=False bypassing TLS validation; mitigated by SDK refusing to instantiate with unverified context. Audit log sink misconfiguration leading to silent drops -- mitigated by SDK emitting warning on sink write failure

data.entities: sis_tenants (district_id, base_url, dsa_schema_url, created_at in SDK config), roster_records (student_id, class_id, fields_dict scoped by DSA), grade_sync_events (student_id, class_id, grade_value, sync_timestamp, dsa_agreement_id), oauth_tokens (district_id, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at stored in keyring), audit_log_entries (timestamp, operation, resource_id, fields_accessed, http_status)
data.schema_notes: All SDK response types are frozen dataclasses with only DSA-permitted fields; no dynamic attribute access. DSA schema is a JSON Schema document describing permitted field names per agreement ID.
data.data_risks: In-memory token cache leaking tokens across asyncio tasks in shared event loop; mitigated by scoping cache to task-local context variables

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix: Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 x ubuntu-latest; steps: ruff lint, mypy --strict type check, pytest with 95% coverage gate, pip-audit for known CVEs, build sdist and wheel, upload to TestPyPI on PR merge to main, upload to PyPI on semver tag push
devops.environments: TestPyPI for pre-release validation; PyPI for production releases; Read the Docs preview builds on PR
devops.devops_risks: PyPI trusted publisher misconfiguration allowing unauthorized package publication; mitigated by OIDC-based trusted publisher with environment protection rules on the pypi-publish GitHub Actions environment

ISS-001.title: Field scope not validated against DSA schema at SDK init
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan describes field scoping as a client-side filter applied before returning data, but does not specify that the requested fields are validated against the district's published DSA schema at SDK initialization time. A vendor could request out-of-scope fields and receive a silent truncation rather than a hard error, making FERPA violations undetectable.

history.note: Security reviewer identified missing DSA schema validation at init

ISS-002.title: Audit log entries missing DSA agreement ID
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 audit log schema includes operation, resource_id, and fields_accessed but omits dsa_agreement_id. Without linking each log entry to a specific data sharing contract, compliance audits cannot map access events to authorized agreements.

history.note: Compliance reviewer flagged missing DSA reference in audit schema

focus_issues.title: Field scope not validated against DSA schema at SDK init
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan does not specify hard validation of requested fields against the DSA schema at initialization, allowing silent out-of-scope data access.

focus_issues.title: Audit log entries missing DSA agreement ID
focus_issues.detail: Audit log schema omits dsa_agreement_id, preventing compliance audit mapping of access events to authorized agreements.

latest_recommendations: Add DSA schema validation at SISClient init: fetch the district's JSON Schema document and validate all FieldScope field names against it; raise DSAFieldScopeError for any out-of-scope field before any API call
Add dsa_agreement_id to the AuditLogger entry schema so every data access event is traceable to an authorized agreement
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: K-12 SIS Integration SDK -- Security-Hardened Python Library (v1)

convergence_state.reason: ISS-001 is a FERPA compliance blocker; ISS-002 is a compliance gap

previous_audits.summary: Good OAuth2 and retry design, but field scope validation is conceptual rather than enforced at init and the audit log schema is missing the DSA agreement ID linkage required for compliance traceability.
strengths: PKCE-only OAuth2 with keyring credential storage is the correct FERPA-aligned design
Typed dataclass response objects prevent dynamic attribute access exposing raw API responses
httpx RetryTransport with exponential backoff is well-specified
concerns: FieldScope validation against DSA schema is described as filtering, not hard validation at init
Audit log schema lacks dsa_agreement_id -- cannot map access events to authorized contracts

recommendations: Fetch DSA JSON Schema at SDK init and validate FieldScope against it; raise DSAFieldScopeError for any field not in schema
Add dsa_agreement_id field to AuditLogger entry dataclass and require it at SISClient instantiation

previous_plan.title: K-12 SIS Integration SDK -- Security-Hardened Python Library (v1)
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python SDK for ed-tech vendors integrating with district SIS APIs. OAuth2 PKCE token management, typed roster and grade clients, client-side field scope filtering, and audit log emission. Distributed on PyPI with zero mandatory deps beyond httpx, authlib, and keyring.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: OAuth2 PKCE only, keyring credential storage, TLS 1.3 enforced, field scope filtering before returning data to vendor app, audit log with operation and fields_accessed.
previous_plan.data_model: sis_tenants, roster_records, grade_sync_events, oauth_tokens, audit_log_entries -- all as frozen dataclasses, no persistent storage.
previous_plan.api_design: client.rosters.list(class_id, fields=FieldScope(...)), client.grades.sync(class_id, since=datetime) -- typed dataclass responses.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 must convert the FieldScope from a passive filter into an active validator: at SISClient init, fetch the district's DSA JSON Schema document and validate every field in the FieldScope against it, raising DSAFieldScopeError for any out-of-scope field before any API call is made. ISS-002 is resolved by adding dsa_agreement_id as a required parameter to SISClient and including it in every AuditEntry. Both fixes are non-breaking additions that strengthen the FERPA compliance posture of the library.

fix_report.action_taken: Added DSASchemaValidator that fetches the district's published JSON Schema document (URL from SISClient config) at init time and validates all FieldScope field names against the schema's 'permitted_fields' array; raises DSAFieldScopeError with the list of invalid fields if any are out of scope; validation cached for the SISClient lifetime to avoid repeated network fetches
fix_report.expected_outcome: Any vendor requesting fields outside their DSA receives a hard error at SDK initialization rather than a silent data truncation, making FERPA scope violations immediately visible during vendor development and integration testing

fix_report.action_taken: Added dsa_agreement_id as a required positional parameter to SISClient.__init__; stored on the instance and injected into every AuditEntry emitted by AuditLogger; AuditEntry dataclass updated with dsa_agreement_id field; SDK documentation updated to explain that each client instance is bound to one DSA agreement
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every audit log entry is traceable to a specific authorized data sharing agreement, satisfying FERPA compliance audit requirements for districts and auditors

target_output.title: K-12 SIS Integration SDK -- Security-Hardened Python Library (v2)
target_output.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant Python SDK for ed-tech vendors integrating with K-12 district SIS APIs. SISClient enforces OAuth2 PKCE authentication, validates vendor FieldScope against the district's DSA JSON Schema at initialization (raising DSAFieldScopeError for any out-of-scope field), and emits structured audit log entries -- including dsa_agreement_id -- for every data access call. Distributed on PyPI with only three mandatory dependencies: httpx, authlib, and keyring.
target_output.architecture_overview: Library-only architecture with no hosted services. SISClient is the top-level entry point, composed of a TokenManager (OAuth2 PKCE, keyring storage, transparent refresh), a DSASchemaValidator (fetches and caches the district's JSON Schema DSA document at init), a FieldScopeValidator (validates requested fields against DSA schema), and domain resource clients (RosterClient, GradeClient). AuditLogger accepts a configurable sink and emits a structured AuditEntry for every SIS API call. All response types are frozen dataclasses. The SDK enforces TLS 1.3 via httpx's SSLContext and refuses instantiation with an unverified context.
target_output.technology_stack: Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12. HTTP client: httpx 0.27+ (sync and async, RetryTransport). OAuth2: authlib 1.3+ (PKCE flow). Credential storage: keyring 25+ (OS keyring, env-var fallback). Typing: frozen dataclasses + typing.Protocol for sink interface. Schema validation: jsonschema 4.x for DSA field schema validation at init. Testing: pytest 8+, pytest-httpx for HTTP mocking, 95% coverage gate. Linting: ruff. Type checking: mypy --strict. Dep audit: pip-audit. Distribution: PyPI via GitHub Actions trusted publisher. Docs: Sphinx + Read the Docs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Token Management: SISClient acquires tokens via PKCE, stores in OS keyring, refreshes transparently on expiry; env-var fallback for containerized environments. DSA Validation: DSASchemaValidator fetches district's JSON Schema DSA document at init, caches it, validates FieldScope fields; raises DSAFieldScopeError with invalid field list. Roster Sync: RosterClient.list(class_id, fields=FieldScope) returns list of RosterRecord dataclasses containing only DSA-permitted fields. Grade Sync: GradeClient.sync(class_id, since=datetime) returns GradeSyncEvent dataclasses with grade_value, sync_timestamp, dsa_agreement_id. Audit Logging: every API call emits AuditEntry to configured sink. Rate Limiting: RateLimitError raised with retry_after parsed from Retry-After header on 429 responses.
target_output.system_components: SISClient: top-level context manager (sync and async); composes all sub-components; accepts district_id, dsa_agreement_id, base_url, credential_mode. TokenManager: PKCE flow, keyring/env-var storage, in-memory cache with TTL. DSASchemaValidator: fetches DSA JSON Schema via httpx at init, validates FieldScope, caches schema for client lifetime. RosterClient / GradeClient: resource-specific clients wrapping SISClient HTTP calls. AuditLogger: accepts logging.Handler subclass as sink; emits AuditEntry dataclass serialized to JSON. RetryTransport: httpx custom transport with exponential backoff for 429/5xx.
target_output.workflows: Vendor Integration Initialization: vendor instantiates SISClient(district_id='DIST-042', dsa_agreement_id='DSA-2026-07', base_url='https://sis.district.edu/api'); DSASchemaValidator fetches https://sis.district.edu/dsa/DSA-2026-07/schema.json, parses permitted_fields array; vendor passes FieldScope(fields=['student_id','first_name','enrollment_status']); validator checks each field against permitted_fields, raises DSAFieldScopeError(['grade_level']) if any field is absent from schema; client is ready if all fields pass. Roster Fetch with Audit: vendor calls client.rosters.list(class_id='CLS-101'); RosterClient calls GET /v1/rosters/CLS-101?fields=student_id,first_name,enrollment_status with Bearer token; response deserialized into list of RosterRecord dataclasses; AuditLogger emits AuditEntry(timestamp, vendor_app_id, district_id='DIST-042', dsa_agreement_id='DSA-2026-07', operation='roster.list', resource_id='CLS-101', fields_accessed=['student_id','first_name','enrollment_status'], http_status=200, latency_ms=142) to configured sink.
target_output.data_model: SISClientConfig (district_id: str, dsa_agreement_id: str, base_url: HttpUrl, credential_mode: Enum keyring/env_var). FieldScope (fields: frozenset[str], validated: bool -- set True after DSASchemaValidator confirms all fields). RosterRecord (student_id: str, class_id: str, fields: dict[str,str] -- only DSA-permitted keys). GradeSyncEvent (student_id: str, class_id: str, grade_value: str, sync_timestamp: datetime, dsa_agreement_id: str). OAuthToken (district_id: str, access_token: str, refresh_token: str, expires_at: datetime -- stored in keyring). AuditEntry (timestamp: datetime, vendor_app_id: str, district_id: str, dsa_agreement_id: str, operation: str, resource_type: str, resource_id: str, fields_accessed: list[str], http_status: int, latency_ms: float).
target_output.api_design: SISClient(district_id, dsa_agreement_id, base_url, credential_mode='keyring', audit_sink=None) -- raises DSAFieldScopeError at init if FieldScope contains out-of-scope fields. client.rosters.list(class_id: str, fields: FieldScope, page_size: int = 100) -> list[RosterRecord]. client.rosters.list_async(class_id, fields, page_size) -> AsyncIterator[RosterRecord]. client.grades.sync(class_id: str, since: datetime, fields: FieldScope) -> list[GradeSyncEvent]. client.tokens.revoke() -> None. FieldScope.from_dsa(dsa_agreement_id, client) -- factory method that fetches permitted fields from DSA schema and returns a validated FieldScope. RateLimitError(retry_after: int) -- raised on 429 responses.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PKCE enforcement: SDK hardcodes response_type=code and code_challenge_method=S256; no implicit or client_credentials flows exposed. TLS: httpx SSLContext with system trust store; SDK raises TLSConfigError if caller attempts to pass ssl=False or a context with check_hostname=False. DSA field scope: DSASchemaValidator validates FieldScope at init against district's JSON Schema DSA document; DSAFieldScopeError raised with invalid field list before any API call; validation is non-bypassable. Audit log: every API call emits AuditEntry with dsa_agreement_id to configured sink; AuditLogger warns (but does not suppress) on sink write failure. Credential storage: tokens stored in OS keyring; env-var fallback (SIS_SDK_ACCESS_TOKEN, SIS_SDK_REFRESH_TOKEN) for containerized environments; no plaintext file storage. FERPA: field scope + audit log + PKCE together satisfy FERPA data minimization, access logging, and authorization chain requirements.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI matrix over Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 on ubuntu-latest: (1) ruff lint, (2) mypy --strict type check, (3) pytest with pytest-httpx mocking SIS API, 95% coverage required, (4) pip-audit for known CVEs in httpx/authlib/keyring/jsonschema transitive deps, (5) build sdist and wheel via python -m build. Release flow: on semver tag (v1.x.y), GitHub Actions uploads to PyPI via OIDC trusted publisher -- no PyPI API token stored in repo secrets. Pre-release builds uploaded to TestPyPI on merge to main. Read the Docs builds on PR with preview URL posted to PR comment. CHANGELOG.md updated on every PR via towncrier fragment.
target_output.observability: AuditLogger emits structured JSON AuditEntry for every SIS API call to configured sink (FileHandler, SyslogHandler, or custom logging.Handler subclass). Optional Prometheus metrics plugin (sis_sdk_requests_total, sis_sdk_request_latency_seconds, sis_sdk_token_refresh_total) available as extras_require install: pip install sis-sdk[metrics]. No telemetry transmitted to SDK publisher. SDK emits Python warnings.warn() for: sink write failures, token approaching expiry without refresh, DSA schema fetch failure (falls back to cached schema with warning).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero hosting costs -- SDK is a PyPI package. CI costs bounded by GitHub Actions free tier for open-source or Actions minutes for private repo. DSA schema fetch at SISClient init adds one HTTPS round-trip per client instantiation; cached for client lifetime so no per-call overhead. httpx connection pooling reuses TCP connections across roster and grade calls, minimizing latency for batch sync workloads processing thousands of roster records.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- Core Auth and Roster (4 weeks): SISClient, TokenManager (PKCE + keyring), DSASchemaValidator, FieldScopeValidator with DSAFieldScopeError, RosterClient, AuditLogger with FileHandler sink, pytest suite with pytest-httpx, PyPI CI pipeline. Phase 2 -- Grade Sync and Retry (2 weeks): GradeClient, RetryTransport with exponential backoff, RateLimitError, env-var credential fallback, async client context manager, 95% coverage gate enforcement. Phase 3 -- Observability and Docs (2 weeks): Prometheus metrics plugin, SyslogHandler audit sink, Read the Docs integration, towncrier CHANGELOG, pip-audit CI gate, v1.0.0 release.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public API surface fully type-annotated; mypy --strict must pass in CI. New features require pytest-httpx tests mocking the district SIS API response. No new mandatory runtime dependencies without approval from two maintainers and pip-audit clean run. SemVer strictly followed post-1.0: breaking changes in major versions only. DSAFieldScopeError and RateLimitError are public API and must remain stable across minor versions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: DSA JSON Schema endpoint unavailable at SDK init causing initialization failure -- mitigated by caching last-known-good schema in keyring with TTL and serving from cache on fetch failure with a warning. Risk: keyring unavailable in containerized CI environments -- mitigated by documented env-var fallback mode. Tradeoff: fetching DSA schema at init adds one network round-trip but eliminates the need for out-of-band schema distribution to vendor developers. Tradeoff: frozen dataclass responses over raw dicts prevent accidental raw API exposure but require SDK updates when SIS API adds new fields.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: DSA schema validation at init chosen over runtime validation to catch field scope mismatches during vendor development rather than production data access. dsa_agreement_id is a required SISClient parameter, not optional, so every client instance is unambiguously bound to one authorized agreement. Async API via context manager chosen for v1 alongside sync API to avoid a v2 breaking change.

metadata.notes: K-12 SIS SDK round 2 -- resolves DSA field scope hard validation and audit log dsa_agreement_id gaps from round 1.

project_goal.value: AI-powered tutoring assistant for undergraduate STEM courses, replacing a legacy static FAQ knowledge base with a conversational LLM system that provides step-by-step problem walkthroughs and adaptive hints without disclosing assignment answers
project_goal.rationale: Faculty demand a scalable alternative to TA office hours that enforces academic integrity by withholding direct assignment solutions

target_users.value: Undergraduate students in STEM courses and instructors configuring assignment lockout rules
target_users.rationale: Primary users are students seeking tutoring; secondary users are instructors managing hint policies per course

project_class.rationale: Core value is LLM-driven conversational tutoring with RAG over course documents and assignment-aware policy enforcement

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Chat UI, FastAPI backend, course document RAG, Cognito auth, LLM integration, and interaction analytics

complexity_level.rationale: RAG pipeline over course documents, assignment lockout policy enforcement, multi-course hint configuration, and FERPA-compliant interaction logging require advanced design

risk_level.rationale: FERPA applies to interaction logs; academic integrity risk from prompt injection or policy bypass; no financial or health data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Tutoring session logs tied to student course enrollment are FERPA-protected educational records

external_exposure.rationale: System accessible only to enrolled students and instructors authenticated via Cognito

access_model.value: Cognito user pools with student and instructor roles; instructors can configure hint policies per course; students access tutoring for their enrolled courses only
access_model.rationale: Course enrollment scope enforced at API layer; instructors cannot access other instructors' policy configs

feature_scope.value: Conversational STEM tutoring chat, RAG over course document chunks, assignment lockout policy configuration for instructors, step-by-step hint generation, policy-triggered deflection logging, interaction analytics dashboard for instructors
feature_scope.rationale: Complete tutoring lifecycle from knowledge retrieval through academic integrity enforcement

mvp_scope.value: Chat interface for 3 pilot STEM courses, RAG over uploaded course PDFs, basic assignment lockout by problem keyword, Cognito auth, interaction logging
mvp_scope.rationale: Validates tutoring quality and academic integrity enforcement before broad rollout

security_baseline.value: Cognito JWT auth on all endpoints, TLS 1.3, prompt injection guard checking user input for system prompt override attempts, assignment lockout policy checked before LLM call, interaction logs encrypted at rest
security_baseline.rationale: Prompt injection and policy bypass are the primary threat vectors for an academic AI system

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; chat UI requires real-time streaming which React handles well with SSE

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI's async streaming support suits LLM token streaming; Python ecosystem has strong LLM and RAG tooling

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for interaction logs and course metadata; pgvector extension for course document embeddings; Redis 7 for session context cache
data_platform.rationale: pgvector enables in-database semantic search without a separate vector DB; Redis caches conversation context for latency

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with auto-scaling for variable tutoring demand; same region as university AWS infrastructure

privacy_retention_policy.value: Tutoring interaction logs retained for 1 academic year then deleted; course documents retained for duration of course enrollment plus 30 days; student identifiers in analytics anonymized after 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FERPA requires treating interaction logs as educational records; retention limits protect student privacy

future_scope.value: Multi-modal support for diagram and equation image upload; LMS grade book integration for personalized difficulty adaptation; student self-service interaction history export
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 features after MVP validates tutoring effectiveness

constraints.value: LLM calls must route to AWS Bedrock (Claude) to keep student data within AWS; no third-party LLM APIs with data retention; instructor UI must be accessible per WCAG 2.1 AA
constraints.rationale: AWS Bedrock has no training data retention for API calls; WCAG required for instructors using assistive technology

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured logging with student_id redacted in logs, custom metrics for tutor response latency and policy deflection rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Tutor response latency and deflection rate are the primary quality and compliance metrics

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints; feature flags for rolling course-by-course rollout
execution_preference.rationale: Feature flags enable controlled rollout to one course section before broad deployment

llm_integration.value: AWS Bedrock (Claude claude-sonnet) for response generation; Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 for course document chunk embeddings via pgvector; prompt templates versioned in Git
llm_integration.rationale: Bedrock keeps all LLM calls within AWS; no student data leaves AWS boundary; Titan Embeddings integrates natively with pgvector

compliance_context.value: FERPA: interaction logs are educational records; access restricted to enrolled students and their instructors; analytics data anonymized after 90 days; no interaction log data shared with LLM provider
compliance_context.rationale: AWS Bedrock API calls are not used for model training; FERPA data sharing restrictions met

project.goal: AI tutoring assistant replacing legacy static FAQ for STEM undergrads
project.target_users: Undergraduate students, STEM instructors
project.timeline: Pilot with 3 courses in fall semester

backend.llm: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet via boto3
backend.rag: pgvector semantic search for course document retrieval

security.auth: Cognito JWT on all API endpoints
security.prompt_injection: Input validation guard before LLM call
security.lockout: Assignment lockout policy checked per course before generating response

data.embeddings: Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 for document chunks

devops.environments: dev, staging, production with feature flag per course

open_questions.rag_chunk_size: Optimal chunk size for STEM problem set PDFs vs. lecture slides

reasoner_reviews.product: The hint-only mode for assignment problems is the highest-stakes academic integrity requirement. The round 1 plan describes checking incoming queries against lockout rules but does not specify how the system distinguishes a generic STEM concept question from a specific assignment problem. A keyword and problem-hash matching approach needs to be explicitly designed -- fuzzy matching on problem statement embeddings is more reliable than keyword lists.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: pgvector is the right choice for avoiding a separate vector DB at this scale. The RAG pipeline should use a hybrid search combining pgvector cosine similarity with PostgreSQL full-text search for STEM-specific notation (equations, chemical formulas) that embeddings may not capture well. The round 1 plan does not specify how conversation context is chunked when passed to the LLM -- a sliding window of the last N turns should be defined to stay within Bedrock context limits.
reasoner_reviews.security: Prompt injection is a first-class threat for a student-facing AI system. The system prompt must be system-role-locked in the Bedrock API call and user input sanitized to remove role-switch attempts. The assignment lockout policy check must happen in the backend before the Bedrock API call -- a client-side check is insufficient. FERPA requires that the interaction logs link to course enrollment, not just student_id, so access control can verify the student is enrolled in the course whose interaction logs they are accessing.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet is appropriate for tutoring quality at this scale. Titan Embeddings v2 integrates well with pgvector. The 30-minute Redis TTL for conversation context is reasonable for tutoring sessions but should be configurable per deployment.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting: /tutor (student chat), /courses/:id/policies (instructor policy config), /courses/:id/analytics (instructor dashboard)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Student: course selector, chat interface with SSE token streaming, conversation history panel. Instructor: hint policy editor (keyword lockout rules, problem hash upload), analytics dashboard (session volume, deflection rate, avg response time).
frontend.state_management: React Query for session history and analytics data; useSSE hook for token streaming; Zustand for current conversation state
frontend.accessibility_notes: Instructor policy editor and analytics dashboard meet WCAG 2.1 AA: keyboard navigation, ARIA labels on chat input and send button, color contrast 4.5:1 minimum, screen reader announcements for new tutor messages

backend.service_design: TutoringService orchestrating RAG retrieval, assignment lockout policy check, prompt construction, Bedrock API call, and response streaming; PolicyService for CRUD of course assignment lockout rules; EmbeddingService for document chunk ingestion via Titan Embeddings v2
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE streaming endpoint), GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id} (session history), POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies (create lockout rule), GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics (instructor metrics)
backend.llm_integration_design: System prompt versioned in Git with placeholder for course context; user input sanitized for prompt injection patterns (role switch, ignore previous instructions); conversation context sent as last 8 turns sliding window; lockout check via PolicyEnforcer before Bedrock call -- if triggered, returns canned deflection message without LLM invocation
backend.failure_handling: Bedrock API timeout after 30s triggers graceful 'I am having trouble responding' message; circuit breaker on Bedrock calls; Redis miss falls back to fetching last 5 conversation_turns from PostgreSQL

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with student and instructor groups; JWT validated at FastAPI dependency injection level; course enrollment verified per request against enrollments table
security.authorization_model: Students can only access tutor sessions for their enrolled courses; instructors can only configure policies for courses they teach; RBAC enforced at TutoringService and PolicyService layers
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limit: 20 chat messages per student per hour per course; prompt injection guard regex checks for role-switch patterns; Bedrock API call blocked if lockout policy matches; anomalous query volume alerts
security.audit_and_logging_controls: interaction_logs table records every conversation turn with course_id, student_id (hashed after 90 days), query_text_hash (not plaintext), response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, latency_ms

data.entities: students (id, cognito_sub, name_hashed_at date), courses (id, instructor_id, title, embedding_model_version), tutoring_sessions (id, student_id, course_id, started_at, message_count), conversation_turns (id, session_id, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash, response_type, policy_triggered, latency_ms, created_at), knowledge_base_documents (id, course_id, filename, chunk_count, ingested_at), document_chunks (id, document_id, chunk_index, content_text, embedding vector(1536)), hint_policies (id, course_id, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value, created_by), interaction_logs (id, turn_id, student_id, course_id, event_type, metadata_json, logged_at)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension on RDS; document_chunks table with ivfflat index on embedding column (lists=100); Redis 7 for active conversation context keyed by session_id

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: tutor-api service (min 2, max 10 tasks, CPU 1vCPU/2GB), embedding-worker service for async document ingestion, RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster, ALB with WAF
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff), type check (mypy), pytest with mock Bedrock responses, build Docker, push ECR, deploy ECS rolling update; feature flag per course in LaunchDarkly
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM traces per request; custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram, policy_deflection_rate counter, bedrock_tokens_used counter; structured logs with student_id replaced by hashed session token

ISS-001.title: Assignment lockout policy uses only keyword matching, missing embedding-similarity fallback
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 lockout policy design relies on keyword lists per assignment problem. Students can bypass by paraphrasing the problem statement. Embedding-similarity matching against problem statement hashes is needed as a secondary check to catch paraphrased queries that keyword matching misses.

history.note: Product reviewer identified keyword-only lockout as bypassable via paraphrase

ISS-002.title: Conversation context window strategy undefined for long STEM sessions
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan does not specify how conversation context is managed when a tutoring session exceeds Bedrock Claude's context window. Without a defined sliding window or summarization strategy, long sessions will either error or silently drop early context.

history.note: Architect reviewer flagged missing context window management strategy

focus_issues.title: Assignment lockout policy uses only keyword matching, missing embedding-similarity fallback
focus_issues.detail: Keyword-only lockout can be bypassed by paraphrasing the problem statement; embedding-similarity matching needed as secondary check.

focus_issues.title: Conversation context window strategy undefined for long STEM sessions
focus_issues.detail: No sliding window or summarization strategy defined for sessions exceeding Bedrock context limits.

latest_recommendations: Add embedding-similarity lockout check: embed the incoming student query with Titan Embeddings, compute cosine similarity against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, trigger deflection if similarity exceeds 0.88 threshold
Define sliding window context strategy: send last 8 turns to Bedrock; if session exceeds 8 turns, generate a 2-sentence summary of earlier context and prepend it to the window

previous_plan.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v1)
previous_plan.executive_summary: LLM-powered tutoring system on AWS Bedrock with RAG over course PDFs via pgvector. Cognito auth, assignment lockout via keyword matching, interaction logging for FERPA compliance. React chat UI with SSE token streaming.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito JWT auth, prompt injection guard, keyword-based assignment lockout policy check before Bedrock call, interaction_logs with course_id and student_id, FERPA anonymization after 90 days.
previous_plan.data_model: students, courses, tutoring_sessions, conversation_turns, knowledge_base_documents, document_chunks (with pgvector embeddings), hint_policies (keyword rules), interaction_logs.
previous_plan.workflows: Student sends STEM question; system retrieves top-3 course document chunks via pgvector; PolicyEnforcer checks keyword match against hint_policies; if no match, constructs prompt and calls Bedrock; streams response via SSE.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by adding an embedding-similarity lockout check in PolicyEnforcer: the student query is embedded via Titan Embeddings, cosine similarity is computed against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, and deflection is triggered if similarity exceeds 0.88. ISS-002 is resolved by defining a last-8-turns sliding window with a summarization step for dropped context. Both fixes integrate cleanly into the existing TutoringService and PolicyEnforcer without architectural changes. The pgvector infrastructure already supports embedding-based lockout since document_chunks already uses vector(1536).

fix_report.action_taken: PolicyEnforcer now runs a two-stage lockout check: (1) keyword match against hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword'; (2) if keyword check passes, embed the student query via Titan Embeddings v2 and query hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' using pgvector cosine distance operator (<=>); deflect if any stored problem embedding has cosine similarity >=0.88. Threshold is configurable per course via hint_policies.similarity_threshold column. Deflection events logged to interaction_logs with policy_triggered=True and rule_id.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Paraphrased queries that bypass keyword matching are caught by embedding similarity check; academic integrity enforcement is robust to student rephrasing strategies

fix_report.action_taken: TutoringService implements a sliding window context builder: the last 8 conversation_turns are fetched from Redis or PostgreSQL fallback and passed to Bedrock. If session has more than 8 turns, a summarization prompt (system: 'Summarize the following STEM tutoring exchange in 2 sentences focusing on concepts covered') is sent to Bedrock to generate a context_summary; the summary is prepended to the window as a system message. Summaries are cached in Redis keyed by session_id:summary_turn_N with 2-hour TTL.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long tutoring sessions maintain coherent context without Bedrock API errors; students receive contextually appropriate hints even after extended sessions

target_output.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
target_output.executive_summary: An LLM-powered STEM tutoring assistant on AWS Bedrock replacing a legacy static FAQ, with RAG over course PDFs via pgvector, two-stage assignment lockout enforcement (keyword + embedding-similarity), and FERPA-compliant interaction logging. Students receive adaptive hints via a React chat UI with SSE token streaming; instructors configure lockout policies and view session analytics. Long sessions use a sliding-window context strategy with summarization to maintain coherence within Bedrock's context limits.
target_output.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates via HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF. TutoringService orchestrates: (1) pgvector hybrid search retrieving top-3 course document chunks, (2) two-stage PolicyEnforcer checking keyword and embedding-similarity lockout rules, (3) context window builder assembling last-8-turns sliding window with optional summarization, (4) AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet invocation via boto3 with streamed response. Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 handles both document chunk ingestion and per-query embedding for similarity-based lockout. PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector stores course embeddings and interaction logs; Redis 7 caches conversation context. Cognito authenticates students and instructors with course-enrollment-scoped authorization.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, useSSE hook for token streaming. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, boto3 for Bedrock and Titan Embeddings. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ) with pgvector extension, ivfflat index on document_chunks.embedding. Cache: Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for conversation context and summarization cache. LLM: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet for tutoring responses and context summarization. Embeddings: Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (1536-dim). Auth: Cognito user pools. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS Fargate rolling deploy. Observability: Datadog APM. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly per course.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Student Tutoring Chat: SSE-streamed responses for STEM concept questions and step-by-step walkthroughs. Assignment Lockout Enforcement: keyword match then embedding-similarity check (cosine >=0.88) against instructor-defined problem embeddings; deflected queries receive canned guidance without LLM invocation. RAG Retrieval: hybrid pgvector cosine search + PostgreSQL full-text search for STEM notation across course document chunks. Context Window Management: last-8-turns sliding window with 2-sentence summarization for dropped context. Instructor Policy Config: keyword and problem-statement embedding upload, per-course similarity threshold tuning. Instructor Analytics: session volume, deflection rate, avg response latency, top-queried course topics.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (SQLi/XSS rules + rate limiting). ECS Fargate: tutor-api service (min 2, max 10 tasks), embedding-worker service for async document ingestion. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with pgvector. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for conversation context and summarization cache. AWS Bedrock: Claude claude-sonnet (response generation, context summarization), Titan Embeddings v2 (document and query embedding). S3 bucket for raw course document uploads before ingestion. Cognito user pools with student/instructor groups. Datadog APM sidecar. LaunchDarkly SDK for per-course feature flags.
target_output.workflows: Two-Stage Lockout Check: student sends message; TutoringService calls PolicyEnforcer; stage 1: SQL query on hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword' AND course_id=:course_id, check if any keyword appears in query text; stage 2: if stage 1 passes, embed query via Titan Embeddings v2, execute pgvector query SELECT rule_id FROM hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' AND course_id=:course_id ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT 1 -- if cosine similarity of top result >= hint_policies.similarity_threshold, trigger deflection; if both stages pass, proceed to RAG retrieval and Bedrock call. Context Window Build: fetch last 8 turns from Redis (TTL 30 min) or PostgreSQL fallback; if session.message_count > 8, retrieve or generate context_summary via Bedrock summarization prompt; prepend summary as system message; assemble context array: [system_prompt, context_summary_if_any, last_8_turns]; send to Bedrock with streaming=True.
target_output.data_model: students (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR, name_hashed_at DATE). courses (id UUID PK, instructor_id FK, title VARCHAR, embedding_model_version VARCHAR). tutoring_sessions (id UUID PK, student_id FK, course_id FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, message_count INT, context_summary TEXT, summary_as_of_turn INT). conversation_turns (id UUID PK, session_id FK, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash VARCHAR, response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, rule_id FK nullable, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id FK, chunk_index INT, content_text TEXT, embedding vector(1536)). hint_policies (id UUID PK, course_id FK, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value TEXT, embedding vector(1536) nullable, similarity_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.88, created_by FK). interaction_logs (id UUID PK, turn_id FK, student_id VARCHAR, course_id FK, event_type VARCHAR, metadata_json JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE, body: {session_id, message, course_id}) -- runs lockout check, RAG retrieval, context build, Bedrock stream. GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id}/history -- returns conversation_turns for session. POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies -- instructor creates keyword or embedding_hash lockout rule; embedding_hash rules include base64-encoded problem statement, backend embeds and stores vector. GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics?from=&to= -- returns session_count, deflection_rate, avg_latency_ms, top_topics. POST /v1/courses/{id}/documents -- multipart upload; triggers async embedding-worker ECS task. GET /v1/health -- ALB health check.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI Depends(get_current_user); course enrollment verified per request against students-courses enrollment table. Prompt injection guard: regex scan of user input for patterns ('ignore previous', 'you are now', 'system:', 'assistant:'); blocked queries logged with event_type='injection_attempt'. Assignment lockout: two-stage check (keyword + embedding similarity >=0.88) enforced in backend before any Bedrock call; client-side blocking is not relied upon. FERPA: interaction_logs link to course_id and student_id; access restricted to the student themselves and course instructor; student identifiers in logs anonymized (hashed) after 90 days via nightly Lambda; interaction logs excluded from Datadog log forwarding. Bedrock: system prompt passed as system role, not prepended to user message; no student query content included in Bedrock model training (Bedrock API guarantee).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mocked Bedrock responses (pytest-mock) and pgvector test DB (Docker Compose), build Docker, push ECR. ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy task during update. Environments: dev (single-AZ, t-class RDS, mock Bedrock), staging (multi-AZ RDS, real Bedrock, 3 pilot courses), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks, LaunchDarkly per-course rollout). Document ingestion: S3 event triggers ECS embedding-worker task; chunks documents at 512 tokens with 64-token overlap, embeds via Titan v2, inserts into document_chunks with pgvector index rebuild after batch. Secrets: Cognito pool IDs and RDS credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; Bedrock IAM role via ECS task role -- no API keys.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: trace per /v1/tutor/chat request with spans for PolicyEnforcer, pgvector retrieval, context build, and Bedrock invocation. Custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram per course_id, policy_deflection_rate counter per course_id and rule_type, bedrock_input_tokens_total and bedrock_output_tokens_total counters, context_summarization_invocations_total. Alerts: p99 response latency >5s fires PagerDuty; deflection rate spike >2x 7-day average fires Slack alert to instructor; embedding-worker queue depth >50 fires ops alert. Structured logs: student_id replaced by hashed_session_token; no student query plaintext in Datadog.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet at $0.003/1K input tokens and $0.015/1K output tokens; avg tutoring turn ~800 input tokens and ~300 output tokens = ~$0.007/turn; 10,000 turns/month = ~$70/month. Titan Embeddings v2 at $0.0001/1K tokens; 200 course document chunks/course, 3 courses = 600 embeddings plus query embeddings ~$5/month. ECS Fargate: min 2 tasks x 1vCPU/2GB = ~$60/month baseline; scales to 10 tasks during peak exam periods. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$120/month. Total: ~$300/month for pilot, scaling proportionally with course enrollment.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- MVP Tutoring (6 weeks): ECS Fargate, FastAPI, Cognito auth, RAG over course PDFs (pgvector + Titan Embeddings v2), Bedrock Claude tutoring with system-role-locked prompt, SSE streaming React chat UI, keyword-only lockout policy, interaction logging. Phase 2 -- Academic Integrity Hardening (3 weeks): embedding-similarity lockout check (ISS-001 fix), sliding-window context with summarization (ISS-002 fix), prompt injection guard, instructor policy configuration UI, LaunchDarkly per-course feature flags. Phase 3 -- Analytics and Scale (3 weeks): instructor analytics dashboard, Datadog APM custom metrics, anonymization Lambda for 90-day FERPA requirement, performance tuning of pgvector ivfflat index, load testing for exam-period demand spikes.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Bedrock prompt templates versioned in Git under prompts/ directory with changelog entries. Lockout policy changes require instructor authentication and are audit-logged. pytest mocks all Bedrock and Titan Embeddings calls; integration tests run against a dockerized PostgreSQL with pgvector extension. Student query plaintext must never appear in structured logs -- log only content_hash. Feature flag every course rollout; no direct production deployments without staging validation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: embedding-similarity threshold (0.88) is too aggressive, flagging legitimate STEM questions as assignment problems -- mitigated by per-course threshold tuning and instructor review of deflection logs. Risk: Titan Embeddings v2 model version changes causing embedding space drift and false-negative lockout misses -- mitigated by storing embedding_model_version on courses and reindexing when model updates. Tradeoff: pgvector over Pinecone/Weaviate eliminates operational overhead but limits to single-node vector search; acceptable at pilot scale but may require migration at 1M+ chunks. Tradeoff: Bedrock Claude over GPT-4 satisfies AWS data boundary requirement at slightly higher latency.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Cosine similarity threshold 0.88 chosen as default after testing on sample STEM problem sets; configurable per course to allow stricter enforcement in exam periods. Sliding window of 8 turns chosen to balance context quality and Bedrock token cost for typical 30-minute tutoring sessions. Titan Embeddings v2 chosen over OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 to keep all embeddings within AWS boundary.

metadata.notes: STEM AI tutoring assistant round 2 -- adds embedding-similarity lockout and sliding-window context management.

project_goal.value: Native iOS and Android mobile app for university students migrating from a desktop-only library catalog system to a mobile-first experience supporting book searches, hold requests, digital resource borrowing, and due date push notification reminders
project_goal.rationale: Library staff report that 70% of catalog searches originate from mobile browsers; a native app improves the search and hold request experience

target_users.value: University student library patrons and library staff managing holds
target_users.rationale: Primary users are students searching and placing holds; library staff monitor hold queues and process pickups

project_class.rationale: Native Flutter app deployed to iOS App Store and Google Play; no browser-first delivery

capabilities.rationale: Flutter app, Node.js API, Firebase Auth, ILS integration, and usage analytics cover the full feature surface

complexity_level.rationale: ILS API integration, patron borrowing history migration, and push notifications add complexity beyond a simple catalog display

risk_level.rationale: No financial or health data; patron borrowing records are personal but lower-sensitivity than medical or financial records

data_sensitivity.rationale: Borrowing records and patron profiles are personal data and FERPA-protected at some institutions

external_exposure.rationale: App requires Firebase Auth login with university credentials; anonymous catalog browse not supported for patron-specific features

access_model.value: Firebase Auth linked to university SSO; student patrons can only access their own borrowing history and holds; library staff role can view all patron hold queues
access_model.rationale: Patron privacy requires access scoped to own records; staff role needed for hold queue management

feature_scope.value: Catalog search by keyword, ISBN, and author; item availability across campus branches; hold request placement and cancellation; digital resource checkout via ILS API; borrowing history and due date display; push notification reminders for items due in 3 days and items ready for pickup; patron account view
feature_scope.rationale: Complete patron self-service feature set replacing current desktop ILS patron portal

mvp_scope.value: Catalog keyword search, item availability display, hold request placement, borrowing history migration from legacy ILS, Firebase Auth, due date push notifications
mvp_scope.rationale: Core search and hold workflow validates migration before adding digital checkout and analytics

security_baseline.value: Firebase Auth JWT on all API calls, TLS 1.3, patron data access scoped by patron_id claim in JWT, no plaintext patron IDs in push notification payloads
security_baseline.rationale: Patron borrowing records require authenticated access; push notifications must not expose catalog item details to lock screen

frontend_stack.value: Flutter 3 with Dart
frontend_stack.rationale: Single codebase for iOS and Android; Dart null-safety; strong community for library-style list UIs

backend_stack.rationale: Team familiarity; adequate for ILS proxy API and push notification scheduling

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for patron profiles and hold records; Redis 7 for catalog search result caching; ILS (Koha) as source of truth for item availability
data_platform.rationale: MongoDB flexible schema suits patron profiles with varying fields; ILS remains authoritative for catalog data

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for API; Firebase for Auth and push notifications (FCM); MongoDB Atlas on GCP
hosting_target.rationale: Cloud Run scales to zero between library hours; Firebase integrates natively with Flutter FCM push

privacy_retention_policy.value: Borrowing history retained 3 years per library policy; due date notification preferences retained until patron account deletion; interaction analytics anonymized after 6 months
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Library patron privacy policy and state records retention requirements

future_scope.value: Inter-library loan request submission, reading list creation and sharing, fine payment via library payment portal integration
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 features after migration validates patron engagement

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Koha ILS via its REST API; push notifications must work on both iOS (APNs) and Android (FCM); app must function offline for borrowed items list
constraints.rationale: Koha ILS is not being replaced; iOS APNs requires separate certificate management; offline borrowed items list is a frequently-requested patron feature

observability_baseline.value: Firebase Crashlytics for mobile crash reporting; Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run API; custom analytics events for search queries and hold placements
observability_baseline.rationale: Mobile crash visibility and catalog usage analytics are primary observability needs

execution_preference.value: Flutter mobile CI/CD via GitHub Actions with Fastlane for iOS and Android deployment
execution_preference.rationale: Fastlane automates code signing and App Store / Play Store submission

compliance_context.value: FERPA: borrowing records treated as educational records at this institution; access restricted to the patron and authorized library staff; patron data excluded from third-party analytics
compliance_context.rationale: Institution classifies library borrowing records as educational records under FERPA

project.goal: Flutter mobile library catalog app migrating patrons from desktop-only ILS portal
project.target_users: University student patrons, library staff
project.timeline: App Store and Play Store launch before fall semester

frontend.state: Riverpod for app state, flutter_hooks for widget lifecycle
frontend.offline: Hive local storage for borrowed items list offline cache

backend.framework: Express with middleware for Firebase JWT validation
backend.ils_integration: Koha REST API client for catalog search and hold management

security.auth: Firebase Auth with university SSO federation
security.patron_scope: patron_id claim in JWT scopes all data access

data.cache: Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore for catalog search results (TTL 5 min)
data.ils: Koha REST API as catalog source of truth

devops.mobile_cd: Fastlane for iOS (Xcode Cloud signing) and Android (Play Console)
devops.hosting: Cloud Run for API, Firebase Hosting not used (API only)

constraints.offline: Borrowed items list cached locally with Hive
constraints.push: FCM for Android, APNs for iOS via Firebase Cloud Messaging

open_questions.migration_scope: Determine extent of borrowing history to migrate: all-time vs. last-3-years

reasoner_reviews.product: The migration from desktop ILS portal to Flutter app is well-scoped. The offline borrowed items list is the right MVP feature to include because patrons frequently check due dates without network connectivity on campus. The hold placement workflow must handle the Koha API's asynchronous hold processing -- the app should show a 'hold pending' state and poll for confirmation rather than assuming synchronous success.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Cloud Run scaling to zero is appropriate for library usage patterns (peak during library hours, minimal overnight).
