Key decisions: Click command groups provide composable, independently testable migration phases. SQLite WAL mode enables crash-safe resumable batches. OS keychain credential storage keeps FERPA-protected student data off disk. Microsoft Graph JSON batching at 20 accounts per envelope is critical to stay within rate limits while achieving acceptable migration throughput.
target_output.title: K-12 District Google Workspace to Microsoft 365 Migration CLI
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Click-based CLI tool for K-12 district IT administrators to migrate 15,000 student and staff Google Workspace accounts to Microsoft 365 Education. Provides dry-run simulation, resumable batch state in SQLite, exponential backoff against Google Admin SDK and Microsoft Graph API rate limits, and FERPA-compliant credential handling via OS keychain. Command groups for account creation, group mapping, calendar migration, and email forwarding enable phased district-wide migration with rollback checkpoints.
target_output.architecture_overview: Local CLI application structured as Click command groups sharing a SQLite state manager and API client factories. The migrate users command creates Microsoft 365 accounts via Graph JSON batching at 20 accounts per batch call, tracking each account status in SQLite for resumability across interruptions. Group migration maps Google Groups to Microsoft 365 Groups by name normalization with a YAML override file for unmatched groups. Calendar migration streams Google Calendar events via paginated list and posts to Microsoft Graph calendar API. All API calls wrapped in exponential backoff with jitter. OAuth2 tokens for Google Admin SDK and Microsoft Graph stored in OS keychain via keyring and refreshed transparently by SDK clients.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. CLI framework: Click 8.x with command groups and context passing for dry-run state. Terminal output: Rich library for progress bars, tables, and live display. API clients: google-api-python-client for Admin SDK and Calendar API; msgraph-sdk for Microsoft Graph. Auth: keyring library for OS keychain token storage; msal for Microsoft OAuth2 PKCE flow. Local state: SQLite 3 via Python stdlib sqlite3 with WAL mode enabled. Distribution: pip and pipx installable wheel from PyPI. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with pytest on Windows and Ubuntu matrix runners.
target_output.functional_feature_map: auth login-google and login-microsoft: OAuth2 browser-based authorization storing refresh tokens in OS keychain. auth status: validates token expiry without exposing token values. migrate users --csv FILE --batch-size INT --dry-run: reads user CSV, creates Microsoft 365 accounts via Graph batch API, tracks status in SQLite per user. migrate groups --dry-run: maps Google Groups to Microsoft 365 Groups with conflict detection and YAML override support. migrate calendar --user-email EMAIL: migrates Google Calendar events to Exchange calendar. migrate forwarding: sets SMTP forwarding from Google Workspace addresses to new Microsoft 365 UPNs. report summary: prints batch success/failure counts with duration. report errors: tabulates failed accounts with API error codes and retry counts. rollback users --to-checkpoint NAME: deletes Microsoft 365 accounts created after named checkpoint.
target_output.system_components: CLI entry point registered as 'district-migrator' in PATH by pip. Click application object with five subcommand groups: auth, migrate, report, rollback, validate. SQLite state manager class opening database with WAL mode and providing parameterized CRUD operations. Google API client factory building Admin SDK DirectoryService and CalendarService from keychain refresh token. Microsoft Graph client factory building GraphServiceClient from MSAL keychain token. Backoff decorator applying exponential retry with jitter on HTTP 429 and 503 responses. Dry-run interceptor checking Click context flag before any mutating API call. Rich Live display for streaming progress during long-running batches.
target_output.workflows: Bulk User Migration Workflow: IT admin runs 'district-migrator migrate users --csv students_2026.csv --dry-run'. Tool reads CSV rows, queries Microsoft Graph GET /users?$filter=mail eq EMAIL for each to detect existing account conflicts, prints Rich table listing 124 conflicts found, exits without writes. Admin resolves conflicts, reruns without --dry-run. Tool creates Microsoft 365 accounts in batches of 20 via Graph POST /$batch, writes each result to user_accounts SQLite table with status succeeded or failed. On completion prints Rich summary: 14,876 succeeded, 124 failed, 0 skipped. Failed accounts retried up to 3 times with backoff. Group Mapping Workflow: Admin runs 'district-migrator migrate groups'. Tool lists all Google Groups via Admin SDK paginated GET, normalizes each name by stripping non-alphanumeric characters and truncating to Microsoft 255-char limit. Unmatched groups are flagged in stdout and SQLite group_mappings table with status=conflict. Admin creates groups_override.yaml with manual mappings. Reruns command with --override-file flag; matched groups created in Microsoft 365 and members added from Google membership lists.
target_output.data_model: migration_batches (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, batch_name TEXT NOT NULL, operation TEXT ENUM users/groups/calendar/forwarding, started_at TEXT, completed_at TEXT, total INTEGER, succeeded INTEGER, failed INTEGER, skipped INTEGER). user_accounts (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, batch_id INTEGER REFERENCES migration_batches, google_user_id TEXT, google_email TEXT NOT NULL, ms365_upn TEXT, display_name TEXT, department TEXT, job_title TEXT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending', error_message TEXT, ms365_object_id TEXT, created_ms365_at TEXT, attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0, INDEX on batch_id+status). group_mappings (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, batch_id INTEGER FK, google_group_email TEXT, ms365_group_id TEXT, display_name TEXT, member_count INTEGER, status TEXT, manual_override INTEGER DEFAULT 0). rollback_checkpoints (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, batch_id INTEGER FK, checkpoint_name TEXT UNIQUE, state_snapshot TEXT, created_at TEXT). error_logs (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, user_id INTEGER REFERENCES user_accounts, operation TEXT, http_status INTEGER, api_error_code TEXT, stack_trace TEXT, recorded_at TEXT).
target_output.api_design: No REST API — CLI commands are the interface. Key external API interactions: Google Admin SDK GET /admin/directory/v1/users?domain=DOMAIN&maxResults=500 for paginated user listing. Google Admin SDK GET /admin/directory/v1/groups for group listing. Microsoft Graph POST /$batch with up to 20-request JSON body for bulk account creation. Microsoft Graph GET /users?$filter=mail eq 'EMAIL' for pre-migration conflict detection. Microsoft Graph POST /groups/{id}/members for group membership. Google Calendar API GET /calendars/primary/events?pageToken=TOKEN for event pagination. Microsoft Graph POST /users/{id}/calendar/events for calendar import. Error handling: HTTP 429 triggers backoff decorator; HTTP 409 on Graph user creation logs conflict to SQLite user_accounts table.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA compliance: student account data stored only in SQLite user_accounts and migration_batches tables with no email body content or calendar event details. SQLite file created with os.chmod(path, 0o600) on POSIX; Windows subprocess call to icacls restricts file to current user SID only. OAuth2 refresh tokens stored in OS keychain under named service keys via keyring — never written to config files, .env, or command-line history. Dry-run flag enforced by interceptor decorator at API call layer. OAuth2 client_id for the registered Azure AD and Google OAuth apps stored in package — delegated user auth only, no client_secret shipped. Audit trail: all migration operations including failures logged to SQLite error_logs with ISO 8601 timestamp for FERPA chain-of-custody documentation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: 'pip install district-migrator' or 'pipx install district-migrator' registers CLI entry point. pyproject.toml specifies python>=3.12, Click>=8.1, rich>=13.0, google-api-python-client>=2.120, msgraph-sdk>=1.5, keyring>=25.0, msal>=1.28. GitHub Actions CI: matrix test on Python 3.12 ubuntu-latest and windows-latest, pytest --cov with 80% coverage gate, mypy strict mode, ruff linting. Release: tag-triggered workflow builds wheel via python -m build and publishes to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing. No containers, no cloud resources, no services — fully local execution on IT admin workstations.
target_output.observability: Rich Live display during migrate users: accounts processed / total, current throughput in accounts/min, estimated time remaining based on rolling average, current batch window number. Rich table at completion: succeeded, failed, skipped counts with total duration and average throughput. SQLite error_logs table queryable via 'report errors' command rendering Rich table with columns: google_email, operation, http_status, api_error_code, attempts. 'report summary' command renders per-batch statistics across all historical migration runs. SIGINT handler prints partial batch summary to stdout and flushes SQLite WAL before exit.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No infrastructure cost — entirely local CLI tool with no cloud services. Python package installed footprint approximately 80 MB including all dependencies. SQLite state file grows to approximately 15 MB for 15,000 accounts with full error log history. At 20-account Microsoft Graph batches with 200ms average round-trip: 750 batch calls for 15,000 accounts taking approximately 38 minutes for account creation phase at steady state. Google Admin SDK user listing for 15,000 accounts requires 30 paginated calls at 500 users/page taking under 2 minutes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): auth command group with Google and Microsoft OAuth2 PKCE flows and keychain storage, migrate users command with dry-run mode and live mode with Graph JSON batching, SQLite schema with WAL mode and state manager class, Rich progress display, GitHub Actions CI on Windows and Ubuntu matrix runners. Phase 2 (3 weeks): migrate groups command with name normalization and YAML override file, rollback users command using checkpoint snapshots, report summary and report errors commands, retry queue processing for failed user accounts. Phase 3 (3 weeks): migrate calendar command for Google-to-Exchange event migration, migrate forwarding command for SMTP forwarding setup via Google Admin SDK, automated post-migration validation comparing Google and Microsoft account counts and group membership parity.
target_output.development_guidelines: Click commands must check context.obj.dry_run before any mutating API call — enforced by a @dry_run_guard decorator, not ad-hoc conditionals. All Google and Microsoft API calls go through the @backoff_retry decorated client methods; no direct SDK calls in command handlers. SQLite queries use parameterized statements exclusively — no string formatting with user data. Test suite uses responses library to mock Google Admin SDK and Microsoft Graph HTTP calls with recorded real API response fixture files. mypy strict mode enabled; all public functions and return types annotated. CLI output in --json mode must be machine-parseable for IT scripting integration.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Microsoft 365 account conflicts for students in a pilot group already migrated manually. Mitigation: dry-run mode checks each email against Graph filter query before any batch creation run, reporting conflicts in a Rich table for admin resolution. Risk: Google Group names contain characters exceeding Microsoft 365 Group name constraints (255 chars, no special characters). Mitigation: normalization function applied before conflict detection; YAML override file provides manual mapping for non-normalizable names. Risk: long-running migration batch interrupted mid-way. Mitigation: SQLite WAL mode ensures atomic writes; resume logic on startup queries user_accounts WHERE status='pending' skipping already-succeeded accounts. Tradeoff: delegated OAuth2 requires IT admin to interactively authenticate in browser on first run on each workstation — simpler and more auditable than service account credential files but requires admin presence.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Click over Typer for mature composable command group patterns and better Windows terminal compatibility. Chose keyring over encrypted config file for credential storage because OS keychain is audited and survives tool upgrades without re-encryption. Decided to use Microsoft Graph JSON batching rather than sequential calls to achieve acceptable throughput within 10,000 req/10min rate limit for 15,000 accounts. Calendar migration scoped to primary calendar only in MVP — shared and resource calendars deferred to Phase 3.

metadata.notes: K-12 district Google Workspace to Microsoft 365 migration CLI with FERPA-compliant credential handling and resumable batch state tracking.

project_goal.value: Cost-optimized static landing page for a nonprofit online tutoring organization connecting low-income K-12 students with volunteer tutors, minimizing hosting costs while absorbing back-to-school traffic spikes
project_goal.rationale: Organization has near-zero hosting budget; CDN-first static approach eliminates server costs entirely

target_users.value: Low-income K-12 families seeking tutoring, prospective volunteer tutors, donor organizations evaluating the program
target_users.rationale: Three distinct visitor segments with different conversion goals on the same landing page

project_class.rationale: Static informational page with no server-side runtime; no user accounts or authenticated features

capabilities.rationale: Static rendering with analytics event tracking; no backend runtime needed

complexity_level.rationale: Static site with CDN delivery and a lightweight Cloudflare Worker for form handling

risk_level.rationale: No user authentication, no database, no payment processing; only public informational content

data_sensitivity.rationale: Landing page collects only volunteer interest form submissions forwarded to Google Sheets; no PII stored on infrastructure

external_exposure.rationale: Public marketing site accessible to all visitors without authentication

access_model.value: No authentication; fully public read-only content
access_model.rationale: Landing page has no login or restricted content

feature_scope.value: Program overview, tutoring subjects by grade level, volunteer tutor profiles, service regions map, testimonials from families, volunteer interest form, donor information section
feature_scope.rationale: Core content sections covering all three visitor segments

mvp_scope.value: Program overview and subject listings, volunteer interest form with Cloudflare Worker forwarding to Google Sheets, basic analytics tracking
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed to launch before back-to-school season

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare; Content-Security-Policy and X-Frame-Options headers; no user data stored on infrastructure
security_baseline.rationale: Standard static site security headers; data never touches hosted infrastructure

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Zero-JS-by-default output ideal for static content performance; TypeScript for type-safe component props

backend_stack.value: Cloudflare Workers (serverless edge function for form submission handling only)
backend_stack.rationale: Eliminates backend hosting costs; Worker forwards form data to Google Sheets API at zero marginal cost

data_platform.value: None -- form submissions forwarded to Google Sheets; no hosted database
data_platform.rationale: Google Sheets is free and sufficient for coordinator review of volunteer applications

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages for static assets; Cloudflare Workers for form endpoint
hosting_target.rationale: Cloudflare Pages free tier handles unlimited bandwidth; ideal for absorbing back-to-school traffic spikes at zero cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: No user data stored on infrastructure; Google Sheets form submissions subject to nonprofit Google Workspace data retention policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Zero data footprint on hosted infrastructure eliminates retention obligations

future_scope.value: Student application form with guardian consent workflow, tutor matching portal with login, donor contribution tracking
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features requiring a backend; deferred to preserve zero-cost launch

constraints.value: Hosting cost must remain at USD 0/month for MVP; no backend server allowed; form handling via serverless edge function only
constraints.rationale: Nonprofit budget constraint; free tier CDN and Workers must cover all traffic

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Analytics for traffic and geography; basic custom event tracking for form submission conversion rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Free analytics sufficient for campaign performance measurement

execution_preference.value: Single sprint delivery; no iterative cycles needed for static content site
execution_preference.rationale: Landing page scope is small enough for single-sprint launch

project.goal: Cost-free static landing page for nonprofit K-12 tutoring program targeting volunteer recruitment and family outreach
project.target_users: K-12 families, prospective volunteer tutors, donor organizations
project.timeline: Launch before August back-to-school season

frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS for utility-first responsive design
frontend.approach: Static site generation with zero JavaScript shipped to browser except form analytics events

backend.framework: Vanilla fetch-based Worker for form POST handling
backend.approach: Worker receives form submission, validates fields, calls Google Sheets API to append row

security.headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options via Cloudflare Transform Rules

data.storage: Google Sheets for volunteer interest form submissions

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for Astro build and Cloudflare Pages deploy
devops.environments: Preview deployments per PR, production on main branch

constraints.backend: No persistent server; serverless edge function only

open_questions.student_form: Will student families also apply via this landing page or only volunteer tutors?

reasoner_reviews.product: The three visitor segments (families seeking tutoring, volunteer tutors, donor organizations) are well-identified. The volunteer interest form is the primary conversion event. The back-to-school traffic spike pattern is predictable; Cloudflare Pages free tier handles unlimited requests making cost a non-issue at any traffic volume. Donor information section should include a direct link to the nonprofit's existing donation platform rather than building custom payment handling.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with zero-JS output is the correct choice for a content-heavy static landing page; all page sections render to HTML at build time. The Cloudflare Worker for form handling is cost-effective and avoids any backend hosting cost. Google Sheets as the coordinator-review destination is pragmatic for the nonprofit's existing tooling. Consider using Astro's Image component for automatic AVIF/WebP conversion to keep page weight low for mobile visitors on slow connections.
reasoner_reviews.security: No authentication and no stored data eliminates most security concerns. Standard security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options) should be set via Cloudflare Transform Rules at the edge. The form submission Worker must validate required fields and impose rate limiting using Cloudflare's built-in request rate limit rules to prevent spam submissions flooding the Google Sheet.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloudflare Pages free tier has no bandwidth limits and Workers free tier allows 100,000 requests per day -- well above any realistic form submission volume. The Google Sheets API has a 300-write-per-minute quota; at any realistic volunteer application rate this will never be approached.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 static site: src/pages/index.astro (main landing page), src/components/ (ProgramOverview, SubjectGrid, ServiceRegionsMap, VolunteerProfiles, TestimonialCarousel, VolunteerForm, DonorSection), src/content/ (Astro content collections for tutoring subjects and testimonials)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout with anchor navigation: #programs (subject grid by grade band K-2, 3-5, 6-8, 9-12), #volunteers (profile cards with subjects and availability), #regions (SVG service area map), #testimonials (family quotes carousel), #apply (volunteer interest form), #donate (donor info with external donation link). Mobile-first responsive layout.
frontend.performance_notes: Astro Image component generates AVIF/WebP with srcset for all volunteer profile photos and program illustrations; lazy loading for below-fold images; no JavaScript bundle shipped to browser except minimal inline analytics event listener for form submission tracking

backend.service_design: Single Cloudflare Worker at /api/volunteer-application: validates required fields (name, email, subjects, availability, service_region), sanitizes inputs, calls Google Sheets API v4 spreadsheets.values.append to add row to volunteer applications sheet, returns JSON success/error response
backend.failure_handling: Worker returns 400 on missing required fields with field-level error messages; 503 with retry-after header if Google Sheets API quota exceeded; 429 enforced by Cloudflare rate limit rule (10 requests per IP per minute)
backend.backend_risks: Google Sheets API OAuth2 service account credentials must be stored as Cloudflare Worker secret; rotation policy should be documented for coordinator handover

security.auth_design: No authentication required for public landing page
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare rate limit rule on /api/volunteer-application: 10 requests per IP per minute; Turnstile CAPTCHA widget on form to block automated spam submissions
security.privacy_controls: Form submission Worker does not log submitted data to Worker logs; data flows directly from Worker to Google Sheets; no PII stored on Cloudflare infrastructure

data.entities: tutoring_subjects (content collection: subject_name, grade_bands, description), volunteer_profiles (content collection: first_name, subject_areas, availability_text, photo_asset), service_regions (content collection: region_name, coverage_description), testimonials (content collection: quote, family_description, grade_level), application_forms (Google Sheets: timestamp, name, email, subjects, availability, service_region, notes)
data.storage_design: All content stored as Astro content collection markdown/YAML files in git repository; form submissions appended to Google Sheets via Worker API call; no database infrastructure
data.analytics_events: Custom event: volunteer_form_submitted (fires on successful Worker 200 response); donor_link_clicked (fires on external donation link click); tracked via Cloudflare Zaraz or lightweight custom beacon

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub repo -> GitHub Actions runs astro build -> deploys to Cloudflare Pages via wrangler pages deploy; Cloudflare Worker deployed separately via wrangler deploy from workers/ directory
devops.environments: Cloudflare Pages preview deployments created automatically for every PR branch; production deployment on push to main; Worker deployed to production environment on main merge
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: checkout, install dependencies (bun install), run astro build, run wrangler pages deploy (preview or production based on branch); separate wrangler deploy job for Worker on main push
devops.cost_controls: Cloudflare Pages free tier: unlimited bandwidth, 500 build minutes/month; Workers free tier: 100K requests/day; total monthly hosting cost: USD 0

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a cost-free nonprofit K-12 tutoring landing page. The hard constraint is zero hosting cost, which drives the choice of Cloudflare Pages (static assets, unlimited bandwidth on free tier) and a Cloudflare Worker for form handling instead of any backend server. Astro 4 zero-JS output is ideal for a content-heavy static page. The volunteer interest form flows to Google Sheets via a Worker, keeping all data off hosted infrastructure. No compliance context needed as no PII is stored.
target_output.title: Nonprofit K-12 Tutoring Organization Landing Page
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-hosting-cost static landing page built with Astro 4 on Cloudflare Pages for a nonprofit connecting low-income K-12 students with volunteer tutors. Targets three visitor segments: families seeking tutoring, prospective volunteers, and donor organizations. Volunteer interest forms are handled by a Cloudflare Worker that appends submissions to Google Sheets, eliminating any backend infrastructure cost. Cloudflare Pages absorbs back-to-school traffic spikes without incremental cost.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture with no server-side runtime. Astro 4 builds all page content to plain HTML/CSS at CI time and deploys to Cloudflare Pages CDN. A single Cloudflare Worker serves as the form submission endpoint, receiving POST requests from the volunteer interest form, validating inputs, and calling the Google Sheets API v4 to append each application row. No database, no application server, no persistent compute. Cloudflare Transform Rules enforce security headers at the edge. Analytics events are captured via Cloudflare Zaraz for form conversion tracking. The entire stack operates within free tier limits at any realistic traffic volume.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3, Astro Image component (AVIF/WebP generation). Form Backend: Cloudflare Worker (TypeScript, Wrangler 3). Data: Google Sheets API v4 via service account credentials stored as Worker secret. Hosting: Cloudflare Pages (free tier, unlimited bandwidth). Analytics: Cloudflare Zaraz for custom event tracking. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Bun package manager, wrangler pages deploy and wrangler deploy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Program Overview section: tutoring subjects organized by grade band (K-2 foundational literacy, 3-5 math and reading, 6-8 pre-algebra and science, 9-12 SAT prep and AP subjects). Volunteer Profiles section: photo cards with subject expertise and availability, loaded from Astro content collection. Service Regions section: SVG map highlighting covered school districts with tooltip descriptions. Testimonials Carousel: rotating family quotes with grade level and subject context. Volunteer Interest Form: name, email, subject checkboxes, availability dropdown, service region selector, brief motivation field -- submits to Cloudflare Worker. Donor Section: program impact statistics (students served, volunteer hours, grade improvement averages) with link to external donation platform.
target_output.system_components: Cloudflare Pages CDN (global edge network serving pre-built Astro HTML/CSS/image assets), Cloudflare Worker at /api/volunteer-application (field validation, spam rate limiting, Google Sheets append), Cloudflare Transform Rules (security headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy), Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA (form spam prevention), Google Sheets API v4 (volunteer applications spreadsheet), GitHub Actions CI (Astro build + Cloudflare deploy).
target_output.workflows: Volunteer Interest Submission: visitor completes form, Cloudflare Turnstile validates human interaction, form POSTs to Cloudflare Worker at /api/volunteer-application; Worker validates required fields (name, email, at least one subject, service_region), rate-checks IP against Cloudflare rule (10 req/minute), calls Google Sheets API spreadsheets.values.append with timestamp and form fields, returns 200 JSON to form component which shows thank-you message; Google Sheets coordinator receives email notification via Sheets native notification rules. Content Update Workflow: program coordinator edits YAML files in src/content/ directory via GitHub web interface, commits to main branch, GitHub Actions triggers astro build and wrangler pages deploy automatically, updated content live within 2 minutes.
target_output.data_model: tutoring_subjects (Astro content collection YAML: subject_id, subject_name, grade_bands[], description, icon_name). volunteer_profiles (Astro content collection YAML: profile_id, first_name, subject_areas[], availability_text, photo_filename, joined_year). service_regions (Astro content collection YAML: region_id, region_name, district_names[], coverage_description). testimonials (Astro content collection YAML: quote, family_description, grade_level, subject, year). application_forms (Google Sheets columns: submission_timestamp, full_name, email_address, subject_areas, weekly_availability, service_region, motivation_text, turnstile_verified). No hosted database.
target_output.api_design: Single Cloudflare Worker endpoint: POST /api/volunteer-application (body: {name: string, email: string, subjects: string[], availability: string, service_region: string, motivation?: string}). Response: 200 {success: true} on successful Sheets append; 400 {error: string, fields: string[]} on validation failure; 429 {error: 'rate_limit_exceeded'} when IP rate limit hit; 503 {error: 'service_unavailable', retry_after: 60} when Google Sheets API quota exceeded. No authentication required.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare (always-redirect HTTP to HTTPS). Security headers via Cloudflare Transform Rules: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; script-src 'self' challenges.cloudflare.com), X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Cloudflare Turnstile CAPTCHA on volunteer form prevents spam submissions. Worker rate limit: 10 requests per IP per minute on /api/volunteer-application. No user data stored on hosted infrastructure; form submissions go directly to Google Sheets. Google Sheets service account credential stored as Cloudflare Worker encrypted secret.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow: on push to main -- bun install, bun run astro build (outputs to dist/), wrangler pages deploy dist/ --project-name tutoring-nonprofit --branch main; separate job: wrangler deploy workers/volunteer-form.ts --name volunteer-application --env production. On PR branches: wrangler pages deploy with --branch [branch-name] creates preview URL automatically. No staging environment needed for static content. Content updates: coordinator pushes YAML edits to main, CI deploys in approximately 90 seconds. Worker secrets managed via wrangler secret put commands (SHEETS_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY, SHEETS_SPREADSHEET_ID).
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Pages Analytics: total requests, unique visitors, geography breakdown, top pages -- available in Cloudflare dashboard at zero cost. Cloudflare Workers Analytics: Worker invocations, error rate, latency p50/p99 for /api/volunteer-application. Custom events via Cloudflare Zaraz: volunteer_form_submitted (fires on 200 response from Worker), donor_link_clicked (fires on external donation link interaction). Weekly Google Sheets row count reviewed by coordinator to track volunteer application volume. No server-side monitoring tools required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Total monthly hosting cost: USD 0. Cloudflare Pages free tier: unlimited bandwidth, 500 build minutes/month (estimated 10 minutes/month actual usage). Workers free tier: 100,000 requests/day (volunteer form will never approach this limit). Google Sheets API: 300 writes/minute quota far exceeds any realistic form submission rate. Back-to-school traffic spike absorbed by Cloudflare global CDN with zero incremental cost. Future paid features (student application with login, payment processing) would introduce hosting costs estimated at USD 20-50/month on Cloudflare Workers Paid plan.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Astro site with program overview, subject grid by grade band, service regions SVG map, testimonials carousel, volunteer interest form with Cloudflare Worker and Google Sheets integration, Cloudflare security headers, basic Zaraz analytics. Phase 2 Enhancement (4 weeks post-launch): Add volunteer profile cards from content collection, donor statistics section with program impact metrics, SEO optimization (OpenGraph meta, sitemap.xml, robots.txt), Lighthouse CI gate (LCP < 2s, CLS < 0.1). Phase 3 Future (deferred): Student application form with guardian email confirmation, volunteer matching portal with login (requires Cloudflare Workers Paid and D1 database).
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components must use TypeScript with typed content collection schemas. No JavaScript shipped to browser except Turnstile widget and minimal Zaraz event calls. All images must use Astro Image component -- no raw img tags. Content updates via YAML content collections only -- no hardcoded text in .astro files. Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions enforces LCP < 2.5s and accessibility score >= 90 before merge to main. Tailwind purge configured in astro.config to strip unused utility classes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Google Sheets API service account credentials rotation is a manual process; if credentials expire unnoticed, form submissions silently fail. Mitigation: set Google Workspace credential expiry alert and document rotation steps for the volunteer coordinator. Risk: Cloudflare Workers free tier 10ms CPU time limit per request may be exceeded if Google Sheets API response is slow. Mitigation: use async fetch with 5s timeout; return 503 to form user rather than timing out silently. Tradeoff: Google Sheets as application backend over a proper database sacrifices querying capability but maintains zero hosting cost appropriate for the nonprofit's budget.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed no student-facing application form in MVP (families contact matched volunteers via email after coordinator review). Decided against self-hosted analytics (Plausible, Umami) to keep costs at zero -- Cloudflare native analytics sufficient for campaign conversion measurement. Cloudflare Turnstile chosen over reCAPTCHA for CAPTCHA to avoid Google dependency and better mobile UX.

metadata.notes: Zero-cost static landing page for nonprofit K-12 tutoring org; Cloudflare Pages + Worker for form handling; no backend server or database.

profile.domain: Higher Education Registrar — End-of-Semester Grade Submission Automation

project_goal.value: Automate end-of-semester grade collection from the LMS, detect missing grades for incomplete rosters, send targeted reminder emails to faculty, and post confirmed grade batches to the SIS at scale across thousands of course sections.
project_goal.rationale: Manual grade collection via web UI is error-prone and misses submission deadlines affecting student transcripts.

target_users.value: Registrar staff who monitor submission progress and trigger overrides; faculty members who receive automated reminders; the SIS receiving final grade posts.
target_users.rationale: Tool is internally operated — students are downstream beneficiaries, not direct users.

project_class.rationale: Scheduled batch jobs, integrations with LMS and SIS APIs, and an admin panel for registrar oversight — no student-facing UI.

capabilities.rationale: Celery batch jobs drive LMS polling, reminder dispatch, and SIS posting; admin panel gives registrar staff visibility and override controls.

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-system integration (LMS, SIS, email) with batch scheduling; no complex real-time requirements but correctness is critical for student records.

risk_level.rationale: Grade data is FERPA-protected; incorrect posts to SIS have direct academic record consequences but no financial or safety risk.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Grade records are student educational records under FERPA; individual grades must not be accessible to unauthorized parties.

external_exposure.rationale: Tool runs within university VPC; LMS and SIS are also internal systems; faculty receive emails via institutional email domain.

access_model.value: Registrar staff access admin panel via Cognito SSO (university IdP federation); LMS and SIS integrations use dedicated service account OAuth2 tokens scoped to grade read/write; faculty receive one-way reminder emails with no login required.
access_model.rationale: Minimal access surface — only registrar staff have interactive access; service accounts have scoped API permissions.

feature_scope.value: LMS grade polling per course section, missing-grade detection against roster records, personalized faculty reminder email generation and dispatch, grade validation (valid grade codes, no missing required fields), SIS grade batch posting, submission deadline tracking, registrar admin panel showing section-by-section completion status, audit log of all grade posts.
feature_scope.rationale: Full automation of the grade submission lifecycle from collection to posting.

mvp_scope.value: LMS polling, missing-grade detection, faculty reminder emails, grade validation, and SIS posting for the current institution's LMS and SIS pair. Admin panel for submission tracking in Phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: Core automation path delivers immediate deadline compliance value; admin panel is a monitoring enhancement.

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all LMS, SIS, and SMTP connections; LMS and SIS service account tokens stored in AWS Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation; grade data not logged in plaintext; Cognito MFA for registrar admin panel.
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA requires confidentiality of grade records; token security prevents unauthorized LMS/SIS access.

frontend_stack.value: Minimal internal admin panel — React 18 with TypeScript, served as a static SPA from S3/CloudFront, communicating with the FastAPI admin endpoints.
frontend_stack.rationale: Lightweight admin visibility tool; no complex frontend framework needed beyond React for registrar staff.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery 5 for batch task orchestration; FastAPI for the admin panel API; Redis as Celery broker and result backend.
backend_stack.rationale: Celery is well-suited for scheduled multi-step batch workflows; FastAPI for the thin admin API.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for grade submissions, roster records, submission deadlines, and audit logs; Redis 7 on Elasticache for Celery broker and task result storage.
data_platform.rationale: Relational model fits the structured grade/roster data; Redis for Celery is standard.

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate for Celery workers and FastAPI; RDS PostgreSQL; Elasticache Redis; S3 + CloudFront for admin SPA.
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers for batch workers scale to zero between semesters, reducing cost.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Grade submission audit logs retained for 7 years per FERPA; reminder email logs retained for 3 years; raw grade data purged after confirmed SIS posting plus 90-day reconciliation window.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FERPA 7-year record retention for educational record transactions; grade data in transit purged after SIS confirmation.

future_scope.value: Multi-institution SaaS mode with per-tenant LMS/SIS configurations; grade change request workflow; instructor-facing portal for self-service submission status.
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap expanding from single-institution to multi-tenant deployment.

constraints.value: Must complete all SIS postings before the official grade-finalization deadline (configurable per semester); LMS polling must not exceed the LMS API rate limit (100 req/min per service account); no grade data written to logs.
constraints.rationale: Deadline compliance is the core business requirement; API rate limits enforced by LMS vendor.

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for ECS task logs and metrics; Celery Flower for task queue monitoring; email delivery tracked via SES bounce/complaint webhooks; SIS posting success/failure per section logged to audit_posts table.
observability_baseline.rationale: Registrar staff need real-time visibility into which sections are pending, reminded, and posted.

execution_preference.value: Celery beat scheduler drives polling and reminder cycles; ad hoc override tasks triggerable by registrar via admin panel; deployment via GitHub Actions to ECS Fargate.
execution_preference.rationale: Scheduled automation with human override capability.

compliance_context.value: FERPA — individual student grade records are educational records; the tool must enforce that only authorized registrar staff and the submitting faculty member can access grades in transit; audit logs of all SIS posts required; no grade data in application logs.
compliance_context.rationale: FERPA applies to all grade record handling; audit trail required for regulatory compliance.

project.goal: Automate LMS-to-SIS grade submission lifecycle with reminder emails and registrar oversight
project.target_users: Registrar staff (admin panel), faculty (email reminders), SIS (grade posting target)
project.timeline: Deploy before spring semester final exam period

backend.framework: Celery 5 with Redis broker; FastAPI for admin API

security.auth: Cognito SSO (federated with university IdP) for admin panel; OAuth2 service accounts for LMS/SIS; SES for email
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 for all external connections; Secrets Manager for tokens

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate (Celery workers and FastAPI), RDS, Elasticache, S3+CloudFront

constraints.deadline: All SIS posts before configurable grade-finalization timestamp
constraints.logging: No grade data in plaintext logs

reasoner_reviews.product: The automation scope covers the full grade submission lifecycle — polling, detection, reminder, validation, and posting. The admin panel for registrar oversight is essential for deadline management across thousands of sections; without it, staff have no visibility into which sections are still pending. The configurable deadline per semester is the right design choice given varying academic calendars.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery beat with a polling task every 15 minutes during the submission window is the right approach. Grade validation (checking valid grade codes against the institution's allowed grade table) must happen in the Celery task before SIS posting, not in the SIS API call — API errors are harder to surface to faculty. Consider a PostgreSQL advisory lock on per-section posting tasks to prevent double-post if two Celery workers race.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA is the primary compliance concern. LMS and SIS service account OAuth2 tokens must be stored in Secrets Manager with automatic rotation — never in environment variables. Grade data must not appear in Celery task arguments (which can be logged by Redis) — pass section IDs only and fetch grades at task execution time from the LMS API.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LMS API rate limiting at 100 req/min with thousands of sections requires a Celery rate_limit setting on the polling task plus a token bucket in Redis. Recommend polling sections in priority order (closest to deadline first) to ensure high-risk sections are checked before rate limit windows close.

backend.service_design: Four Celery task types: PollSectionGradesTask (fetches grades for one section from LMS, detects missing), SendReminderTask (generates and sends faculty reminder email via SES), ValidateAndPostGradesTask (validates grade codes, posts batch to SIS API), CloseSubmissionTask (marks section as finalized in audit_posts). Celery beat schedules PollSectionGradesTask every 15 minutes during the active submission window (configurable start/end per semester).
backend.api_patterns: FastAPI admin endpoints: GET /sections?status=pending|reminded|posted|closed (paginated section list), GET /sections/{id}/grades (grade snapshot for registrar review), POST /sections/{id}/remind (manually trigger reminder for a section), POST /sections/{id}/post (manually trigger SIS posting), GET /audit-posts (audit log of all SIS posts with timestamps and response codes).
backend.failure_handling: SIS posting failures logged to audit_posts with error_code and retry_at; automatic retry up to 3 times with 5-minute backoff; after 3 failures, section flagged as posting_failed in admin panel for manual registrar intervention. LMS API 429 responses handled by Celery rate limiter — task requeued after rate limit window. PostgreSQL advisory lock (pg_try_advisory_lock) per section_id prevents concurrent posting tasks.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat schedule: grade polling every 15 minutes (window start to deadline); reminder dispatch at T-48h, T-24h, T-4h before deadline; nightly reconciliation job comparing SIS posted grades against LMS final grades for discrepancy detection; post-deadline summary email to registrar with completion stats.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool federated with university SAML IdP; registrar staff authenticate via university SSO; Cognito issues JWT tokens for FastAPI authorization; MFA enforced for all admin panel users.
security.authorization_model: Single role: registrar_staff — all admin panel users have equivalent read and trigger permissions; SIS and LMS service accounts have no admin panel access; Cognito groups enforce role.
security.secrets_management: LMS OAuth2 token stored in Secrets Manager as JSON {client_id, client_secret, token_url}; SIS API key in Secrets Manager; automatic 90-day rotation via Lambda rotation function; ECS task role has GetSecretValue permission for specific ARNs only; tokens fetched at Celery task startup, never passed as task arguments.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_posts table: section_id, faculty_id, grade_count, post_timestamp, sis_response_code, posted_by (Celery task or registrar user ID). reminder_events table: section_id, faculty_id, sent_at, trigger_type (scheduled/manual). No grade values written to any log — only section_id and status transitions.

data.entities: course_sections (section_id UUID PK, course_code, section_number, faculty_id, term_id, lms_section_id, sis_section_id, submission_status ENUM pending/grades_complete/reminded/posting_failed/posted/closed, last_polled_at, grade_count, missing_count). roster_records (id UUID PK, section_id FK, student_id, lms_enrollment_id, grade_submitted BOOL). grade_submissions (id UUID PK, section_id FK, student_id, grade_code, submitted_at, lms_submission_id) — stored transiently, purged after SIS post + 90 days. submission_deadlines (id UUID PK, term_id, deadline_at, polling_window_start, polling_window_end). audit_posts (id UUID PK, section_id FK, posted_at, sis_response_code, grade_count, retry_count, posted_by_task_id). reminder_events (id UUID PK, section_id FK, faculty_id, sent_at, trigger_type ENUM scheduled/manual).
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ for reliability; grade_submissions table uses row-level security (faculty_id must match session context for registrar queries); Redis used only as Celery broker and result backend — no grade data stored in Redis.
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_posts and reminder_events retained 7 years (FERPA). grade_submissions purged via nightly job after confirmed SIS posting plus 90-day reconciliation window. roster_records retained for 7 years. Celery task result records in Redis evicted after 24 hours.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster: two services — grade-automation-worker (Celery beat + worker, 2-4 tasks, scales up during submission window), grade-admin-api (FastAPI, 1-2 tasks behind ALB). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ in private subnet. Elasticache Redis in private subnet. S3 + CloudFront for admin React SPA.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: black/flake8/mypy lint, pytest with factory_boy fixtures for Celery task unit tests, integration test against LocalStack for SES and Secrets Manager, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Feature branches deploy to dev environment automatically on PR open.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Logs for ECS task stdout (INFO level only, no grade values). Celery Flower UI on internal ALB for task queue visibility. SES metrics: bounce_rate, complaint_rate. Custom CloudWatch metric: sections_pending_count published every poll cycle — alarm if sections remain at T-2h before deadline.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling update with minimum 50% healthy — previous task definition available for immediate rollback. RDS automated daily snapshots with point-in-time recovery. Schema migrations run as ECS one-off tasks during maintenance windows only.

target_output.thinking_summary: Grade submission automation for a registrar's office is correctness-critical — a missed posting or double-post has direct transcript consequences. The key architectural decision is to pass only section_ids through Celery task arguments (never grade data), fetching from LMS at execution time to keep grades out of Redis task queues. PostgreSQL advisory locks prevent double-posting race conditions. The polling priority queue (closest-to-deadline sections first) ensures high-risk sections are never starved when LMS rate limits constrain throughput.
target_output.title: Registrar Grade Submission Automation — LMS-to-SIS Pipeline with Faculty Reminder Engine
target_output.executive_summary: A Celery-driven automation tool that polls the LMS every 15 minutes during the grade submission window, detects incomplete section rosters, dispatches personalized faculty reminder emails via SES at T-48h/T-24h/T-4h thresholds, validates grade codes against the institution's allowed grade table, and posts confirmed grade batches to the SIS API at scale across thousands of course sections. A React admin panel gives registrar staff real-time section completion visibility and manual override controls. FERPA compliance enforced by keeping grade values out of all logs and queues — section IDs flow through Celery, grades fetched at task execution time.
target_output.architecture_overview: Celery beat scheduler running on ECS Fargate triggers PollSectionGradesTask every 15 minutes during the configured submission window. Each task fetches one section's grades from the LMS OAuth2 API, compares against roster_records to detect missing grades, and updates course_sections.submission_status. SES dispatches reminder emails at scheduled thresholds and on registrar manual trigger. ValidateAndPostGradesTask validates grade codes against the institution's allowed_grades table and calls the SIS REST API with the grade batch; PostgreSQL advisory lock (pg_try_advisory_lock) on section_id prevents concurrent post attempts. FastAPI serves the admin panel API behind an ALB; the React SPA is served from CloudFront. All grade values are fetched at task execution time from the LMS — never stored in Redis or Celery task arguments.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Celery 5.3, FastAPI 0.111. Queue: Redis 7 Elasticache (Celery broker and results, task results TTL 24h). Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ. Email: AWS SES with bounce/complaint webhooks. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager (LMS OAuth2 token, SIS API key, 90-day rotation). Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, TanStack Query. Hosting: ECS Fargate (Celery workers and FastAPI), S3 + CloudFront (admin SPA), ALB. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS rolling deploy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Grade Polling: Celery beat triggers PollSectionGradesTask per section during submission window; sections prioritized by time-to-deadline to prevent starving high-risk sections under LMS rate limits. Missing Grade Detection: compares LMS grade response against roster_records; updates missing_count and submission_status. Reminder Engine: SendReminderTask generates personalized email listing missing student IDs and submission deadline; dispatched at T-48h, T-24h, T-4h and on manual registrar trigger. Grade Validation: ValidateAndPostGradesTask checks each grade_code against allowed_grades lookup, rejects invalid codes before SIS call. SIS Posting: grade batch POSTed to SIS REST API; response code and grade_count written to audit_posts; section status set to posted on 2xx, posting_failed on persistent error. Admin Panel: paginated section list filtered by status; manual remind and post triggers; audit_posts log view.
target_output.system_components: ECS Fargate grade-automation-worker service (Celery beat scheduler and worker processes, scales 2-6 tasks during submission window). ECS Fargate grade-admin-api service (FastAPI, 1-2 tasks, ALB target group). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (course_sections, roster_records, grade_submissions, audit_posts, reminder_events tables). Elasticache Redis 7 (Celery broker queue and result backend). SES (reminder email dispatch, bounce processing). Secrets Manager (LMS and SIS credentials). S3 plus CloudFront (admin React SPA). ALB (FastAPI ingress, internal only). Cognito user pool (registrar staff authentication, SAML federation).
target_output.workflows: Grade Polling and Reminder Cycle: Celery beat fires PollSectionGradesTask for each active section ordered by (deadline_at - now()) ASC; task fetches OAuth2 token from Secrets Manager, calls GET /lms/api/v1/sections/{lms_section_id}/grades, compares returned student grade list against roster_records, computes missing_count, updates course_sections; if missing_count > 0 and T-48h threshold crossed, enqueues SendReminderTask with section_id and faculty_id; SES sends email listing missing student names and deadline. SIS Grade Posting: when LMS polling detects grades_complete status, ValidateAndPostGradesTask is enqueued; task acquires pg_try_advisory_lock(section_id); fetches grades from LMS API; validates each grade_code against allowed_grades table; constructs SIS batch payload; POSTs to SIS REST endpoint; writes result to audit_posts with sis_response_code; on success sets section status to posted; on failure increments retry_count and sets retry_at; releases advisory lock.
target_output.data_model: course_sections (section_id UUID PK, course_code VARCHAR, section_number VARCHAR, faculty_id UUID, term_id UUID, lms_section_id VARCHAR, sis_section_id VARCHAR, submission_status ENUM pending/grades_complete/reminded/posting_failed/posted/closed, last_polled_at TIMESTAMP, grade_count INT, missing_count INT). roster_records (id UUID PK, section_id UUID FK, student_id UUID, lms_enrollment_id VARCHAR, grade_submitted BOOL). grade_submissions (id UUID PK, section_id UUID FK, student_id UUID, grade_code VARCHAR, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, purge_after TIMESTAMP). submission_deadlines (id UUID PK, term_id UUID, deadline_at TIMESTAMP, polling_window_start TIMESTAMP, polling_window_end TIMESTAMP). allowed_grades (id UUID PK, institution_id UUID, grade_code VARCHAR, grade_points DECIMAL, is_passing BOOL). audit_posts (id UUID PK, section_id UUID FK, posted_at TIMESTAMP, sis_response_code INT, grade_count INT, retry_count INT, posted_by_task_id VARCHAR). reminder_events (id UUID PK, section_id UUID FK, faculty_id UUID, sent_at TIMESTAMP, trigger_type ENUM scheduled/manual).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI admin endpoints at /api/v1: GET /sections?term_id={}&status={}&page={} (paginated section list sorted by deadline proximity), GET /sections/{id} (section detail with grade_count, missing_count, last_polled_at), GET /sections/{id}/audit-posts (post history), POST /sections/{id}/remind (trigger manual reminder, returns 202 Accepted), POST /sections/{id}/post (trigger manual SIS posting, returns 202 Accepted), GET /audit-posts?term_id={}&page={} (institution-wide posting audit log), GET /health (checks DB connectivity and Redis ping). All endpoints require Cognito JWT in Authorization header.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FERPA compliance: grade values never stored in Redis task arguments — Celery tasks receive section_id only, fetch grades from LMS at execution time; grade_submissions table has RLS policy restricting row access to registrar role; no grade data written to CloudWatch Logs. LMS OAuth2 tokens in Secrets Manager with 90-day automatic rotation; ECS task IAM role allows GetSecretValue for specific ARNs. SIS API key in Secrets Manager with same rotation policy. Cognito MFA enforced for admin panel users. PostgreSQL advisory locks prevent concurrent grade posting for the same section. TLS 1.3 on all outbound connections to LMS, SIS, and SES. audit_posts and reminder_events provide immutable transaction history for FERPA compliance reviews.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: black/flake8/mypy lint, pytest unit tests for Celery tasks with mocked LMS/SIS clients, integration tests against LocalStack SES and Secrets Manager, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy (minimum 50% healthy tasks). Environments: dev (single ECS task, RDS t3.micro), staging (Multi-AZ RDS, 2 Celery tasks, LMS/SIS sandbox endpoints), production (RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium, Celery worker auto-scaling 2-6 tasks based on queue depth). Submission window: ECS scheduled scaling action increases worker count to 6 from 48h before deadline to 2h after. Secrets Manager rotation: Lambda rotates LMS and SIS tokens every 90 days; ECS tasks pick up new token on next fetch at task startup. Schema migrations run as ECS one-off tasks during maintenance windows, never during active submission periods.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs: ECS stdout at INFO level (task start/complete, section_id, status transitions — no grade values). CloudWatch custom metrics: sections_pending_count published every poll cycle, sections_posting_failed_count, reminder_emails_sent_today. Celery Flower UI on internal ALB: task queue depth, active workers, task failure rate. SES metrics: bounce_rate, complaint_rate (alarm if >2%). Alarm: sections_pending_count > 0 at T-2h before deadline triggers PagerDuty and registrar email. Alarm: sections_posting_failed_count > 0 triggers Slack notification to registrar team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ~$280/month during active semester. ECS Fargate: $60/month at 4 worker tasks during submission windows, $15/month off-peak at 1 task. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ t3.medium: $120/month. Elasticache Redis t3.micro: $25/month. SES: ~$5/month for thousands of reminder emails. Scheduled scaling to 6 tasks adds ~$20 incremental per submission window. Annual cost approximately $4,000 including development and staging environments.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Celery beat polling framework, LMS OAuth2 integration, missing-grade detection, SES reminder emails at T-48h and T-24h, ValidateAndPostGradesTask with SIS posting and advisory lock, audit_posts logging, GitHub Actions CI, staging environment against LMS/SIS sandboxes. Phase 2 (3 weeks): React admin panel (section status dashboard, manual remind and post triggers, audit log view), Cognito SSO integration, Celery Flower monitoring setup, T-4h reminder and post-deadline reconciliation job. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Multi-term support with configurable submission_deadlines, SES bounce/complaint handling, CloudWatch alarms and Grafana dashboard, load test with simulated 3,000-section semester.
target_output.development_guidelines: Celery task arguments must never include grade values — pass section_id only. All Celery tasks must be idempotent: check audit_posts before SIS posting, check reminder_events before sending email. PostgreSQL advisory locks required on ValidateAndPostGradesTask per section_id. No plaintext grade codes in log messages — use grade_count metrics only. pytest coverage gate at 80% for Celery tasks; LocalStack integration tests required for SES and Secrets Manager interactions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LMS API rate limit (100 req/min) with 3,000 sections at 15-minute polling intervals requires exactly 50 sections/min — no headroom. Mitigation: priority queue ordering by deadline proximity; Celery rate_limit=50/m on PollSectionGradesTask; request elevated rate limit from LMS vendor during submission windows. Risk: SIS API downtime during grade-finalization deadline. Mitigation: retry up to 3 times with 5-minute backoff; alert registrar on posting_failed; grade_submissions table supports manual re-post after SIS recovery. Tradeoff: fetching grades from LMS at task execution time rather than caching adds LMS API calls but is required by FERPA — grade values must not traverse Celery queues.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Grade data kept out of Celery task arguments — section_id only passed, LMS API called at task start to keep grades off Redis queues. PostgreSQL advisory lock chosen over Redis distributed lock to avoid TTL expiry edge cases on concurrent posting tasks. Celery beat used for scheduling over AWS EventBridge — simpler within existing Celery/Redis stack. LMS OAuth2 service account with grade-read scope and SIS REST API with grade-write scope confirmed with respective vendors.

metadata.notes: First-pass grade submission automation; FERPA-compliant Celery pipeline with LMS polling, SES reminders, and SIS posting across thousands of course sections.

profile.domain: University Campus Health Services Mobile App

project_goal.value: Mobile app for university health services enabling students to schedule appointments, complete pre-visit intake forms, view visit summaries, and message campus healthcare providers with HIPAA-compliant architecture for student health records
project_goal.rationale: Replace phone-only appointment booking and paper intake forms with a mobile-first patient experience

target_users.value: University students as patients and campus healthcare providers as clinical staff
target_users.rationale: Dual-persona app with patient-facing features for students and provider-facing clinical tools

project_class.rationale: React Native for iOS and Android serving students as the primary channel for health services access

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, realtime, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full-featured health services app needs push notifications, EHR integration, secure messaging, and care analytics

complexity_level.rationale: HIPAA compliance, EHR FHIR API integration, secure messaging, and multi-factor auth add substantial complexity

risk_level.rationale: Student health records are protected health information under HIPAA; data breach carries significant regulatory liability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student health records at campus health services are covered by HIPAA as protected health information

external_exposure.rationale: All features require student or provider authentication; no unauthenticated access permitted

access_model.value: Cognito with MFA for all users; RBAC with patient and provider roles; provider role scoped to own patients only
access_model.rationale: HIPAA minimum necessary standard requires role-based access scoped to treatment relationship

feature_scope.value: Appointment scheduling with provider availability, pre-visit intake forms, push notification reminders, visit summary viewing, secure provider-patient messaging, prescription refill requests, immunization record viewing
feature_scope.rationale: Core health services patient portal features identified in student and provider discovery sessions

mvp_scope.value: Appointment scheduling, pre-visit intake forms, push notification reminders, visit summary viewing
mvp_scope.rationale: Core scheduling and intake features reduce phone call volume by an estimated 60% which is the primary business driver

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption at rest via AWS KMS, TLS 1.3 in transit, Cognito MFA for all users, PHI access audit logging, BAA with AWS
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA technical safeguards require encryption, access controls, and audit logging for all PHI

frontend_stack.value: React Native 0.74 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Single codebase for iOS and Android with team TypeScript expertise

backend_stack.rationale: Fastify's schema-based request validation reduces PHI handling errors; Node.js matches frontend TypeScript skillset

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for patient and appointment data with KMS encryption; Redis 7 for session caching
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL supports complex appointment scheduling queries; Redis caches provider availability without storing PHI

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in HIPAA-eligible configuration, us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: HIPAA-eligible AWS services with BAA required for PHI processing

privacy_retention_policy.value: Student health records retained 10 years per state health records law; audit logs retained 6 years per HIPAA; app access logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: State health records retention law extends beyond HIPAA minimum 6-year requirement

future_scope.value: Telehealth video visit integration, prescription refill requests, immunization record management, lab result notifications
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 clinical features planned after MVP appointment scheduling adoption is validated

constraints.value: Must integrate with existing Epic EHR via FHIR R4 API; budget $2000/month; deploy us-east-1 only for HIPAA data residency
constraints.rationale: Epic EHR is the system of record; HIPAA data residency and budget are hard constraints

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with PHI field masking in logs, CloudWatch for infrastructure metrics, PagerDuty for PHI access anomaly alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: PHI must not appear in APM traces; anomaly detection on PHI access patterns is a HIPAA audit control

execution_preference.value: 2-week agile sprints with security review gate before each production deploy
execution_preference.rationale: HIPAA risk management requires security review of each deployment touching PHI handling code

compliance_context.value: HIPAA and HITECH compliance required; BAA with AWS; campus health services are a HIPAA covered entity; student health records at campus health services fall under HIPAA not FERPA per FERPA health records exception
compliance_context.rationale: University health clinics treating students are HIPAA covered entities; the FERPA health records exception applies

project.goal: Replace phone-based appointment booking with HIPAA-compliant mobile app for 12,000 enrolled students
project.target_users: Students as patients and campus healthcare providers
project.timeline: Q4 production launch for spring semester enrollment surge

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for local UI state
frontend.push: Expo Notifications for iOS and Android push notifications

backend.api: REST over HTTPS; WebSocket for secure messaging real-time updates

security.auth: Cognito with MFA, biometric unlock via device keychain
security.encryption: KMS for PHI at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with KMS encryption
data.cache: ElastiCache Redis for provider availability and session data

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with security scan gate

reasoner_reviews.product: Appointment scheduling and intake forms are the right MVP scope. Reducing phone call volume is a measurable success metric. The dual-persona architecture for patients and providers needs careful UX separation — providers should have a distinct navigation context from the patient portal view. Secure messaging is a Phase 1+ feature and correctly deferred.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Epic FHIR R4 integration is the highest-risk technical dependency. The FHIR adapter service should be isolated as a separate module to contain Epic API changes. PostgreSQL is appropriate for appointment scheduling with complex availability queries. Redis for provider availability caching avoids repeated FHIR calls during peak booking windows.
reasoner_reviews.security: HIPAA technical safeguards require: encryption at rest with KMS, TLS 1.3 in transit, unique user identification via Cognito, automatic logoff after 15-minute inactivity, audit logs for all PHI access. The mobile app must not cache PHI in device local storage — React Native AsyncStorage is not encrypted and must not hold patient health data.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Epic FHIR R4 sandbox access must be established before development begins — Epic's partner onboarding process takes 4-6 weeks. The $2000/month budget is feasible for the MVP scope with ECS Fargate at moderate traffic. HIPAA data residency in us-east-1 is a hard constraint; no multi-region deployment without legal sign-off.

backend.service_design: Fastify API with domain modules: appointments (scheduling, availability, cancellation), intake (form templates, submissions), patients (profile, PHI management), providers (availability management, patient queue), fhir_adapter (Epic FHIR R4 client, resource mapping). FHIR adapter isolated as a separate module to contain Epic API surface area.
backend.api_patterns: POST /v1/appointments (book), GET /v1/appointments?patient_id= (list), PATCH /v1/appointments/:id/cancel. POST /v1/intake/:appointment_id (submit form). GET /v1/patients/:id/visit-summaries (list visit summaries from FHIR). WebSocket /ws/messages for real-time secure messaging. All endpoints require Cognito JWT; provider endpoints require provider role claim.
backend.failure_handling: Epic FHIR API circuit breaker with 30-second open window on consecutive failures; cached provider availability served from Redis during FHIR outages. Appointment booking idempotency keys to prevent double-booking on mobile retry. Dead letter queue for failed appointment confirmation push notifications.

frontend.app_structure: React Native app with two navigation stacks: PatientStack (appointment booking, intake, visit history) and ProviderStack (patient queue, appointment management, intake review). Stack determined by Cognito role claim at login.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Student login with Cognito MFA and biometric unlock. Appointment booking: select provider, browse availability calendar, confirm booking, receive push confirmation. Intake form: pre-visit questionnaire rendered from JSON template with required field validation. Visit summary: read-only view of provider notes from FHIR Encounter resource.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: No PHI stored in AsyncStorage — React Native AsyncStorage is unencrypted. Session tokens stored in Expo SecureStore (iOS Keychain, Android Keystore). Automatic screen lock after 5-minute background state via AppState listener. Screenshot prevention enabled for PHI screens via React Native's FLAG_SECURE on Android.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with HIPAA-eligible configuration: SRP protocol, MFA required for all users, custom Lambda trigger for university SSO federation via SAML. Biometric unlock via device keychain stores Cognito refresh token only — does not bypass MFA on first login.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every PHI access event logged to append-only audit_log table: user_id, resource_type, resource_id, action, ip_address, device_id, timestamp. Logs forwarded to CloudWatch Logs with 6-year retention. Datadog APM configured with PHI field masking rules blocking patient_id, dob, and diagnosis fields from APM traces.
security.incident_response_notes: PHI breach detection: Datadog monitor on unusual PHI access patterns (>50 patient records accessed by single provider in 1 hour triggers PagerDuty alert). HIPAA breach notification procedure documented: 60-day notification window to affected individuals, HHS notification for breaches >500 records.

data.entities: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub, student_id, first_name_encrypted, last_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, email_encrypted, phone_encrypted, created_at). appointments (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, provider_id FK, appointment_type ENUM wellness/sick_visit/mental_health/immunization, scheduled_at, duration_minutes, status ENUM scheduled/completed/cancelled/no_show, cancellation_reason). intake_submissions (id UUID PK, appointment_id FK, form_template_id FK, responses_encrypted JSONB, submitted_at). providers (id UUID PK, cognito_sub, name, specialty, availability_schedule JSONB). audit_logs (id UUID PK, user_id, resource_type, resource_id, action, ip_address, device_id, timestamp).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with KMS encryption at rest. PHI fields encrypted at application layer with AWS Encryption SDK before storage. S3 with KMS for intake form document attachments. ElastiCache Redis for provider availability slots and session data — no PHI stored in Redis.
data.retention_and_deletion: Patient health records retained 10 years per state law in RDS with Glacier archive after 3 years. Audit logs retained 6 years in CloudWatch Logs. Appointment records for cancelled/no-show appointments retained 2 years.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1 with ALB and WAF. API service on Fargate: 2-4 tasks auto-scaling on CPU 70% threshold. RDS Multi-AZ PostgreSQL in same VPC. ElastiCache Redis in same VPC. S3 intake document bucket with server-side KMS encryption. Cognito user pool in us-east-1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, jest unit tests, OWASP Dependency Check for CVE scanning, Docker build, ECR push, ECS deploy to staging, Postman API smoke tests, security review gate (manual approval for PHI-touching code paths), promote to production rolling update.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on ECS Fargate sidecar with PHI field masking. CloudWatch structured logs with correlation IDs. Custom metrics: appointment booking rate, intake submission rate, FHIR API latency, push notification delivery rate. PagerDuty alerts: API error rate >1%, p99 latency >3s, RDS connections >80%, PHI access anomaly.

ISS-001.title: PHI stored in React Native AsyncStorage without encryption
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan specified AsyncStorage for caching appointment details and patient name for display. AsyncStorage is unencrypted on Android and is accessible to root processes. Any PHI in AsyncStorage constitutes a HIPAA technical safeguard failure.

history.note: Identified in security audit of mobile storage approach

ISS-002.title: Missing FHIR adapter circuit breaker causing appointment booking failures during Epic outages
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan had no failure isolation for the Epic FHIR API dependency. When Epic maintenance windows occur, appointment booking and visit summary features fail completely rather than degrading gracefully with cached data.

history.note: Identified in architect review of Epic dependency risk

ISS-003.title: Audit log table missing device_id field required by HIPAA audit controls
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 audit_log schema included user_id, resource_type, and action but omitted device_id. HIPAA requires tracking the workstation or device from which PHI was accessed.

history.note: Identified in compliance review of audit log schema

focus_issues.title: PHI stored in React Native AsyncStorage without encryption
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan specified AsyncStorage for caching appointment details and patient name. AsyncStorage is unencrypted and constitutes a HIPAA technical safeguard failure.

focus_issues.title: Missing FHIR adapter circuit breaker causing appointment booking failures during Epic outages

previous_plan.title: Campus Health Services Mobile App — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React Native mobile app for university health services with Fastify backend on ECS Fargate, PostgreSQL for appointments and PHI, and Epic FHIR R4 integration for visit summaries.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA, KMS encryption at rest, TLS 1.3. PHI cached in AsyncStorage for offline display. Audit log: user_id, resource_type, action, timestamp.
