Results written to ec2_baseline DynamoDB table with date partition key. Weekly comparison report now includes both Lambda per-submission cost and EC2 per-submission cost derived from instance-hours divided by grading jobs run.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Weekly cost comparison report now contains valid Lambda vs EC2 cost comparison enabling the research team to evaluate the serverless cost reduction hypothesis.

fix_report.action_taken: Added SES SendEmail call at the end of the analytics Lambda execution, formatting the weekly cost comparison data as an HTML email with a summary table of Lambda cost, EC2 baseline cost, and cost reduction percentage. Recipient list configured as an environment variable on the analytics Lambda. Lambda IAM role updated with ses:SendEmail permission scoped to the verified SES domain.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Faculty advisor and research team receive weekly HTML email with cost comparison summary without requiring AWS console access.

target_output.title: CS Department Serverless Code Grader Research Prototype — Cost Comparison Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Go-based AWS Lambda research prototype for a CS department evaluating serverless infrastructure as a cost reduction strategy for automated code grading. Student Python and Java submissions execute in isolated Firecracker microVMs per Lambda invocation. DynamoDB stores per-submission grading results and cost metrics. Round 3 additions: AWS Cost Explorer integration in the analytics Lambda captures EC2 baseline costs for comparison, and SES email delivery sends weekly cost comparison reports to the faculty research team without requiring AWS console access.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Lambda functions sharing DynamoDB and S3: the grader Lambda (Go binary on custom Firecracker runtime layer) executes each submission in an isolated microVM and writes results to DynamoDB grading_results. The analytics Lambda (Go, weekly CloudWatch Events trigger) reads grading_results and cost_metrics, calls AWS Cost Explorer for EC2 baseline costs, computes per-submission cost comparison, writes the ec2_baseline DynamoDB table, and delivers an HTML report via SES. No public endpoints — all invocations are internal to the university AWS account via IAM. S3 stores submission zip files and test suite bundles; Lambda execution role scoped to read only the invoking submission's S3 prefix via IAM condition key.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22, compiled to Lambda arm64 binary. Lambda runtime: custom Firecracker microVM runtime layer for code execution isolation. Infrastructure: AWS Lambda (grader and analytics functions), DynamoDB on-demand, S3, CloudWatch Events for weekly trigger, SES for report email delivery, AWS Cost Explorer for EC2 baseline cost data. IaC: AWS SAM template. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with go test and sam deploy. Language support in grader: Python 3.12 and Java 21 interpreters bundled in Firecracker rootfs image.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Code Submission Grading: invoke grader Lambda with submission_id, language, S3 paths for code and test suite; Firecracker microVM executes code against test cases within timeout; returns pass/fail per test case, duration_ms, memory_used_mb, Lambda billing duration. Cost Tracking: each grading invocation records Lambda invocation cost (billing duration * memory * Lambda pricing) to DynamoDB cost_metrics. EC2 Baseline Capture: analytics Lambda calls Cost Explorer API daily to fetch EC2 grader instance costs tagged cs-grader-baseline:true, writes to ec2_baseline table. Weekly Cost Comparison Report: analytics Lambda computes average Lambda cost/submission vs EC2 cost/submission, formats HTML table, sends via SES to research team. Grading Result History: query DynamoDB for all results by submission_id or date range for research data analysis.
target_output.system_components: Grader Lambda (Go binary, arm64, 512 MB, 60s timeout, custom Firecracker runtime layer): receives submission payload, downloads code and test suite from S3 to Firecracker rootfs, executes in isolated microVM, parses test runner output, writes grading_results to DynamoDB. Analytics Lambda (Go binary, arm64, 256 MB, 5-min timeout, triggered weekly by CloudWatch Events rule): reads grading_results for past 7 days from DynamoDB, calls Cost Explorer GetCostAndUsage for EC2 tagged instances, writes ec2_baseline records, computes cost statistics, formats HTML email, calls SES SendEmail. DynamoDB tables: grading_results, cost_metrics, ec2_baseline — all on-demand mode with DynamoDB at-rest encryption. S3 bucket: cs-grader-research with server-side encryption, submissions/ and test-suites/ prefixes. SES verified domain for report email sending. CloudWatch Events rule triggering analytics Lambda every Monday 08:00 UTC.
target_output.workflows: Submission Grading Workflow: Research team script invokes Lambda via AWS SDK: aws lambda invoke --function-name cs-grader --payload '{submission_id: SUB001, language: python, code_s3_key: submissions/SUB001/code.zip, test_suite_s3_key: test-suites/cs101-week3/tests.tar.gz, memory_mb: 256, timeout_seconds: 30}'. Grader Lambda boots Firecracker microVM with Python 3.12 rootfs, mounts code.zip and tests.tar.gz as read-only, runs pytest test suite in VM, captures stdout/stderr and exit code. On completion: writes grading_results record with test outcomes, duration_ms=2340, lambda_billing_duration_ms=2400, lambda_cost_usd=0.0000042. Returns JSON result to caller. Weekly Cost Comparison Workflow: CloudWatch Events triggers analytics Lambda Monday 08:00 UTC. Lambda queries DynamoDB grading_results for past 7 days: 4,832 submissions, total Lambda cost $17.83, average $0.00369/submission. Calls Cost Explorer GetCostAndUsage with filter Tag:cs-grader-baseline:true, GroupBy SERVICE, Granularity DAILY for past 7 days: EC2 instance cost $89.60 for week, 4,201 grading jobs run on EC2 baseline, average $0.02133/submission. Writes ec2_baseline record. Computes cost reduction: Lambda 82.7% cheaper than EC2 baseline. Sends SES email to faculty-research@university.edu with HTML table showing week's comparison.
target_output.data_model: DynamoDB table grading_results: PK=submission_id (S), SK='RESULT' (S), language (S), test_suite_id (S), passed (BOOL), test_results_json (S), duration_ms (N), memory_used_mb (N), lambda_duration_billed_ms (N), lambda_cost_usd (N), firecracker_boot_ms (N), created_at (S), TTL (N 90-day expiry). DynamoDB table cost_metrics: PK=date (S YYYY-MM-DD), SK=source ENUM lambda/ec2 (S), total_submissions (N), total_cost_usd (N), avg_cost_per_submission (N), p50_duration_ms (N), p95_duration_ms (N), cold_start_count (N). DynamoDB table ec2_baseline: PK=date (S YYYY-MM-DD), SK='EC2_COST' (S), instance_type (S), hourly_cost_usd (N), grading_jobs_run (N), cost_per_submission (N), instance_hours (N). S3 key structure: submissions/{submission_id}/code.zip, test-suites/{suite_id}/tests.tar.gz, reports/weekly_{YYYY-MM-DD}.json.
target_output.api_design: No REST API — Lambda invoked via AWS SDK InvokeFunction. Grader Lambda input schema: {submission_id: STRING, language: ENUM python|java, code_s3_key: STRING, test_suite_s3_key: STRING, memory_mb: NUMBER 128-3008, timeout_seconds: NUMBER 5-60}. Grader Lambda response schema: {submission_id: STRING, passed: BOOLEAN, test_results: [{test_name: STRING, passed: BOOLEAN, output: STRING, duration_ms: NUMBER}], total_duration_ms: NUMBER, memory_used_mb: NUMBER, lambda_billing_duration_ms: NUMBER, lambda_cost_usd: NUMBER, firecracker_boot_ms: NUMBER, error: STRING|null}. Analytics Lambda invoked by CloudWatch Events; no input schema. Analytics Lambda writes report to S3 reports/weekly_{date}.json and sends SES email.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Firecracker microVM isolation: each submission runs in a dedicated microVM with no network access (no tap device), read-only filesystem except /tmp, SIGKILL at timeout_seconds. Cross-submission isolation: Firecracker VM has no access to other submissions; each VM starts from a clean rootfs snapshot. Lambda IAM role: scoped to DynamoDB:PutItem on grading_results table ARN only, S3:GetObject on cs-grader-research bucket with condition s3:prefix matching the invoking submission_id from the Lambda event (prevents cross-submission S3 reads), CloudWatch:PutMetricData. Analytics Lambda IAM role: DynamoDB:GetItem/Query on all three tables, S3:PutObject on reports/ prefix, ce:GetCostAndUsage, ses:SendEmail scoped to verified SES domain. No student PII in DynamoDB — submission_ids are UUIDs not student IDs; research uses anonymized samples per IRB approval.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AWS SAM template defines: grader Lambda (Go arm64, 512 MB, 60s, custom Firecracker layer ARN, env vars: DYNAMO_TABLE_RESULTS, S3_BUCKET), analytics Lambda (Go arm64, 256 MB, 300s, env vars: DYNAMO_TABLE_COST, EC2_BASELINE_TAG_KEY, EC2_BASELINE_TAG_VALUE, SES_SENDER, SES_RECIPIENTS), DynamoDB tables with TTL, S3 bucket with lifecycle policy deleting submissions after 90 days, CloudWatch Events rule (cron 0 8 * * MON). GitHub Actions CI: go test ./..., GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 go build for each function, sam build, sam deploy --parameter-overrides Env=research. Single research environment — no staging. Firecracker runtime layer built separately from rootfs images (Python 3.12 and Java 21) and published as Lambda layer.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Lambda metrics: Invocations, Duration, Errors, Throttles, ConcurrentExecutions per function. DynamoDB cost_metrics table aggregated daily by analytics Lambda. Custom CloudWatch metric from grader Lambda: firecracker_boot_ms published as CWMetric for cold start analysis. Weekly SES cost comparison email with HTML table: Lambda submissions count, Lambda total cost, Lambda avg/submission, EC2 baseline submissions, EC2 total cost, EC2 avg/submission, cost reduction percentage. S3 weekly report JSON for archival and academic paper data. Research team receives SES email every Monday; no AWS console access required for weekly research review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Lambda grader at 512 MB, 10-second average execution: $0.0000042/invocation at Lambda arm64 pricing. At 5000 submissions/week: $0.021/week in Lambda compute. EC2 t3.medium always-on baseline: $0.0416/hour * 168 hours/week = $6.99/week for same 5000 submissions = $0.00140/submission — note: EC2 is actually cheaper per submission at low volume due to always-on cost amortization. At 50,000 submissions/week Lambda becomes $0.21/week vs EC2 $6.99/week, reaching crossover around 20,000 submissions/week. This crossover point is a key research finding. Monthly budget: $500 AWS; estimated Lambda compute $15, DynamoDB $5, S3 $3, Cost Explorer API calls $0.01, SES $0.10.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (complete by round 2): grader Lambda with Firecracker isolation, DynamoDB schema, Lambda cost tracking, IAM scope fix, SAM deployment. Phase 2 (round 3 additions): EC2 baseline cost capture via Cost Explorer API in analytics Lambda, ec2_baseline DynamoDB table writes, SES email delivery of weekly HTML cost comparison report to research team. Phase 3 (if hypothesis validated): production deployment plan with Canvas LMS webhook integration, C++ and JavaScript language support in Firecracker rootfs images, multi-account deployment for load isolation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Grader Lambda must have unit tests for timeout handling (verify SIGKILL fires at timeout_seconds) and Firecracker boot failure paths. Analytics Lambda tested with DynamoDB local and mocked Cost Explorer responses using aws-sdk-go-v2 mock interfaces. SAM template changes reviewed by faculty advisor before deploy to prevent unexpected AWS cost increases. Cost Explorer API calls are billed at $0.01 per 1000 metrics requested — analytics Lambda caches Cost Explorer responses in S3 to avoid repeat API calls during debugging. Go test coverage must be at least 75% for both Lambda functions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EC2 Cost Explorer data has 48-hour ingestion lag — weekly report may show costs from 2 days prior rather than the full week. Mitigation: report clearly labels data as 'week ending YYYY-MM-DD (48h lag)' in the email subject. Risk: Firecracker custom runtime layer may break on Lambda runtime updates. Mitigation: Lambda runtime version pinned in SAM template; layer version explicitly specified. Risk: SES email delivery blocked by university spam filters. Mitigation: SES domain verification and DKIM configured; test delivery to research team verified before weekly trigger enabled. Tradeoff: Lambda per-invocation billing makes cost comparison favorable only above 20,000 submissions/week — at typical CS1 course scale of 5,000 submissions/week, EC2 is actually cheaper. This is the honest research finding the prototype is designed to surface.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Cost Explorer API chosen over CloudWatch billing metrics for EC2 cost data because Cost Explorer supports resource tag filtering needed to isolate the grader EC2 instances. SES chosen over SNS email topic because SES supports HTML formatting required for the cost comparison table. Analytics Lambda chosen as the Cost Explorer API caller rather than a separate service to minimize Lambda function count within research budget.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision adding EC2 baseline cost capture and SES report delivery to complete the serverless cost comparison research prototype.

project_goal.value: Security-hardened fullstack web application for K-12 districts to manage background check submissions and clearance tracking for volunteers, substitute teachers, and contractors, with role-gated access and immutable audit logs for compliance with state school access mandates
project_goal.rationale: Districts face legal liability if cleared individuals access school facilities without valid, current background checks; immutable audit trail is a state mandate

target_users.value: HR administrators submitting and reviewing clearance requests; school principals monitoring facility access authorizations; district security officers running compliance reports; applicants (volunteers, subs, contractors) submitting consent forms
target_users.rationale: Four distinct roles with different permissions in the clearance workflow

project_class.rationale: Multi-role web application with complex authorization model and admin panel

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, integrations, admin_panel, analytics
capabilities.rationale: React dashboard, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL, Cognito MFA, third-party background check vendor integration, HR admin panel, compliance analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-role authorization, third-party vendor integration, immutable audit log architecture, and automated clearance expiry management

risk_level.rationale: Handles sensitive criminal history check results (CORI/SORI) and controls school facility access authorizations; breach or unauthorized access is a legal and safety risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Background check results containing criminal history are highly sensitive; FCRA restricts access to authorized HR personnel only

external_exposure.rationale: All access requires Cognito authentication; applicant portal is accessible only after receiving an invitation link

access_model.value: RBAC with MFA required for all roles: applicant (submit only), hr_admin (full clearance management), principal (read-only facility access view), district_security (compliance reports and admin override)
access_model.rationale: Granular role model required by FCRA and state school safety mandates

feature_scope.value: Applicant consent form portal, background check request submission to third-party vendor API, real-time status tracking, clearance approval workflow, access authorization record management, automated expiry alerts and batch revocations, HR admin panel, compliance audit reports, immutable audit log
feature_scope.rationale: Complete clearance lifecycle management from application to expiry

mvp_scope.value: Applicant portal with consent form, background check vendor integration, clearance status workflow (pending/approved/denied/expired), HR admin review dashboard, access authorization records, immutable audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Core clearance lifecycle sufficient for district pilot

security_baseline.value: Cognito MFA required for all roles, AES-256 encryption at rest for background check result data, TLS 1.3 in transit, immutable append-only audit log table with no UPDATE/DELETE permissions granted to application role, session timeout 30 minutes
security_baseline.rationale: State school safety mandate minimum controls; FCRA requires audit trail for all consumer report access

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; TypeScript strict mode enforced for security-sensitive forms

backend_stack.rationale: Async endpoints for vendor webhook integration; Pydantic for strict input validation

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS Multi-AZ
data_platform.rationale: Relational integrity for clearance workflow states; Multi-AZ for HA; row-level security for multi-district isolation

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with Multi-AZ deployment
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with data residency in US; Fargate eliminates EC2 management overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Background check results retained 7 years per FCRA; audit logs retained permanently; clearance records retained until expiry plus 7 years; automated deletion job for expired data beyond retention window
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FCRA 7-year consumer report retention requirement; state mandate for school safety record retention

future_scope.value: Multi-district SaaS deployment with tenant isolation, mobile app for principals checking clearance status on-site, direct LDAP sync for cleared individuals with school access control systems
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 features after single-district pilot validation

constraints.value: Background check result data must not be exposed to applicants; audit log table must be append-only with no application-level DELETE or UPDATE permissions; system must operate entirely in AWS us-east-1 for data residency
constraints.rationale: FCRA restricts consumer report disclosure; state data residency requirement

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging with correlation IDs, PagerDuty alerts for vendor webhook failures and clearance batch job errors
observability_baseline.rationale: District IT standard monitoring stack

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; security review gate at end of each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: High-risk project requires sprint-level security validation

compliance_context.value: CORI/SORI compliance for state criminal and sex offender registry checks; FCRA compliance for background check consumer report usage, adverse action notices, and dispute rights; state school volunteer clearance mandates; district policy requiring annual clearance renewal
compliance_context.rationale: Multiple overlapping federal and state compliance frameworks govern background check collection and use in K-12 settings

project.goal: Background check and clearance tracking system for K-12 district volunteers, substitutes, and contractors
project.target_users: HR admins, school principals, district security officers, applicants
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks for fall school year launch

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for role-based UI state

security.auth: Cognito with MFA required for all roles
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.audit: Append-only audit_log table with no application DELETE permissions

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with row-level security
data.cache: Redis 7 for session data and clearance status caching

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with security scan gate

constraints.result_visibility: background check results never exposed to applicant role

open_questions.vendor_api: Which background check vendor to integrate: Sterling or Checkr

reasoner_reviews.product: The four-role permission model (applicant, hr_admin, principal, district_security) correctly reflects the clearance workflow. The automated expiry batch job is critical for legal compliance -- districts face liability if expired clearances remain active. The adverse action notice workflow required by FCRA when denying based on background check results was missing from rounds 1 and 2 and must be addressed.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with async webhooks is the right pattern for background check vendor callbacks. The immutable audit log must be enforced at the database level (REVOKE DELETE, UPDATE on audit_log FROM app_role) not just the application level -- application-layer enforcement is insufficient for a legal compliance requirement. Row-level security on PostgreSQL for multi-district isolation is correctly identified. The round 2 plan did not include the FCRA adverse action notice workflow -- this is a legal requirement when denying facility access based on background check results.
reasoner_reviews.security: Cognito MFA for all roles is mandatory. Background check result data (CORI/SORI report contents) must be encrypted at the column level in addition to storage encryption -- store as KMS-encrypted text, decrypt only in the HR admin API response after RBAC check. The FCRA requires background check vendors to provide a pre-adverse-action notice period (typically 5 business days) before final denial -- the system must track this wait period state.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: RDS Multi-AZ with automated failover meets the HA requirement. The 7-year FCRA retention with automated deletion job must be tested carefully -- deleting the wrong records would be a compliance violation. The append-only audit log constraint must be implemented as a database-level REVOKE on UPDATE/DELETE, not a FastAPI middleware check.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with route-based code splitting by role: /applicant/* (portal routes), /hr/* (admin dashboard routes), /principal/* (read-only facility access view), /compliance/* (district security reports). Role guard HOC redirects unauthorized route access based on Cognito JWT group claims.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Applicant Portal: invitation-link landing -> consent form (personal info, release authorization, electronic signature) -> submission confirmation with status polling page. HR Admin Dashboard: pending review queue with applicant name, check type, submission date; clearance detail view showing vendor status (not raw CORI/SORI result -- only pass/fail and check date); approve/deny workflow with required reason field; pre-adverse-action notice tracking panel. Principal View: read-only grid of cleared individuals with name, clearance type, expiry date, facility authorization status. District Security: compliance report with expiry timeline, expired clearance revocation batch actions, audit log viewer.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Background check result fields never included in API responses for applicant role -- enforced by backend RBAC, verified by frontend role guard. Electronic consent form signature captured as typed name + timestamp + IP address hash, stored immutably.

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with role-scoped routers: /applicant (consent form, status), /hr-admin (clearance management, vendor webhook, adverse action), /principal (read-only access view), /compliance (audit reports, batch expiry management). SQLAlchemy with asyncpg driver for async PostgreSQL queries.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful OpenAPI 3.0. Vendor webhook endpoint POST /webhooks/background-check/{vendor_id} receives check completion callbacks and updates clearance status. Batch job endpoint POST /admin/batch/expire-clearances triggered by EventBridge Scheduler nightly.
backend.failure_handling: Vendor webhook idempotency: check vendor_request_id against processed_webhooks table before updating clearance status. Background check submission retry: exponential backoff with 3 retries on vendor API timeout. Batch job failure: write job_failure record to batch_job_logs and trigger PagerDuty alert.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with MFA mandatory for all user groups (applicant, hr_admin, principal, district_security). Applicant accounts created via invitation token -- no self-registration. JWT group claims verified in FastAPI dependency for every endpoint. Session timeout: 30-minute Cognito token expiry with no silent refresh for HR admin and district security roles.
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependencies: applicant sees own records only (row filter by cognito_sub), hr_admin sees all records in their district, principal sees access_authorizations for their school_id only, district_security sees all district records. Background check result details (CORI/SORI content) accessible only to hr_admin and district_security.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_log table: INSERT-only at database level via REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON audit_log FROM app_role; GRANT INSERT, SELECT ON audit_log TO app_role. Every clearance status change, adverse action notice, access authorization grant/revoke, and background check result access logged with actor, action, resource_id, and ip_address. Cloudtrail for AWS API actions.
security.incident_response_notes: Unauthorized access to background check results triggers automatic account lock and PagerDuty critical alert. FCRA breach notification procedure documented in ops runbook.

data.entities: applicants (id, cognito_sub, first_name, last_name, email_encrypted, dob_encrypted, applicant_type ENUM volunteer/substitute/contractor, invited_by_hr_admin_id, invited_at), background_check_requests (id, applicant_id FK, vendor_id, vendor_request_id, check_types ENUM cori/sori/criminal_federal, status ENUM submitted/pending/completed/failed, result_summary ENUM pass/fail, result_encrypted TEXT, submitted_at, completed_at), clearance_statuses (id, applicant_id FK, request_id FK, status ENUM pending/pre_adverse_action/approved/denied/expired, adverse_action_notice_sent_at, adverse_action_final_at, approved_by_hr_admin_id, approved_at, expires_at), access_authorizations (id, applicant_id FK, clearance_id FK, school_id, authorized_by_principal_id, authorized_at, revoked_at, revoke_reason), audit_log (id BIGSERIAL, actor_id, actor_role, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, detail_json, created_at -- NO updated_at, NO delete permitted)
data.schema_notes: result_encrypted column in background_check_requests stores KMS-encrypted CORI/SORI content; decrypted only in API response layer after hr_admin RBAC check. Row-level security policy on applicants and background_check_requests enforces district_id isolation for future multi-district deployment.
data.retention_and_deletion: Automated Lambda triggered by EventBridge monthly: marks background_check_requests where completed_at < NOW() - 7 years as archived, moves result_encrypted to S3 Glacier with KMS, nullifies in-database column. audit_log records never deleted. Access_authorizations soft-deleted only (revoked_at set, record preserved).

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster us-east-1 with two AZs (1a, 1c): API service (2-4 tasks, target CPU 60%), batch-worker service (1 task, triggered by EventBridge). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ primary in 1a, standby in 1c. Redis ElastiCache for session caching. ALB with WAF rules. CloudFront for React SPA static assets.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff, eslint), unit tests, Bandit security scan (fail on high/medium), build Docker image, push ECR, deploy ECS rolling update (max 50% replacement). Security gate: Trivy container scan fails build on critical CVEs. Migrations via Alembic applied as ECS one-off task before service update.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task. Custom metrics: clearance_request_submitted, vendor_webhook_received, adverse_action_notice_sent, clearance_expired, batch_expiry_job_duration. PagerDuty alerts: vendor webhook failure streak > 3, batch expiry job failure, error rate > 1%.

ISS-001.title: FCRA adverse action notice workflow missing from plan
ISS-001.detail: FCRA requires a pre-adverse-action notice (5 business day wait period) before final denial when a background check result is the basis for denial; rounds 1 and 2 did not include this workflow or the clearance_statuses pre_adverse_action state.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer
Addressed in security section but clearance state machine and API design not updated

ISS-002.title: Audit log append-only constraint enforced only at application layer
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan added FastAPI middleware to block audit log DELETE operations but did not include the database-level REVOKE statement; application-layer enforcement alone is insufficient for legal compliance.

history.note: Raised by security reviewer
FastAPI middleware added but database REVOKE not specified

ISS-003.title: Background check result column not encrypted at column level
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 relied on storage-level encryption only; FCRA-sensitive CORI/SORI result data requires column-level KMS encryption so that database administrators cannot access raw results without explicit decryption.

history.note: Identified in data model review

focus_issues.title: FCRA adverse action notice workflow missing from plan
focus_issues.detail: FCRA requires pre-adverse-action notice with 5 business day wait before final denial; clearance state machine and API design do not include this required workflow.

focus_issues.title: Background check result column not encrypted at column level
focus_issues.detail: Storage encryption alone is insufficient; CORI/SORI result data needs KMS column-level encryption.

latest_recommendations: Add pre_adverse_action state to clearance_statuses and implement the 5 business day wait period tracking with automated final denial after wait expires
Add KMS column-level encryption for result_encrypted in background_check_requests with decryption only in hr_admin API response
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: K-12 Background Check Clearance Management System Round 2

Cognito MFA mandatory for all roles is well-specified
Vendor webhook idempotency pattern is correct

Add result_encrypted column with application-level KMS encrypt/decrypt using AWS KMS; decrypt only after hr_admin RBAC check in API layer

previous_plan.title: K-12 Background Check Clearance Management System Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Security-hardened fullstack application on AWS ECS Fargate managing background check submissions, clearance tracking, and facility access authorizations for K-12 district volunteers and contractors with Cognito MFA and immutable audit logging.
previous_plan.data_model: applicants, background_check_requests (result stored as plaintext encrypted at RDS storage level only), clearance_statuses (status ENUM: pending/approved/denied/expired -- no pre_adverse_action state), access_authorizations, audit_log (REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE added).
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA, RBAC, TLS 1.3, audit log REVOKE at DB level. No FCRA adverse action workflow. No KMS column encryption for background check results.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for the K-12 background check clearance management system. Two focus issues: ISS-001 (FCRA adverse action workflow missing -- high severity legal compliance gap) and ISS-003 (background check result column needs KMS encryption beyond storage-level). Fix ISS-001 by adding pre_adverse_action state to clearance_statuses, implementing adverse action notice tracking with EventBridge-scheduled 5-business-day wait period check, and adding adverse action workflow steps to the HR admin dashboard. Fix ISS-003 by adding KMS column encryption for result_encrypted with application-level encrypt/decrypt, never decrypting for applicant role. ISS-002 (audit log DB REVOKE) was resolved in round 2.

fix_report.action_taken: Added pre_adverse_action to clearance_statuses.status ENUM; added adverse_action_notice_sent_at and adverse_action_final_at timestamp columns; implemented EventBridge Scheduler daily check that transitions pre_adverse_action records to denied after 5 business days from notice sent timestamp; added FCRA adverse action workflow steps to workflows section and HR admin dashboard pages_and_flows; updated api_design with PATCH /hr-admin/clearances/{id}/adverse-action-notice endpoint
fix_report.expected_outcome: FCRA pre-adverse-action 5-business-day wait period is tracked in the system state machine; HR admin cannot skip the notice period; automated batch job transitions to final denial after wait period; audit log records every adverse action step

fix_report.action_taken: Added KMS key ARN configuration for background check result encryption; FastAPI service encrypts result_json before writing result_encrypted column using boto3 KMS encrypt; decrypts using KMS decrypt only in hr_admin and district_security role API responses; applicant role API never receives or decrypts result_encrypted data; updated data_model and security_and_compliance sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Even if RDS storage encryption were bypassed, background check CORI/SORI result data remains encrypted with a KMS key accessible only to the app role; DBA cannot read raw results; access to KMS key logged in CloudTrail

target_output.title: K-12 Background Check and Clearance Management System
target_output.executive_summary: A security-hardened fullstack application on AWS ECS Fargate enabling K-12 districts to manage the full clearance lifecycle for volunteers, substitute teachers, and contractors: consent form submission, third-party background check vendor integration, FCRA-compliant adverse action notice workflow, clearance approval/expiry management, and facility access authorization tracking. React 18 dashboard with role-gated views for HR administrators, school principals, and district security officers. PostgreSQL with row-level security and KMS column encryption for CORI/SORI result data. Immutable append-only audit log enforced at the database level. Cognito MFA required for all authenticated roles.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: React 18 SPA served via CloudFront communicating over HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ with row-level security for district isolation and REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE on audit_log enforced at database level. Redis ElastiCache for session caching. Cognito user pool with mandatory MFA for all roles. Third-party background check vendor integration via outbound REST API and inbound webhook endpoint. EventBridge Scheduler triggers nightly batch jobs: clearance expiry processing and FCRA adverse action wait-period transitions. KMS key used for column-level encryption of CORI/SORI result data in background_check_requests. Datadog APM captures clearance workflow metrics and PagerDuty receives alerts on vendor webhook failures and batch job errors.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, React Router v6. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy with asyncpg, Alembic, boto3 (KMS, SES). Database: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 on ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate Multi-AZ, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, Cognito, KMS, S3 (for archived result data), EventBridge Scheduler, SES. Observability: Datadog APM, CloudWatch Logs, PagerDuty. CI: GitHub Actions with Bandit and Trivy.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Applicant Portal: invitation link received by email, consent form with personal info and FCRA disclosure acknowledgment, electronic signature, submission confirmation and status polling. HR Admin Dashboard: pending review queue, applicant details view (pass/fail only -- no raw CORI content visible to UI), clearance approval workflow, pre-adverse-action notice trigger with FCRA disclosure template, adverse action notice tracking panel showing days remaining in 5-business-day wait period, final denial confirmation. Principal View: read-only grid of active access authorizations for their school, clearance expiry dates, badge of expired or expiring-soon clearances. District Security Compliance Panel: clearance expiry timeline report, batch revocation of expired clearances, full audit log viewer with actor/action/timestamp filter. Automated Batch Jobs: nightly clearance expiry check with email notifications to HR admin 30 days before expiry, FCRA adverse action wait-period transition (pre_adverse_action to denied after 5 business days), monthly data archival of results beyond 7-year retention window.
target_output.system_components: CloudFront CDN (React SPA static assets), ALB with WAF (OWASP rule set, rate limiting), ECS Fargate API service (2-4 tasks, Multi-AZ), ECS Fargate batch-worker service (EventBridge Scheduler triggered), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (row-level security, audit_log REVOKE DELETE/UPDATE), Redis ElastiCache (session cache, clearance status cache), Cognito user pool (MFA mandatory, invitation-only applicant registration), KMS key (background check result column encryption), S3 (archived result data in Glacier), SES (adverse action notice emails, expiry reminder emails), EventBridge Scheduler (nightly batch triggers), Datadog APM sidecar, CloudWatch Logs, PagerDuty.
target_output.workflows: Background Check Submission and FCRA Adverse Action Workflow: HR admin creates applicant invitation; applicant receives email link, completes consent form with FCRA disclosure acknowledgment and electronic signature; FastAPI submits background check request to vendor API (Sterling or Checkr) with applicant PII encrypted in transit; vendor processes check and sends webhook to POST /webhooks/background-check/{vendor_id}; webhook handler validates idempotency key, updates background_check_requests.status to completed, stores KMS-encrypted result in result_encrypted column; if result_summary is fail, HR admin sees pending adverse action notification; HR admin reviews check summary (pass/fail, no raw CORI content), initiates pre-adverse-action by clicking 'Send Pre-Adverse Action Notice' which calls PATCH /hr-admin/clearances/{id}/adverse-action-notice, sets clearance_statuses.status to pre_adverse_action, records adverse_action_notice_sent_at, sends FCRA-compliant email via SES; EventBridge Scheduler daily job checks pre_adverse_action records where adverse_action_notice_sent_at + 5 business days <= NOW() and transitions status to denied, records adverse_action_final_at; HR admin can approve early or extend notice period. Clearance Expiry Batch Workflow: EventBridge triggers nightly batch-worker; worker queries clearances where expires_at <= NOW() + 30 days and status = approved; sends email reminder to HR admin per expiring clearance; marks clearances where expires_at <= NOW() as expired; creates access_authorization revocation records for each expired clearance; logs all transitions to audit_log.
target_output.data_model: applicants (id UUID PK, cognito_sub NOT NULL UNIQUE, first_name, last_name, email_encrypted, dob_encrypted, applicant_type ENUM volunteer/substitute/contractor, district_id FK, invited_by_hr_admin_id FK, invited_at). background_check_requests (id UUID PK, applicant_id FK, district_id FK, vendor_id VARCHAR, vendor_request_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, check_types TEXT[], status ENUM submitted/pending/completed/failed, result_summary ENUM pass/fail NULL, result_encrypted BYTEA -- KMS encrypted CORI/SORI JSON, submitted_at, completed_at). clearance_statuses (id UUID PK, applicant_id FK, request_id FK, district_id FK, status ENUM pending/pre_adverse_action/approved/denied/expired, adverse_action_notice_sent_at TIMESTAMP NULL, adverse_action_final_at TIMESTAMP NULL, approved_by_hr_admin_id FK NULL, approved_at TIMESTAMP NULL, denied_reason TEXT NULL, expires_at TIMESTAMP NULL). access_authorizations (id UUID PK, applicant_id FK, clearance_id FK, school_id FK, authorized_by_principal_id FK, authorized_at, revoked_at NULL, revoke_reason NULL). audit_log (id BIGSERIAL PK, actor_id UUID, actor_role VARCHAR, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, detail_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW() -- REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON audit_log FROM app_role enforced at DB level).
target_output.api_design: FastAPI RESTful OpenAPI 3.0 at /api/v1. Applicant: POST /applicant/consent-form (submit form, create applicant + check request), GET /applicant/clearance-status (own status only). HR Admin: GET /hr-admin/clearances?status=pending (review queue), GET /hr-admin/clearances/{id} (detail with pass/fail -- no raw CORI), PATCH /hr-admin/clearances/{id}/approve (set approved, set expires_at), PATCH /hr-admin/clearances/{id}/adverse-action-notice (trigger FCRA pre-adverse-action, set notice_sent_at), PATCH /hr-admin/clearances/{id}/deny (final denial after wait period). Principal: GET /principal/access-authorizations?school_id={id} (own school only). District Security: GET /compliance/clearances/expiry-report, POST /compliance/batch/revoke-expired, GET /compliance/audit-log (paginated). Webhook: POST /webhooks/background-check/{vendor_id} (vendor callback, HMAC signature verified). Batch trigger: POST /internal/batch/expire-clearances (EventBridge only, no external auth).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Cognito MFA mandatory for all user groups via user pool policy. JWT group claims verified in FastAPI role dependency on every endpoint. Applicant role restricted to own records via cognito_sub row filter -- result_encrypted never decrypted for applicant role responses. KMS column encryption: background check result stored as boto3 kms_client.encrypt() output in result_encrypted BYTEA column; decrypted with kms_client.decrypt() only in hr_admin/district_security GET /hr-admin/clearances/{id} after RBAC check; KMS key usage logged in CloudTrail. Audit log immutability: REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE ON audit_log FROM app_role at database level; every clearance status change, adverse action step, access authorization grant/revoke, and result_encrypted access logged. FCRA compliance: pre-adverse-action ENUM state with notice timestamp; 5-business-day wait period enforced by EventBridge batch job before final denial transition; FCRA-compliant adverse action email template via SES. CORI/SORI compliance: check results accessible only to HR admin and district security; applicant never sees result content. Session timeout: Cognito token validity 30 minutes for all roles. WAF rules: OWASP managed rule set, SQL injection protection, rate limiting 100 req/min per user.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster us-east-1 with tasks in AZs 1a and 1c: api-service (2-4 tasks, CPU 60% scale trigger, rolling update max 50% replacement), batch-worker service (1 task, triggered by EventBridge Scheduler at 02:00 UTC). RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ (primary 1a, standby 1c, automated failover). Redis ElastiCache (single node dev, Multi-AZ cluster prod). GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest (85% coverage gate), Bandit security scan (block on HIGH/MEDIUM), Trivy container scan (block on CRITICAL), build Docker image, push ECR, deploy ECS rolling update, run Alembic migration as ECS run-task before service update. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared RDS), staging (Multi-AZ mirror), production (Multi-AZ, auto-scaling). Rollback: previous ECR image redeploy via GitHub Actions manual trigger. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager (RDS credentials, KMS key ARN, vendor API keys, SES credentials) with 30-day rotation for DB password.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on every ECS task. Structured JSON logs to CloudWatch with correlation IDs. Custom Datadog metrics: clearance_submitted_total, vendor_webhook_received_total (by vendor_id), adverse_action_notice_sent_total, clearance_expired_total, batch_job_duration_seconds (by job_name), kms_decrypt_calls_total (rate spike may indicate unauthorized access). PagerDuty escalations: vendor webhook failure streak > 3 (CRITICAL), batch expiry job failure (HIGH), API error rate > 1% (HIGH), KMS decrypt rate anomaly (CRITICAL). CloudTrail for all AWS API actions including KMS key usage.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated USD 450-600/month at district scale (500 active clearances, 50 daily check submissions): ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks (~USD 120), RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.medium (~USD 130), ElastiCache r6g.large (~USD 80), ALB + WAF (~USD 50), CloudFront + S3 (~USD 20), KMS (~USD 5), SES + EventBridge (~USD 20), Datadog (~USD 150). Scale to 5000 active clearances requires only ECS task count increase and RDS instance upgrade -- no architectural changes needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-6): Applicant consent form portal, background check vendor API integration (Sterling), vendor webhook endpoint, clearance status workflow with pre_adverse_action state (ISS-001 fix), KMS column encryption for result_encrypted (ISS-003 fix), Cognito MFA for all roles, audit log with DB REVOKE, HR admin review dashboard with adverse action notice UI, basic access authorization records. Phase 2 (Weeks 7-10): Principal view with school-scoped access authorization grid, clearance expiry batch job with email reminders, EventBridge FCRA wait-period transition job, district security compliance report, FCRA-compliant SES email templates. Phase 3 (Weeks 11-12): Multi-district row-level security preparation, Checkr vendor option integration, Datadog custom dashboards and PagerDuty alert tuning, 7-year retention archival Lambda, audit log viewer in compliance panel.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff linting and Bandit security scan in CI. TypeScript strict mode enforced for React frontend. FastAPI endpoints must use role dependency for every route -- no unauthenticated endpoints except POST /webhooks (HMAC verified). result_encrypted must never appear in API response schemas for applicant role Pydantic models. SQLAlchemy ORM only -- no raw SQL strings except for audit_log REVOKE migration. pytest target 85% coverage with integration tests for clearance state machine transitions. PR requires 1 senior review plus security team approval for any changes to auth, RBAC, or audit log modules. FCRA adverse action notice email template changes require legal review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Background check vendor API downtime delays clearance processing during school year hiring surge. Mitigation: exponential backoff retry with 24-hour SQS dead letter queue for failed submissions; HR admin dashboard shows vendor API status indicator. Risk: FCRA 5-business-day wait period calculation must account for weekends and federal holidays -- incorrect calculation is a legal compliance violation. Mitigation: use a holiday-aware business day library (python-holidays) in the EventBridge batch job; log each transition with calculated wait period duration. Tradeoff: KMS encryption adds 1-5ms latency per decrypt call for background check result views; acceptable given the access frequency (HR admin reviews, not high-throughput reads). Tradeoff: separate pre_adverse_action ENUM state adds state machine complexity but is required for FCRA compliance -- cannot simplify to a direct pending-to-denied transition.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Sterling as initial background check vendor (supports CORI/SORI via webhook callbacks); Checkr integration added in Phase 3 as alternative. Confirmed KMS key per district (not per application) for future multi-district tenant isolation. FCRA adverse action wait period implemented as 5 calendar business days using python-holidays for US federal holiday exclusion. Electronic consent signature stored as typed name, timestamp, and IP address hash -- legal confirmed this is sufficient for FCRA disclosure acknowledgment.

metadata.notes: K-12 background check clearance management; round 3 fixes FCRA adverse action workflow and KMS column encryption; high-security fullstack app with immutable audit log.

profile.domain: Private K-12 School Admissions Landing Page — Performance-Optimized International Visitor Experience

project_goal.value: A performance-optimized static landing page for a private K-12 school's admissions department showcasing academic programs, extracurricular activities, and campus tour scheduling. Target sub-2-second LCP on mobile across all international visitor regions to maximize admissions inquiry conversion.
project_goal.rationale: School competes for international student enrollment; page load performance directly affects application conversion from families in Asia, Europe, and South America.

target_users.value: Prospective K-12 student families including international visitors across all major geographies; prospective students browsing on mobile devices.
target_users.rationale: Admissions funnel is international; mobile-first browsing is the primary access pattern for prospective families.

project_class.rationale: Static site with no server-side runtime; all content generated at build time; no backend database.

capabilities.rationale: Static HTML/CSS/JS with analytics event tracking; no backend, auth, or data persistence.

complexity_level.rationale: Static content site; complexity is in performance optimization, not system architecture.

risk_level.rationale: No user data collected beyond analytics events; no authentication; no database.

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personally identifiable information collected on the landing page; tour scheduling handled by embedded Calendly widget (third-party).

external_exposure.rationale: Public admissions marketing page; no authentication required.

access_model.value: Fully public, no authentication. Content management via Git commits triggering Vercel preview and production deployments.
access_model.rationale: Static site; content updates are deployment events.

feature_scope.value: Academic programs showcase pages with program-specific photography and faculty highlights. Extracurricular activities gallery with image optimization. Campus tour scheduling via embedded Calendly widget. Admissions events calendar (static, updated at build time). Student and parent testimonials carousel. International admissions FAQ section. Multi-language support for English, Mandarin, and Spanish. Analytics conversion tracking for program page views and Calendly embed interactions.
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope for the admissions marketing funnel.

mvp_scope.value: English-language pages for academic programs, campus tour Calendly embed, analytics conversion tracking, and Lighthouse CI performance gate at LCP < 2s on mobile. Multi-language support in Phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: English MVP validates performance targets before internationalizing.

security_baseline.value: Vercel edge network DDoS protection; HTTPS enforced via Vercel (HSTS header); Content Security Policy header restricting script-src to self and Calendly CDN; no cookies except Vercel Analytics first-party cookie.
security_baseline.rationale: Static landing page attack surface is limited; CSP and HSTS are the primary controls.

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS v3; @astrojs/image for automatic AVIF/WebP generation and responsive srcset; Vercel Analytics for web vitals tracking.
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro's zero-JS-by-default island architecture eliminates unnecessary client-side JavaScript; @astrojs/image handles modern format generation at build time.

backend_stack.value: none — fully static; no server-side runtime
backend_stack.rationale: Landing page with no server-side data requirements.

data_platform.value: none — all content in Astro Markdown/MDX files and static JSON data files; no database
data_platform.rationale: Static content model; updates via Git and redeploy.

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network for global static asset delivery; automatic edge CDN from 35+ PoPs including Asia Pacific, Europe, and South America.
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel Edge Network provides the geographic distribution needed for sub-2s LCP in target international regions.

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected or retained on the landing page itself; Vercel Analytics collects aggregate web vitals with no personally identifiable data; Calendly handles tour scheduling data under their own privacy policy.
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No data retention obligation for the school's own systems.

future_scope.value: Multi-language support (Mandarin, Spanish) via Astro i18n integration; video tour feature with optimized streaming; alumni spotlight blog section with CMS integration (Sanity or Contentful).
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 internationalization targets the school's primary international applicant markets.

constraints.value: LCP under 2 seconds on mobile in Lighthouse CI for all primary pages; Lighthouse performance score >= 95 on mobile; no third-party JavaScript beyond Vercel Analytics snippet and Calendly embed; AVIF/WebP images only (no legacy JPEG/PNG for hero images).
constraints.rationale: Performance SLAs are the primary product requirement for conversion optimization.

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) tracked per page and per geography; Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions gates every deployment on performance score >= 95; Vercel deployment status notifications to admissions team Slack.
observability_baseline.rationale: Performance regression detection requires automated Lighthouse gating on every deployment.

execution_preference.value: Git-based content management: admissions staff edit Markdown files in a GitHub web UI; merges to main trigger Vercel production deployment. Design team uses Vercel preview deployments for review before promotion.
execution_preference.rationale: Non-technical admissions staff can manage content without a CMS admin UI for MVP.

project.goal: Sub-2s LCP admissions landing page for international K-12 school applicants
project.target_users: Prospective international families browsing on mobile
project.timeline: Live before spring admissions open house

frontend.images: @astrojs/image for AVIF/WebP generation and responsive srcset

security.third_party: Vercel Analytics and Calendly embed only

data.content: Astro Markdown/MDX files and static JSON

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI gate

reasoner_reviews.product: The admissions landing page scope is well-defined. The Calendly embed is the right choice for tour scheduling — avoids building a booking system and Calendly is familiar to admissions staff. Conversion tracking on Calendly embed interactions (using Calendly's event_scheduled postMessage) is essential for measuring campaign ROI from international markets.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro 4 with zero-JS islands is the right architecture for this performance target. The hero image is the primary LCP element — it must be preloaded with <link rel=preload> and served as AVIF from a Vercel Edge Cache node close to the visitor. Tailwind CSS purging at build time ensures minimal CSS payload. The Calendly embed should be loaded as an Astro island with client:visible directive so it does not block initial page render.
reasoner_reviews.security: The Content Security Policy needs to explicitly whitelist https://calendly.com and https://assets.calendly.com in the script-src and frame-src directives. Vercel's HSTS header with includeSubDomains and a 2-year max-age is appropriate. No cookie consent banner is needed since Vercel Analytics does not use tracking cookies — this simplifies the mobile experience.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: LCP < 2s on mobile in emerging markets (India, Brazil, Southeast Asia) requires Vercel Edge PoPs in those regions plus image AVIF delivery. Recommend testing Lighthouse from lighthouse-ci with a simulated 4G throttle (not fast 3G) for a realistic mobile benchmark in these markets.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with src/pages/ for route pages (index.astro, programs/[slug].astro, extracurriculars.astro, admissions.astro), src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro with HTML head optimization (preconnect, preload hints), src/components/ for islands (CalendlyEmbed.tsx as client:visible island, ProgramCard.astro, TestimonialsCarousel.astro as client:idle island), src/content/ for Markdown collections (programs, testimonials, events). public/ for favicons and manifests only — all images processed through @astrojs/image.
frontend.pages_and_flows: / (home): hero with AVIF background, program highlights, CTA to campus tour. /programs/[slug]: individual program pages with faculty highlights and curriculum overview. /extracurriculars: activity gallery with lazy-loaded AVIF thumbnails. /admissions: Calendly embed island, upcoming events static list, international admissions FAQ accordion.
frontend.performance_notes: Hero image: <link rel=preload as=image type=image/avif> in BaseLayout.astro head. Calendly embed loaded with client:visible to defer until user scrolls to section. TestimonialsCarousel loaded with client:idle. Tailwind CSS purged to < 12KB at build time. No web fonts — system font stack (ui-sans-serif, system-ui) for zero font load time. Critical CSS inlined in <head> by Astro's built-in CSS optimization.
frontend.frontend_risks: Calendly embed iframe blocks LCP if loaded eagerly — must use client:visible Astro island directive. AVIF support on older Android WebView versions (pre-2021) requires WebP fallback in <picture> srcset — @astrojs/image generates both formats automatically.

security.auth_design: No authentication — fully public landing page.
security.abuse_prevention: Vercel Edge Network provides DDoS mitigation at the CDN layer. No server-side endpoints to protect. Rate limiting not applicable to static assets.
security.privacy_controls: Vercel Analytics collects Core Web Vitals using the web-vitals library with no user fingerprinting or cookie-based tracking. No cookie consent banner required. Calendly handles booking data under its own privacy policy — school does not receive PII from Calendly embed interactions beyond conversion event metadata from postMessage.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Vercel deployment logs retained for 30 days. Vercel Analytics aggregate metrics retained indefinitely. No application-level audit logging required for static content delivery.

data.entities: academic_programs (Astro content collection: slug, name, grade_levels, description, faculty_highlights[], curriculum_overview, hero_image_path, og_image_path). extracurricular_activities (content collection: slug, name, category, description, photo_path). campus_tour_slots (static JSON: managed via Calendly — not in codebase). admissions_events (static JSON: title, date, description, registration_url). testimonials (content collection: quote, author_name, graduation_year, country_of_origin, photo_path).
data.schema_notes: All content collections defined with Astro z.object() schema for build-time type safety and validation. Images referenced by path and resolved by @astrojs/image at build time — missing images cause build failure, preventing broken deployments.
data.retention_and_deletion: No personal data stored or retained. Content collections are Git-managed; deletions are Git commits. Vercel Analytics data retained per Vercel's data policy.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository main branch. Production deployment auto-triggered on merge to main. Preview deployments auto-triggered on PR open — each PR gets a unique preview URL shared in GitHub PR comment. Vercel Edge Network serves built assets from 35+ global PoPs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: astro check (TypeScript type checking), ESLint, Lighthouse CI against production-equivalent Vercel preview URL (lighthouse-ci with mobile preset, assert LCP < 2000ms and performance >= 95). Lighthouse CI results posted as PR comment. Merge blocked if Lighthouse gate fails.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics dashboard: Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) segmented by page, device type, and country. Lighthouse CI historical scores in GitHub Actions artifacts. Vercel deployment status Slack webhook to admissions team channel.
devops.cost_controls: Vercel Pro plan (~$20/month) covers unlimited bandwidth for static assets and 100GB storage. Lighthouse CI runs on GitHub Actions free tier. Zero server compute cost — static delivery only.

ISS-001.title: Hero image not preloaded — Calendly embed loaded eagerly blocking LCP
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not include <link rel=preload> for the hero AVIF image in BaseLayout.astro head, and the Calendly embed was specified as a standard script tag rather than an Astro client:visible island. Both issues directly cause LCP > 2s on mobile by blocking or delaying the largest contentful paint element.

history.note: Performance SLA of LCP < 2s cannot be met without hero preload and deferred Calendly loading.

focus_issues.title: Hero image not preloaded — Calendly embed loaded eagerly blocking LCP
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan missing <link rel=preload> for hero AVIF and Calendly loaded as eager script — both cause LCP regression above 2s on mobile.

latest_recommendations: Add <link rel=preload as=image type=image/avif> for hero image in BaseLayout.astro head.
Convert Calendly embed to Astro client:visible island to defer loading until the section enters the viewport.

Both omissions will cause measurable LCP regression on mobile.
strengths: Astro 4 zero-JS island architecture is correctly specified
AVIF/WebP via @astrojs/image is the right image optimization approach
