previous_plan.title: K-12 Admissions Landing Page — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Astro 4 static site on Vercel Edge Network targeting sub-2s LCP for international mobile visitors. AVIF/WebP images via @astrojs/image. Calendly embed for tour scheduling. Lighthouse CI gate in GitHub Actions.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generation. Tailwind CSS. @astrojs/image for modern format generation. Vercel Edge CDN for global delivery. Calendly embed via script tag on admissions page.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse gate. Vercel auto-deploy on main merge. Preview deployments per PR.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 with two specific additions: hero image preload link in BaseLayout.astro head with fetchpriority=high, and Calendly embed converted to an Astro island with client:visible directive. Both changes directly address the LCP regression. The CSP header must be updated to explicitly whitelist calendly.com script-src and frame-src since the Calendly island still loads the Calendly script — just deferred until visible.

fix_report.action_taken: Added <link rel=preload as=image type=image/avif fetchpriority=high href={heroImageUrl}> to BaseLayout.astro <head> section, ensuring the hero AVIF is fetched at highest browser priority before CSS and JavaScript. Converted CalendlyEmbed component from an eager <script> tag to an Astro island (CalendlyEmbed.tsx) with client:visible directive so Calendly scripts load only when the admissions section scrolls into the viewport. Updated CSP header in vercel.json to add https://calendly.com and https://assets.calendly.com to both script-src and frame-src directives.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved — hero preload eliminates LCP discovery delay; deferred Calendly removes render-blocking from initial page load; CSP updated to permit Calendly scripts only when the island activates.

target_output.title: K-12 Admissions Landing Page — Performance-Optimized Global Delivery with Astro 4 and Vercel Edge
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 landing page for a private K-12 school's admissions department, deployed to Vercel's Edge Network for sub-2-second LCP on mobile across international visitor geographies including Asia Pacific, Europe, and South America. Zero-JS island architecture eliminates unnecessary client-side JavaScript — only the Calendly tour scheduling embed (deferred with client:visible) and Vercel Analytics snippet load on the client. Hero images generated as AVIF with WebP fallback by @astrojs/image at build time and preloaded in the HTML head with fetchpriority=high. Lighthouse CI gates every deployment at LCP < 2000ms and performance score >= 95 on mobile.
target_output.architecture_overview: Astro 4 static site generation produces pure HTML files with inlined critical CSS from Tailwind's purged output at build time. Each page is a .astro file with content sourced from Astro content collections (Markdown/MDX for academic programs, extracurriculars, testimonials) and static JSON (admissions events). Server-side JavaScript runs only at build time — zero server runtime in production. BaseLayout.astro injects <link rel=preload as=image type=image/avif fetchpriority=high> for the above-the-fold hero image on each page. CalendlyEmbed.tsx is an Astro island loaded with client:visible — Calendly scripts are deferred until the admissions section scrolls into view, preventing any render-blocking on initial load. Vercel Edge Network serves all assets from 35+ PoPs; Vercel's CDN layer handles DDoS protection and HTTPS termination. GitHub Actions runs Lighthouse CI against the Vercel preview deployment on each PR, blocking merge if LCP >= 2000ms or performance < 95.
target_output.technology_stack: Static Site Generator: Astro 4.8 with TypeScript. Styling: Tailwind CSS v3 (purged to < 12KB at build time). Image Optimization: @astrojs/image (AVIF + WebP generation, responsive srcset). Booking Embed: Calendly (client:visible island). Analytics: Vercel Analytics (Core Web Vitals, no PII). Hosting: Vercel Edge Network (35+ global PoPs). CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI (mobile preset, LCP < 2000ms gate, performance >= 95 gate). Content: Astro content collections with z.object() schema validation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Home Page: hero with preloaded AVIF background (fetchpriority=high), program highlights grid linking to program detail pages, CTA button to /admissions tour scheduling section. Academic Programs: /programs/[slug] dynamic routes generated from programs content collection at build time; faculty highlights, curriculum overview, grade level badges. Extracurriculars Gallery: lazy-loaded AVIF thumbnails (loading=lazy, client:idle animation), category filter as static HTML links. Admissions Page: CalendlyEmbed.tsx island (client:visible — loads Calendly JS only when section enters viewport), upcoming events static list, international FAQ accordion (zero-JS CSS-only :target accordion). Testimonials Carousel: client:idle island loading after main content; quotes with student country of origin for international social proof.
target_output.system_components: Astro build pipeline (build-time SSG: content collection loader, @astrojs/image AVIF/WebP generator, Tailwind CSS purger, critical CSS inliner). BaseLayout.astro (HTML head with preload hints, meta tags, CSP meta equivalent, Vercel Analytics snippet). Page components (index.astro, programs/[slug].astro, extracurriculars.astro, admissions.astro). Island components: CalendlyEmbed.tsx (client:visible), TestimonialsCarousel.tsx (client:idle). Vercel Edge Network (static asset CDN, DDoS protection, HTTPS termination, 35+ global PoPs). GitHub Actions CI (Lighthouse CI runner against Vercel preview URLs).
target_output.workflows: Content Update Workflow: admissions staff edits a program Markdown file in GitHub web UI (or via local Git clone), opens a PR; GitHub Actions runs astro check type validation and Lighthouse CI against the auto-generated Vercel preview URL; Lighthouse results posted as PR comment; on passing gates, admissions director merges PR; Vercel triggers production build and deploy within 60 seconds; new content live globally via Edge CDN. Campus Tour Booking: prospective family visits /admissions page; as they scroll, CalendlyEmbed island activates and loads Calendly inline widget; family selects tour date and submits; Calendly postMessage event_scheduled triggers a Vercel Analytics custom event for conversion tracking; admissions staff receives Calendly email notification — no data touches the school's own systems.
target_output.data_model: academic_programs (Astro content collection, z.object schema: slug STRING, name STRING, grade_levels STRING[], description STRING, faculty_highlights ARRAY{name, title, bio}, curriculum_overview STRING, hero_image_path STRING, og_image_path STRING). extracurricular_activities (collection: slug STRING, name STRING, category ENUM arts/sports/stem/service, description STRING, photo_path STRING). admissions_events (static src/data/events.json: title STRING, date STRING ISO8601, description STRING, registration_url STRING). testimonials (collection: quote STRING, author_name STRING, graduation_year INT, country_of_origin STRING, photo_path STRING). No database. All images validated at build time by @astrojs/image — missing image path causes build failure.
target_output.api_design: No API endpoints — fully static. Client-side integrations: Vercel Analytics web-vitals script (inlined in BaseLayout.astro, ~1.5KB); Calendly inline embed (loaded via CalendlyEmbed.tsx island when visible, ~85KB deferred). Conversion event: window.addEventListener('message', (e) => { if (e.data.event === 'calendly.event_scheduled') { va.track('tour_booked') } }) within CalendlyEmbed island.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel with HSTS header: Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload. Content Security Policy via vercel.json headers: script-src 'self' https://assets.calendly.com https://va.vercel-scripts.com; frame-src https://calendly.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' (required for Tailwind critical inline CSS); img-src 'self' data: https://calendly.com; default-src 'none'. Calendly iframe sandboxed with allow='camera; microphone; payment=()'. No cookies set by the school's own systems — Vercel Analytics is cookieless. No personal data collected or stored on school infrastructure.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions workflow on PR: 1) astro check (TypeScript type checking via Astro LSP), 2) ESLint, 3) Vercel preview deployment (triggered by Vercel GitHub integration — URL available as environment variable), 4) Lighthouse CI against preview URL with mobile device emulation and 4G throttle (lhci autorun --collect.url={preview_url} --assert.preset=lighthouse:recommended --assert.assertions.largest-contentful-paint=error:2000 --assert.assertions.interactive=warning:3500), 5) Lighthouse results posted as PR comment via lhci github-app. Merge blocked if any error assertion fails. On merge to main: Vercel production deployment triggered automatically (build time ~45 seconds). Rollback: Vercel instant rollback to any previous deployment via dashboard or CLI (vercel rollback). Content management: admissions staff use GitHub web editor for Markdown files; design team use Vercel branch previews for visual review.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics: Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) tracked per page route and segmented by country, device type (mobile/desktop), and connection speed. Custom conversion events: 'tour_booked' (Calendly event_scheduled), 'program_page_view' (per program slug). Lighthouse CI historical scores stored as GitHub Actions artifacts — performance trend visible in Actions tab. Vercel deployment notifications posted to admissions Slack channel via Vercel GitHub integration comments and optional Slack webhook. No server-side monitoring needed — static CDN delivery.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro: $20/month covers unlimited bandwidth for static assets, 100GB storage, and preview deployments. @astrojs/image build processing adds ~30 seconds to build time with 50 images. Lighthouse CI runs on GitHub Actions free tier (2000 minutes/month). Calendly: managed under school's existing Calendly subscription. Total hosting cost: $20/month regardless of traffic volume — CDN edge delivery scales automatically to any traffic spike during international admissions season.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (2 weeks): Astro 4 project setup with BaseLayout.astro (hero preload, CSP headers), programs content collection (3 flagship programs), admissions page with CalendlyEmbed client:visible island, Vercel Analytics conversion tracking, Lighthouse CI GitHub Actions gate at LCP < 2s. Phase 2 (2 weeks): remaining program pages, extracurriculars gallery with lazy-loaded AVIF thumbnails, testimonials carousel (client:idle), admissions events JSON feed, mobile performance tuning to achieve >= 95 Lighthouse score across all pages. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Astro i18n integration for Mandarin (zh-CN) and Spanish (es) language variants, hreflang meta tags, per-language hero image variants, Vercel Analytics language-segmented conversion tracking.
target_output.development_guidelines: All images must be referenced through @astrojs/image or components — no raw tags except for inline SVGs. Hero images on each page must have a in BaseLayout.astro props (passed as heroImage prop to layout). Calendly embed and any future third-party widget must be implemented as Astro islands with appropriate client: directive (client:visible for below-fold content). Lighthouse CI gate must not be bypassed — PR merge blocked on LCP regression. Tailwind classes are purge-safe by default with Astro integration; avoid dynamic class construction with string concatenation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: AVIF not supported on Android WebView < 2021 and Safari < 16.4. Mitigation: @astrojs/image generates WebP fallback automatically via <picture> srcset — older clients receive WebP. Tradeoff: zero-JS island architecture means testimonials carousel and Calendly embed require explicit Astro island wrappers, adding minor development overhead versus a SPA framework — this overhead is justified by the LCP performance gain. Risk: Calendly embed design changes (new script URL or frame origin) could break CSP. Mitigation: Lighthouse CI will catch CSP violation as a console error in the audit; update vercel.json CSP when Calendly changes are detected.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Hero preload: <link rel=preload as=image type=image/avif fetchpriority=high> in BaseLayout.astro head confirmed — eliminates LCP discovery latency for hero image. Calendly embed: client:visible Astro island confirmed — defers Calendly JS bundle until section is visible, removing it from critical render path. CSP: calendly.com added to script-src and frame-src — required for island activation. Font loading: system font stack (ui-sans-serif, system-ui) confirmed over web fonts — eliminates font render-blocking entirely.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 (hero preload + Calendly deferred island); strong performance-optimized Astro 4 landing page for international K-12 admissions.

project_goal.value: API-first service for K-12 school districts providing a unified standards-alignment API, allowing ed-tech applications to query Common Core and state-specific academic standards by grade, subject, and standard code, and check which curriculum materials are aligned to specific standards
project_goal.rationale: Fragmented standards data across 50 state DOE websites forces every ed-tech vendor to maintain their own standards database; a unified API eliminates this redundancy

target_users.value: Ed-tech application developers and curriculum publishers integrating standards data into their products
target_users.rationale: Developer-facing API; end users are the students and teachers using ed-tech products that consume this API

project_class.rationale: Pure API layer with no frontend; consumed programmatically by ed-tech developer applications

capabilities.rationale: Public API with API key auth, rich standards data model, usage analytics for developer portal

complexity_level.rationale: Hierarchical standards taxonomy with alignment claim workflow adds data modeling complexity; API itself is read-heavy REST

risk_level.rationale: Standards data is publicly available information; no PII, financial, or health data; API key abuse is the primary risk

data_sensitivity.rationale: Academic standards text is public domain government information; curriculum alignment claims are commercial metadata with no sensitive content

external_exposure.rationale: Developer-facing API accessible to any registered ed-tech developer globally; API keys required but registration is open

access_model.value: API key authentication with developer registration portal; keys scoped to tier (free/standard/enterprise) with corresponding rate limits
access_model.rationale: Developer API key model standard for public developer APIs; tiered access supports freemium monetization model

feature_scope.value: Standards querying by framework, grade band, and subject; standard code lookup; prerequisite and successor standard traversal; curriculum alignment claim submission and approval workflow; webhook notifications for alignment status changes; API usage analytics per key
feature_scope.rationale: Full standards API feature set covering query, hierarchy, alignment claims, and developer engagement features

mvp_scope.value: Standards querying by framework and grade band, standard code lookup, API key authentication with rate limiting, developer registration
mvp_scope.rationale: Core standards read API is the primary developer need; alignment claim workflow is Phase 2

security_baseline.value: API key validation on every request, rate limiting per key via Redis token bucket, TLS 1.3 in transit, WAF on ECS ALB for DDoS protection
security_baseline.rationale: Standard public API security controls; no sensitive data reduces security complexity

frontend_stack.value: none — pure API service; developer documentation hosted on Stoplight or Redocly separately
frontend_stack.rationale: API service project class; developer portal and docs are separate concerns

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI's automatic OpenAPI schema generation matches the API-first development approach; Python team has domain expertise in standards data

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for standards taxonomy and alignment claims; Redis 7 for response caching and rate limiting
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL ltree extension models hierarchical standards taxonomy; Redis caches popular standard lookups

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with ALB
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with auto-scaling for variable developer traffic; ALB supports WAF for public API protection

privacy_retention_policy.value: API access logs retained 90 days; standards data retained indefinitely as reference content; alignment claims retained 5 years for curriculum publisher record-keeping
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Standards data is reference content; alignment claims are business records for curriculum publishers

future_scope.value: GraphQL endpoint for flexible standards querying, LMS plugin for direct Canvas and Schoology integration, standards change notification webhooks when state DOEs update standards
future_scope.rationale: GraphQL and LMS integrations are Phase 3 features post-alignment claim workflow launch

constraints.value: Must include CCSS, NGSS, and at least 10 state-specific standards frameworks at launch; API response time p95 < 200ms for standard lookups; budget $600/month
constraints.rationale: Framework coverage and latency targets are developer adoption requirements; budget governs infrastructure choices

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM for API latency per endpoint, per-consumer API usage dashboards, error rate alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: Per-consumer usage dashboards support developer support and abuse detection

execution_preference.value: API-first with OpenAPI 3.1 spec frozen before implementation; weekly releases with semantic versioning
execution_preference.rationale: API-first development ensures stable contract for early developer partners during build phase

project.goal: Unified K-12 academic standards API serving CCSS, NGSS, and 10+ state frameworks to ed-tech developers
project.target_users: Ed-tech developers and curriculum publishers
project.timeline: Q2 public launch with CCSS and NGSS coverage

backend.api: REST with OpenAPI 3.1 spec; versioned under /v1/

security.auth: API key header X-API-Key validated against PostgreSQL with Redis cache
security.rate_limiting: Redis token bucket per API key tier

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 with ltree extension for standards hierarchy
data.cache: Redis 7 for standard lookup responses and rate limit buckets

devops.ci: GitHub Actions with pytest and OpenAPI validation gates
devops.hosting: ECS Fargate with ALB and WAF

constraints.frameworks: CCSS, NGSS, 10+ state frameworks at launch

reasoner_reviews.product: The standards query API is well-scoped for MVP. The alignment claim submission and editorial review workflow is correctly deferred to Phase 2 — it adds significant complexity and the core value is the standards query layer. Developer registration and API key issuance need a lightweight self-service flow; even a simple email-verified signup form reduces friction for early adopters.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with PostgreSQL ltree is an excellent choice for the hierarchical standards taxonomy. ltree supports ancestor/descendant queries needed for grade band rollups and standard hierarchy traversal without recursive CTEs. Redis caching of popular standard lookups is critical for the p95 < 200ms latency target — cold PostgreSQL ltree queries on large frameworks like CCSS can take 50-100ms. The alignment claim editorial workflow in Phase 2 should be modeled as a state machine with explicit status transitions.
reasoner_reviews.security: API key validation must check both the key existence and the key's rate limit tier on every request. Storing API keys as bcrypt hashes in PostgreSQL with a Redis lookup cache (key hash -> tier metadata) balances security and latency. WAF on ALB should be configured with AWS Managed Rules for API abuse patterns in addition to the OWASP Core Rule Set.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: PostgreSQL ltree with proper GiST indexing achieves sub-10ms standard lookup latency, making the p95 < 200ms target achievable with Redis caching. The $600/month budget is achievable: ECS Fargate 2 tasks ~$80, RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.small ~$40, ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.micro ~$25, ALB ~$25, data transfer ~$20. Datadog APM is the main cost risk — team tier pricing could push over budget; consider Datadog's developer plan.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with route modules: /v1/standards (query and lookup), /v1/frameworks (framework metadata), /v1/grades (grade band definitions), /v1/alignments (curriculum material alignment claims), /v1/auth (API key registration and management). Dependency injection for database session, Redis client, and API key validator middleware.
backend.api_patterns: GET /v1/standards?framework=CCSS&grade=K-2&subject=ELA&page=1&limit=50 — filtered standard listing. GET /v1/standards/{standard_code} — single standard with description and hierarchy. GET /v1/standards/{standard_code}/related — prerequisite and successor standards via ltree. GET /v1/frameworks — list available frameworks with coverage metadata. POST /v1/alignments — submit curriculum alignment claim (Phase 2). GET /v1/alignments?material_id=&standard_code= — alignment lookup.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on CPU 60% threshold. Redis caches standard lookup responses with 1-hour TTL — standards data changes infrequently. Popular framework listings cached at Redis with 6-hour TTL. PostgreSQL read replica for analytics queries to avoid contacting primary.

security.auth_design: API key stored as SHA-256 hash in PostgreSQL api_keys table with tier, rate_limit_per_hour, owner_email. Redis key-value cache stores {key_hash: {tier, rate_limit}} with 5-minute TTL to reduce PostgreSQL lookups. X-API-Key header validated on every request by FastAPI dependency.
security.abuse_prevention: Redis token bucket per API key: free tier 500 req/hour, standard tier 5000 req/hour, enterprise tier 50000 req/hour. Burst allowance 2x sustained rate. 429 response with Retry-After header. IP-level WAF rate limiting as secondary defense for key farming attempts.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: API access log to CloudWatch: api_key_hash, endpoint, framework_queried, response_time_ms, status_code, timestamp. Datadog custom metric: requests_per_key per 5-minute window for abuse detection dashboard.

data.entities: standards_frameworks (id UUID PK, code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE, name, publisher, version, coverage_grade_bands TEXT[], active BOOLEAN). academic_standards (id UUID PK, framework_id FK, standard_code VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE, path ltree, parent_code VARCHAR(50), grade_band VARCHAR(20), subject VARCHAR(50), description TEXT, description_ts tsvector GENERATED, strand TEXT, depth_level INT). grade_bands (id UUID PK, framework_id FK, code VARCHAR(10), label VARCHAR(50), min_grade INT, max_grade INT). alignment_claims (id UUID PK, material_id VARCHAR(100), material_title TEXT, standard_id FK, alignment_type ENUM primary/supplemental, submitter_api_key_hash VARCHAR(64), status ENUM pending/approved/rejected, reviewed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, reviewer_notes TEXT). api_keys (id UUID PK, key_hash VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE, tier ENUM free/standard/enterprise, owner_email TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE).
data.schema_notes: PostgreSQL ltree extension installed for academic_standards.path column enabling ancestor/descendant queries with GiST index. tsvector on description for full-text standard search. GiST index on standards path for hierarchy traversal. Composite index on framework_id + grade_band + subject for filtered listing queries.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service 2-4 tasks behind ALB with WAF (OWASP Core Rule Set and AWS Managed Rules for APIs). RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.t3.small in same VPC private subnet. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.micro. ECR for container images. Route53 CNAME for api.standards-api.org.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with 80% coverage, mypy strict, ruff lint, OpenAPI schema validation via schemathesis, Docker build and ECR push, ECS staging deploy, smoke test suite against staging API, manual promote to production via GitHub environment approval.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on ECS sidecar: trace all API requests with endpoint, framework_queried, api_key_tier tags. Custom dashboard: requests per framework, top standards queried, rate limit hit rate per tier, p95 latency per endpoint. Alert: p95 latency > 200ms for /v1/standards lookups, error rate > 0.5%.

ISS-001.title: PostgreSQL ltree path format inconsistency between frameworks causes hierarchy traversal failures
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan imported CCSS and NGSS standards without normalizing their code formats to a consistent ltree path structure. CCSS uses dot notation (CCSS.ELA-Literacy.RL.K.1) but NGSS uses hyphen notation (NGSS-K-PS1-1). FastAPI ltree queries using <@ (ancestor) and @> (descendant) operators fail cross-framework when paths have inconsistent separator characters.

history.note: Identified during NGSS data import testing

ISS-002.title: Rate limiter Redis key uses raw API key value instead of key hash
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 rate limiter implementation stored the raw X-API-Key header value as the Redis rate limit bucket key. If Redis memory is inspected or Redis is misconfigured for auth, raw API keys could be exposed. Rate limit Redis keys should use the SHA-256 hash of the key, consistent with the PostgreSQL storage approach.

history.note: Found in security review of Redis key naming

focus_issues.title: PostgreSQL ltree path inconsistency causing hierarchy traversal failures across frameworks
focus_issues.detail: Inconsistent ltree path formats between CCSS and NGSS break ancestor/descendant operator queries.

focus_issues.title: Rate limiter stores raw API key in Redis instead of hash
focus_issues.detail: Raw API key value used as Redis rate limit bucket key, exposing keys if Redis is inspected.

latest_recommendations: Define a canonical ltree path normalization function applied at import time that converts all framework codes to underscore-separated lowercase paths, e.g. ccss.ela_literacy.rl.k.1
Update rate limiter to use SHA-256 hash of the API key as the Redis bucket key, consistent with PostgreSQL key_hash storage
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: K-12 Academic Standards Alignment API — Round 1

convergence_state.reason: ltree path inconsistency breaks core hierarchy traversal feature

previous_audits.summary: Strong API-first design with appropriate PostgreSQL ltree modeling, but two implementation issues undermine the hierarchy traversal feature and API key security. Both are fixable in round 2.
strengths: FastAPI + ltree combination is architecturally sound for hierarchical standards data
Tiered API key model with Redis rate limiting is well-designed
OpenAPI 3.1 spec-first approach is the right development methodology
concerns: ltree path normalization gap will cause silent query failures across CCSS and NGSS
Raw API key in Redis rate limiter is a security anti-pattern
blocking_issues: ltree path inconsistency means GET /v1/standards/CCSS.ELA-Literacy.RL.K.1/related returns incorrect or empty results for cross-framework hierarchy queries
recommendations: Implement a path normalizer in the data import pipeline converting all standard codes to lowercase underscore-separated ltree format before insertion
Change Redis rate limit bucket key to sha256(api_key) in the FastAPI dependency

issue_updates.title: ltree path inconsistency
issue_updates.detail: CCSS and NGSS path formats incompatible for ltree operators

issue_updates.title: Raw API key in Redis
