previous_plan.title: K-12 Academic Standards Alignment API — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: FastAPI standards API with PostgreSQL ltree taxonomy and Redis rate limiting. Two issues: ltree path format inconsistency and raw API key in Redis rate limiter.
previous_plan.data_model: academic_standards with ltree path column. CCSS imported as 'CCSS.ELA-Literacy.RL.K.1' and NGSS as 'NGSS-K-PS1-1' — incompatible ltree formats.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: API key auth with rate limiting. Redis bucket key uses raw X-API-Key header value — security anti-pattern.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision of the K-12 standards alignment API. ISS-001 fix: define a canonical ltree path normalization function applied at import time, converting all framework standard codes to lowercase alphanumeric underscore-separated labels (e.g., ccss.ela_literacy.rl.k.1 and ngss.k_ps1_1). The path normalizer runs in the data import pipeline before insertion and is also applied to incoming query parameters in the FastAPI endpoint. ISS-002 fix: update the rate limiter Redis key construction to use hashlib.sha256(api_key.encode()).hexdigest() matching the PostgreSQL key_hash storage pattern.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented normalize_standard_path(code: str) -> str function in the data import pipeline that lowercases all characters, replaces hyphens and dots with underscores, and removes non-alphanumeric characters except underscores, producing valid ltree label sequences. Re-imported CCSS and NGSS with normalized paths (e.g., ccss.ela_literacy.rl.k.1, ngss.k_ps1_1). FastAPI /v1/standards/{standard_code} endpoint applies same normalization to the path parameter before ltree query.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ltree ancestor/descendant operators work consistently across all frameworks; GET /v1/standards/{code}/related returns correct hierarchy results for all imported frameworks.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated RateLimitDependency FastAPI dependency to compute sha256_key = hashlib.sha256(api_key.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest() and use sha256_key as the Redis token bucket key prefix. The sha256_key matches the key_hash stored in PostgreSQL, enabling correlation between rate limit bucket and key metadata without exposing the raw key value in Redis.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Raw API key values no longer stored in Redis; rate limit bucket keys are SHA-256 hashes consistent with PostgreSQL key_hash column.

target_output.title: K-12 Academic Standards Alignment API — Unified Standards Query Service
target_output.executive_summary: A Python FastAPI service on AWS ECS Fargate providing ed-tech developers with a unified REST API for querying K-12 academic standards across CCSS, NGSS, and 10+ state-specific frameworks. PostgreSQL 16 with ltree extension models the hierarchical standards taxonomy for efficient grade-band and hierarchy traversal queries. Redis powers both API key rate limiting and standard lookup response caching. Round 2 fixes: canonical ltree path normalization across all frameworks enables consistent hierarchy traversal, and rate limiter updated to use API key SHA-256 hashes in Redis.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single FastAPI service on ECS Fargate behind ALB with WAF, serving the OpenAPI 3.1-specified standards API. PostgreSQL 16 with ltree extension stores the standards taxonomy using normalized lowercase underscore-separated path labels consistent across all frameworks. Redis serves dual purpose: response cache for standard lookups (1-hour TTL) and rate limit token buckets per API key SHA-256 hash. Data import pipeline (Python scripts, run manually when frameworks are updated) applies normalize_standard_path() normalization before PostgreSQL insertion. Developer API keys registered via a lightweight sign-up endpoint, stored as SHA-256 hashes in PostgreSQL with tier and rate limit metadata cached in Redis for 5 minutes to minimize database lookups on every request.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Pydantic v2 for request/response models, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with asyncpg driver for async PostgreSQL queries. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS with ltree extension and GiST index on standards path, tsvector for full-text search; Redis 7 on ElastiCache for caching and rate limiting. Hosting: ECS Fargate 2-4 tasks, ALB with AWS WAF (OWASP CRS and API abuse managed rules), ECR. Auth: API key validation with SHA-256 hashing, Redis token bucket rate limiter. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with pytest, mypy, schemathesis OpenAPI validation. Observability: Datadog APM.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Standards Query: GET /v1/standards with framework, grade_band, subject, and full-text search query filters; returns paginated list of matching standards with code, description, grade band, and hierarchy path. Standard Lookup: GET /v1/standards/{standard_code} returning full standard detail with normalized ltree path. Standards Hierarchy: GET /v1/standards/{standard_code}/related returning direct prerequisites (ancestors), successor standards (descendants), and sibling standards at the same level using PostgreSQL ltree @>, <@, and ~ operators. Framework Catalog: GET /v1/frameworks listing all available frameworks with grade band coverage and standard count. Grade Band Definitions: GET /v1/frameworks/{framework_code}/grades for grade band label and range metadata. Alignment Claims: POST /v1/alignments to submit curriculum material alignment claim for editorial review (Phase 2). Developer Key Management: POST /v1/auth/register for API key issuance; DELETE /v1/auth/keys/{key_id} for revocation.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI application: mounted on ECS Fargate container (Python 3.12 slim image, 512 MB memory). Standards query router with ltree-aware SQLAlchemy queries. API key validator FastAPI dependency: checks Redis for cached {sha256_hash: tier_metadata} dict; on miss queries PostgreSQL api_keys table and populates Redis cache with 5-min TTL. Rate limiter FastAPI dependency: Redis token bucket using sha256(api_key) as bucket key prefix; refills at tier rate per hour. normalize_standard_path function applied to all path parameters in standards endpoints. Data import scripts: Python scripts for each framework (CCSS, NGSS, state DOE exports) applying normalize_standard_path before bulk PostgreSQL insertion via COPY. RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.t3.small with ltree extension, GiST index on academic_standards.path, tsvector index on description. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.micro for caching and rate limiting. ALB access logs to S3. Datadog APM agent as ECS sidecar.
target_output.workflows: Standards Query Workflow: Ed-tech developer sends GET /v1/standards?framework=ccss&grade_band=3-5&subject=mathematics&q=fractions with X-API-Key header. FastAPI API key dependency computes sha256(key), checks Redis for tier metadata, on miss queries PostgreSQL api_keys WHERE key_hash=sha256. Rate limiter checks token bucket at Redis key ratelimit:{sha256_key}, decrements token, returns 429 if bucket empty. Standards router queries PostgreSQL: SELECT * FROM academic_standards WHERE framework_id=CCSS AND grade_band='3-5' AND subject='mathematics' AND description_ts @@ plainto_tsquery('fractions'). Returns paginated JSON with 50 matching standards. Hierarchy Traversal Workflow: Developer sends GET /v1/standards/ccss.math.content.3.nf.a.1/related. FastAPI applies normalize_standard_path to path parameter: ccss.math.content.3.nf.a.1 (already normalized). PostgreSQL ltree query: SELECT * FROM academic_standards WHERE path @> 'ccss.math.content.3.nf'::ltree AND path <> 'ccss.math.content.3.nf.a.1'::ltree (ancestors). Second query: SELECT * FROM academic_standards WHERE path <@ 'ccss.math.content.3.nf.a.1'::ltree AND nlevel(path) = nlevel('ccss.math.content.3.nf.a.1'::ltree) + 1 (immediate descendants). Returns {ancestors: [...], descendants: [...], siblings: [...]}.
target_output.data_model: standards_frameworks (id UUID PK, code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE e.g. 'ccss', name, publisher, version, active_since DATE, grade_bands TEXT[]). academic_standards (id UUID PK, framework_id FK, standard_code VARCHAR(80) e.g. 'CCSS.ELA-Literacy.RL.K.1', normalized_code VARCHAR(80) e.g. 'ccss.ela_literacy.rl.k.1', path ltree NOT NULL e.g. 'ccss.ela_literacy.rl.k.1', grade_band VARCHAR(20), subject VARCHAR(50), strand TEXT, description TEXT, description_ts tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (to_tsvector('english', description)) STORED, depth_level INT, is_leaf BOOLEAN). grade_bands (id UUID PK, framework_id FK, code VARCHAR(20), label VARCHAR(50), min_grade INT, max_grade INT). alignment_claims (id UUID PK, material_id VARCHAR(100), material_title TEXT, standard_id FK, alignment_type ENUM primary/supplemental, submitter_key_hash VARCHAR(64), status ENUM pending/approved/rejected, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). api_keys (id UUID PK, key_hash VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE, tier ENUM free/standard/enterprise, owner_email TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ, revoked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). Indexes: GiST on academic_standards.path, GIN on description_ts, composite on framework_id+grade_band+subject.
target_output.api_design: Base URL: /v1/. All responses application/json; charset=utf-8. Auth: X-API-Key header required on all endpoints. GET /v1/standards?framework=STRING&grade_band=STRING&subject=STRING&q=STRING&page=INT&limit=INT(max 100) — returns {standards: [{standard_code, normalized_code, description, grade_band, subject, framework}], total, page, limit}. GET /v1/standards/{standard_code} — single standard detail with path. GET /v1/standards/{standard_code}/related — returns {ancestors: [...], descendants: [...], siblings: [...]}. GET /v1/frameworks — returns [{code, name, publisher, total_standards, grade_bands}]. GET /v1/frameworks/{code}/grades — grade band definitions. POST /v1/alignments body {material_id, material_title, standard_code, alignment_type} — returns 202 Accepted with alignment claim ID. GET /v1/alignments?material_id=STRING&standard_code=STRING. POST /v1/auth/register body {email} — returns {api_key, tier: free, rate_limit: 500}. Error format: {error: STRING, code: STRING, request_id: STRING}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API key authentication: X-API-Key validated by FastAPI dependency using SHA-256 hash lookup against PostgreSQL api_keys table (cached in Redis for 5 minutes per key). Revoked keys return 401. Rate limiting: Redis token bucket using sha256(api_key) as bucket key prefix — raw key value never stored in Redis. Free tier 500 req/hour, standard 5000 req/hour, enterprise 50000 req/hour; 429 with Retry-After header on exhaustion. TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB. WAF on ALB: AWS Managed Rules for API protection blocking SQL injection, XSS, and known API abuse patterns; IP rate limiting 10000 req/hour per source IP as secondary DDoS defense. No sensitive data in request or response bodies — standards text is public domain.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service: 2-4 tasks, 512 MB memory, 0.5 vCPU, auto-scale on CPU 60% threshold. RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.t3.small in private subnet, 20 GB SSD, 7-day automated backup to S3. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.micro with in-transit encryption. ALB with access logs to S3. ECR repository for API container image. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: pytest --cov=app --cov-fail-under=80, mypy --strict, ruff check, schemathesis run --checks all against OpenAPI spec, docker build and ECR push, ECS staging deploy, curl-based smoke tests against /v1/frameworks and /v1/standards, manual GitHub environment approval gate, ECS production rolling update. Environment promotion: dev -> staging -> production with separate RDS and Redis per environment.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM on ECS Fargate sidecar: trace all API requests tagged with framework_queried, api_key_tier, endpoint. Custom Datadog dashboard: requests per framework per hour, top 20 standard codes queried, rate limit hit rate by tier (free/standard/enterprise), p50/p95/p99 latency per endpoint, Redis cache hit rate for standard lookups. Alert policies: p95 latency > 200ms on /v1/standards/* for 5 minutes, API error rate > 0.5%, rate limit hit rate > 20% for free tier (indicates abuse). CloudWatch ALB access logs exported to S3 for 90-day retention.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $520/month at moderate developer traffic: ECS Fargate 2 tasks avg ~$80, RDS db.t3.small ~$40, ElastiCache cache.t3.micro ~$25, ALB ~$25, WAF ~$30, ECR ~$5, data transfer ~$15, Datadog dev plan ~$300. Fits within $600/month. Redis caching of popular standard lookups expected to achieve 80% cache hit rate, reducing PostgreSQL load significantly during high-traffic periods. ECS scales to 4 tasks for traffic spikes; Redis ElastiCache scales by upgrading instance class.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): PostgreSQL schema with ltree extension and normalized path import, CCSS and NGSS framework imports, standards query and lookup endpoints, API key registration and rate limiting with SHA-256 hashing, ECS Fargate deployment with WAF, OpenAPI 3.1 spec published to Stoplight. Phase 2 (4 weeks): 10 additional state framework imports (California, Texas, New York, Florida priority), alignment claim submission and editorial review workflow, webhook notifications for alignment status changes, Datadog APM dashboards. Phase 3 (4 weeks): GraphQL endpoint for flexible standards querying, developer portal improvements including usage analytics dashboard per API key, standards change notification system when state DOEs update frameworks.
target_output.development_guidelines: normalize_standard_path function is a pure function with unit tests for every supported framework format including CCSS dot notation, NGSS hyphen notation, and state DOE formats. FastAPI dependencies for API key validation and rate limiting must have independent unit tests with mocked Redis and PostgreSQL. schemathesis OpenAPI spec validation runs in CI against the live staging API after deploy. Alembic migrations for schema changes; no manual SQL. mypy strict mode enforced; Pydantic v2 models for all request and response bodies with explicit field validation. New framework imports require a dry-run script that validates all standard codes produce valid ltree paths before insertion.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: State DOE standards data in inconsistent formats requiring framework-specific normalize_standard_path logic. Mitigation: normalize_standard_path has a configurable framework_code parameter allowing per-framework normalization rules; new frameworks added via configuration not code changes. Risk: Large frameworks like CCSS with 10,000+ standards causing slow initial data import. Mitigation: PostgreSQL COPY command for bulk insertion with ANALYZE afterward to update query planner statistics. Risk: API key abuse via key sharing or scraping standards data. Mitigation: WAF IP rate limiting as secondary control; anomaly detection Datadog monitor on single key using >80% of hourly rate limit. Tradeoff: normalized_code stored alongside original standard_code in database to preserve original format for display while using normalized form for ltree operations.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose PostgreSQL ltree over nested set model for standards hierarchy because ltree supports variable-depth paths and O(log n) subtree queries without rebalancing on inserts. Selected API key auth over OAuth2 for the public developer API because the standards data is non-sensitive and key-based auth is standard for open developer APIs at this scale. Chose ECS Fargate over Lambda because the always-on traffic pattern of a public API makes Lambda cold starts a poor fit for the p95 < 200ms latency target.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of K-12 standards alignment API fixing ltree path normalization inconsistency and Redis rate limiter key security.

project_goal.value: Data-rich landing page for a university institutional research office publishing annual campus fact books, accreditation data, and enrollment statistics with interactive D3.js charts, downloadable datasets, and filterable tables powered by a static data layer generated at build time from institutional databases
project_goal.rationale: The IR office needs a public-facing data publication platform that eliminates the PDF-only fact book format; all published data is pre-approved aggregate statistics with no PII

target_users.value: Accreditation reviewers examining institutional data, prospective students and families reviewing enrollment trends, state higher education board staff, journalists and researchers accessing campus statistics, faculty accessing department enrollment and retention data
target_users.rationale: Multiple external stakeholder groups with different data consumption needs from the same fact book dataset

project_class.rationale: Static site with no server-side runtime; all data embedded in static JSON at build time

capabilities.rationale: D3.js interactive visualizations and Vercel Analytics for page engagement tracking; no backend API needed

complexity_level.rationale: D3.js interactive charts with client-side filtering and downloadable dataset generation adds complexity above a standard static site

risk_level.rationale: No user authentication, no database, no PII; only publicly approved aggregate institutional statistics

data_sensitivity.rationale: Data is approved for public release but derived from internal institutional databases; cell suppression rules prevent re-identification of small cohorts

external_exposure.rationale: Institutional research data is publicly required for accreditation and IPEDS reporting

access_model.value: No authentication; fully public read-only data publication
access_model.rationale: Institutional fact book data is public record

feature_scope.value: Enrollment statistics by year, college, and student classification; demographic breakdowns with cell suppression for small cohorts; retention and graduation rate charts; accreditation metric tables; downloadable CSV and Excel datasets; fact book edition archive; interactive D3.js filterable charts
feature_scope.rationale: Covers all data types from the current fact book

mvp_scope.value: Enrollment trend charts (5-year history), demographic breakdown tables, graduation rate visualizations, CSV download for each data section, accreditation metrics table for current year
mvp_scope.rationale: Core sections required for upcoming accreditation review

security_baseline.value: HTTPS via Vercel; security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options); FERPA cell suppression for cohorts below N=5 applied at build pipeline before data is published
security_baseline.rationale: FERPA requires suppression of data cells that could re-identify individual students; suppression applied before data enters the static layer

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript and D3.js v7
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js App Router with static export; D3.js for data-driven SVG chart rendering with client-side filter interactions

backend_stack.value: None -- fully static site; build pipeline Python scripts generate static JSON from database exports
backend_stack.rationale: No runtime server needed; data pipeline runs at build time only

data_platform.value: Static JSON data files generated by Python build pipeline from IR analyst CSV exports; no hosted database
data_platform.rationale: All data is pre-approved aggregate statistics; real-time database queries not required

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with static export deployment
hosting_target.rationale: Global CDN delivery for accreditation reviewers and state board staff who may access from any region

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data published; FERPA cell suppression applied before build; published data retained indefinitely as part of institutional record
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Institutional fact book data must be archived for accreditation body reference

future_scope.value: Real-time enrollment dashboard for internal IR staff (requires backend), faculty-facing departmental data extracts, API endpoint for IPEDS automated submission
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features requiring backend infrastructure; deferred to keep public fact book static

constraints.value: All published data must pass FERPA cell suppression (N < 5 displayed as '<5'); CSV downloads must include suppression notes; no dynamic database queries at runtime -- data layer is static JSON only
constraints.rationale: FERPA compliance is a hard constraint on all published student data

observability_baseline.value: Vercel Analytics for page views and geography; custom events for chart filter interactions and CSV download clicks to measure which data sections have highest engagement
observability_baseline.rationale: IR office wants to understand which fact book sections are most used by accreditors and prospective students

execution_preference.value: Annual release cycle aligned with fall enrollment census; build pipeline runs after IR analyst certifies data exports
execution_preference.rationale: Fact book publication follows the institutional data certification calendar

project.goal: Public-facing institutional fact book with interactive enrollment and accreditation data visualizations
project.target_users: Accreditation reviewers, prospective students, state board staff, journalists, faculty
project.timeline: Live before November accreditation self-study submission

frontend.framework: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router, output: 'export'
frontend.charting: D3.js v7 for enrollment trend lines, demographic bar charts, graduation rate gauges
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS with university brand colors

security.ferpa: Cell suppression N < 5 applied in Python build pipeline before JSON generation

data.format: Static JSON data files in public/data/ directory
data.pipeline: Python build scripts processing IR analyst CSV exports with suppression validation

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for build pipeline and Vercel deployment
devops.environments: Preview per PR, production on main

constraints.ferpa: No cell with N < 5 published; suppression notes in CSV downloads
constraints.runtime: No backend API calls at runtime -- static JSON only

open_questions.archive: How many historical fact book editions to display in edition archive

reasoner_reviews.product: The institutional fact book use case is well-matched to static site generation -- data is certified annually and does not require real-time queries. The five visitor segments (accreditation reviewers, prospective students, state board staff, journalists, faculty) have largely overlapping data needs but different download requirements. The round 1 plan generated CSV downloads dynamically in the browser from static JSON, which is correct. The missing piece from round 1 is the build pipeline's FERPA cell suppression validation step -- it was described but the mechanism for surfacing suppression failures to the IR analyst before publication was not specified.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js static export with D3.js client-side rendering is the correct architecture. The build pipeline Python scripts should run as a GitHub Actions step before the Next.js build, ingesting IR analyst CSV exports, applying suppression rules, writing validated JSON to public/data/, and failing the build if any cell violates the N < 5 threshold without suppression. The round 1 plan used a manual suppression step outside CI -- this must be automated and fail-safe.
reasoner_reviews.security: FERPA cell suppression is the primary compliance concern and it must be enforced automatically in CI rather than relying on IR analyst manual review. The build pipeline should reject any CSV export containing a cell with N < 5 that is not already marked for suppression, and output a clear error report listing the offending cells for the analyst to address. This prevents accidental FERPA violations from human error.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The annual release cycle with IR analyst certification is a workflow constraint that must be reflected in the deployment process -- the GitHub Actions workflow should require a manual approval gate (GitHub Environment protection rule) after the suppression validation step and before the Vercel production deploy, so the IR office director can sign off before data goes live.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router static export: app/page.tsx (hub with section navigation), app/enrollment/page.tsx, app/demographics/page.tsx, app/retention/page.tsx, app/accreditation/page.tsx, app/archive/page.tsx. Shared components: DataSection (title, chart, filter controls, download button), ChartEnrollmentTrend (D3.js line chart), ChartDemographicBreakdown (D3.js stacked bar), TableAccreditationMetrics (sortable), SuppressionNote (standardized cell suppression disclosure).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Enrollment page: five-year enrollment trend D3.js line chart with filter controls (college dropdown, student classification radio), total headcount summary stats, download CSV button. Demographics page: stacked bar chart by race/ethnicity and first-generation status, with year selector, suppression notes for cohorts below threshold. Retention and Graduation page: graduation rate gauge chart by cohort entry year, retention rate trend line, 150% graduation rate comparison. Accreditation page: tabular metrics required by HLC with current year values and 5-year trend indicators.
frontend.performance_notes: D3.js charts rendered client-side after hydration; chart data loaded from static JSON in public/data/ via fetch on component mount; JSON files pre-compressed with Brotli by Vercel CDN; chart SVG elements use will-change transform for animation performance

backend.service_design: No runtime backend. Build pipeline: Python 3.12 scripts in scripts/ directory run as GitHub Actions steps: ingest_csv.py reads IR analyst exports, apply_suppression.py validates and applies N < 5 suppression rules, generate_json.py writes typed JSON to public/data/, validate_schema.py runs Pydantic validation on output JSON.
backend.failure_handling: Build pipeline fails with detailed error report if any enrollment cell has count < 5 without suppression marker; error output lists table name, dimension values, and cell count for analyst remediation; Vercel deploy blocked until pipeline passes

security.auth_design: No authentication -- fully public site
security.privacy_controls: FERPA suppression applied at pipeline layer before any data enters static JSON; suppressed cells replaced with string '<5' and flagged in suppression_notes array in JSON; CSV download generator includes suppression notes column
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Build pipeline logs suppression decisions (cells suppressed, cells passed) to GitHub Actions artifact for IR office audit trail of what was suppressed

data.entities: enrollment_statistics (year, college_name, student_classification, headcount -- suppressed if < 5), demographic_breakdowns (year, race_ethnicity_category, first_gen_flag, headcount -- suppressed), retention_rates (cohort_year, cohort_size, retained_year2, retained_year3, graduated_4yr, graduated_6yr), accreditation_metrics (metric_id, metric_name, category, current_value, prior_year_value, benchmark_value, trend ENUM improving/stable/declining), fact_book_editions (edition_year, published_date, pdf_url, data_snapshot_url), suppression_notes (table_name, dimension_description, reason, applied_at)
data.schema_notes: All JSON files in public/data/ typed with TypeScript interfaces in types/fact-book.ts; Zod validation in build pipeline Python scripts ensures shape consistency before Next.js build runs

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions pipeline: (1) checkout, (2) bun install, (3) run Python build scripts (ingest + suppression + JSON generation + validation), (4) manual approval gate via GitHub Environment 'production' requiring IR director approval, (5) next build with output: export, (6) Vercel deploy
devops.ci_cd_design: Separate CI jobs: build-pipeline job (Python scripts, artifact: public/data/ JSON files, suppression report); wait-for-approval job (GitHub Environment protection rule gate); deploy job (next build + Vercel deploy using pipeline artifact). Preview deployments on PR branches skip approval gate.
devops.environments: Preview: automatic on PR (uses sample anonymized data, not live IR exports). Production: requires manual IR director approval gate before deploy.

ISS-001.title: FERPA cell suppression validation not automated in CI -- relies on manual analyst review
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described suppression as a manual step performed by the IR analyst before CSV export; if the analyst misses a cell with N < 5, it could be published without suppression, violating FERPA.

history.note: Identified in security review; suppression must be automated as a build gate

ISS-002.title: No manual approval gate before production deploy of certified data
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 CI deployed directly to production on main branch push; the IR director must be able to review the suppression report and sign off before data goes live.

history.note: Raised by IR office workflow review

focus_issues.title: FERPA cell suppression validation not automated in CI
focus_issues.detail: Suppression relies on manual analyst review; automated build gate required to prevent accidental publication of un-suppressed student data.

latest_recommendations: Add automated FERPA cell suppression validation as a GitHub Actions build step that reads all enrollment and demographic CSV exports, flags any row with count < 5 that lacks a suppression marker, and fails the build with a detailed error report listing offending cells
Add a GitHub Environment protection rule for the production Vercel deploy requiring manual approval from the IR director role after the suppression validation step passes
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: University Institutional Research Fact Book Site Round 1

convergence_state.reason: FERPA suppression automation missing -- high severity compliance gap

previous_audits.summary: Good static site architecture with appropriate D3.js charting approach. The FERPA cell suppression gap is a compliance risk that must be automated. Interactive filtering and CSV downloads are well-designed.
strengths: Static JSON data layer with Next.js static export is correct for annually-updated institutional data
D3.js client-side filtering allows enrollment chart filtering without any backend
CSV download generation from static JSON is efficient and eliminates download server costs
concerns: FERPA suppression is a manual pre-step outside CI -- automation is required to prevent human error
No manual approval gate before production deploy; IR director has no sign-off step
recommendations: Automate suppression validation in CI as a fail-fast build gate with detailed error output for analyst remediation
Add GitHub Environment protection rule requiring IR director approval before production deploy

issue_updates.title: FERPA suppression not automated in CI
issue_updates.detail: Manual suppression outside CI is error-prone and insufficient for FERPA compliance

issue_updates.title: No IR director approval gate before production deploy
issue_updates.detail: CI deploys directly to production without sign-off

previous_plan.title: University Institutional Research Fact Book Site Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Static Next.js 14 site publishing annual institutional fact book with D3.js interactive enrollment and demographic charts, downloadable CSV datasets, and accreditation metric tables generated from static JSON built from IR analyst CSV exports.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions builds Next.js static export and deploys to Vercel on main branch push. IR analyst manually applies FERPA suppression to CSV exports before providing them to the build pipeline. No approval gate before production deploy.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: HTTPS via Vercel. FERPA cell suppression described as manual pre-step by IR analyst. CSP and X-Frame-Options headers via vercel.json.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for the university institutional research fact book static site. The high-severity issue is that FERPA cell suppression relies on manual IR analyst review rather than automated CI validation. Fix by adding a Python build step in GitHub Actions that reads all enrollment and demographic CSV exports, validates every cell with headcount against the N < 5 threshold, fails the build with a per-cell error report if any un-suppressed cells are found, and outputs a suppression audit log artifact. Also add a GitHub Environment protection rule requiring IR director approval before the production Vercel deploy to give the office a sign-off checkpoint. Both fixes are in the deployment pipeline, not the frontend architecture.

fix_report.action_taken: Added automated FERPA suppression validation step to GitHub Actions workflow: Python apply_suppression.py reads all enrollment and demographic CSV exports, checks every count cell against N < 5 threshold, applies '<5' suppression to flagged cells, emits a suppression_report.json artifact listing all suppressed cells with dimension values and all passed cells with counts, fails the GitHub Actions step (exit 1) if any CSV contains a cell with count < 5 that is not already marked for suppression by the IR analyst; downstream JSON generation only runs after suppression validation passes; updated deployment_and_operations section
fix_report.expected_outcome: No un-suppressed student count below N=5 can reach the published static JSON; build fails with actionable error listing offending cells before data is embedded in any Next.js bundle; suppression audit log available as GitHub Actions artifact for IR compliance records

target_output.title: University Institutional Research Fact Book
target_output.executive_summary: A statically-generated public data publication site for a university institutional research office, built with Next.js 14 and D3.js on Vercel, presenting annual fact book enrollment statistics, demographic breakdowns, retention and graduation rates, and accreditation metrics as interactive filterable charts and downloadable CSV datasets. All data is generated at build time from IR analyst CSV exports via a Python pipeline that enforces FERPA cell suppression (N < 5) before any data enters the static JSON layer. A GitHub Environment protection rule requires IR director approval before production deployment. No backend runtime or database; all chart filtering and downloads are client-side.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static architecture: Python build pipeline processes IR analyst CSV exports through FERPA suppression validation and generates typed static JSON data files, which are then consumed by a Next.js 14 static export build that renders D3.js chart components and embeds data directly in the JavaScript bundle. Deployed to Vercel Edge Network for global CDN delivery. No runtime API calls; all enrollment filtering and CSV downloads execute client-side in the browser using the pre-built static data. GitHub Actions enforces the two-stage pipeline: suppression validation (automated, fail-fast) followed by IR director manual approval gate before any Vercel production deploy. Vercel Analytics tracks which data sections and chart filters receive the most engagement from accreditation reviewers and prospective students.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router, output: 'export' mode, D3.js v7 (SVG chart rendering), Tailwind CSS, React hooks for filter state. Build Pipeline: Python 3.12 (pandas for CSV processing, Pydantic for JSON schema validation). Hosting: Vercel Edge Network (global CDN, Brotli compression). Analytics: Vercel Analytics (cookieless, custom events for chart_filter_changed and csv_download_clicked). CI/CD: GitHub Actions with GitHub Environment protection rule for production approval. Content: Static JSON files in public/data/ generated by build pipeline.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Enrollment Statistics Section: D3.js line chart of 5-year total headcount trend with filter controls (college dropdown, student classification radio: full-time/part-time/undergraduate/graduate); suppressed cells displayed as '<5' with suppression note tooltip. Demographic Breakdown Section: D3.js stacked bar chart by race/ethnicity and first-generation status with year selector; cell suppression applied for cohort sizes below threshold. Retention and Graduation Section: graduation rate gauge per cohort entry year, 4-year and 6-year rates, retention year-over-year trend line. Accreditation Metrics Section: tabular display of HLC required metrics with current year values, prior year comparison, benchmark values, and trend indicator badges. Downloadable Datasets: per-section CSV download button that generates a formatted CSV in-browser from static JSON, including suppression_notes column for any suppressed cells. Fact Book Archive: links to prior year editions as static PDF and data snapshot downloads.
target_output.system_components: Vercel Edge CDN (global delivery of Next.js static HTML/CSS/JS and public/data/ JSON files), GitHub Actions CI pipeline (build-pipeline job: Python suppression validation + JSON generation; production-approval job: GitHub Environment gate; deploy job: next build + Vercel deploy), Python build scripts (scripts/ingest_csv.py, scripts/apply_suppression.py, scripts/generate_json.py, scripts/validate_schema.py), Pydantic JSON schema validators (types/fact-book.ts TypeScript interfaces + Python equivalents), Vercel Analytics (custom events: chart_filter_changed with {section, filter_key, filter_value}, csv_download_clicked with {section}).
target_output.workflows: Annual Fact Book Publication Workflow: IR analyst exports certified data tables from institutional reporting database as CSV files (enrollment by college/classification, demographics by race/ethnicity, retention cohort data, accreditation metrics); analyst uploads CSVs to the GitHub repository data-exports/ directory via PR; GitHub Actions CI triggers build-pipeline job which runs apply_suppression.py -- if any enrollment or demographic count cell contains a value < 5 without existing suppression marker, job exits 1 and posts a PR comment listing offending cells with dimension values for analyst to address; once suppression validation passes, generate_json.py produces typed JSON in public/data/ and validate_schema.py confirms Pydantic schema compliance; after build-pipeline job passes, CI creates a pending deployment to the production GitHub Environment, notifying the IR director via email for manual approval review; IR director reviews suppression_report.json artifact (list of all suppressed cells and counts) in GitHub Actions, clicks Approve; deploy job runs next build and Vercel production deploy. Client-Side Chart Filter Workflow: visitor selects college from dropdown on enrollment page; React state update triggers D3.js chart re-render filtering static JSON data by college_name dimension; Vercel Analytics track() fires chart_filter_changed event with filter values for engagement analysis.
target_output.data_model: enrollment_statistics.json (array of {year: int, college_name: string, student_classification: string, headcount: int | '<5', suppressed: boolean}). demographic_breakdowns.json (array of {year: int, race_ethnicity_category: string, first_gen_flag: boolean, headcount: int | '<5', suppressed: boolean}). retention_rates.json (array of {cohort_entry_year: int, cohort_size: int, retained_year2_pct: float, retained_year3_pct: float, graduated_4yr_pct: float, graduated_6yr_pct: float}). accreditation_metrics.json (array of {metric_id: string, metric_name: string, category: string, current_value: string, prior_year_value: string, benchmark_value: string, trend: 'improving' | 'stable' | 'declining'}). fact_book_editions.json (array of {edition_year: int, published_date: string, pdf_url: string, data_snapshot_url: string}). suppression_report.json (build artifact only, not published: {suppressed_cells: [{table, dimension_values, original_count}], passed_cells_count: int, generated_at: string}).
target_output.api_design: No runtime API. Client-side data access via fetch() from static JSON files at Vercel CDN paths: /data/enrollment_statistics.json, /data/demographic_breakdowns.json, /data/retention_rates.json, /data/accreditation_metrics.json, /data/fact_book_editions.json. CSV download: browser-side generation using PapaParse or manual CSV string construction from loaded JSON, triggering Blob download with Blob URL. Analytics events: Vercel Analytics track('chart_filter_changed', {section: 'enrollment', filter_key: 'college_name', filter_value: college}) and track('csv_download_clicked', {section: 'demographics'}).
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced by Vercel for all deployments. Security headers via vercel.json: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' va.vercel-scripts.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'), X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. FERPA compliance: automated suppression validation in CI (Python apply_suppression.py) replaces any count < 5 with string '<5' and flags suppressed: true; validation step fails the build if any CSV contains an un-suppressed sub-threshold count; suppression_report.json artifact retained in GitHub Actions for 90 days as IR compliance audit log. CSV downloads include a suppression_notes column documenting each suppressed cell dimension for accreditation reviewer transparency. No PII published; all data is aggregate institutional statistics.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions three-job workflow: (1) build-pipeline -- bun install, python scripts/ingest_csv.py, python scripts/apply_suppression.py (exit 1 on unsuppressed cells, uploads suppression_report.json artifact), python scripts/generate_json.py, python scripts/validate_schema.py, next build with output: export (generates dist/); (2) production-approval -- waits on GitHub Environment 'fact-book-production' with required reviewer (IR Director GitHub account), review link shows suppression_report.json artifact; (3) deploy -- runs on approval, calls Vercel CLI deploy with dist/ directory to production. PR branches: skip production-approval job, deploy directly to Vercel preview URL using sample anonymized data (not live IR exports). Annual publication: IR analyst opens PR with certified CSV exports in data-exports/, CI runs suppression validation, IR director approves, deploy triggers.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics: total page views per section (enrollment, demographics, retention, accreditation), unique visitors by day, geography of accreditation reviewer traffic. Custom events: chart_filter_changed (section, filter_key, filter_value) to measure which enrollment dimensions are most explored, csv_download_clicked (section) to measure which datasets are most downloaded. IR office reviews engagement dashboard before the next annual edition to understand which data sections need expansion. Vercel build logs accessible for pipeline debugging. GitHub Actions suppression_report.json artifact retained 90 days for compliance audit records.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan: USD 20/month (shared with other university Vercel projects under team plan). Vercel Analytics included. GitHub Actions: well within free tier for annual build frequency (approximately 10 production builds per year during data revision cycles). Total infrastructure cost: less than USD 5/month allocated to this project. CDN auto-scales to any accreditation review traffic surge with no additional cost or capacity planning.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (Weeks 1-5): Python suppression validation pipeline with automated CI gate (ISS-001 fix), GitHub Environment approval gate for IR director (ISS-002 fix), Next.js static site with enrollment trend charts and demographic breakdown, accreditation metrics table, CSV downloads for each section, Vercel Analytics with custom events. Phase 2 (Weeks 6-8): Retention and graduation rate D3.js gauge charts, fact book archive page with prior editions, mobile-responsive D3.js charts, Lighthouse CI gate (LCP < 2.5s). Phase 3 (Future): Real-time enrollment dashboard for internal IR staff (requires a separate authenticated backend project), IPEDS automated submission API endpoint, faculty-facing departmental extract downloads.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for all Next.js components. D3.js chart components must be wrapped in a useEffect hook to run only client-side (avoid SSR issues with SVG dimensions). Chart data typed with TypeScript interfaces matching Python Pydantic validation schemas to ensure build pipeline and frontend agree on data shape. Python scripts formatted with Black and validated with flake8 in CI. No hardcoded enrollment data in .tsx files -- all data consumed from public/data/ JSON only. CSV download generation must always include suppression_notes column even when no cells are suppressed (empty array is valid).
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: IR analyst uploads CSV exports with incorrect column headers not matching expected schema -- Python ingest_csv.py will exit 1 with a column-name mismatch error. Mitigation: maintain a CSV column specification document in the repository for analysts to reference; ingest script error message lists expected vs found column names. Risk: D3.js chart SVG rendering on older browsers used by accreditation review staff. Mitigation: include a static HTML table fallback for each chart section that renders without JavaScript for accessibility and browser compatibility. Tradeoff: client-side CSV generation over a server-side download API eliminates backend costs but means very large datasets (>10,000 rows) may cause brief UI blocking; acceptable given institutional data sizes (enrollment datasets are typically < 1,000 rows after aggregation).
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed 5 years of historical enrollment data displayed in trend charts (fact book covers current year plus 4 prior years). Decided to show all prior editions in the archive going back 10 years as PDF links (PDFs hosted in university S3 bucket, not Vercel). Confirmed PapaParse library for CSV generation in browser over manual string construction for correct RFC 4180 compliance including field quoting. Suppression threshold confirmed as N < 5 (standard higher education FERPA guidance) applied to all demographic and enrollment count fields.

metadata.notes: University institutional research fact book static site; round 2 fixes automated FERPA cell suppression validation in CI pipeline and adds IR director approval gate.

profile.domain: University Financial Aid Office — Title IV Disbursement Reconciliation and Federal Reporting Pipeline

project_goal.value: Ingest federal Title IV aid disbursement records, reconcile them against student enrollment status and SAP evaluations, and produce required federal reporting files (COD and NSLDS) for FSA portal submission — automating a process that is currently performed manually in spreadsheets under strict federal deadline pressure.
project_goal.rationale: Manual reconciliation of Title IV disbursements against enrollment and SAP data creates compliance risk; late or erroneous COD/NSLDS filings trigger audit findings and potential loss of institutional eligibility.

target_users.value: Financial aid counselors who review exception reports; financial aid directors who approve federal submissions; the FSA portal receiving COD and NSLDS files.
target_users.rationale: Internal operations team and federal regulatory system as downstream consumer.

project_class.rationale: Batch ingestion, reconciliation, validation, and federal file generation — no interactive user-facing application.

capabilities.rationale: Airflow DAGs for batch orchestration, PostgreSQL for reconciliation data, SIS and LMS integrations for enrollment/SAP feeds, analytics aggregations for counselor exception dashboards.

complexity_level.rationale: Federal file format specifications (COD and NSLDS flat-file layouts) are complex and version-controlled by FSA; reconciliation logic spans multiple systems with strict accuracy requirements.

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect federal submissions can result in regulatory findings, repayment demands, or loss of Title IV eligibility for the institution — high financial and legal risk.

data_sensitivity.rationale: Student financial aid records including disbursement amounts are financial records under FERPA and GLBA Safeguards Rule; federal student aid data is protected under HEA Title IV regulations.

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline is internal; COD and NSLDS files transmitted to FSA portal via FSA-prescribed secure file transfer (not the pipeline's own API surface).

access_model.value: Azure AD (Entra ID) authentication for Airflow UI and exception dashboard; financial aid counselors have read access to reconciliation exceptions; financial aid directors have approval authority for federal file transmission; service principals used for SIS and LMS API integrations.
access_model.rationale: FERPA and GLBA require access controls restricting student financial data to authorized staff.

feature_scope.value: Airflow DAGs: Title IV disbursement ingestion from SIS, enrollment status snapshot from SIS, SAP evaluation ingestion, disbursement-to-enrollment reconciliation with exception flagging, COD file generation per FSA specification, NSLDS file generation per FSA specification, FSA portal submission via SAIG enrollment (SFTP), delivery confirmation audit trail, exception dashboard data refresh. Counselor exception view for SAP-flagged disbursements and enrollment threshold mismatches.
feature_scope.rationale: Complete Title IV compliance reporting pipeline.

mvp_scope.value: Disbursement ingestion, enrollment reconciliation, SAP flag detection, and COD file generation with manual counselor review step before FSA transmission. NSLDS and automated SAIG transmission in Phase 2.
mvp_scope.rationale: COD is the higher-priority federal reporting obligation; manual review gate reduces risk of erroneous first submission.

security_baseline.value: Azure AD authentication for all human access; service principal credentials in Azure Key Vault; TLS 1.3 for SIS and LMS API connections; PostgreSQL encrypted at rest via Azure Disk Encryption; student financial data not written to Airflow task logs; audit trail for all federal file transmissions.
security_baseline.rationale: GLBA Safeguards Rule and HEA Title IV require confidentiality of student financial aid records.

frontend_stack.value: none — data pipeline with no user-facing application; exception visibility provided via an existing internal BI tool (Power BI) connected to PostgreSQL reconciliation views
frontend_stack.rationale: Financial aid counselors use existing Power BI dashboards; no need to build a separate frontend.

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 for DAG orchestration; Python dbt for reconciliation transformation models; Azure Container Apps for Airflow worker execution.
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow is the institutional standard for batch pipelines; dbt models provide version-controlled SQL transformations for reconciliation logic.

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server as the reconciliation data store and dbt target; no cache layer required for batch pipeline.
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL flexible server supports dbt transformations and Power BI DirectQuery connections for exception dashboards.

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps for Airflow scheduler and workers; Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Azure Blob Storage for federal file staging and archival; Azure Key Vault for secrets.
hosting_target.rationale: University is Azure-native; Azure Container Apps provides serverless container scaling for batch workers.

privacy_retention_policy.value: Disbursement and enrollment reconciliation records retained for 7 years per FERPA and HEA requirements; COD and NSLDS file archives retained for 10 years per FSA audit requirements; SAP evaluation records retained for 7 years; Airflow task logs purged after 90 days (no student financial data in logs).
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: HEA Title IV record retention requirements exceed standard FERPA 7-year minimum for federal aid files.

future_scope.value: Automated SAIG SFTP transmission for COD and NSLDS files replacing manual upload; direct integration with COD web services API; multi-year historical reconciliation for program review preparation.
future_scope.rationale: Automation of FSA file transmission eliminates the remaining manual step in the pipeline.

constraints.value: COD and NSLDS files must conform precisely to FSA's published file layout specifications (version-specific per aid year); federal reporting deadlines are regulatory and non-negotiable; student financial data must not appear in Airflow task logs or any unencrypted storage.
constraints.rationale: FSA will reject malformed files; regulatory deadlines carry audit risk.

observability_baseline.value: Airflow UI for DAG run monitoring; Azure Monitor for Container Apps task execution metrics; custom Azure Monitor alerts on DAG failures and reconciliation exception counts; audit_transmissions table for all federal file submission events.
observability_baseline.rationale: Financial aid directors need immediate notification of DAG failures near reporting deadlines.

execution_preference.value: Airflow DAGs triggered on schedule (nightly for enrollment snapshots, event-driven for disbursement ingestion triggers from SIS); manual trigger available for financial aid director to initiate federal file generation on demand.
execution_preference.rationale: Nightly reconciliation provides daily exception visibility; on-demand generation supports deadline flexibility.

compliance_context.value: HEA Title IV regulations — direct loan and Pell Grant disbursement reporting obligations to COD and NSLDS; FSA Annual Audit requirements for Title IV fund administration; FERPA — student financial aid records are educational records restricted to authorized staff; GLBA Safeguards Rule — student financial data requires technical, administrative, and physical safeguards; potential SAP appeal records may be subject to institutional review.
compliance_context.rationale: Title IV compliance is the primary purpose of this pipeline; FERPA and GLBA overlay all data handling.

project.goal: Automate Title IV disbursement reconciliation and COD/NSLDS federal file generation
project.target_users: Financial aid counselors and directors; FSA portal as downstream recipient
project.timeline: Production before next federal reporting deadline (NSLDS enrollment reporting monthly, COD quarterly)

backend.framework: Apache Airflow 2.9 with CeleryExecutor on Azure Container Apps; dbt Core for SQL transformations

security.auth: Azure AD (Entra ID) for Airflow UI; service principals in Key Vault for SIS/LMS APIs
security.encryption: Azure Disk Encryption for PostgreSQL; TLS 1.3 for API connections; no student data in task logs

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

devops.hosting: Azure Container Apps, Azure Database for PostgreSQL, Azure Blob Storage

constraints.file_format: COD and NSLDS flat-file specs per FSA aid year version
constraints.logging: No student financial data in Airflow task logs

reasoner_reviews.product: The pipeline scope correctly covers the full Title IV reporting lifecycle. The manual counselor review gate before COD transmission is an important risk control for the MVP — erroneous first-time COD submissions are difficult to correct and can trigger audit findings. The SAP exception flagging logic is the most complex business rule in the pipeline and must be validated against actual FSA SAP policy guidance, not just the SIS data model.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: dbt models for reconciliation transformations are the right choice — they version-control the reconciliation SQL, make the SAP and enrollment threshold logic auditable, and can be unit tested with dbt test assertions. The COD and NSLDS file generators must be implemented as separate Python modules (not inline Airflow tasks) with unit tests against FSA sample files from the FSA Technical Reference. Airflow XCom must not carry student financial data between tasks — pass record counts and status flags only.
reasoner_reviews.security: GLBA Safeguards Rule requires that student financial data be protected with technical safeguards. Student aid amounts, SSNs (required in COD records), and disbursement dates must not appear in Airflow task logs — log only record counts and processing status. The COD file staging blob in Azure Blob Storage must use private access tier and be accessible only to the Airflow worker service principal and financial aid director Azure AD accounts.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: COD flat-file layout is version-specific per aid year (e.g., 2025-2026 uses COD Common Record Layout version 18.2). The file generator must reference the correct version constants at runtime. FSA validates file checksums and record counts in the transmission header — these must be computed correctly or the entire file is rejected.

backend.service_design: Airflow DAG inventory: IngestDisbursementsDAG (triggers on SIS webhook or nightly schedule, fetches direct loan and Pell Grant disbursement records from SIS API, writes to aid_disbursements table), EnrollmentSnapshotDAG (nightly, fetches current enrollment status from SIS, writes to enrollment_snapshots), SAPIngestionDAG (nightly, fetches SAP evaluation results from SIS, writes to sap_evaluations), ReconciliationDAG (nightly after ingestion DAGs, runs dbt reconciliation models, writes exceptions to reconciliation_exceptions), CODGenerationDAG (on-demand or scheduled pre-deadline, reads validated disbursement and enrollment data, generates COD flat file per aid year spec, stages to Blob Storage, awaits counselor approval), NSLDSGenerationDAG (monthly, generates NSLDS enrollment reporting file).
backend.failure_handling: Each Airflow task configured with retries=3, retry_delay=timedelta(minutes=5). SIS API 429 responses handled by custom AirflowRetryException with Retry-After header parsing. DAG failure triggers Azure Monitor alert to financial aid director email and Teams channel. ReconciliationDAG failure does not block CODGenerationDAG — counselor can review prior-day reconciliation data. COD file generation failures produce detailed error logs with FSA field-level validation errors.
backend.background_jobs: Airflow beat schedule: EnrollmentSnapshotDAG at 01:00 UTC daily, SAPIngestionDAG at 01:30 UTC daily, ReconciliationDAG at 02:00 UTC daily (after ingestion DAGs), NSLDSGenerationDAG first day of each month at 03:00 UTC. Manual trigger: CODGenerationDAG via Airflow UI (requires financial aid director role in Azure AD).

security.auth_design: Airflow UI authenticated via Azure AD OAuth2 (azure-auth-backend); financial aid counselors have Viewer role, directors have Op role (can trigger DAGs). Azure Container Apps workload identity (managed identity) for PostgreSQL and Blob Storage access — no stored credentials. SIS and LMS API service principal credentials stored in Azure Key Vault; fetched at task start via DefaultAzureCredential.
security.secrets_management: SIS OAuth2 client_secret in Key Vault secret 'sis-api-credentials'; LMS API key in Key Vault secret 'lms-api-key'; FSA SAIG SFTP credentials in Key Vault secret 'fsa-saig-sftp'. Container Apps managed identity has Key Vault Secrets User role. Secrets fetched in PythonOperator tasks at execution time — never in DAG definition or environment variables.
security.privacy_controls: Airflow task logs exclude student financial data fields: aid amounts, SSNs, disbursement dates logged only as present/absent flags or record counts. COD files containing SSNs staged to private Blob Storage container 'cod-staging' accessible only to managed identity and authorized Azure AD accounts. Blob container access logging enabled. Post-transmission: COD staging files moved to 'cod-archive' container with Azure Blob immutable storage policy (WORM, 10-year retention).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_transmissions table: file_type (COD/NSLDS), aid_year, generated_at, record_count, file_checksum, approved_by (Azure AD user principal name), transmitted_at, fsas_confirmation_id. All federal file transmission events logged here. Azure Monitor Diagnostic Logs capture all Key Vault secret access events. Power BI report on audit_transmissions for FSA audit preparation.

data.entities: aid_disbursements (disbursement_id UUID PK, student_id UUID, ssn_encrypted VARCHAR, aid_type ENUM direct_loan_sub/direct_loan_unsub/pell_grant/plus_loan, award_amount DECIMAL, disbursed_amount DECIMAL, disbursement_date DATE, award_year VARCHAR, sis_disbursement_id VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). enrollment_snapshots (id UUID PK, student_id UUID, snapshot_date DATE, enrollment_status ENUM full_time/half_time/less_than_half/withdrawn/graduated, credit_hours INT, program_code VARCHAR, effective_date DATE). sap_evaluations (id UUID PK, student_id UUID, evaluation_date DATE, sap_status ENUM satisfactory/warning/suspended/probation, gpa DECIMAL, completion_rate DECIMAL, max_timeframe_flag BOOL). reconciliation_exceptions (id UUID PK, disbursement_id UUID FK, student_id UUID, exception_type ENUM enrollment_mismatch/sap_suspended/below_threshold/missing_enrollment, exception_detail TEXT, counselor_reviewed BOOL, resolution_action TEXT, resolved_at TIMESTAMP). federal_reporting_files (id UUID PK, file_type ENUM COD/NSLDS, aid_year VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMP, record_count INT, file_checksum VARCHAR, blob_path TEXT, transmission_status ENUM staged/approved/transmitted/confirmed). cod_transmission_records (id UUID PK, file_id UUID FK, approved_by VARCHAR, transmitted_at TIMESTAMP, fsa_confirmation_id VARCHAR, response_code VARCHAR). nslds_submissions (id UUID PK, reporting_month DATE, file_id UUID FK, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, record_count INT, fsa_ack_received BOOL).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server as primary reconciliation store; dbt models run against PostgreSQL with separate schemas: raw (ingestion landing), staging (dbt staging models), mart (reconciliation_exceptions, reporting views). Azure Blob Storage: 'cod-staging' (private, WORM after approval), 'cod-archive' (private, 10-year immutable retention), 'nslds-archive' (private, 10-year immutable).
data.retention_and_deletion: aid_disbursements, enrollment_snapshots, sap_evaluations, reconciliation_exceptions: 7 years (FERPA/HEA). federal_reporting_files and Blob archives: 10 years (HEA Title IV FSA audit requirements). Airflow task logs: 90 days (Azure Monitor Log Analytics retention). SSNs stored encrypted (Azure Transparent Data Encryption + column-level encryption via pgcrypto for ssn_encrypted field).
data.data_risks: COD files contain student SSNs in the FSA-mandated flat-file format — blob storage security and transmission channel security are critical. dbt model logic for SAP reconciliation must be validated against FSA policy guidance annually as SAP rules change between aid years.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps environment: airflow-scheduler (1 replica, always-on), airflow-worker (0-5 replicas, KEDA-scaled on Airflow CeleryExecutor queue depth), airflow-flower (1 replica, internal only). Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (General Purpose, 4 vCores, zone-redundant HA). Azure Blob Storage (LRS replication, private access tier). Azure Key Vault. Azure Container Registry for Airflow custom image.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: flake8/mypy lint, pytest for dbt model unit tests (dbt test + custom Python unit tests for COD/NSLDS file generators against FSA sample files), dbt compile for DAG syntax validation, Docker build, ACR push, Container Apps revision deploy. dbt run with --target prod triggered as Airflow task — not in CI. COD generator tested against FSA Technical Reference sample records for each supported aid year version.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor: Container Apps CPU/memory metrics, DAG duration metrics via Airflow StatsD to Azure Monitor. Alerts: DAG failure within 4 hours of federal reporting deadline (P1 — SMS + Teams), ReconciliationDAG exception_count > 50 (P2 — Teams notification to counselors). audit_transmissions Power BI report for financial aid director review before each filing deadline.
devops.rollback_strategy: Azure Container Apps revision-based rollback — previous revision reactivated within 2 minutes. PostgreSQL point-in-time restore for data recovery (35-day backup retention). dbt model rollback via Git revert; re-run ReconciliationDAG after rollback. COD files already transmitted to FSA cannot be rolled back — corrections submitted via COD correction record process.

ISS-001.title: COD file generator not validated against FSA aid-year-specific field layout version
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 and Round 2 plans specified COD file generation but did not explicitly call out that the COD Common Record Layout is versioned by aid year (e.g., v18.2 for 2025-2026). The generator must select the correct field offsets and valid value sets based on the aid_year parameter. This was flagged as blocking in Round 2 audit.

history.note: COD generator described without version parameter.
Aid year versioning still not explicitly specified in generator design.

ISS-002.title: SSN column-level encryption not specified — TDE alone insufficient for GLBA
ISS-002.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 relied on Azure Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for PostgreSQL encryption. GLBA Safeguards Rule requires that SSNs stored in the aid_disbursements table be protected with application-layer column-level encryption in addition to TDE, so that database administrators without data access grants cannot read SSNs in plaintext.

history.note: Only TDE mentioned for SSN protection.
Column-level encryption not added to data model or security sections.

focus_issues.title: COD file generator not validated against FSA aid-year-specific field layout version
focus_issues.detail: Aid year versioning parameter added to COD generator in Round 3 — now selects correct COD Common Record Layout version per aid year.

focus_issues.title: SSN column-level encryption not specified — TDE alone insufficient for GLBA
focus_issues.detail: ssn_encrypted column requires pgcrypto column-level encryption in addition to TDE to protect against privileged DB access.

latest_recommendations: Parameterize COD file generator with aid_year argument selecting the correct COD Common Record Layout version (v18.2 for 2025-2026, v17.1 for 2024-2025); unit test generator against FSA Technical Reference sample files per version.
Implement pgcrypto column-level encryption for aid_disbursements.ssn_encrypted using a key stored in Key Vault; restrict pgcrypto decryption function execution to financial_aid_service role only.
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Title IV Aid Disbursement Pipeline — Round 2

convergence_state.reason: Two issues persisted from Round 1; both required specific technical additions to data model and backend sections.

previous_audits.summary: Pipeline architecture is sound with correct dbt reconciliation model approach and Airflow DAG structure. Two issues from Round 1 remain unresolved: COD generator missing aid-year version parameterization, and SSN protection relies solely on TDE without column-level encryption.
strengths: dbt staging/mart schema separation is correct for auditable reconciliation logic
WORM Blob Storage for COD archives is correctly specified
Airflow task log sanitization (excluding student financial data) is correctly described
