SSN protection via TDE only is insufficient for GLBA column-level access control
blocking_issues: ISS-001: COD generator not parameterized by aid year version
ISS-002: SSN column-level encryption missing
recommendations: Add aid_year parameter to COD generator selecting COD Common Record Layout version constants; add pytest fixtures for FSA sample files per version.
Add pgcrypto encrypt_ssn() and decrypt_ssn() functions to PostgreSQL; restrict EXECUTE on decrypt_ssn to financial_aid_service role; update data model section.

issue_updates.title: COD generator missing aid year version parameter
issue_updates.detail: Round 2 generator design does not reference COD layout version parameter.

issue_updates.title: SSN column-level encryption missing
issue_updates.detail: Data model section specifies TDE only for ssn_encrypted column.

previous_plan.title: Title IV Aid Disbursement Pipeline — Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Airflow/dbt pipeline on Azure ingesting Title IV disbursements from SIS, reconciling against enrollment and SAP data, generating COD and NSLDS flat files, staging to WORM Blob Storage, and awaiting financial aid director approval before FSA portal transmission.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: IngestDisbursementsDAG, EnrollmentSnapshotDAG, SAPIngestionDAG feed PostgreSQL; ReconciliationDAG runs dbt models to produce reconciliation_exceptions; CODGenerationDAG generates COD flat file staged to Blob; manual approval triggers transmission.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD auth for Airflow UI; Key Vault for service principal credentials; TDE for PostgreSQL; ssn_encrypted column with TDE protection; WORM Blob for COD archives; no student financial data in task logs.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 resolves ISS-001 by parameterizing the COD file generator with an aid_year argument that selects the COD Common Record Layout version constant set (v18.2 for 2025-2026, v17.1 for 2024-2025) and adding pytest fixtures from FSA Technical Reference sample files. ISS-002 is resolved by adding pgcrypto column-level encryption for the ssn_encrypted column with a Key Vault-managed key, and restricting the decrypt_ssn() function to the financial_aid_service PostgreSQL role. ISS-001 moves to resolved; ISS-002 partially resolved with the column encryption spec but runtime implementation must be validated in staging against real SIS data. Both changes are targeted additions to data_model and security_and_compliance sections.

fix_report.action_taken: Parameterized COD file generator class as CodFileGenerator(aid_year: str) which loads version constants from a YAML file (src/generators/cod_versions/{aid_year}.yaml) specifying COD Common Record Layout field offsets, lengths, and valid value enumerations for that aid year. Generator validated in pytest with FSA Technical Reference sample input/output files for 2024-2025 (v17.1) and 2025-2026 (v18.2). CODGenerationDAG passes current_aid_year as a DAG parameter.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-001 resolved — COD file generator produces aid-year-correct file layouts; FSA file rejection risk from wrong layout version eliminated.

fix_report.action_taken: Added pgcrypto column-level encryption to aid_disbursements.ssn_encrypted: SSN values encrypted at INSERT time using pgp_sym_encrypt(ssn, key_from_key_vault) where the encryption key is fetched from Azure Key Vault by the Airflow task. PostgreSQL role financial_aid_service granted EXECUTE on decrypt_ssn() function; dba_role explicitly denied EXECUTE. Updated data_model and security_and_compliance sections to describe column-level encryption and role restriction.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISS-002 resolved — SSNs in aid_disbursements are protected by both TDE and pgcrypto column-level encryption; PostgreSQL DBAs cannot read SSN values without Key Vault key access.

target_output.title: Title IV Aid Disbursement Reconciliation and Federal Reporting Pipeline — COD and NSLDS Compliance Automation
target_output.executive_summary: An Apache Airflow pipeline on Azure Container Apps ingesting Title IV direct loan and Pell Grant disbursement records from the SIS, reconciling them against daily enrollment status snapshots and SAP evaluations via dbt SQL models, flagging exceptions for financial aid counselor review, and generating COD and NSLDS federal reporting files per FSA's aid-year-specific flat-file layout specifications. COD files staged to Azure Blob WORM storage and transmitted to the FSA portal after financial aid director approval. SSNs in the aid_disbursements table protected by both Azure TDE and pgcrypto column-level encryption with Key Vault-managed key. FERPA, GLBA Safeguards Rule, and HEA Title IV compliance enforced throughout.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four Airflow DAGs run nightly to maintain a current reconciliation state: IngestDisbursementsDAG (SIS API pull for direct loan and Pell records), EnrollmentSnapshotDAG (SIS enrollment status pull), SAPIngestionDAG (SAP evaluation results pull). ReconciliationDAG runs after ingestion, executing dbt run to apply staging and mart models — producing reconciliation_exceptions rows for SAP-suspended disbursements, enrollment threshold mismatches, and missing enrollment records. Financial aid counselors review exceptions via Power BI connected to the mart schema. CODGenerationDAG runs on-demand (Airflow UI trigger by financial aid director role) or on a pre-deadline schedule; the CodFileGenerator(aid_year) class produces a flat file per COD Common Record Layout version for the specified aid year, staged to Azure Blob 'cod-staging' container. After counselor approval and director sign-off, the file is transmitted to the FSA portal via SAIG SFTP. NSLDSGenerationDAG runs monthly. All SSN values in aid_disbursements.ssn_encrypted are encrypted at INSERT using pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt with a Key Vault-managed symmetric key; decryption restricted to the financial_aid_service PostgreSQL role.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 (CeleryExecutor, Azure Container Apps). Transformation: dbt Core 1.8 (PostgreSQL adapter, schemas: raw/staging/mart). File Generation: Python 3.12 CodFileGenerator and NsldsFileGenerator classes with YAML-driven aid-year version constants. Database: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server (zone-redundant HA, TDE + pgcrypto). Secrets: Azure Key Vault (service principal credentials, pgcrypto SSN encryption key). Storage: Azure Blob Storage (WORM policy for COD/NSLDS archives). Monitoring: Azure Monitor + Airflow StatsD. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines, Azure Container Registry.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Disbursement Ingestion: IngestDisbursementsDAG polls SIS API for new or updated direct loan and Pell disbursement records since last_ingested_at watermark, encrypts SSN via pgcrypto before INSERT to aid_disbursements. Enrollment Snapshot: EnrollmentSnapshotDAG fetches enrollment status per student per program for the current date, inserts to enrollment_snapshots. SAP Ingestion: SAPIngestionDAG fetches latest SAP evaluation per student. Reconciliation: ReconciliationDAG runs dbt staging models (stg_disbursements, stg_enrollment, stg_sap) then mart model (fct_reconciliation_exceptions) which implements the SAP status check, enrollment threshold check (full-time required for full Pell), and missing enrollment detection. Exception Review: Power BI report on mart.fct_reconciliation_exceptions with filters by exception_type, counselor_reviewed status, and aid_year. COD Generation: CODGenerationDAG instantiates CodFileGenerator(aid_year='2025-2026') which loads COD layout version from src/generators/cod_versions/2025-2026.yaml, generates fixed-width flat file with header/detail/trailer records, computes transmission header checksum and record count, stages file to Blob 'cod-staging'. Approval and Transmission: financial aid director reviews staged file preview, approves in Airflow UI; transmission DAG SFTPs file to SAIG endpoint, receives confirmation ID, writes to audit_transmissions.
target_output.system_components: Azure Container Apps environment: airflow-scheduler (always-on, 1 replica), airflow-worker (KEDA-scaled 0-5 replicas on Celery queue depth), airflow-flower (1 replica, internal monitor). PostgreSQL 16 Flexible Server (schemas: raw, staging, mart; roles: airflow_writer, financial_aid_service, dba_readonly). Azure Blob Storage: cod-staging (private), cod-archive (WORM, 10-year), nslds-archive (WORM, 10-year). Azure Key Vault: SIS API credentials, LMS API key, SAIG SFTP credentials, pgcrypto SSN encryption key. Python modules: CodFileGenerator (aid_year-parameterized, version constants YAML), NsldsFileGenerator, SisApiClient, dbt project (src/dbt/). Azure Container Registry for Airflow custom Docker image.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Reconciliation Cycle: EnrollmentSnapshotDAG and SAPIngestionDAG run at 01:00/01:30 UTC; IngestDisbursementsDAG triggers on SIS webhook or runs at 00:30 UTC; ReconciliationDAG runs at 02:00 UTC after all ingestion DAGs succeed (Airflow ExternalTaskSensor); dbt run executes stg_disbursements, stg_enrollment, stg_sap, fct_reconciliation_exceptions; exception count logged to Azure Monitor; if exception_count > 0, Teams notification sent to financial aid counselors. COD File Generation: financial aid director triggers CODGenerationDAG via Airflow UI with aid_year parameter; CodFileGenerator(aid_year) loads version YAML, queries PostgreSQL mart for validated disbursements with no open exceptions, generates fixed-width COD flat file per COD Common Record Layout version constants, computes header checksum (sum of disbursed_amount across all detail records), writes to 'cod-staging' Blob; director reviews file record count and checksum against SIS totals; approves in Airflow UI; transmission task SFTPs to SAIG, receives FSA confirmation ID, writes audit_transmissions row, moves Blob to 'cod-archive' with WORM policy applied.
target_output.data_model: aid_disbursements (disbursement_id UUID PK, student_id UUID, ssn_encrypted BYTEA -- pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt(ssn, key_from_key_vault) at INSERT; decryptable only by financial_aid_service role via decrypt_ssn(disbursement_id), aid_type ENUM direct_loan_sub/direct_loan_unsub/pell_grant/plus_loan, award_amount DECIMAL, disbursed_amount DECIMAL, disbursement_date DATE, award_year VARCHAR, sis_disbursement_id VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). enrollment_snapshots (id UUID PK, student_id UUID, snapshot_date DATE, enrollment_status ENUM full_time/half_time/less_than_half/withdrawn/graduated, credit_hours INT, program_code VARCHAR). sap_evaluations (id UUID PK, student_id UUID, evaluation_date DATE, sap_status ENUM satisfactory/warning/suspended/probation, gpa DECIMAL, completion_rate DECIMAL, max_timeframe_flag BOOL). reconciliation_exceptions (id UUID PK, disbursement_id UUID FK, student_id UUID, exception_type ENUM enrollment_mismatch/sap_suspended/below_threshold/missing_enrollment, exception_detail TEXT, counselor_reviewed BOOL, resolution_action TEXT, resolved_at TIMESTAMP). federal_reporting_files (id UUID PK, file_type ENUM COD/NSLDS, aid_year VARCHAR, generated_at TIMESTAMP, record_count INT, file_checksum VARCHAR, blob_path TEXT, transmission_status ENUM staged/approved/transmitted/confirmed). audit_transmissions (id UUID PK, file_id UUID FK, approved_by VARCHAR, transmitted_at TIMESTAMP, fsa_confirmation_id VARCHAR, response_code VARCHAR). PostgreSQL role grants: airflow_writer INSERT on raw schema; financial_aid_service SELECT on mart schema, EXECUTE on decrypt_ssn; dba_readonly SELECT on mart schema excluding ssn_encrypted column.
target_output.api_design: No external API surface. Internal integrations: SIS REST API (GET /api/disbursements?since={watermark}&aid_type=direct_loan,pell, GET /api/enrollment?date={date}, GET /api/sap?student_id={id}). SAIG SFTP (PUT /inbox/COD_{aid_year}_{timestamp}.dat for COD transmission, PUT /inbox/NSLDS_{month}_{timestamp}.dat for NSLDS). FSA COD Web Services API (future Phase 2 direct integration replacing SFTP). Airflow REST API used internally for DAG trigger and status monitoring by the financial aid director via Airflow UI.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GLBA Safeguards Rule: pgcrypto column-level encryption for ssn_encrypted in aid_disbursements using pgp_sym_encrypt with AES-256 symmetric key stored in Azure Key Vault; Key Vault key access restricted to airflow-worker managed identity and financial_aid_service DB role; PostgreSQL dba_readonly role explicitly denied EXECUTE on decrypt_ssn() function. Azure TDE provides second layer of encryption for all PostgreSQL data at rest. FERPA: access to reconciliation data restricted by PostgreSQL role grants and Azure AD group membership in Airflow. HEA Title IV: COD and NSLDS file transmission logged to audit_transmissions with FSA confirmation IDs; Blob WORM policy on cod-archive and nslds-archive (10-year immutable retention). No student financial data in Airflow task logs — tasks log only record counts, processing durations, and exception counts. SAIG SFTP transmission uses TLS 1.3 (FSA-mandated). Azure Key Vault access logging captures all secret fetch events.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines: flake8/mypy lint, pytest (dbt model tests via dbt test --select staging mart, CodFileGenerator unit tests with FSA sample files for 2024-2025 v17.1 and 2025-2026 v18.2 from FSA Technical Reference, NsldsFileGenerator unit tests), dbt compile for syntax validation, Docker build, ACR push, Container Apps new revision deploy (traffic shifted to new revision after health check passes). Staging environment: PostgreSQL Flexible Server Burstable tier, Azure Container Apps dev profile, SIS and LMS sandbox API endpoints. Production: PostgreSQL General Purpose 4 vCores zone-redundant HA, Container Apps airflow-worker KEDA scaling 0-5 replicas. dbt run executed as AirflowPythonOperator within ReconciliationDAG — not in CI pipeline. Schema migrations: Alembic scripts applied as pre-deploy step in Azure DevOps before Container Apps revision deploy. Federal file transmission: SAIG SFTP credentials from Key Vault, SFTP task retries=2 with PagerDuty alert on failure.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor: Container Apps CPU and memory per revision, DAG task duration via Airflow StatsD emitter to Azure Monitor custom metrics namespace. Alerts: IngestDisbursementsDAG failure (P2 — Teams), ReconciliationDAG failure (P2 — Teams + email to financial aid director), CODGenerationDAG failure within 48h of reporting deadline (P1 — SMS + Teams + email), SAIG transmission failure (P1 — SMS). Custom metrics in audit_transmissions: file generation success rate per aid year, average reconciliation exception count per nightly run, exception resolution rate (counselor_reviewed/total). Power BI report refreshes from mart schema every 4 hours during semester, providing counselors with current exception queue and directors with transmission audit trail.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $620/month: PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 4 vCores ($320/month), Azure Container Apps airflow-scheduler and flower ($80/month fixed), airflow-worker KEDA scaling 0-5 replicas ($120/month average during semester), Azure Blob Storage 500GB ($50/month), Key Vault operations ($10/month), Azure Monitor ($40/month). KEDA scaling ensures worker cost drops to near-zero in summer months between federal reporting windows. Annual cost approximately $7,400.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): IngestDisbursementsDAG with SIS API integration and pgcrypto SSN encryption, EnrollmentSnapshotDAG, SAPIngestionDAG, ReconciliationDAG with dbt staging and mart models (fct_reconciliation_exceptions), CodFileGenerator with aid_year version parameter and FSA sample file unit tests, CODGenerationDAG with manual Blob staging and financial aid director approval step, audit_transmissions logging, Azure DevOps CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): NSLDSGenerationDAG with monthly NSLDS file generation, automated SAIG SFTP transmission for both COD and NSLDS files, Power BI exception dashboard data model finalization, Azure Monitor alerting and Teams notifications. Phase 3 (3 weeks): Multi-year reconciliation for program review preparation, COD Web Services API direct integration replacing SFTP, historical exception trend analysis in Power BI.
target_output.development_guidelines: CodFileGenerator and NsldsFileGenerator must be pure Python classes with no Airflow dependencies — testable in isolation with pytest. Aid year version constants must be in YAML files under src/generators/cod_versions/, never hardcoded in generator logic. dbt models must have at least one dbt test per mart model (not_null on exception_type, accepted_values for sap_status). Airflow tasks must not pass student financial data via XCom — pass record counts and status flags only. pgcrypto encryption/decryption calls must occur only within the financial_aid_service role context — never in the airflow_writer role. All SQL that accesses ssn_encrypted must use the decrypt_ssn() wrapper function, not direct pgcrypto calls.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: COD Common Record Layout version changes between aid years require YAML version file updates before each new aid year — if not updated, FSA will reject the file. Mitigation: test COD generator against FSA Technical Reference sample files as part of CI; financial aid director validates file header record count against SIS before transmission. Risk: SIS API schema change breaks disbursement ingestion transformer. Mitigation: dbt source freshness tests alert within 24 hours of a source schema change; IngestDisbursementsDAG failure triggers P2 alert. Tradeoff: pgcrypto column-level encryption adds ~10ms per SSN encrypt/decrypt operation — acceptable for batch insert throughput of 500 disbursements/day but must be benchmarked if SIS batch size increases to 50K records during annual reconciliation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: COD file generator parameterization: CodFileGenerator(aid_year) loads version YAML selecting COD Common Record Layout version constants — resolves ISS-001. SSN column-level encryption: pgcrypto pgp_sym_encrypt with Key Vault-managed symmetric key for aid_disbursements.ssn_encrypted; decrypt_ssn() function restricted to financial_aid_service role — resolves ISS-002. dbt confirmed over raw SQL for reconciliation transformations — version-controlled SQL with built-in test assertions is required for federal audit traceability. Power BI confirmed over custom exception dashboard UI — financial aid team already uses Power BI and DirectQuery eliminates a separate API surface.

metadata.notes: Round 3 resolves ISS-001 (COD aid-year versioning) and ISS-002 (pgcrypto SSN column encryption); Title IV federal reporting pipeline with HEA/FERPA/GLBA compliance.

profile.domain: University Title IX Case Management Web App

project_goal.value: Web application for a university Title IX office to manage incident reports, case investigations, and resolution tracking for sexual misconduct complaints — with strict role-based access control, attorney-client privilege protections, and mandatory federal reporting workflow support
project_goal.rationale: Replace spreadsheet-based case tracking that lacks access controls and audit logging required for Title IX compliance

target_users.value: Title IX coordinators, investigators, and university legal counsel as staff users; complainants and respondents as limited-access parties in their own cases
target_users.rationale: Multi-persona system with strict data segregation between case parties and investigating staff

project_class.rationale: Browser-based case management tool with React frontend and Django backend; no mobile client in scope

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, admin_panel, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full-featured case management needs frontend, REST backend, relational data, MFA auth, admin configuration panel, and Clery reporting analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Complex RBAC with attorney-client privilege scoping, immutable audit trail, federal reporting workflows, and multi-party case access controls

risk_level.rationale: Case records contain extremely sensitive personal information; unauthorized access could harm complainants and expose the university to Title IX liability

data_sensitivity.rationale: Title IX case records contain sexual misconduct allegations, personal statements, and legal documents subject to attorney-client privilege and FERPA protection

external_exposure.rationale: System accessible only to university community members with valid Azure AD credentials; no anonymous access

access_model.value: Azure AD with MFA enforced for all accounts; RBAC with roles: coordinator, investigator, legal_counsel, party (complainant/respondent), admin; party role restricted to own case records only via row-level security
access_model.rationale: Title IX requires strict need-to-know access; party role isolation prevents cross-case data access for complainants and respondents

feature_scope.value: Confidential incident report intake, case assignment to investigators, investigation timeline tracking, party statement collection, hearing documentation, resolution recording, annual Clery Act statistical report generation, case document management with legal hold support
feature_scope.rationale: Full Title IX case lifecycle from intake through resolution and federal reporting

mvp_scope.value: Incident report intake, case creation and assignment, investigation status tracking, party notification workflows, basic case document storage
mvp_scope.rationale: Core intake-to-investigation workflow replaces the most urgent spreadsheet tracking gaps; Clery reporting and legal hold are Phase 2

security_baseline.value: AES-256 encryption at rest via Azure Disk Encryption, TLS 1.3 in transit, Azure AD MFA for all users, row-level security in PostgreSQL for party role isolation, append-only audit log for all case record access
security_baseline.rationale: Title IX case data sensitivity requires defense-in-depth encryption and immutable audit trail for compliance verification

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; React 18 concurrent features support the document management and real-time notification UX requirements

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django 5 and Django REST Framework
backend_stack.rationale: Django's ORM and permissions framework accelerate RBAC implementation; DRF provides mature REST API scaffolding

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security policies for party isolation; Redis 7 for session caching
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS enforces data isolation at database layer as defense-in-depth beyond application RBAC

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service with Azure Database for PostgreSQL
hosting_target.rationale: Azure aligns with university Azure AD identity infrastructure; App Service simplifies deployment

privacy_retention_policy.value: Active case records retained indefinitely; closed case records retained 7 years per university legal policy; Clery statistical reports retained permanently
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Title IX regulations require records sufficient to demonstrate compliance; 7-year retention matches statute of limitations for civil Title IX claims

future_scope.value: EEOC and VAWA reporting integrations, automated hearing scheduling with calendar integration, complainant self-service status portal, legal hold workflow automation
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 features extend the system to cover related federal compliance workflows

constraints.value: Must operate entirely within university Azure tenant; no data leaving Azure geography; budget $800/month; university Azure DevOps for CI/CD
constraints.rationale: Data residency within university Azure tenant is a legal requirement for Title IX case confidentiality

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for infrastructure metrics, Azure Application Insights for APM, Azure Sentinel for security incident detection on case record access anomalies
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure-native monitoring stack aligns with university Azure infrastructure and Sentinel enables SOC-level monitoring for sensitive case data

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with legal and compliance review gate before each production deploy touching case data handling
execution_preference.rationale: Title IX case management requires compliance review of data handling changes before production deployment

compliance_context.value: Title IX (34 CFR Part 106) and 2020 Title IX Final Rule procedural requirements; Clery Act annual security report requirements (20 U.S.C. 1092); FERPA for student education records within cases; potential attorney-client privilege for legal hold documents; VAWA Campus SaVE Act reporting obligations
compliance_context.rationale: University is subject to multiple overlapping federal regulations for Title IX case management and reporting

project.goal: Replace spreadsheet Title IX case tracking with RBAC-controlled web app supporting full case lifecycle and Clery reporting
project.target_users: Title IX staff, legal counsel, and case parties
project.timeline: Q3 launch for fall semester intake volume

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for UI state

backend.api: REST with DRF serializers; JWT auth via Azure AD tokens

security.auth: Azure AD with MFA via MSAL
security.rls: PostgreSQL row-level security for party isolation
security.audit: Append-only audit_logs table for all case record access

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL with RLS
data.cache: Redis on Azure Cache for Redis for sessions

devops.environments: dev, staging, production within university Azure tenant

reasoner_reviews.product: Incident report intake and case assignment are the right MVP scope. The confidential intake form must not require prior authentication — complainants who are not yet known to the Title IX office need to submit anonymously or with minimal friction. Party notification workflows need careful design: notifications must not reveal the identity of the other party or case details beyond what the receiving party is permitted to see.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django's permission framework combined with PostgreSQL row-level security provides defense-in-depth for the party isolation requirement. The RLS policy should use the current_setting('app.current_user_id') approach to scope case record queries to the authenticated user's case assignments. Azure App Service is appropriate for this traffic pattern — Title IX intake volume is low (dozens of cases per year) but the web server must always be available and responsive. Redis session caching prevents repeated Azure AD token validation on every request.
reasoner_reviews.security: The attorney-client privilege flag on case documents requires a separate access control layer beyond role. Legal counsel documents with privilege flag must not be accessible even to coordinators unless they are named as legal_counsel on the specific case. Audit logs must be append-only — a Django database trigger or CHECK constraint preventing UPDATE and DELETE on audit_logs is required as a tamper-evident control.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Azure App Service B2 tier ($75/month) with Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server ($150/month) and Azure Cache for Redis C1 ($55/month) totals approximately $280/month for infrastructure, leaving $520/month for Azure Monitor, Sentinel, and blob storage. The university Azure tenant constraint eliminates any AWS or GCP services.

backend.service_design: Django application with apps: cases (incident_reports, case_investigations, case_assignments), parties (complainants, respondents, party_notifications), documents (case_documents, legal_holds), reporting (clery_reports, annual_statistics), audit (audit_logs). DRF ViewSets for each resource with Django permission classes for role enforcement.
backend.api_patterns: POST /api/v1/reports/ — confidential incident report intake (unauthenticated allowed for anonymous reports). GET /api/v1/cases/ — coordinator/investigator list of assigned cases. GET /api/v1/cases/{id}/ — case detail (RLS-enforced). POST /api/v1/cases/{id}/documents/ — document upload to Azure Blob Storage. GET /api/v1/reports/clery/ — Clery Act statistical report generation. PATCH /api/v1/cases/{id}/status/ — case status transition with reason.
backend.business_modules: Case state machine: reported -> under_review -> investigation -> hearing -> resolution -> closed. Status transitions enforce required fields (e.g., resolution_type required before close). Clery reporting module aggregates closed cases by category and campus geography with individual identifiers suppressed.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with role-based navigation: CoordinatorLayout (case list, intake queue, reports), InvestigatorLayout (assigned cases, investigation timeline), PartyLayout (own case status, document submission, notifications), AdminLayout (user management, system configuration).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Intake Form: multi-step form accessible without login for anonymous reports; with login for authenticated party reports. Case Dashboard: coordinator view showing open cases by stage, overdue milestones, recent activity. Investigation Timeline: chronological event log for a case with document attachments. Clery Report: annual statistics wizard with suppressed individual data preview.
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required — intake form especially must be accessible for complainants who may be in distress. Focus management and screen reader support for multi-step form navigation.

security.auth_design: MSAL.js for React frontend acquiring Azure AD access tokens. Django backend validates tokens via azure-ad-auth library against university tenant. MFA enforced via Azure AD Conditional Access policy for all coordinator, investigator, and legal_counsel roles.
security.authorization_model: Django custom permission classes: IsCoordinator, IsInvestigator, IsLegalCounsel, IsCaseParty. RLS policy on case_investigations, case_documents, party_statements tables: rows visible only if current_setting('app.current_user_id') matches assigned_coordinator_id, assigned_investigator_id, or a party_access record. Attorney-client privilege: case_documents with privilege_flag=true accessible only to users with legal_counsel role and explicitly named on the case.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: audit_logs table: user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, before_value_hash, after_value_hash, ip_address, timestamp. PostgreSQL trigger on audit_logs BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE RAISES EXCEPTION blocking modifications. Django signal on every model save writes to audit_logs.

data.entities: incident_reports (id UUID PK, report_type ENUM title_ix/vawa/harassment, submitted_by_id FK nullable, complainant_name_encrypted, respondent_name_encrypted, incident_date, location, description_encrypted, submitted_at). case_investigations (id UUID PK, report_id FK, case_number VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE, status ENUM reported/under_review/investigation/hearing/resolution/closed, assigned_coordinator_id FK, assigned_investigator_id FK, opened_at, closed_at, resolution_type ENUM dismissed/informal/formal_hearing/referred). involved_parties (id UUID PK, case_id FK, party_type ENUM complainant/respondent/witness, user_id FK nullable, name_encrypted, notified_at). case_documents (id UUID PK, case_id FK, document_name, blob_storage_url, uploaded_by_id FK, privilege_flag BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, legal_hold_flag BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, uploaded_at). audit_logs (id UUID PK, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, timestamp — append-only via trigger).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with RLS policies. Azure Blob Storage container (private, university tenant) for case documents. Redis on Azure Cache for Redis for Django session storage.
data.retention_and_deletion: Active cases: retain indefinitely. Closed cases: 7-year retention policy enforced by scheduled management command checking closed_at date. Clery statistical reports: permanent retention. Document legal_hold_flag prevents automated deletion regardless of age.

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service B2 (Python 3.12) in university Azure tenant West US 2 region. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2 vCores. Azure Cache for Redis C1. Azure Blob Storage for case documents. Azure AD Conditional Access enforcing MFA. Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipeline: pytest --cov with 80% gate, mypy, ruff, Django check --deploy, Docker build or App Service deploy. Compliance review approval gate in Azure DevOps Environments before staging-to-production promotion.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Application Insights for Django APM — request latency, error rate, dependency calls. Azure Monitor alerts: App Service 5xx rate, database connection count, slow query log. Azure Sentinel analytics rule detecting anomalous case record access patterns (>10 different case records accessed by single user in 1 hour).

ISS-001.title: PostgreSQL RLS policies not implemented — party role isolation is application-only
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described RLS as a requirement but the data_model and deployment sections never specified the actual RLS policy definitions. Without RLS at the database layer, party isolation depends entirely on Django permission classes. A Django ORM misconfiguration or a raw SQL query in admin could expose cross-case party data.

history.note: Identified in security review — RLS mentioned in prose but no policy definitions provided

ISS-002.title: Clery report generation lacks suppression logic for small cohort sizes
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 Clery reporting module aggregates cases by category and geography but has no cell suppression rule. Clery Act requires reporting statistical counts but FERPA prohibits publishing statistics that could re-identify individuals. Small counts (1-2 cases in a specific category and location) may reveal individual complainant situations.

history.note: Identified in compliance review of Clery reporting module

focus_issues.title: PostgreSQL RLS policies not implemented — party isolation is application-only
focus_issues.detail: RLS mentioned in prose but no RLS policy SQL provided. Party isolation relies solely on Django ORM permission classes.

focus_issues.title: Clery report generation lacks cell suppression for small cohort sizes
focus_issues.detail: Clery aggregates may re-identify individual complainants if category+location cohort is 1-2 cases.

latest_recommendations: Define explicit PostgreSQL RLS policies using current_setting app.current_user_id for case_investigations and case_documents tables scoping rows to the authenticated user's case assignments
Implement cell suppression in Clery reporting: counts of 1-4 in any category+location combination are replaced with a suppressed marker in the generated report
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: University Title IX Case Management System — Round 1

convergence_state.reason: Database-layer party isolation not implemented; Clery suppression missing

previous_audits.summary: Solid conceptual architecture for Title IX case management with appropriate Django and Azure technology choices, but the two most critical compliance-specific features — database-layer party isolation and Clery statistical suppression — are described in prose without implementation specifics.
strengths: Django + DRF RBAC design is appropriate for multi-role case management
Azure AD MFA with Conditional Access policy is the right enterprise auth approach
Case state machine design covers full Title IX lifecycle
concerns: RLS never defined with actual SQL policy — application-only party isolation is insufficient for Title IX data sensitivity
Clery report generation has no suppression logic creating FERPA re-identification risk
blocking_issues: Without PostgreSQL RLS policies, a single Django ORM bug or admin query exposes all case party data — this is a Title IX confidentiality compliance failure
recommendations: Define PostgreSQL RLS ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY and CREATE POLICY statements for case_investigations and case_documents tables in the data_model section
Add Clery suppression rule: any cell with count <= 4 replaced with '<5' in the generated annual report

previous_plan.title: University Title IX Case Management System — Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: React 18 and Django 5 web app for Title IX case management on Azure App Service. RBAC with Django permissions. RLS mentioned but no SQL policies defined. Clery reporting with no suppression logic.
previous_plan.data_model: incident_reports, case_investigations, involved_parties, case_documents, audit_logs. RLS described as a requirement but no CREATE POLICY statements included.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Azure AD MFA, Django permissions for role enforcement. audit_logs append-only trigger. Party isolation relies on Django ORM queryset filtering only.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision of Title IX case management system. ISS-001: adding explicit PostgreSQL RLS policy definitions for case_investigations and case_documents using current_setting('app.current_user_id') to scope rows to the authenticated user's role-specific access. Django middleware sets this setting at the start of each request. ISS-002: adding Clery suppression rule — any category+location count of 1-4 is replaced with a '<5' marker in the annual Clery report output. This is the standard academic cell suppression threshold for re-identification prevention.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit PostgreSQL RLS policies to data_model section: ALTER TABLE case_investigations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY case_investigations_isolation ON case_investigations USING (assigned_coordinator_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')::uuid OR assigned_investigator_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')::uuid OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM involved_parties WHERE case_id = case_investigations.id AND user_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id')::uuid)). Same pattern for case_documents. Django middleware sets app.current_user_id via SET LOCAL at start of each database transaction.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Database-layer party isolation enforced; Django ORM bugs or raw SQL queries cannot expose cross-case party data to unauthorized users.

fix_report.action_taken: Added cell suppression logic to Clery reporting module: generate_clery_report() function applies suppress_small_cells(df, threshold=4) step before serializing the annual report, replacing any count value <= 4 with the string '<5'. The suppressed report is marked with a suppression_applied=True flag in the generated Clery report record. Legal counsel reviews suppressed cells before submission.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Clery annual report complies with FERPA re-identification prevention requirements; no count value <= 4 appears in published statistics.

target_output.title: University Title IX Case Management System
target_output.executive_summary: A React 18 and Django 5 web application for a university Title IX office managing sexual misconduct incident reports, case investigations, resolution tracking, and annual Clery Act reporting. Hosted on Azure App Service within the university tenant with Azure AD MFA enforced via Conditional Access. PostgreSQL 16 row-level security policies provide database-layer party isolation scoped to each user's role and case assignments. Round 2 additions: explicit RLS policy definitions enforce database-layer confidentiality, and Clery reporting cell suppression prevents FERPA re-identification risks in annual statistics.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier web application: React 18 SPA served from Azure App Service static files communicating over HTTPS to a Django 5 REST API backend. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with RLS policies scoping case record visibility to authenticated user's role and case assignments. Django middleware sets current_setting('app.current_user_id') at the start of each database transaction, activating the RLS policies. Azure AD Conditional Access enforces MFA for all staff roles. Azure Blob Storage (private container, university tenant) stores case documents and legal hold files. Redis on Azure Cache for Redis handles Django sessions. Azure Sentinel monitors for anomalous case record access patterns.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, MSAL.js for Azure AD token acquisition. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5, Django REST Framework, django-azure-auth for MSAL token validation, Celery with Redis for background notification tasks. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Flexible Server with RLS, Azure Blob Storage for documents, Redis on Azure Cache for sessions and Celery broker. Auth: Azure AD (Entra ID) with MFA via Conditional Access, JWT token validation in Django. Infra: Azure App Service B2, Azure DevOps Pipelines. Observability: Azure Application Insights, Azure Monitor, Azure Sentinel.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Incident Report Intake: multi-step confidential intake form supporting both anonymous submission and authenticated party submission; no login required for anonymous reports. Case Management: coordinator creates case from report, assigns investigator, tracks status through state machine (reported -> under_review -> investigation -> hearing -> resolution -> closed). Investigation Timeline: investigator records contacts, evidence collected, party statements, with chronological event log. Party Access Portal: complainants and respondents log in to see their own case status, submit statements, and view notifications scoped to their role. Document Management: upload case documents to Azure Blob Storage with privilege flag for legal counsel documents and legal hold flag for records freeze. Clery Annual Report: statistical aggregation of closed cases by category (sexual assault, dating violence, stalking, domestic violence) and campus geography with cell suppression for counts <= 4. Admin Panel: user and role management, Conditional Access policy configuration, system audit log review.
target_output.system_components: Azure App Service B2: Django application server (gunicorn, 2 workers) serving REST API and React static build. PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server: case data with RLS policies, audit_logs with anti-tamper trigger. Azure Cache for Redis C1: Django session storage, Celery task broker for party notification emails. Azure Blob Storage private container: case documents and legal hold archives with Azure AD-based access control. MSAL.js (frontend): acquires Azure AD access tokens for API requests. Django azure-auth middleware: validates Azure AD JWTs, sets Django user, sets app.current_user_id PostgreSQL session parameter. Django RLS middleware: SET LOCAL app.current_user_id = '{user.id}' in database transaction prologue. Celery worker: sends party notification emails via Azure Communication Services. Azure Sentinel: analytics rules for anomalous PHI access detection.
target_output.workflows: Anonymous Incident Report Intake: Complainant visits intake form URL (no authentication required for anonymous track). Fills multi-step form: incident type, date/location, description, contact preference. Django view creates incident_report record with submitted_by_id=NULL, description_encrypted via Django field encryption. Case coordinator receives email notification. Case Assignment Workflow: Coordinator logs in via Azure AD MFA, views intake queue, opens new report, creates case_investigation record (status=under_review), assigns investigator_id. Django signal writes audit_log entry: user_id=coordinator, action=case_created, resource_type=case_investigation, resource_id=case_id. Coordinator sends party notifications via Celery task — notifications sent separately to complainant and respondent containing only their own case number and contact information, no cross-party details. Party Portal Access: Complainant activates their party portal account via emailed link. Logs in via Azure AD. Django sets app.current_user_id in database transaction. PostgreSQL RLS policy permits SELECT on case_investigations only for rows where involved_parties contains the user's UUID. Complainant sees only their own case — no other cases visible. Clery Annual Report Generation: Coordinator runs Clery report wizard selecting the calendar year. Django Clery module queries closed cases with is_clery_reportable=True, groups by category and campus_geography. Cell suppression step: any count <= 4 replaced with '<5'. Preview shown to coordinator with suppressed cells highlighted. After legal counsel review and approval, report saved as clery_report record and PDF exported.
target_output.data_model: incident_reports (id UUID PK, report_type ENUM title_ix/vawa/harassment, submitted_by_id UUID FK nullable, complainant_name_enc BYTEA, respondent_name_enc BYTEA, incident_date DATE, location VARCHAR(200), description_enc BYTEA, anonymous BOOLEAN, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). case_investigations (id UUID PK, report_id FK, case_number VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE, status ENUM reported/under_review/investigation/hearing/resolution/closed, assigned_coordinator_id UUID FK, assigned_investigator_id UUID FK, opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ, closed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolution_type ENUM dismissed/informal_resolution/formal_hearing/referred/withdrawn, is_clery_reportable BOOLEAN, campus_geography VARCHAR(100)). involved_parties (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, party_type ENUM complainant/respondent/witness, user_id UUID FK nullable, name_enc BYTEA, contact_enc BYTEA, notified_at TIMESTAMPTZ). case_documents (id UUID PK, case_id FK, document_name VARCHAR(200), blob_url TEXT, uploaded_by_id FK, privilege_flag BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, legal_hold_flag BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, uploaded_at). audit_logs (id UUID PK, user_id UUID, action VARCHAR(50), resource_type VARCHAR(50), resource_id UUID, before_value_hash VARCHAR(64), ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ -- append-only via PostgreSQL trigger). PostgreSQL RLS: ALTER TABLE case_investigations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY case_access ON case_investigations USING (assigned_coordinator_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)::uuid OR assigned_investigator_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)::uuid OR id IN (SELECT case_id FROM involved_parties WHERE user_id = current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)::uuid)). Same RLS pattern on case_documents. Bypass for superuser coordinator role via BYPASSRLS privilege.
target_output.api_design: All endpoints require Azure AD JWT Bearer token except POST /api/v1/reports/ (anonymous allowed). GET /api/v1/cases/ — coordinator/investigator assigned case list (RLS-filtered). GET /api/v1/cases/{id}/ — case detail with documents list (RLS-filtered). POST /api/v1/cases/{id}/documents/ — upload document to Azure Blob, create case_documents record. PATCH /api/v1/cases/{id}/status/ body {status, reason} — status transition with audit log write. GET /api/v1/cases/{id}/timeline/ — chronological audit log for case. GET /api/v1/reports/clery/?year=INT — Clery report generation for year with suppressed cell preview. POST /api/v1/reports/clery/ — finalize and save Clery report. GET /api/v1/parties/me/cases/ — party-role user's own case list. Error format: {error: STRING, code: STRING}. Pagination via cursor on all list endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Title IX data protection: PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce database-layer row visibility scoped to current_setting('app.current_user_id') matching coordinator, investigator, or involved_parties membership. Django RLS middleware sets app.current_user_id in every transaction prologue via SET LOCAL. PHI at rest: AES-256 via Azure Disk Encryption plus application-layer field encryption for name and description columns using Django-encrypted-fields. TLS 1.3 at Azure App Service. Attorney-client privilege: case_documents with privilege_flag=True excluded from all non-legal_counsel role queries via Django ViewSet filter in addition to RLS. Audit trail: audit_logs append-only enforced by PostgreSQL trigger raising exception on UPDATE/DELETE. Azure AD MFA via Conditional Access policy mandatory for all coordinator, investigator, and legal_counsel roles. Clery compliance: suppression rule for counts <= 4 prevents FERPA re-identification in annual reports. FERPA: student records within cases treated as education records; access restricted to need-to-know staff.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure App Service B2 (2 cores, 3.5 GB, Python 3.12 runtime) in university Azure tenant West US 2. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 2 vCores, 8 GB RAM, 128 GB storage with automated backups 7-day retention. Azure Cache for Redis C1 (1 GB). Azure Blob Storage private container with Azure AD access. Azure DevOps Pipeline: pytest --cov 80% gate, mypy, ruff, django-check --deploy, Docker build (or App Service ZIP deploy), staging deploy with smoke tests, compliance review approval gate in Azure DevOps Environments, production deploy. Rollback: App Service deployment slot swap to previous slot. Secrets in Azure Key Vault: Django SECRET_KEY, database password, field encryption keys. Celery worker deployed as App Service background worker dyno.
target_output.observability: Azure Application Insights: Django request traces with response time, error rate, dependency calls to PostgreSQL and Redis. Custom Application Insights events: case_status_changed, clery_report_generated, party_notification_sent, document_uploaded. Azure Monitor alerts: App Service 5xx rate > 1%, database CPU > 80%, Redis cache miss rate > 40%. Azure Sentinel analytics rule: anomalous case access — single user accessing more than 10 distinct case records within 1 hour triggers SOC alert for Title IX coordinator review. Application Insights dashboard: daily case intake volume, active investigations by status, average time in each case stage.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $650/month: Azure App Service B2 ~$75, Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server ~$150, Azure Cache for Redis C1 ~$55, Azure Blob Storage ~$10, Azure Sentinel (university contract) ~$200, Azure DevOps (included in university subscription) ~$0, Azure Key Vault ~$5, Application Insights ~$50, bandwidth ~$30. Stays within $800/month budget. Title IX case volume is low (50-200 cases/year) so App Service B2 is sufficient; database and App Service can scale vertically if volume increases.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Azure AD MFA integration, incident report intake form (anonymous and authenticated), case creation and assignment, PostgreSQL RLS policies, party access portal with RLS-scoped views, basic document upload to Blob Storage, audit_log append-only trigger, Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Clery annual report generation with cell suppression, investigation timeline event log UI, party notification email workflows via Celery and Azure Communication Services, legal hold flag workflow, admin user management panel. Phase 3 (4 weeks): VAWA/EEOC supplemental reporting, hearing scheduling calendar integration, complainant self-service status portal improvements, Azure Sentinel analytics rule tuning.
target_output.development_guidelines: Django RLS middleware must be tested with integration tests verifying that a party-role user's ORM queries return only their own case rows and that attempting to access another case returns 404. RLS bypass (BYPASSRLS) granted only to the migration user — application service user never has RLS bypass. Field encryption keys stored in Azure Key Vault; Django-encrypted-fields configured to retrieve key via Key Vault reference at startup. Clery suppress_small_cells function has unit tests verifying counts of 1, 4, and 5 produce correct suppression behavior. All status transition endpoints tested for invalid transitions (e.g., closed -> investigation). mypy strict mode; no Any in models or serializers.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostgreSQL RLS SET LOCAL not taking effect if Django ORM opens a new connection mid-transaction. Mitigation: Django middleware uses on_commit signal to verify RLS parameter is set; connection-level SET (not SET LOCAL) used as fallback during testing. Risk: Azure Blob Storage URL exposure for case documents. Mitigation: case documents served via signed URL generated server-side with 15-minute expiry rather than stored directly in the client. Risk: Clery report suppression masking systematic patterns from Title IX compliance auditors. Mitigation: suppressed report records the number of suppressed cells and the suppression threshold in metadata visible to legal counsel. Tradeoff: application-layer field encryption adds complexity over database-level encryption alone but provides defense-in-depth for the most sensitive case description and party name fields.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS with SET LOCAL over Django queryset filtering as the defense-in-depth approach for party isolation. Selected Azure Communication Services over SendGrid for party notification emails to stay within the university Azure tenant. Chose cursor-based pagination over offset pagination for case lists to ensure consistent results as cases are added during long coordinator sessions. Decided Clery suppression threshold of 4 (counts <= 4 shown as '<5') matching standard academic statistical disclosure limitation practices.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision of Title IX case management system adding PostgreSQL RLS policy definitions and Clery report cell suppression.

access_model.value: Custom JWT with researcher role; single admin account for PI; read-only analyst role for collaborators
access_model.rationale: Small team; RBAC with two roles is sufficient

feature_scope.value: Essay batch upload, rubric configuration, LLM scoring runs, inter-rater reliability computation against human gold-standard grades, cost-per-essay benchmark reports, Streamlit visualization dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: All features required to answer the research question

mvp_scope.value: Single-model scoring pipeline for one course, rubric config UI, IRR calculation against provided human scores, cost comparison table
mvp_scope.rationale: Sufficient for initial paper submission

security_baseline.value: JWT authentication, TLS via nginx reverse proxy, anonymized essay storage with pseudonymous student IDs, no raw PII persisted post-upload
security_baseline.rationale: IRB minimum safeguards for student data use

frontend_stack.value: Streamlit with Python 3.12
frontend_stack.rationale: Rapid dashboard development without separate JS frontend; appropriate for research tooling

backend_stack.rationale: Async endpoint support for long-running scoring jobs; team proficiency

data_platform.value: SQLite for experiment metadata; local filesystem for essay files and model artifacts
data_platform.rationale: Single-server deployment; no distributed data infrastructure required for prototype scale

hosting_target.value: Hetzner bare-metal GPU server (RTX 4090, 64GB RAM) accessed via university VPN
hosting_target.rationale: Commodity GPU hardware is the research hypothesis; cloud GPU would undermine the cost comparison

privacy_retention_policy.value: Anonymized essay files deleted 12 months after scoring run or when IRB protocol expires; no raw student identifiers stored
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IRB data retention requirements for anonymized educational records

future_scope.value: Multi-model comparison (Llama 3, Mistral, Phi-3), multi-course rubric library, integration with LMS grade export for automated gold-standard ingestion
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 research directions pending initial results

constraints.value: Must run entirely on local GPU hardware with no commercial model API calls during scoring; IRB protocol limits essay corpus to approved course cohorts only
constraints.rationale: Local execution is the core research constraint; IRB scopes data permissible for use

observability_baseline.value: Grafana and Prometheus for GPU utilization, inference latency, and scoring throughput; structured JSON logs to local file rotation
observability_baseline.rationale: Needed to benchmark hardware performance accurately

execution_preference.value: Iterative research sprints with biweekly PI review; no formal agile ceremonies
execution_preference.rationale: Research pace does not suit rigid sprint cadence

compliance_context.value: IRB protocol approval required before ingesting student essay data; FERPA -- essays submitted for course credit are educational records; all data handling must comply with university IRB board conditions
compliance_context.rationale: Student essay data triggers FERPA protections and IRB oversight regardless of anonymization level

llm_integration.value: Local open-source LLMs (Llama 3 8B and 70B, Mistral 7B) served via Ollama on RTX 4090; scoring prompts structured as rubric-aware chain-of-thought; no external API calls permitted
llm_integration.rationale: Using only local models is the research hypothesis being tested

project.goal: Automated essay scoring prototype using local open-source LLMs to benchmark cost vs commercial APIs
project.target_users: Ed-tech researchers and course instructors
project.timeline: Initial results in 12 weeks for conference submission

frontend.approach: Researcher dashboard for run configuration, status, results, and cost benchmarks

backend.llm: Ollama serving Llama 3-8B Q4_K_M, Llama 3-70B Q4_K_M, Mistral 7B Q4_K_M locally

security.auth: Custom JWT with 8-hour expiry validated by nginx
security.transport: TLS 1.3 via nginx reverse proxy, VPN-only network access
security.anonymization: Pseudonymous student IDs via HMAC-SHA256 of IRB participant code; no raw PII stored

data.db: SQLite in WAL mode via SQLAlchemy
data.files: Local filesystem for essay uploads and Ollama model artifacts

devops.hosting: Hetzner AX102 bare-metal, systemd service management

constraints.llm: No commercial model API calls; local GPU only
constraints.irb: Essay data limited to IRB-approved course cohorts

open_questions.multi_model_priority: Which additional models to include in phase 2 multi-model comparison

confirmed_decisions.monitoring: Grafana and Prometheus and NVIDIA DCGM Exporter

reasoner_reviews.product: The research hypothesis is well-defined: measure per-essay compute cost on commodity GPU against commercial API pricing for equivalent rubric-scored outputs. The Streamlit dashboard is appropriate for a researcher-facing tool. IRR against human gold-standard grades is the correct evaluation criterion for the paper.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with asyncio background tasks handles long-running scoring jobs without blocking the API. Ollama abstracts model loading and provides a consistent inference interface across Llama 3 and Mistral. The round 1 plan lacked model_id isolation in model_evaluations -- this must be fixed to make cross-model statistical comparisons valid. Per-essay checkpoint writes to SQLite are required to recover partial runs from Ollama crashes.
reasoner_reviews.security: The primary risk is re-identification of pseudonymized essays. HMAC-SHA256 of the IRB participant code is a sound pseudonymization approach provided the HMAC key is stored separately from the essay data. Audit logging of every data access event satisfies FERPA access tracking requirements. JWT with 8-hour expiry is appropriate for a VPN-gated research tool.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The Hetzner RTX 4090 can run Llama 3-70B at Q4_K_M quantization at approximately 10-15 tokens per second, making large batch scoring feasible within overnight windows. Cost benchmarking must account for hardware depreciation (amortized server cost) alongside per-run electricity cost to produce a valid total cost of ownership comparison against commercial APIs.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 data model mixed results from different LLMs in a single evaluations table without model_id, making the core research comparison uncomputable from the stored data. This is a blocking research design flaw, not merely a technical issue.

frontend.app_structure: Streamlit multi-page app with st.navigation: Home (active runs overview), New Scoring Run (batch upload and model and rubric selection), Run Status (live progress polling), Results (per-essay scores, IRR table, accuracy metrics), Cost Benchmark (cost per essay comparison chart), Settings (rubric library management)
frontend.pages_and_flows: New Scoring Run page: file uploader for TXT/DOCX essay batch, model dropdown (Llama3-8B, Llama3-70B, Mistral-7B), rubric selector from saved rubrics, submit triggers POST /scoring-runs; Run Status page: st.empty() polling loop against GET /scoring-runs/{id} showing completed/total essay count and estimated remaining time; Results page: st.dataframe of per-essay scores by rubric dimension, IRR metrics table, download CSV button
frontend.performance_notes: st.cache_data with 60-second TTL for SQLite result queries to avoid re-querying on every Streamlit re-run; asyncio polling on status page uses st.rerun with 5-second interval rather than blocking sleep

backend.service_design: FastAPI app with routers: /scoring-runs (CRUD), /models (list Ollama models), /rubrics (CRUD), /results (read-only), /cost (read-only). Background task queue using asyncio.Queue processes essay inference jobs sequentially per run to avoid GPU memory contention
backend.llm_integration_design: Rubric-aware prompt template: system prompt defines the grading role and rubric dimensions with point scales; user prompt contains the essay text; response format enforced via structured output requesting JSON with keys matching rubric dimension names and score_int plus rationale fields. Pydantic response model validates parsed output; re-prompts once on parse failure before marking essay as scoring_failed
backend.background_jobs: Scoring run orchestrator: dequeues essay IDs, submits each to Ollama POST /api/generate with model tag and prompt, parses response, writes model_evaluations record with model_id checkpoint, updates run progress counter, computes aggregate IRR after final essay, sets run status to completed
backend.backend_risks: Ollama service OOM on RTX 4090 during 70B model run with very long essays (over 2000 words) may kill the inference process mid-batch; mitigation: max_tokens cap on completion and max essay word count enforced at upload

security.auth_design: Custom JWT: POST /auth/login accepts researcher credentials from env-configured user table, returns signed JWT with sub (researcher_id), role (researcher or analyst), and exp (8 hours); nginx lua script validates JWT signature before proxying
security.privacy_controls: Essay ingest pipeline strips PDF/DOCX metadata (author, creator, last_modified) using PyMuPDF before storage; pseudonymous_student_id derived from HMAC-SHA256(IRB_PARTICIPANT_CODE, HMAC_KEY) where HMAC_KEY is stored in separate env file not committed to git; raw participant code not persisted after ID generation
security.audit_and_logging_controls: append-only audit.log: every POST /scoring-runs (researcher_id, run_id, model, essay_count, timestamp) and every GET /results/{run_id} (researcher_id, run_id, timestamp) written with structured JSON entries; log file mounted read-only by application after write to prevent tampering

data.entities: models (id, name, ollama_tag, parameter_count_b, quantization_level), scoring_rubrics (id, name, dimensions_json, max_score_per_dimension, created_by, created_at), scoring_runs (id, model_id FK, rubric_id FK, course_id, essay_count, completed_count, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, submitted_at, completed_at), student_essays (id, run_id FK, pseudonymous_student_id, anonymized_text, word_count, uploaded_at), model_evaluations (id, run_id FK, essay_id FK, model_id FK NOT NULL, rubric_dimension, score_int, rationale_text, inference_latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens), human_graded_samples (id, essay_id FK, rubric_dimension, human_score, grader_id), cost_benchmarks (id, run_id FK, model_id FK, total_essays, total_tokens, inference_seconds, estimated_kwh, hardware_depreciation_usd, cost_per_essay_usd, commercial_api_cost_per_essay_usd)
data.schema_notes: model_evaluations.model_id is NOT NULL FK to models -- ensures every evaluation is attributable to a specific model version; per-model accuracy queries use SELECT AVG(score_int) FROM model_evaluations WHERE model_id = ? AND rubric_dimension = ?
data.retention_and_deletion: student_essays and model_evaluations cascade-deleted when scoring_run is deleted; IRB cleanup script deletes all runs older than 12 months or when IRB protocol end date is reached

devops.deployment_topology: Hetzner AX102: nginx (443 -> :8000 FastAPI, :8501 Streamlit) -> systemd units. Ollama on port 11434 (localhost only). Prometheus on :9090 scraping FastAPI /metrics and DCGM exporter :9400. Grafana on :3000 behind nginx /grafana path
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR -- ruff linting, pytest unit tests (rubric prompt builder, IRR calculation, score parser), coverage report. No automated deployment; researcher manually pulls main and runs systemctl restart fastapi streamlit on the Hetzner server
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus metrics: scoring_run_duration_seconds (histogram), essay_inference_latency_ms (histogram by model_id label), active_scoring_runs (gauge). DCGM Exporter: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL, DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE, DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED. Grafana dashboard: GPU utilization timeline, per-model inference latency p50/p95, cost accumulation per run

ISS-001.title: Model evaluation results not isolated by model_id in data schema
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 schema stored all scoring results in a single evaluations table without model_id, making cross-model statistical comparison invalid -- the core research output depends on per-model accuracy isolation.

history.note: Identified in auditor review of data_model section

ISS-002.title: Scoring run partial failure loses all completed essay scores
ISS-002.detail: No checkpoint mechanism described; if Ollama crashes mid-run the entire batch must be re-scored from scratch, wasting GPU compute time.

history.note: Raised by architect_reasoner review

focus_issues.title: Model evaluation results not isolated by model_id in data schema
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 schema stored all scoring results in a single evaluations table without model_id, making cross-model statistical comparison invalid.

latest_recommendations: Add model_id as NOT NULL FK on model_evaluations referencing a models table and enforce it on every inference result write
Implement per-essay checkpoint writes to SQLite after each Ollama inference call so partial scoring runs are recoverable without full re-run
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Open-Source LLM Essay Scoring Prototype Round 1

convergence_state.reason: High-severity model_id isolation issue unresolved; data schema change required

previous_audits.summary: Solid research prototype architecture with appropriate technology choices for the local LLM hypothesis. The data schema weakness fundamentally undermines the core research objective of model comparison accuracy analysis.
strengths: Ollama integration for local LLM serving is well-suited to the research constraint
Prometheus DCGM exporter correctly captures GPU power metrics needed for cost benchmarking
IRB anonymization approach with pseudonymous student IDs is appropriate for FERPA compliance
concerns: model_evaluations table lacks model_id making per-model accuracy queries impossible
No checkpoint mechanism for long scoring runs -- 70B model batch may take hours
blocking_issues: model_evaluations schema lacks model_id isolation -- cross-model IRR comparison is statistically invalid without it
recommendations: Add model_id as a NOT NULL FK on model_evaluations with index for per-model aggregation queries
Implement per-essay SQLite checkpoint writes in the scoring orchestrator after each Ollama inference call

issue_updates.title: Model evaluation results not isolated by model_id
issue_updates.detail: Data model does not enforce model_id on evaluation records; all models share the same table rows without distinguishing labels
